name: ACCDATA_EL1
description: Holds the lower 32 bits of the data that is stored by an ST64BV0, Single-copy atomic 64-byte EL0 store instruction.

fields.name: ACCDATA
fields.description: Accelerator Data field. Holds bits[31:0] of the data that is stored by an ST64BV0 instruction.

name: ACTLRMASK_EL1
description: Mask register to prevent updates of fields in ACTLR_EL1 on writes to ACTLR_EL1 or ACTLRALIAS_EL1.

name: ACTLRMASK_EL2
description: Mask register to prevent updates of fields in ACTLR_EL2 on writes.

name: ACTLR_EL1
description: Provides IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED configuration and control options for execution at EL1 and EL0. Arm recommends the contents of this register have no effect on the PE when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, and instead the configuration and control fields are provided by the ACTLR_EL2 register. This avoids the need for software to manage the contents of these register when switching between a Guest OS and a Host OS.

name: ACTLR_EL2
description: Provides IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED configuration and control options for EL2. Arm recommends the contents of this register are updated to apply to EL0 when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, gaining configuration and control fields from the ACTLR_EL1 . This avoids the need for software to manage the contents of these register when switching between a Guest OS and a Host OS.

name: ACTLR_EL3
description: Provides IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED configuration and control options for EL3.

name: AFSR0_EL1
description: Provides additional IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED fault status information for exceptions taken to EL1.

name: AFSR0_EL2
description: Provides additional IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED fault status information for exceptions taken to EL2.

name: AFSR0_EL3
description: Provides additional IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED fault status information for exceptions taken to EL3.

name: AFSR1_EL1
description: Provides additional IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED fault status information for exceptions taken to EL1.

name: AFSR1_EL2
description: Provides additional IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED fault status information for exceptions taken to EL2.

name: AFSR1_EL3
description: Provides additional IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED fault status information for exceptions taken to EL3.

name: AIDR_EL1
description: Provides IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED identification information. The value of this register must be interpreted in conjunction with the value of MIDR_EL1 .

name: ALLINT
description: Allows access to the all interrupt mask bit.

fields.name: ALLINT
fields.description: All interrupt mask. An interrupt is controlled by PSTATE.ALLINT when all of the following apply: SCTLR_ELx .NMI is 1. The interrupt is targeted at ELx. Execution is at ELx.

values.label: This control does not cause any interrupts to be masked.
If SCTLR_ELx.NMI is 1 and execution is at ELx, an IRQ or FIQ interrupt that is targeted to ELx, with or without Superpriority, is masked.

name: AMAIR2_EL1
long_name: Extended Auxiliary Memory Attribute Indirection Register (EL1)
description: Provides IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED memory attributes for the memory regions specified by MAIR2_EL1 .

name: AMAIR2_EL2
long_name: Extended Auxiliary Memory Attribute Indirection Register (EL2)
description: Provides IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED memory attributes for the memory regions specified by MAIR2_EL2 .

name: AMAIR2_EL3
long_name: Extended Auxiliary Memory Attribute Indirection Register (EL3)
description: Provides IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED memory attributes for the memory regions specified by MAIR2_EL3 .

name: AMAIR_EL1
description: Provides IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED memory attributes for the memory regions specified by MAIR_EL1 .

name: AMAIR_EL2
description: Provides IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED memory attributes for the memory regions specified by MAIR_EL2 .

name: AMAIR_EL3
description: Provides IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED memory attributes for the memory regions specified by MAIR_EL3 .

name: AMCFGR_EL0
description: Global configuration register for the activity monitors. Provides information on supported features, the number of counter groups implemented, the total number of activity monitor event counters implemented, and the size of the counters. AMCFGR_EL0 is applicable to both the architected and the auxiliary counter groups.

fields.name: NCG
fields.description: Defines the number of counter groups implemented, minus one.

values.label: One counter group implemented.
Two counter groups implemented.

fields.name: HDBG
fields.description: Halt-on-debug supported. This feature must be supported, and so this bit is 0b1 .

values.label: AMCR_EL0 .HDBG is RES0 .
AMCR_EL0 .HDBG is read/write.

fields.name: SIZE
fields.description: Defines the size of the activity monitor event counters, minus one. The counters are 64-bit, so the value of this field is 0b111111 . This field is used by software to determine the spacing of the counters in the memory-map. The counters are at doubleword-aligned addresses.

fields.name: N
fields.description: Defines the number of activity monitor event counters implemented in all groups, minus one.

name: AMCG1IDR_EL0
long_name: Activity Monitors Counter Group 1 Identification Register
description: Defines which auxiliary counters are implemented, and which of them have a corresponding virtual offset register, AMEVCNTVOFF1<n>_EL2 implemented.

fields.name: AMEVCNTOFF1<n>_EL2
fields.description: Indicates which implemented auxiliary counters have a corresponding virtual offset register, AMEVCNTVOFF1<n>_EL2 implemented.

values.label: AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 does not have an offset, or is not implemented.
The offset AMEVCNTVOFF1<n>_EL2 is implemented for AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 .

fields.name: AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0
fields.description: Indicates which auxiliary counters AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 are implemented.

values.label: AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 is not implemented.
AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 is implemented.

name: AMCGCR_EL0
long_name: Activity Monitors Counter Group Configuration Register
description: Provides information on the number of activity monitor event counters implemented within each counter group.

fields.name: CG1NC
fields.description: Counter Group 1 Number of Counters. The number of counters in the auxiliary counter group.

values.label: The number of counters.

fields.name: CG0NC
fields.description: Counter Group 0 Number of Counters. The number of counters in the architected counter group.

name: AMCNTENCLR0_EL0
long_name: Activity Monitors Count Enable Clear Register 0
description: Disable control bits for the architected activity monitors event counters, AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 .

fields.name: P<n>
fields.description: Activity monitor event counter disable bit for AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 . AMCGCR_EL0 .CG0NC identifies the number of architected activity monitor event counters. In an implementation that includes FEAT_AMUv1 , the number of architected activity monitor event counters is 4. Possible values of each bit are:

values.label: When read, means that AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 is disabled. When written, has no effect.
When read, means that AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 is enabled. When written, disables AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 .

name: AMCNTENCLR1_EL0
long_name: Activity Monitors Count Enable Clear Register 1
description: Disable control bits for the auxiliary activity monitors event counters, AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 .

fields.name: P<n>
fields.description: Activity monitor event counter disable bit for AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 . Possible values of each bit are:

values.label: When read, means that AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 is disabled. When written, has no effect.
When read, means that AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 is enabled. When written, disables AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 .

name: AMCNTENSET0_EL0
long_name: Activity Monitors Count Enable Set Register 0
description: Enable control bits for the architected activity monitors event counters, AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 .

fields.name: P<n>
fields.description: Activity monitor event counter enable bit for AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 . AMCGCR_EL0 .CG0NC identifies the number of architected activity monitor event counters. In an implementation that includes FEAT_AMUv1 , the number of architected activity monitor event counters is 4. Possible values of each bit are:

values.label: When read, means that AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 is disabled. When written, has no effect.
When read, means that AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 is enabled. When written, enables AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 .

name: AMCNTENSET1_EL0
long_name: Activity Monitors Count Enable Set Register 1
description: Enable control bits for the auxiliary activity monitors event counters, AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 .

fields.name: P<n>
fields.description: Activity monitor event counter enable bit for AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 . Possible values of each bit are:

values.label: When read, means that AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 is disabled. When written, has no effect.
When read, means that AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 is enabled. When written, enables AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 .

name: AMCR_EL0
description: Global control register for the activity monitors implementation. AMCR_EL0 is applicable to both the architected and the auxiliary counter groups.

values.label: System register reads of AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 return the event count at all implemented and enabled Exception levels.
If the current Exception level is the highest implemented Exception level, system register reads of AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 return the event count. Otherwise, reads of AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 return a zero value.

fields.name: HDBG
fields.description: This bit controls whether activity monitor counting is halted when the PE is halted in Debug state.

values.label: Activity monitors do not halt counting when the PE is halted in Debug state.
Activity monitors halt counting when the PE is halted in Debug state.

name: AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0
description: Provides access to the architected activity monitor event counters.

fields.name: ACNT
fields.description: Architected activity monitor event counter n. Value of architected activity monitor event counter n, where n is the number of this register and is a number from 0 to 3. If all of the following are true, reads of the AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 registers from EL0 or EL1 return (PCount<63:0> - AMEVCNTVOFF0<n>_EL2 <63:0>), where PCount is the physical count returned when AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 is read from EL2 or EL3: FEAT_AMUv1p1 is implemented. HCR_EL2 .AMVOFFEN and SCR_EL3 .AMVOFFEN are 1. EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}.

name: AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0
description: Provides access to the auxiliary activity monitor event counters.

fields.name: ACNT
fields.description: Auxiliary activity monitor event counter n. Value of auxiliary activity monitor event counter n, where n is the number of this register and is a number from 0 to 15. If all of the following are true, reads of the AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 registers from EL0 or EL1 return (PCount<63:0> - AMEVCNTVOFF1<n>_EL2 <63:0>), where PCount is the physical count returned when AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 is read from EL2 or EL3: FEAT_AMUv1p1 is implemented. HCR_EL2 .AMVOFFEN and SCR_EL3 .AMVOFFEN are 1. AMCR_EL0 .CG1RZ is 0. EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}.

name: AMEVCNTVOFF0<n>_EL2
long_name: Activity Monitors Event Counter Virtual Offset Registers 0
description: Holds the 64-bit virtual offset for architected activity monitor events.

name: AMEVCNTVOFF1<n>_EL2
long_name: Activity Monitors Event Counter Virtual Offset Registers 1
description: Holds the 64-bit virtual offset for auxiliary activity monitor events.

name: AMEVTYPER0<n>_EL0
description: Provides information on the events that an architected activity monitor event counter AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 counts.

fields.name: evtCount
fields.description: Event to count. The event number of the event that is counted by the architected activity monitor event counter AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 . The value of this field is architecturally mandated for each architected counter. The following table shows the mapping between required event numbers and the corresponding counters:

values.label: Processor frequency cycles.
Constant frequency cycles.
Instructions retired.
Memory stall cycles.

name: AMEVTYPER1<n>_EL0
description: Provides information on the events that an auxiliary activity monitor event counter AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 counts.

fields.name: evtCount
fields.description: Event to count. The event number of the event that is counted by the auxiliary activity monitor event counter AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 . It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED what values are supported by each counter. If software writes a value to this field which is not supported by the corresponding counter AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 , then: It is UNPREDICTABLE which event will be counted. The value read back is UNKNOWN . The event counted by AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 might be fixed at implementation. In this case, the field is read-only and writes are UNDEFINED . If the corresponding counter AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 is enabled, writes to this register have UNPREDICTABLE results.

name: AMUSERENR_EL0
description: Global user enable register for the activity monitors. Enables or disables EL0 access to the activity monitors. AMUSERENR_EL0 is applicable to both the architected and the auxiliary counter groups.

fields.name: EN
fields.description: Traps EL0 accesses to the activity monitors registers to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to the following registers are trapped, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : AMCFGR_EL0 , AMCGCR_EL0 , AMCNTENCLR0_EL0 , AMCNTENCLR1_EL0 , AMCNTENSET0_EL0 , AMCNTENSET1_EL0 , AMCR_EL0 , AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 , AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 , AMEVTYPER0<n>_EL0 , and AMEVTYPER1<n>_EL0 . In AArch32 state, MRC and MCR accesses to the following registers are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 , MRRC and MCRR accesses are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x04 : AMCFGR , AMCGCR , AMCNTENCLR0 , AMCNTENCLR1 , AMCNTENSET0 , AMCNTENSET1 , AMCR , AMEVCNTR0<n> , AMEVCNTR1<n> , AMEVTYPER0<n> , and AMEVTYPER1<n> .

values.label: EL0 accesses to the activity monitors registers are trapped.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped. Software can access all activity monitor registers at EL0.

name: APAS
description: Associate a location with a specified PA space, for locations that are protected by a memory-side PAS filter.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with the NSE field, selects the target physical address space. NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 When Secure state is implemented, Secure. Otherwise reserved. 0b0 0b1 Non-secure. 0b1 0b0 Root. 0b1 0b1 Realm.

fields.name: NSE
fields.description: Together with the NS field, selects the target physical address space. For a description of the values derived by evaluating NS and NSE together, see .NS.

fields.name: PA
fields.description: Bits [55:6] of the Physical Address. Bits of Xt corresponding to physical address bits above the implemented physical address size indicated in ID_AA64MMFR0_EL1 .PARange are RES0 . For example, if 44 bits of PA space are implemented, then Xt[55:44] are RES0 .

fields.name: TargetAttributes
fields.description: The Target Attributes field is encoded as follows:

values.label: Default behavior applies.
IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .

name: APDAKeyHi_EL1
long_name: Pointer Authentication Key A for Data (bits[127:64])
description: Holds bits[127:64] of key A used for authentication of data pointer values. The term APDAKey_EL1 is used to describe the concatenation of APDAKeyHi_EL1 : APDAKeyLo_EL1 .

fields.name: APDAKeyHi
fields.description: 64 bit value, bits[127:64] of the 128 bit pointer authentication key value.

name: APDAKeyLo_EL1
long_name: Pointer Authentication Key A for Data (bits[63:0])
description: Holds bits[63:0] of key A used for authentication of data pointer values. The term APDAKey_EL1 is used to describe the concatenation of APDAKeyHi_EL1 : APDAKeyLo_EL1 .

fields.name: APDAKeyLo
fields.description: 64 bit value, bits[63:0] of the 128 bit pointer authentication key value.

name: APDBKeyHi_EL1
long_name: Pointer Authentication Key B for Data (bits[127:64])
description: Holds bits[127:64] of key B used for authentication of data pointer values. The term APDBKey_EL1 is used to describe the concatenation of APDBKeyHi_EL1 : APDBKeyLo_EL1 .

fields.name: APDBKeyHi
fields.description: 64 bit value, bits[127:64] of the 128 bit pointer authentication key value.

name: APDBKeyLo_EL1
long_name: Pointer Authentication Key B for Data (bits[63:0])
description: Holds bits[63:0] of key B used for authentication of data pointer values. The term APDBKey_EL1 is used to describe the concatenation of APDBKeyHi_EL1 : APDBKeyLo_EL1 .

fields.name: APDBKeyLo
fields.description: 64 bit value, bits[63:0] of the 128 bit pointer authentication key value.

name: APGAKeyHi_EL1
long_name: Pointer Authentication Key A for Code (bits[127:64])
description: Holds bits[127:64] of key used for generic pointer authentication code. The term APGAKey_EL1 is used to describe the concatenation of APGAKeyHi_EL1 : APGAKeyLo_EL1 .

fields.name: APGAKeyHi
fields.description: 64 bit value, bits[127:64] of the 128 bit pointer authentication key value.

name: APGAKeyLo_EL1
long_name: Pointer Authentication Key A for Code (bits[63:0])
description: Holds bits[63:0] of key used for generic pointer authentication code. The term APGAKey_EL1 is used to describe the concatenation of APGAKeyHi_EL1 : APGAKeyLo_EL1 .

fields.name: APGAKeyLo
fields.description: 64 bit value, bits[63:0] of the 128 bit pointer authentication key value.

name: APIAKeyHi_EL1
long_name: Pointer Authentication Key A for Instruction (bits[127:64])
description: Holds bits[127:64] of key A used for authentication of instruction pointer values. The term APIAKey_EL1 is used to describe the concatenation of APIAKeyHi_EL1 : APIAKeyLo_EL1 .

fields.name: APIAKeyHi
fields.description: 64 bit value, bits[127:64] of the 128 bit pointer authentication key value.

name: APIAKeyLo_EL1
long_name: Pointer Authentication Key A for Instruction (bits[63:0])
description: Holds bits[63:0] of key A used for authentication of instruction pointer values. The term APIAKey_EL1 is used to describe the concatenation of APIAKeyHi_EL1 : APIAKeyLo_EL1 .

fields.name: APIAKeyLo
fields.description: 64 bit value, bits[63:0] of the 128 bit pointer authentication key value.

name: APIBKeyHi_EL1
long_name: Pointer Authentication Key B for Instruction (bits[127:64])
description: Holds bits[127:64] of key B used for authentication of instruction pointer values. The term APIBKey_EL1 is used to describe the concatenation of APIBKeyHi_EL1 : APIBKeyLo_EL1 .

fields.name: APIBKeyHi
fields.description: 64 bit value, bits[127:64] of the 128 bit pointer authentication key value.

name: APIBKeyLo_EL1
long_name: Pointer Authentication Key B for Instruction (bits[63:0])
description: Holds bits[63:0] of key B used for authentication of instruction pointer values. The term APIBKey_EL1 is used to describe the concatenation of APIBKeyHi_EL1 : APIBKeyLo_EL1 .

fields.name: APIBKeyLo
fields.description: 64 bit value, bits[63:0] of the 128 bit pointer authentication key value.

name: AT S12E0R
long_name: Address Translate Stages 1 and 2 EL0 Read
description: Performs stage 1 and 2 address translation, with permissions as if reading from the given virtual address from EL0, using the following translation regime: When EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Security state described by the current Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS}: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, the EL1&0 translation regime. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, the EL2&0 translation regime. Otherwise, the EL1&0 translation regime. When FEAT_RME is implemented, if the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS} is a reserved value, this instruction is UNDEFINED at EL3.

fields.name: IA
fields.description: Input address for translation. The resulting address can be read from the PAR_EL1 . If the address translation instructions are targeting a translation regime that is using AArch32, and so has a VA of only 32 bits, then VA[63:32] is RES0 .

name: AT S12E0W
long_name: Address Translate Stages 1 and 2 EL0 Write
description: Performs stage 1 and 2 address translation, with permissions as if writing to the given virtual address from EL0, using the following translation regime: When EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Security state described by the current Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS}: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, the EL1&0 translation regime. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, the EL2&0 translation regime. Otherwise, the EL1&0 translation regime. When FEAT_RME is implemented, if the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS} is a reserved value, this instruction is UNDEFINED at EL3.

fields.name: IA
fields.description: Input address for translation. The resulting address can be read from the PAR_EL1 . If the address translation instructions are targeting a translation regime that is using AArch32, and so has a VA of only 32 bits, then VA[63:32] is RES0 .

name: AT S12E1R
long_name: Address Translate Stages 1 and 2 EL1 Read
description: Performs stage 1 and 2 address translation, with permissions as if reading from the given virtual address from EL1, or from EL2 if the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, using the following translation regime: When EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Security state described by the current Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS}: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, the EL2&0 translation regime, accessed from EL2. Otherwise, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. When FEAT_RME is implemented, if the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS} is a reserved value, this instruction is UNDEFINED at EL3.

fields.name: IA
fields.description: Input address for translation. The resulting address can be read from the PAR_EL1 . If the address translation instructions are targeting a translation regime that is using AArch32, and so has a VA of only 32 bits, then VA[63:32] is RES0 .

name: AT S12E1W
long_name: Address Translate Stages 1 and 2 EL1 Write
description: Performs stage 1 and 2 address translation, with permissions as if writing to the given virtual address from EL1, or from EL2 if the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, using the following translation regime: When EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Security state described by the current Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS}: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, the EL2&0 translation regime, accessed from EL2. Otherwise, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. When FEAT_RME is implemented, if the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS} is a reserved value, this instruction is UNDEFINED at EL3.

fields.name: IA
fields.description: Input address for translation. The resulting address can be read from the PAR_EL1 . If the address translation instructions are targeting a translation regime that is using AArch32, and so has a VA of only 32 bits, then VA[63:32] is RES0 .

name: AT S1E0R
description: Performs stage 1 address translation, with permissions as if reading from the given virtual address from EL0, using the following translation regime: When EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Security state described by the current Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS}: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, the EL1&0 translation regime. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, the EL2&0 translation regime. Otherwise, the EL1&0 translation regime. When FEAT_RME is implemented, if the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS} is a reserved value, this instruction is UNDEFINED at EL3.

fields.name: IA
fields.description: Input address for translation. The resulting address can be read from the PAR_EL1 . If the address translation instructions are targeting a translation regime that is using AArch32, and so has a VA of only 32 bits, then VA[63:32] is RES0 .

name: AT S1E0W
description: Performs stage 1 address translation, with permissions as if writing to the given virtual address from EL0, using the following translation regime: When EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Security state described by the current Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS}: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, the EL1&0 translation regime. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, the EL2&0 translation regime. Otherwise, the EL1&0 translation regime. When FEAT_RME is implemented, if the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS} is a reserved value, this instruction is UNDEFINED at EL3.

fields.name: IA
fields.description: Input address for translation. The resulting address can be read from the PAR_EL1 . If the address translation instructions are targeting a translation regime that is using AArch32, and so has a VA of only 32 bits, then VA[63:32] is RES0 .

name: AT S1E1A
long_name: Address Translate Stage 1 EL1 Without Permission checks
description: Performs a stage 1 address translation, while ignoring the permission checks using the following translation regime: When EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Security state described by the current Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS}: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, the EL2&0 translation regime, accessed from EL2. Otherwise, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. When FEAT_RME is implemented, if the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS} is a reserved value, this instruction is UNDEFINED at EL3.

fields.name: IA
fields.description: Input address for translation. The resulting address can be read from the PAR_EL1 .

name: AT S1E1R
description: Performs stage 1 address translation, with permissions as if reading from the given virtual address from EL1, or from EL2 if the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, using the following translation regime: When EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Security state described by the current Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS}: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, the EL2&0 translation regime, accessed from EL2. Otherwise, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. When FEAT_RME is implemented, if the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS} is a reserved value, this instruction is UNDEFINED at EL3.

fields.name: IA
fields.description: Input address for translation. The resulting address can be read from the PAR_EL1 . If the address translation instructions are targeting a translation regime that is using AArch32, and so has a VA of only 32 bits, then VA[63:32] is RES0 .

name: AT S1E1RP
long_name: Address Translate Stage 1 EL1 Read PAN
description: Performs a stage 1 address translation, where the value of PSTATE.PAN determines if a read from a location will generate a Permission fault for a privileged access, using the following translation regime: When EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Security state described by the current Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS}: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, the EL2&0 translation regime, accessed from EL2. Otherwise, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. When FEAT_RME is implemented, if the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS} is a reserved value, this instruction is UNDEFINED at EL3.

fields.name: IA
fields.description: Input address for translation. The resulting address can be read from the PAR_EL1 . If the address translation instructions are targeting a translation regime that is using AArch32, and so has a VA of only 32 bits, then VA[63:32] is RES0 .

name: AT S1E1W
description: Performs stage 1 address translation, with permissions as if writing to the given virtual address from EL1, or from EL2 if the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, using the following translation regime: When EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Security state described by the current Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS}: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, the EL2&0 translation regime, accessed from EL2. Otherwise, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. When FEAT_RME is implemented, if the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS} is a reserved value, this instruction is UNDEFINED at EL3.

fields.name: IA
fields.description: Input address for translation. The resulting address can be read from the PAR_EL1 . If the address translation instructions are targeting a translation regime that is using AArch32, and so has a VA of only 32 bits, then VA[63:32] is RES0 .

name: AT S1E1WP
long_name: Address Translate Stage 1 EL1 Write PAN
description: Performs a stage 1 address translation, where the value of PSTATE.PAN determines if a write to a location will generate a Permission fault for a privileged access, using the following translation regime: When EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Security state described by the current Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS}: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, the EL2&0 translation regime, accessed from EL2. Otherwise, the EL1&0 translation regime, accessed from EL1. When FEAT_RME is implemented, if the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS} is a reserved value, this instruction is UNDEFINED at EL3.

fields.name: IA
fields.description: Input address for translation. The resulting address can be read from the PAR_EL1 . If the address translation instructions are targeting a translation regime that is using AArch32, and so has a VA of only 32 bits, then VA[63:32] is RES0 .

name: AT S1E2A
long_name: Address Translate Stage 1 EL2 Without Permission checks
description: Performs stage 1 address translation as defined for EL2, while ignoring permissions checks from the given virtual address. When FEAT_RME is implemented, if the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .{NSE, NS} is a reserved value, this instruction is UNDEFINED at EL3.

fields.name: IA

name: BRB IALL

name: BRB INJ

name: BRBCR_EL1
description: Controls the Branch Record Buffer.

fields.name: EXCEPTION
fields.description: Enable the recording of entry to EL1 via an exception.

values.label: Disable the recording of Branch records for exceptions when taken to EL1.
Enable the recording of Branch records for exceptions when taken to EL1.

fields.name: ERTN
fields.description: Allow the recording Branch records for exception return instructions from EL1.

values.label: Disable the recording Branch records for exception return instructions from EL1.
Enable the recording Branch records for exception return instructions from EL1.
Branch recording is not affected by this control.
If either EL2 is not implemented or BRBCR_EL2 .FZPSS is 1, then a BRBE freeze event occurs when a successful Capture event occurs.
Branch recording is not affected by this control.

A BRBE freeze event occurs when the PE is in a Non-prohibited region, BRBFCR_EL1 .PAUSED is 0, and any of the following applies: For any event counter PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 in the first range, PMOVSCLR_EL0 [m] is 1, and either FEAT_SEBEP is not implemented or PMEVTYPER<m>_EL0 .SYNC is 0. FEAT_PMUv3_ICNTR is implemented, PMOVSCLR_EL0 .F0 is 1, and either FEAT_SEBEP is not implemented or PMICFILTR_EL0 .SYNC is 0. For more information about event counter ranges, see MDCR_EL2 .HPMN.
Virtual timestamp. The BRBE recorded timestamp is the physical counter value, minus the value of CNTVOFF_EL2 .

Guest physical timestamp. The BRBE recorded timestamp is the physical counter value minus a physical offset. If any of the following are true, the physical offset is zero, otherwise the physical offset is the value of CNTPOFF_EL2 : EL3 is implemented and SCR_EL3 .ECVEn == 0. EL2 is implemented and CNTHCTL_EL2 .ECV == 0. FEAT_ECV_POFF is not implemented.
Physical timestamp. The BRBE recorded timestamp is the physical counter value.
Disable the recording of mispredict information.
Allow the recording of mispredict information.

values.label: Disable the recording of cycle count information.
Allow the recording of cycle count information.

fields.name: CC
fields.description: The number of PE clock cycles since the last Branch record entry. The format of this field uses a mantissa and exponent to express the cycle count value, as follows: CC bits[7:0] indicate the mantissa M. CC bits[13:8] indicate the exponent E. The cycle count is expressed using the following function: if IsZero(E), then UInt(M) else UInt('1':M:Zeros(UInt(E)-1)) If required, the cycle count is rounded to a multiple of 2 (E-1) towards zero before being encoded. A value of all ones in both the mantissa and exponent indicates the cycle count value exceeded the size of the cycle counter.

name: BRBSRC<n>_EL1
long_name: Branch Record Buffer Source Address Register <n>
description: The source address of Branch record n + ( BRBFCR_EL1 .BANK × 32).

fields.name: ADDRESS
fields.description: Source virtual address of the Branch record. When an indirect write occurs with a value with ADDRESS bits [63:P] indicating an invalid address, an UNKNOWN value which indicates an invalid address is written to bits [63:P]. An invalid address is: For an address in a translation regime with 2 VA ranges, with bits [63:P] not all zeroes or all ones. For an address in a translation regime with a single VA range, with bits [63:P] not all zeroes. P is defined as: 56, when FEAT_LVA3 is implemented. 52, when FEAT_LVA is implemented. 48, otherwise. The value in bits [P-1:0] is the value written. When an indirect write occurs with a value with ADDRESS bits [63:P] indicating a valid address, the written value is written to bits [63:0], and a read of the register returns the written value.

name: BRBSRCINJ_EL1
long_name: Branch Record Buffer Source Address Injection Register
description: The source address of a Branch record for injection.

fields.name: ADDRESS
fields.description: Source virtual address of the Branch record. When a direct write occurs with a value with ADDRESS bits [63:P] indicating an invalid address, an UNKNOWN value which indicates an invalid address is written to bits [63:P]. An invalid address is: For an address in a translation regime with 2 VA ranges, with bits [63:P] not all zeroes or all ones. For an address in a translation regime with a single VA range, with bits [63:P] not all zeroes. P is defined as: 56, when FEAT_LVA3 is implemented. 52, when FEAT_LVA is implemented. 48, otherwise. The value in bits [P-1:0] is the value written. When a direct write occurs with a value with ADDRESS bits [63:P] indicating a valid address, the written value is written to bits [63:0], and a read of the register returns the written value.

fields.name: ADDRESS
fields.description: Target virtual address of the Branch record. When an indirect write occurs with a value with ADDRESS bits [63:P] indicating an invalid address, an UNKNOWN value which indicates an invalid address is written to bits [63:P]. An invalid address is: For an address in a translation regime with 2 VA ranges, with bits [63:P] not all zeroes or all ones. For an address in a translation regime with a single VA range, with bits [63:P] not all zeroes. P is defined as: 56, when FEAT_LVA3 is implemented. 52, when FEAT_LVA is implemented. 48, otherwise. The value in bits [P-1:0] is the value written. When an indirect write occurs with a value with ADDRESS bits [63:P] indicating a valid address, the written value is written to bits [63:0], and a read of the register returns the written value.

name: BRBTS_EL1
description: Captures the Timestamp value on a BRBE freeze event.

fields.name: TS
fields.description: Timestamp value at the time of a BRBE freeze event.

name: CCSIDR2_EL1
description: Provides the information about the architecture of the currently selected cache from bits[63:32] of CCSIDR_EL1 .

fields.name: NumSets
fields.description: (Number of sets in cache) - 1, therefore a value of 0 indicates 1 set in the cache. The number of sets does not have to be a power of 2.

name: CCSIDR_EL1
description: Provides information about the architecture of the currently selected cache.

fields.name: NumSets
fields.description: (Number of sets in cache) - 1, therefore a value of 0 indicates 1 set in the cache. The number of sets does not have to be a power of 2.

fields.name: Associativity
fields.description: (Associativity of cache) - 1, therefore a value of 0 indicates an associativity of 1. The associativity does not have to be a power of 2.

fields.name: LineSize
fields.description: (Log 2 (Number of bytes in cache line)) - 4. For example: For a line length of 16 bytes: Log 2 (16) = 4, LineSize entry = 0. This is the minimum line length. For a line length of 32 bytes: Log 2 (32) = 5, LineSize entry = 1. The C++ 17 specification has two defined parameters relating to the granularity of memory that does not interfere. For generic software and tools, Arm will set the hardware_destructive_interference_size parameter to 256 bytes and the hardware_constructive_interference_size parameter to 64 bytes. When FEAT_MTE2 is implemented, where a cache only holds Allocation tags, this field is RES0 .

name: CFP RCTX
long_name: Control Flow Prediction Restriction by Context
description: Control Flow Prediction Restriction by Context applies to all Control Flow Prediction Resources that predict execution based on information gathered within the target execution context or contexts. Control flow predictions determined by the actions of code in the target execution context or contexts appearing in program order before the instruction cannot exploitatively control speculative execution occurring after the instruction is complete and synchronized. This instruction is guaranteed to be complete following a DSB that covers both read and write behavior on the same PE as executed the original restriction instruction, and a subsequent context synchronization event is required to ensure that the effect of the completion of the instructions is synchronized to the current execution. This instruction does not require the invalidation of prediction structures so long as the behavior described for completion of this instruction is met by the implementation. On some implementations the instruction is likely to take a significant number of cycles to execute. This instruction is expected to be used very rarely, such as on the roll-over of an ASID or VMID, but should not be used on every context switch.

fields.name: GVMID
fields.description: Execution of this instruction applies to all VMIDs or a specified VMID.

values.label: Applies to specified VMID for an EL0 or EL1 target execution context.
Applies to all VMIDs for an EL0 or EL1 target execution context.

fields.name: VMID
fields.description: Only applies when bit[48] is 0 and the target execution context is either: EL1. EL0 when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}. Otherwise this field is RES0 . When the instruction is executed at EL1, this field is treated as the current VMID. When the instruction is executed at EL0 and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, this field is treated as the current VMID. When the instruction is executed at EL0 and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, this field is ignored. If EL2 is not implemented or not enabled for the target Security state, this field is RES0 . If the implementation supports 16 bits of VMID, then the upper 8 bits of the VMID must be written to 0 by software when the context being affected only uses 8 bits.

fields.name: NSE
fields.description: Together with the NS field, selects the Security state. For a description of the values derived by evaluating NS and NSE together, see CFP_RCTX.NS.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with the NSE field, selects the Security state. NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 When Secure state is implemented, Secure. Otherwise reserved. 0b0 0b1 Non-secure. 0b1 0b0 Root. 0b1 0b1 Realm.

values.label: Secure state.
Non-secure state.

fields.name: EL
fields.description: Exception Level. Indicates the Exception level of the target execution context.

values.label: EL0.
EL1
EL2
EL3

fields.name: GASID
fields.description: Execution of this instruction applies to all ASIDs or a specified ASID.

values.label: Applies to specified ASID for an EL0 target execution context.
Applies to all ASIDs for an EL0 target execution context.

fields.name: ASID
fields.description: Only applies for an EL0 target execution context and when bit[16] is 0. Otherwise, this field is RES0 . When the instruction is executed at EL0, this field is treated as the current ASID. If the implementation supports 16 bits of ASID, then the upper 8 bits of the ASID must be written to 0 by software when the context being affected only uses 8 bits.

name: CLIDR_EL1
description: Identifies the type of cache, or caches, that are implemented at each level and can be managed using the architected cache maintenance instructions that operate by set/way, up to a maximum of seven levels. Also identifies the Level of Coherence (LoC) and Level of Unification (LoU) for the cache hierarchy.

fields.name: Ttype<n>
fields.description: Tag cache type. Indicate the type of cache that is implemented and can be managed using the architected cache maintenance instructions that operate by set/way at each level, from Level 1 up to a maximum of seven levels of cache hierarchy.

values.label: No Tag Cache.
Separate Allocation Tag Cache.
Unified Allocation Tag and Data cache, Allocation Tags and Data in unified lines.
Unified Allocation Tag and Data cache, Allocation Tags and Data in separate lines.

fields.name: ICB
fields.description: Inner cache boundary. This field indicates the boundary for caching Inner Cacheable memory regions.

values.label: Not disclosed by this mechanism.
L1 cache is the highest Inner Cacheable level.
L2 cache is the highest Inner Cacheable level.
L3 cache is the highest Inner Cacheable level.
L4 cache is the highest Inner Cacheable level.
L5 cache is the highest Inner Cacheable level.
L6 cache is the highest Inner Cacheable level.
L7 cache is the highest Inner Cacheable level.

fields.name: LoUU
fields.description: Level of Unification Uniprocessor for the cache hierarchy. For a description of the values of this field, see Terminology for Clean, Invalidate, and Clean and Invalidate instructions . This field does not describe the requirements for instruction cache invalidation. See CTR_EL0 .DIC. When FEAT_S2FWB is implemented, the architecture requires that this field is zero so that no levels of data cache need to be cleaned in order to manage coherency with instruction fetches.

fields.name: LoC
fields.description: Level of Coherence for the cache hierarchy. For a description of the values of this field, see Terminology for Clean, Invalidate, and Clean and Invalidate instructions .

fields.name: LoUIS
fields.description: Level of Unification Inner Shareable for the cache hierarchy. For a description of the values of this field, see Terminology for Clean, Invalidate, and Clean and Invalidate instructions . This field does not describe the requirements for instruction cache invalidation. See CTR_EL0 .DIC. When FEAT_S2FWB is implemented, the architecture requires that this field is zero so that no levels of data cache need to be cleaned in order to manage coherency with instruction fetches.

fields.name: Ctype<n>
fields.description: Cache Type fields. Indicate the type of cache that is implemented and can be managed using the architected cache maintenance instructions that operate by set/way at each level, from Level 1 up to a maximum of seven levels of cache hierarchy. Possible values of each field are:

values.label: No cache.
Instruction cache only.
Data cache only.
Separate instruction and data caches.
Unified cache.

name: CNTFRQ_EL0
description: This register is provided so that software can discover the effective frequency of the system counter. It must be programmed with this value as part of system initialization. The value of the register is not interpreted by hardware.

fields.name: ClockFreq
fields.description: Clock frequency. Indicates the effective frequency of the system counter, in Hz.

name: CNTHCTL_EL2
description: Controls the generation of an event stream from the physical counter, and access from EL1 to the physical counter and the EL1 physical timer.

fields.name: CNTPMASK
fields.description: This bit is RES0 in Non-secure and Secure state.

values.label: This control has no effect on CNTP_CTL_EL0 .IMASK.
CNTP_CTL_EL0 .IMASK behaves as if set to 1 for all purposes other than a direct read of the field.

fields.name: CNTVMASK
fields.description: This bit is RES0 in Non-secure and Secure state.

values.label: This control has no effect on CNTV_CTL_EL0 .IMASK.
CNTV_CTL_EL0 .IMASK behaves as if set to 1 for all purposes other than a direct read of the field.

fields.name: EVNTIS
fields.description: Controls the scale of the generation of the event stream.

values.label: The CNTHCTL_EL2.EVNTI field applies to CNTPCT_EL0 [15:0].
The CNTHCTL_EL2.EVNTI field applies to CNTPCT_EL0 [23:8].

fields.name: EL1NVVCT
fields.description: When HCR_EL2 .TGE is 0 and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{NV2, NV1, NV} is {1, 0, 1}, traps EL1 accesses to the specified EL1 virtual timer registers using the EL02 descriptors to EL2 as follows: Accesses to CNTV_CTL_EL02 and CNTV_CVAL_EL02 are trapped to EL2 and reported with EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
EL1 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL2.

fields.name: EL1NVPCT
fields.description: When HCR_EL2 .TGE is 0 and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{NV2, NV1, NV} is {1, 0, 1}, traps EL1 accesses to the specified EL1 physical timer registers using the EL02 descriptors to EL2 as follows: Accesses to CNTP_CTL_EL02 and CNTP_CVAL_EL02 are trapped to EL2 and reported with EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
EL1 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL2.

fields.name: EL1TVCT
fields.description: Traps EL0 and EL1 accesses to the EL1 virtual counter registers to EL2 when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state, as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to CNTVCT_EL0 and CNTVCTSS_EL0 are trapped to EL2 and reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 , unless they are trapped by CNTKCTL_EL1 .EL0VCTEN. In AArch32 state, accesses to CNTVCT are trapped to EL2 and reported with EC syndrome value 0x04 , unless they are trapped by CNTKCTL_EL1 .EL0VCTEN or CNTKCTL .PL0VCTEN.

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
EL0 and EL1 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL2.

fields.name: EL1TVT
fields.description: When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, traps EL0 and EL1 accesses to the EL1 virtual timer registers to EL2, when EL2 is enabled for the current Security state, as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to the following registers are trapped to EL2 and reported with EC syndrome value 0x18 , unless they are trapped by CNTKCTL_EL1 .EL0VTEN: CNTV_CTL_EL0 . CNTV_CVAL_EL0 . CNTV_TVAL_EL0 . In AArch32 state, MRC and MCR accesses to the following registers are trapped to EL2 and reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 , and MCRR and MRRC accesses are trapped to EL2 and reported using EC syndrome value 0x04 , unless they are trapped by CNTKCTL_EL1 .EL0VTEN or CNTKCTL .PL0VTEN: CNTV_CTL . CNTV_CVAL . CNTV_TVAL .

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
EL0 and EL1 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL2.

fields.name: ECV
fields.description: Enables the Enhanced Counter Virtualization functionality registers. When the Enhanced Counter Virtualization is enabled, the behavior is as follows: An MRS to CNTPCT_EL0 from either EL0 or EL1 that is not trapped will return the value ( PCount <63:0> - CNTPOFF_EL2 <63:0>). The EL1 physical timer interrupt is triggered when (( PCount <63:0> - CNTPOFF_EL2 <63:0>) - PCVal <63:0>) is greater than or equal to 0. PCount <63:0> is the physical count returned when CNTPCT_EL0 is read from EL2 or EL3. PCVal <63:0> is the EL1 physical timer compare value for this timer.

values.label: Enhanced Counter Virtualization functionality is disabled.
Enhanced Counter Virtualization functionality is enabled.

fields.name: EL1PTEN
fields.description: When HCR_EL2 .TGE is 0, traps EL0 and EL1 accesses to the EL1 physical timer registers to EL2 when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state, as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to the following registers are trapped to EL2 and reported with EC syndrome value 0x18 , unless they are trapped by CNTKCTL_EL1 .EL0PTEN: CNTP_CTL_EL0 . CNTP_CVAL_EL0 . CNTP_TVAL_EL0 . CNTP_CVAL . CNTP_TVAL .

values.label: EL0 and EL1 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL2.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.description: When HCR_EL2 .TGE is 0, traps EL0 and EL1 accesses to the EL1 physical counter registers to EL2 when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state, as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to CNTPCT_EL0 and CNTPCTSS_EL0 are trapped to EL2 and reported with EC syndrome value 0x18 , unless they are trapped by CNTKCTL_EL1 .EL0PCTEN. In AArch32 state, MRRC or MCRR accesses to CNTPCT are trapped to EL2 and reported with EC syndrome value 0x04 , unless they are trapped by CNTKCTL_EL1 .EL0PCTEN or CNTKCTL .PL0PCTEN.

values.label: EL0 and EL1 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL2.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

CNTP_CVAL_EL0 . CNTP_TVAL_EL0 . fields.name: EL0VTEN

CNTV_CVAL_EL0 . CNTV_TVAL_EL0 . In AArch32 state, MRC and MCR accesses to the following registers are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 and MRRC and MCRR accesses are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x04 : CNTV_CTL . CNTV_CVAL . CNTV_TVAL .

values.label: EL0 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL2.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: EVNTI
fields.description: Selects which bit of CNTPCT_EL0 , as seen from EL2, is the trigger for the event stream generated from that counter when that stream is enabled. If FEAT_ECV is implemented, and CNTHCTL_EL2.EVNTIS is 1, this field selects a trigger bit in the range 8 to 23 of CNTPCT_EL0 . Otherwise, this field selects a trigger bit in the range 0 to 15 of CNTPCT_EL0 .

fields.name: EVNTDIR
fields.description: Controls which transition of the CNTPCT_EL0 trigger bit, as seen from EL2 and defined by EVNTI, generates an event when the event stream is enabled.

values.label: A 0 to 1 transition of the trigger bit triggers an event.
A 1 to 0 transition of the trigger bit triggers an event.

fields.name: EVNTEN
fields.description: Enables the generation of an event stream from CNTPCT_EL0 as seen from EL2.

values.label: Disables the event stream.
Enables the event stream.

fields.name: EL0VCTEN
fields.description: When HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, traps EL0 accesses to the frequency register and virtual counter registers to EL2, as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to the following registers are trapped to EL2 and reported with EC syndrome value 0x18 : CNTVCT_EL0 . CNTVCTSS_EL0 . CNTFRQ_EL0 if CNTHCTL_EL2 .EL0PCTEN is 0. In AArch32 state, MRC and MCR accesses to the following registers are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 and MRRC and MCRR accesses are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x04 : CNTVCT and if CNTHCTL .EL0PCTEN is 0, CNTFRQ .

values.label: EL0 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL2.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: EL0PCTEN
fields.description: Traps EL0 accesses to the frequency register and physical counter registers to EL2, as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to the following registers are trapped to EL2 and reported with EC syndrome value 0x18 : CNTPCT_EL0 . CNTPCTSS_EL0 . CNTFRQ_EL0 if CNTHCTL_EL2 .EL0VCTEN is 0. In AArch32 state, MRC and MCR accesses to the following registers are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 and MRRC and MCRR accesses are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x04 : CNTPCT and if CNTHCTL_EL2 .EL0VCTEN is 0, CNTFRQ .

values.label: From AArch64 state: EL0 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL2.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

name: CNTHPS_CTL_EL2
long_name: Counter-timer Secure Physical Timer Control Register (EL2)
description: Control register for the Secure EL2 physical timer.

fields.name: ISTATUS
fields.description: The status of the timer. This bit indicates whether the timer condition is met:

values.label: Timer condition is not met.
Timer condition is met.

fields.name: IMASK
fields.description: Timer interrupt mask bit. Permitted values are:

values.label: Timer interrupt is not masked by the IMASK bit.
Timer interrupt is masked by the IMASK bit.

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description: Enables the timer. Permitted values are:

values.label: Timer disabled.
Timer enabled.

name: CNTHPS_CVAL_EL2
long_name: Counter-timer Secure Physical Timer CompareValue Register (EL2)
description: Holds the compare value for the Secure EL2 physical timer.

fields.name: CompareValue
fields.description: Holds the EL2 physical timer CompareValue. When CNTHPS_CTL_EL2 .ENABLE is 1, and TimerConditionMet is TRUE for the Secure EL2 physical timer, the timer condition is met and all of the following are true: CNTHPS_CTL_EL2 .ISTATUS is set to 1. If CNTHPS_CTL_EL2 .IMASK is 0, an interrupt is generated. TimerConditionMet is defined by 'Operation of the CompareValue views of the timers' . The CompareValue view of the timer acts like a 64-bit upcounter timer. When CNTHPS_CTL_EL2 .ENABLE is 0, the timer condition is not met, but CNTPCT_EL0 continues to count. If the Generic counter is implemented at a size less than 64 bits, then this field is permitted to be implemented at the same width as the counter, and the upper bits are RES0 . The value of this field is treated as zero-extended in all counter calculations.

name: CNTHPS_TVAL_EL2
long_name: Counter-timer Secure Physical Timer TimerValue Register (EL2)
description: Holds the timer value for the Secure EL2 physical timer.

fields.name: TimerValue
fields.description: The TimerValue view of the EL2 physical timer. On a read of this register: If CNTHPS_CTL_EL2 .ENABLE is 0, the value returned is UNKNOWN . If CNTHPS_CTL_EL2 .ENABLE is 1, the value returned is ( CNTHPS_CVAL_EL2 - CNTPCT_EL0 ). On a write of this register, CNTHPS_CVAL_EL2 is set to ( CNTPCT_EL0 + TimerValue), where TimerValue is treated as a signed 32-bit integer. When CNTHPS_CTL_EL2 .ENABLE is 1, the timer condition is met when ( CNTPCT_EL0 - CNTHPS_CVAL_EL2 ) is greater than or equal to zero. This means that TimerValue acts like a 32-bit downcounter timer. When the timer condition is met: CNTHPS_CTL_EL2 .ISTATUS is set to 1. If CNTHPS_CTL_EL2 .IMASK is 0, an interrupt is generated. When CNTHPS_CTL_EL2 .ENABLE is 0, the TimerValue cannot be read but continues to decrement. When the timer is enabled, the TimerValue represents the elapsed time whether that time was spent enabled or disabled.

name: CNTKCTL_EL1
description: When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, this register does not cause any event stream from the virtual counter to be generated, and does not control access to the counters and timers. The access to counters and timers at EL0 is controlled by CNTHCTL_EL2 . When FEAT_VHE is not implemented, or when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, this register controls the generation of an event stream from the virtual counter, and access from EL0 to the physical counter, virtual counter, EL1 physical timers, and the virtual timer.

fields.name: CNTPMASK

fields.name: CNTVMASK
fields.description: Reserved for software use in nested virtualization.

name: CNTPCT_EL0
description: Reads of CNTPCT_EL0 return the 64-bit physical count value minus a physical offset.

fields.name: PhysicalCount
fields.description: Physical count value. If the access is not trapped and all of the following are true, then reads of CNTPCT_EL0 from EL0 or EL1 return (PhysicalCountInt<63:0> - CNTPOFF_EL2 <63:0>): EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state. CNTHCTL_EL2 .ECV is 1. SCR_EL3 .ECVEn is 1. The Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}. Otherwise, reads of CNTPCT_EL0 return PhysicalCountInt<63:0>. PhysicalCountInt is defined by 'The Physical Counter' .

name: CNTPOFF_EL2
description: Holds the 64-bit physical offset. This is the offset for the AArch64 EL1 physical timer and counter when Enhanced Counter Virtualization is enabled.

When CNTPS_CTL_EL1 .ENABLE is 1, and TimerConditionMet is TRUE for the EL1 secure physical timer, the timer condition is met and all of the following are true: CNTPS_CTL_EL1 .ISTATUS is set to 1. If CNTPS_CTL_EL1 .IMASK is 0, an interrupt is generated. TimerConditionMet is defined by 'Operation of the CompareValue views of the timers' . The CompareValue view of the timer acts like a 64-bit upcounter timer. When CNTPS_CTL_EL1 .ENABLE is 0, the timer condition is not met, but CNTPCT_EL0 continues to count. If the Generic counter is implemented at a size less than 64 bits, then this field is permitted to be implemented at the same width as the counter, and the upper bits are RES0 . The value of this field is treated as zero-extended in all counter calculations.

name: CNTPS_TVAL_EL1
long_name: Counter-timer Physical Secure Timer TimerValue Register
description: Holds the timer value for the secure physical timer, usually accessible at EL3 but configurably accessible at EL1 in Secure state.

fields.name: TimerValue
fields.description: The TimerValue view of the secure physical timer. On a read of this register: If CNTPS_CTL_EL1 .ENABLE is 0, the value returned is UNKNOWN . If CNTPS_CTL_EL1 .ENABLE is 1, the value returned is ( CNTPS_CVAL_EL1 - CNTPCT_EL0 ). On a write of this register, CNTPS_CVAL_EL1 is set to ( CNTPCT_EL0 + TimerValue), where TimerValue is treated as a signed 32-bit integer. When CNTPS_CTL_EL1 .ENABLE is 1, the timer condition is met when ( CNTPCT_EL0 - CNTPS_CVAL_EL1 ) is greater than or equal to zero. This means that TimerValue acts like a 32-bit downcounter timer. When the timer condition is met: CNTPS_CTL_EL1 .ISTATUS is set to 1. If CNTPS_CTL_EL1 .IMASK is 0, an interrupt is generated. When CNTPS_CTL_EL1 .ENABLE is 0, the TimerValue cannot be read but continues to decrement. When the timer is enabled, the TimerValue represents the elapsed time whether that time was spent enabled or disabled.

name: CNTP_CTL_EL0
description: Control register for the EL1 physical timer.

fields.name: ISTATUS
fields.description: The status of the timer. This bit indicates whether the timer condition is met:

values.label: Timer condition is not met.
Timer condition is met.

fields.name: IMASK
fields.description: Timer interrupt mask bit. Permitted values are:

values.label: Timer interrupt is not masked by the IMASK bit.
Timer interrupt is masked by the IMASK bit.

fields.name: ENABLE
fields.description: Enables the timer. Permitted values are:

values.label: Timer disabled.
Timer enabled.

name: CNTP_CVAL_EL0
description: Holds the compare value for the EL1 physical timer.

fields.name: CompareValue
fields.description: Holds the EL1 physical timer CompareValue. When CNTP_CTL_EL0 .ENABLE is 1, and TimerConditionMet is TRUE for the EL1 physical timer, the timer condition is met and all of the following are true: CNTP_CTL_EL0 .ISTATUS is set to 1. If CNTP_CTL_EL0 .IMASK is 0, an interrupt is generated. TimerConditionMet is defined by 'Operation of the CompareValue views of the timers' . The CompareValue view of the timer acts like a 64-bit upcounter timer. When CNTP_CTL_EL0 .ENABLE is 0, the timer condition is not met, but CNTPCT_EL0 continues to count. If the Generic counter is implemented at a size less than 64 bits, then this field is permitted to be implemented at the same width as the counter, and the upper bits are RES0 . The value of this field is treated as zero-extended in all counter calculations.

name: CNTP_TVAL_EL0
description: Holds the timer value for the EL1 physical timer.

fields.name: TimerValue
fields.description: The TimerValue view of the EL1 physical timer. On a read of this register: If CNTP_CTL_EL0 .ENABLE is 0, the value returned is UNKNOWN . If CNTP_CTL_EL0 .ENABLE is 1, the value returned is ( CNTP_CVAL_EL0 - CNTPCT_EL0 ). On a write of this register, CNTP_CVAL_EL0 is set to ( CNTPCT_EL0 + TimerValue), where TimerValue is treated as a signed 32-bit integer. When CNTP_CTL_EL0 .ENABLE is 1, the timer condition is met when ( CNTPCT_EL0 - CNTP_CVAL_EL0 ) is greater than or equal to zero. This means that TimerValue acts like a 32-bit downcounter timer. When the timer condition is met: CNTP_CTL_EL0 .ISTATUS is set to 1. If CNTP_CTL_EL0 .IMASK is 0, an interrupt is generated. When CNTP_CTL_EL0 .ENABLE is 0, the TimerValue cannot be read but continues to decrement. When the timer is enabled, the TimerValue represents the elapsed time whether that time was spent enabled or disabled. The value of CNTPCT_EL0 used in these calculations is the value seen at the Exception level that the CNTPCT_EL0 register is being read or written from.

name: CNTVCTSS_EL0
description: Reads of CNTVCTSS_EL0 return the 64-bit physical count value minus a virtual offset.

fields.name: SSVirtualCount
fields.description: Self-synchronized virtual count value. When EL2 is implemented, if the access is not trapped, and any of the following are true, then reads of CNTVCTSS_EL0 from EL0 and EL1 return (PhysicalCountInt<63:0> - CNTVOFF_EL2<63:0>): All of the following are true: The Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}. CNTVCTSS_EL0 is read from EL0. All of the following are true: The Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is 0. CNTVCTSS_EL0 is read from EL2. CNTVCTSS_EL0 is read from EL1 or EL3. Otherwise reads of CNTVCTSS_EL0 return PhysicalCountInt<63:0>. PhysicalCountInt is defined by 'The Physical Counter' .

name: CNTVCT_EL0
description: Reads of CNTVCT_EL0 return the 64-bit physical count value minus a virtual offset.

fields.name: VirtualCount
fields.description: Virtual count value. When EL2 is implemented, if the access is not trapped, and any of the following are true, then reads of CNTVCT_EL0 from EL0 and EL1 return (PhysicalCountInt<63:0> - CNTVOFF_EL2<63:0>): All of the following are true: The Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}. CNTVCT_EL0 is read from EL0. All of the following are true: The Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is 0. CNTVCT_EL0 is read from EL2. CNTVCT_EL0 is read from EL1 or EL3. Otherwise reads of CNTVCT_EL0 return PhysicalCountInt<63:0>. PhysicalCountInt is defined by 'The Physical Counter' .

name: CNTVOFF_EL2
description: Holds the 64-bit virtual offset. This is the offset for the AArch64 virtual timers and counters.

fields.name: VOffset
fields.description: Virtual offset. If the Generic counter is implemented at a size less than 64 bits, then this field is permitted to be implemented at the same width as the counter, and the upper bits are RES0 . The value of this field is treated as zero-extended in all counter calculations.

description: Holds the compare value for the virtual timer.

When CNTV_CTL_EL0 .ENABLE is 1, and TimerConditionMet is TRUE for the EL1 virtual timer, the timer condition is met and all of the following are true: CNTV_CTL_EL0 .ISTATUS is set to 1. If CNTV_CTL_EL0 .IMASK is 0, an interrupt is generated. TimerConditionMet is defined by 'Operation of the CompareValue views of the timers' . The CompareValue view of the timer acts like a 64-bit upcounter timer. When CNTV_CTL_EL0 .ENABLE is 0, the timer condition is not met, but CNTVCT_EL0 continues to count. If the Generic counter is implemented at a size less than 64 bits, then this field is permitted to be implemented at the same width as the counter, and the upper bits are RES0 . fields.name: TimerValue

If CNTV_CTL_EL0 .ENABLE is 1, the value returned is ( CNTV_CVAL_EL0 - CNTVCT_EL0 ). On a write of this register, CNTV_CVAL_EL0 is set to ( CNTVCT_EL0 + TimerValue), where TimerValue is treated as a signed 32-bit integer. When CNTV_CTL_EL0 .ENABLE is 1, the timer condition is met when ( CNTVCT_EL0 - CNTV_CVAL_EL0 ) is greater than or equal to zero. This means that TimerValue acts like a 32-bit downcounter timer. When the timer condition is met: CNTV_CTL_EL0 .ISTATUS is set to 1. If CNTV_CTL_EL0 .IMASK is 0, an interrupt is generated. When CNTV_CTL_EL0 .ENABLE is 0, the TimerValue cannot be read but continues to decrement. When the timer is enabled, the TimerValue represents the elapsed time whether that time was spent enabled or disabled.

name: CONTEXTIDR_EL1
description: Identifies the current Process Identifier. The value of the whole of this register is called the Context ID and is used by: The debug logic, for Linked and Unlinked Context ID matching. The trace logic, to identify the current process. The significance of this register is for debug and trace use only.

fields.name: PROCID
fields.description: Process Identifier. This field must be programmed with a unique value that identifies the current process. In AArch32 state, when TTBCR .EAE is set to 0, CONTEXTIDR .ASID holds the ASID. In AArch64 state, CONTEXTIDR_EL1 is independent of the ASID, and for the EL1&0 translation regime either TTBR0_EL1 or TTBR1_EL1 holds the ASID.

name: CONTEXTIDR_EL2
description: Identifies the current Process Identifier for EL2. The value of the whole of this register is called the Context ID and is used by: The debug logic, for Linked and Unlinked Context ID matching. The trace logic, to identify the current process. The significance of this register is for debug and trace use only.

fields.name: PROCID
fields.description: Process Identifier. This field must be programmed with a unique value that identifies the current process.

name: COSP RCTX
long_name: Clear Other Speculative Prediction Restriction by Context
description: Clear Other Speculative Prediction Restriction by Context applies to all prediction resources not managed by other speculation restriction System instructions. The actions of code in the target execution context or contexts appearing in program order before the instruction cannot exploitatively control any predictions occurring after the instruction is complete and synchronized. This instruction applies to all speculative access except: Cache Prefetch predictions. Control Flow predictions. Data Value predictions. This instruction is guaranteed to be complete following a DSB that covers both read and write behavior on the PE that executed the original restriction instruction, and a subsequent Context Synchronization event is required to ensure that the effect of the completion of the instructions is synchronized to the current execution. This instruction does not require the invalidation of Cache Allocation Resources so long as the behavior described for completion of this instruction is met by the implementation. On some implementations, the instruction is likely to take a significant number of cycles to execute. This instruction is expected to be used rarely, such as on the roll-over of an ASID or VMID, but should not be used on every context switch. This instruction applies to speculative accesses resulting from address predictions.

fields.name: GVMID
fields.description: Execution of this instruction applies to all VMIDs or a specified VMID.

values.label: Applies to specified VMID for an EL0 or EL1 target execution context.
Applies to all VMIDs for an EL0 or EL1 target execution context.

fields.name: VMID
fields.description: Only applies when bit[48] is 0 and the target execution context is either: EL1. EL0 when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}. Otherwise this field is RES0 . When the instruction is executed at EL1, this field is treated as the current VMID. When the instruction is executed at EL0 and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, this field is treated as the current VMID. When the instruction is executed at EL0 and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, this field is ignored. If EL2 is not implemented or not enabled for the target Security state, this field is RES0 . If the implementation supports 16 bits of VMID, then the upper 8 bits of the VMID must be written to 0 by software when the context being affected only uses 8 bits.

fields.name: NSE
fields.description: Together with the NS field, selects the Security state. For a description of the values derived by evaluating NS and NSE together, see COSP_RCTX.NS.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with the NSE field, selects the Security state. NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 When Secure state is implemented, Secure. Otherwise reserved. 0b0 0b1 Non-secure. 0b1 0b0 Root. 0b1 0b1 Realm.

values.label: Secure state.
Non-secure state.

fields.name: EL
fields.description: Exception Level. Indicates the Exception level of the target execution context.

values.label: EL0.
EL1
EL2
EL3

fields.name: GASID
fields.description: Execution of this instruction applies to all ASIDs, or a specified ASID.

values.label: Applies to specified ASID for an EL0 target execution context.
Applies to all ASIDs for an EL0 target execution context.

fields.name: ASID
fields.description: Only applies to an EL0 target execution context and when bit[16] is 0. Otherwise, this field is RES0 . When the instruction is executed at EL0, this field is treated as the current ASID. If the implementation supports 16 bits of ASID, then the upper 8 bits of the ASID must be written to 0 by software when the context being affected only uses 8 bits.

name: CPACRMASK_EL1
long_name: Architectural Feature Access Control Masking Register
description: Mask register to prevent updates of fields in CPACR_EL1 on writes to CPACR_EL1 or CPACRALIAS_EL1.

values.label: CPACR_EL1 .TCPAC is writeable.
CPACR_EL1 .TCPAC is not writeable.
CPACR_EL1 .TAM is writeable.
CPACR_EL1 .TAM is not writeable.
CPACR_EL1 .E0POE is writeable.
CPACR_EL1 .E0POE is not writeable.
CPACR_EL1 .TTA is writeable.
CPACR_EL1 .TTA is not writeable.
CPACR_EL1 .SMEN is writeable.
CPACR_EL1 .SMEN is not writeable.
CPACR_EL1 .FPEN is writeable.
CPACR_EL1 .FPEN is not writeable.
CPACR_EL1 .ZEN is writeable.

name: CPACR_EL1
description:
Controls access to trace, 
SME, Streaming SVE, 
SVE, 
and Advanced SIMD and floating-point functionality.

fields.name: TCPAC
fields.description: Reserved for software use in nested virtualization.

fields.name: TAM
fields.description: Reserved for software use in nested virtualization.

fields.name: E0POE
fields.description: Enable access to POR_EL0 . Traps EL0 accesses to POR_EL0 , from AArch64 state only to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled in the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: This control causes EL0 access to POR_EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: TTA
fields.description: Traps EL0 and EL1 System register accesses to all implemented trace registers from both Execution states to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled in the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to trace registers are trapped, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . In AArch32 state, MRC and MCR accesses to trace registers are trapped, reported using EC syndrome value 0x05 . In AArch32 state, MRRC and MCRR accesses to trace registers are trapped, reported using EC syndrome value 0x0C .

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
This control causes EL0 and EL1 System register accesses to all implemented trace registers to be trapped.

fields.name: SMEN
fields.description: Traps execution at EL1 and EL0 of SME instructions, SVE instructions when FEAT_SVE is not implemented or the PE is in Streaming SVE mode, and instructions that directly access the SVCR or SMCR_EL1 System registers to EL1, or to EL2 when EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1. When instructions that directly access the SVCR System register are trapped with reference to this control, the MSR SVCRSM , MSR SVCRZA , and MSR SVCRSMZA instructions are also trapped. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x1D , with an ISS code of 0x0000000 . This field does not affect whether Streaming SVE or SME register values are valid. A trap taken as a result of CPACR_EL1.SMEN has precedence over a trap taken as a result of CPACR_EL1.FPEN.

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at EL1 and EL0 to be trapped.
This control causes execution of these instructions at EL0 to be trapped, but does not cause execution of any instructions at EL1 to be trapped.
This control causes execution of these instructions at EL1 and EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: FPEN
fields.description:
Traps execution at EL1 and EL0 of instructions that access the Advanced SIMD and floating-point registers from both Execution states to EL1, reported using EC syndrome value 0x07 , or to EL2 reported using EC syndrome value 0x00 when EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to FPMR , FPCR , FPSR , and any of the SIMD and floating-point registers V0-V31, including their views as D0-D31 registers or S0-31 registers. In AArch32 state, FPSCR , and any of the SIMD and floating-point registers Q0-15, including their views as D0-D31 registers or S0-31 registers. Traps execution at EL1 and EL0 of 
SME and 
SVE instructions
 to EL1, or to EL2 when EL2 is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1.
The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x07 . A trap taken as a result of CPACR_EL1.SMEN has precedence over a trap taken as a result of CPACR_EL1.FPEN. A trap taken as a result of CPACR_EL1.ZEN has precedence over a trap taken as a result of CPACR_EL1.FPEN.

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at EL1 and EL0 to be trapped.
This control causes execution of these instructions at EL0 to be trapped, but does not cause execution of any instructions at EL1 to be trapped.
This control causes execution of these instructions at EL1 and EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: ZEN
fields.description: Traps execution at EL1 and EL0 of SVE instructions when the PE is not in Streaming SVE mode, and instructions that directly access the ZCR_EL1 System register to EL1, or to EL2 when EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x19 . A trap taken as a result of CPACR_EL1.ZEN has precedence over a trap taken as a result of CPACR_EL1.FPEN.

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at EL1 and EL0 to be trapped.
This control causes execution of these instructions at EL0 to be trapped, but does not cause execution of any instructions at EL1 to be trapped.
This control causes execution of these instructions at EL1 and EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.

name: CPP RCTX
long_name: Cache Prefetch Prediction Restriction by Context
description: Cache Prefetch Prediction Restriction by Context applies to all Cache Allocation Resources that predict cache allocations based on information gathered within the target execution context or contexts. The actions of code in the target execution context or contexts appearing in program order before the instruction cannot exploitatively control cache prefetch predictions occurring after the instruction is complete and synchronized. This instruction applies to all: Instruction caches. Data caches. TLB prefetching hardware used by the executing PE that applies to the supplied context or contexts. This instruction is guaranteed to be complete following a DSB that covers both read and write behavior on the same PE as executed the original restriction instruction, and a subsequent context synchronization event is required to ensure that the effect of the completion of the instructions is synchronized to the current execution. This instruction does not require the invalidation of Cache Allocation Resources so long as the behavior described for completion of this instruction is met by the implementation. On some implementations the instruction is likely to take a significant number of cycles to execute. This instruction is expected to be used very rarely, such as on the roll-over of an ASID or VMID, but should not be used on every context switch.

fields.name: GVMID
fields.description: Execution of this instruction applies to all VMIDs or a specified VMID.

values.label: Applies to specified VMID for an EL0 or EL1 target execution context.
Applies to all VMIDs for an EL0 or EL1 target execution context.

fields.name: VMID
fields.description: Only applies when bit[48] is 0 and the target execution context is either: EL1. EL0 when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}. Otherwise this field is RES0 . When the instruction is executed at EL1, this field is treated as the current VMID. When the instruction is executed at EL0 and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, this field is treated as the current VMID. When the instruction is executed at EL0 and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, this field is ignored. If EL2 is not implemented or not enabled for the target Security state, this field is RES0 . If the implementation supports 16 bits of VMID, then the upper 8 bits of the VMID must be written to 0 by software when the context being affected only uses 8 bits.

fields.description: Together with the NS field, selects the Security state. For a description of the values derived by evaluating NS and NSE together, see CPP_RCTX.NS.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with the NSE field, selects the Security state. NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 When Secure state is implemented, Secure. Otherwise reserved. 0b0 0b1 Non-secure. 0b1 0b0 Root. 0b1 0b1 Realm.

values.label: Secure state.
Non-secure state.

fields.description: Exception Level. Indicates the Exception level of the target execution context.

values.label: EL0.
EL1
EL2
EL3
