fields.description: Execution of this instruction applies to all ASIDs or a specified ASID.

values.label: Applies to specified ASID for an EL0 target execution context.
Applies to all ASIDs for an EL0 target execution context.

fields.name: ASID
fields.description: Only applies for an EL0 target execution context and when bit[16] is 0. Otherwise, this field is RES0 . When the instruction is executed at EL0, this field is treated as the current ASID. If the implementation supports 16 bits of ASID, then the upper 8 bits of the ASID must be written to 0 by software when the context being affected only uses 8 bits.

name: CPTRMASK_EL2
description: Mask register to prevent updates of fields in CPTR_EL2 on writes.

values.label: CPTR_EL2 .TCPAC is writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .TCPAC is not writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .TAM is writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .TAM is not writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .E0POE is writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .E0POE is not writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .TTA is writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .TTA is not writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .SMEN is writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .SMEN is not writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .FPEN is writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .FPEN is not writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .ZEN is writeable.
CPTR_EL2 .ZEN is not writeable.

name: CPTR_EL2
description:
Controls trapping to EL2 of accesses to CPACR , CPACR_EL1 , trace, Activity Monitor, 
SME, Streaming SVE, 
SVE, 
and Advanced SIMD and floating-point functionality.

fields.name: TCPAC
fields.description: In AArch64 state, traps accesses to CPACR_EL1 from EL1 to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . In AArch32 state, traps accesses to CPACR from EL1 to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 .

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
EL1 accesses to CPACR_EL1 and CPACR are trapped to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state.

fields.name: TAM
fields.description: Trap Activity Monitor access. Traps EL1 and EL0 accesses to all Activity Monitor registers to EL2, as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to the following registers are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : AMUSERENR_EL0 , AMCFGR_EL0 , AMCGCR_EL0 , AMCNTENCLR0_EL0 , AMCNTENCLR1_EL0 , AMCNTENSET0_EL0 , AMCNTENSET1_EL0 , AMCR_EL0 , AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 , AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 , AMEVTYPER0<n>_EL0 , and AMEVTYPER1<n>_EL0 . In AArch32 state, MRC or MCR accesses to the following registers are trapped to EL2 and reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 : AMUSERENR , AMCFGR , AMCGCR , AMCNTENCLR0 , AMCNTENCLR1 , AMCNTENSET0 , AMCNTENSET1 , AMCR , AMEVTYPER0<n> , and AMEVTYPER1<n> . In AArch32 state, MRRC or MCRR accesses to AMEVCNTR0<n> and AMEVCNTR1<n> , are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x04 .

values.label: Accesses from EL1 and EL0 to Activity Monitor registers are not trapped.
Accesses from EL1 and EL0 to Activity Monitor registers are trapped to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state.

fields.name: E0POE
fields.description: Enable access to POR_EL0 . Traps EL0 accesses to POR_EL0 to EL2, from AArch64 state only. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: This control causes EL0 access to POR_EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: TTA
fields.description: Traps System register accesses to all implemented trace registers from both Execution states to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state, as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to trace registers with op0=2, op1=1, and CRn< 0b1000 are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . In AArch32 state, MRC or MCR accesses to trace registers with cpnum=14, opc1=1, and CRn< 0b1000 are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x05 .

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

Any attempt at EL0, EL1 or EL2, to execute a System register access to an implemented trace register is trapped to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state, unless HCR_EL2 .TGE is 0 and it is trapped by CPACR .NSTRCDIS or CPACR_EL1 .TTA. When HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, any attempt at EL0 or EL2 to execute a System register access to an implemented trace register is trapped to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state.

fields.name: SMEN
fields.description: Traps execution at EL2, EL1, and EL0 of SME instructions, SVE instructions when FEAT_SVE is not implemented or the PE is in Streaming SVE mode, and instructions that directly access the SVCR , SMCR_EL1 , or SMCR_EL2 System registers to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state. When instructions that directly access the SVCR System register are trapped with reference to this control, the MSR SVCRSM , MSR SVCRZA , and MSR SVCRSMZA instructions are also trapped. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x1D , with an ISS code of 0x0000000 . This field does not affect whether Streaming SVE or SME register values are valid. A trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL2.SMEN has precedence over a trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL2.FPEN.

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at EL2, EL1, and EL0 to be trapped.

When HCR_EL2 .TGE is 0, this control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped. When HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, this control causes execution of these instructions at EL0 to be trapped, but does not cause execution of any instructions at EL2 to be trapped.
This control causes execution of these instructions at EL2, EL1, and EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: FPEN
fields.description:
Traps execution at EL2, EL1, and EL0 of instructions that access the Advanced SIMD and floating-point registers from both Execution states to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x07 . Traps execution at EL2, EL1, and EL0 of 
SME and 
SVE instructions
 to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state.
The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x07 . When FEAT_FPMR is implemented, traps execution at EL2, EL1, and EL0 of instructions that access FPMR to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x07 A trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL2.SMEN has precedence over a trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL2.FPEN. A trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL2.ZEN has precedence over a trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL2.FPEN.

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at EL2, EL1, and EL0 to be trapped.

When HCR_EL2 .TGE is 0, this control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped. When HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, this control causes execution of these instructions at EL0 to be trapped, but does not cause execution of any instructions at EL2 to be trapped.
This control causes execution of these instructions at EL2, EL1, and EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: ZEN
fields.description: Traps execution at EL2, EL1, and EL0 of SVE instructions when the PE is not in Streaming SVE mode, and instructions that directly access the ZCR_EL1 or ZCR_EL2 System registers to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x19 . A trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL2.ZEN has precedence over a trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL2.FPEN.

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at EL2, EL1, and EL0 to be trapped.

When HCR_EL2 .TGE is 0, this control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped. When HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, this control causes execution of these instructions at EL0 to be trapped, but does not cause execution of any instructions at EL2 to be trapped.
This control causes execution of these instructions at EL2, EL1, and EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.

name: CPTR_EL3
description:
Controls trapping to EL3 of accesses to CPACR , CPACR_EL1 , HCPTR , CPTR_EL2 , trace, Activity Monitor, 
SME, Streaming SVE, 
SVE, 
and Advanced SIMD and floating-point functionality.

fields.name: TCPAC
fields.description: Traps all of the following to EL3, from both Execution states and any Security state. EL2 accesses to CPTR_EL2 , reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 , or HCPTR , reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 . EL2 and EL1 accesses to CPACR_EL1 reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 , or CPACR reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 . When CPTR_EL3.TCPAC is:

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
EL2 accesses to the CPTR_EL2 or HCPTR , and EL2 and EL1 accesses to the CPACR_EL1 or CPACR , are trapped to EL3, unless they are trapped by CPTR_EL2 .TCPAC.

fields.name: TAM
fields.description: Trap Activity Monitor access. Traps EL2, EL1, and EL0 accesses to all Activity Monitor registers to EL3. Accesses to the Activity Monitors registers are trapped as follows: In AArch64 state, the following registers are trapped to EL3 and reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : AMUSERENR_EL0 , AMCFGR_EL0 , AMCGCR_EL0 , AMCNTENCLR0_EL0 , AMCNTENCLR1_EL0 , AMCNTENSET0_EL0 , AMCNTENSET1_EL0 , AMCR_EL0 , AMEVCNTR0<n>_EL0 , AMEVCNTR1<n>_EL0 , AMEVTYPER0<n>_EL0 , and AMEVTYPER1<n>_EL0 . In AArch32 state, accesses with MRC or MCR to the following registers reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 : AMUSERENR , AMCFGR , AMCGCR , AMCNTENCLR0 , AMCNTENCLR1 , AMCNTENSET0 , AMCNTENSET1 , AMCR , AMEVTYPER0<n> , and AMEVTYPER1<n> . In AArch32 state, accesses with MRRC or MCRR to the following registers, reported using EC syndrome value 0x04 : AMEVCNTR0<n> , AMEVCNTR1<n> .

values.label: Accesses from EL2, EL1, and EL0 to Activity Monitor registers are not trapped.
Accesses from EL2, EL1, and EL0 to Activity Monitor registers are trapped to EL3.

fields.name: TTA
fields.description: Traps System register accesses. Accesses to the trace registers, from all Exception levels, any Security state, and both Execution states are trapped to EL3 as follows: In AArch64 state, Trace registers with op0=2, op1=1, and CRn< 0b1000 are trapped to EL3 and reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . In AArch32 state, accesses using MCR or MRC to the Trace registers with cpnum=14, opc1=1, and CRn< 0b1000 are reported using EC syndrome value 0x05 .

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
Any System register access to the trace registers is trapped to EL3, unless it is trapped by CPACR .TRCDIS, CPACR_EL1 .TTA, or CPTR_EL2 .TTA.

fields.name: ESM
fields.description: Traps execution of SME instructions, SVE instructions when FEAT_SVE is not implemented or the PE is in Streaming SVE mode, and instructions that directly access the SMCR_EL1 , SMCR_EL2 , SMCR_EL3 , SMPRI_EL1 , SMPRIMAP_EL2 , or SVCR System registers, from all Exception levels and any Security state, to EL3. When instructions that directly access the SVCR System register are trapped with reference to this control, the MSR SVCRSM , MSR SVCRZA , and MSR SVCRSMZA instructions are also trapped. When direct accesses to SMPRI_EL1 and SMPRIMAP_EL2 are trapped, the exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . Otherwise, the exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x1D , with an ISS code of 0x0000000 . This field does not affect whether Streaming SVE or SME register values are valid. A trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL3.ESM has precedence over a trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL3.TFP.

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at all Exception levels to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: TFP
fields.description:
Traps execution of instructions which access the Advanced SIMD and floating-point functionality, from all Exception levels, any Security state, and both Execution states, to EL3. This includes the following registers, all reported using EC syndrome value 0x07 : FPCR , FPSR , FPEXC32_EL2 , and any of the SIMD and floating-point registers V0-V31, including their views as D0-D31 registers or S0-S31 registers. If FEAT_FPMR is implemented, FPMR . MVFR0 , MVFR1 , MVFR2 , FPSCR , FPEXC , and any of the SIMD and floating-point registers Q0-Q15, including their views as D0-D31 registers or S0-S31 registers. VMSR accesses to FPSID . Permitted VMSR accesses to FPSID are ignored, but for the purposes of this trap the architecture defines a VMSR access to the FPSID from EL1 or higher as an access to a SIMD and floating-point register. Traps execution at all Exception levels of 
SME and 
SVE instructions
 to EL3 from any Security state.
The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x07 . A trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL3.ESM has precedence over a trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL3.TFP. A trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL3.EZ has precedence over a trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL3.TFP.

values.label: This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.
This control causes execution of these instructions at  all Exception levels to be trapped.

fields.name: EZ
fields.description: Traps execution of SVE instructions when the PE is not in Streaming SVE mode, and instructions that directly access the ZCR_EL3 , ZCR_EL2 , or ZCR_EL1 System registers, from all Exception levels and any Security state, to EL3. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x19 . A trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL3.EZ has precedence over a trap taken as a result of CPTR_EL3.TFP.

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at  all Exception levels to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.

name: CSSELR_EL1
description: Selects the current Cache Size ID Register, CCSIDR_EL1 , by specifying the required cache level and the cache type (either instruction or data cache).

values.label: Data, Instruction or Unified cache.
Separate Allocation Tag cache.
Level 1 cache.
Level 2 cache.
Level 3 cache.
Level 4 cache.
Level 5 cache.
Level 6 cache.
Level 7 cache.
Data or unified cache.
Instruction cache.

name: CTR_EL0
description: Provides information about the architecture of the caches.

fields.name: TminLine
fields.description: Tag minimum Line. Log 2 of the number of words covered by Allocation Tags in the smallest cache line of all caches which can contain Allocation tags that are controlled by the PE. For an implementation with cache lines containing 64 bytes of data and 4 Allocation Tags, this will be log 2 (64/4) = 4. For an implementation with Allocations Tags in separate cache lines of 128 Allocation Tags per line, this will be log 2 (128*16/4) = 9.

fields.name: DIC
fields.description: Instruction cache invalidation requirements for data to instruction coherence.

values.label: Instruction cache invalidation to the Point of Unification is required for data to instruction coherence.
Instruction cache invalidation to the Point of Unification is not required for data to instruction coherence.

fields.name: IDC
fields.description: Data cache clean requirements for instruction to data coherence. The meaning of this bit is:

values.label: Data cache clean to the Point of Unification is required for instruction to data coherence, unless CLIDR_EL1 .LoC == 0b000 or ( CLIDR_EL1 .LoUIS == 0b000 and CLIDR_EL1 .LoUU == 0b000 ).
Data cache clean to the Point of Unification is not required for instruction to data coherence.

fields.name: CWG
fields.description: Cache writeback granule. Log 2 of the number of words of the maximum size of memory that can be overwritten as a result of the eviction of a cache entry that has had a memory location in it modified. A value of 0b0000 indicates that this register does not provide Cache writeback granule information and either: The architectural maximum of 512 words (2KB) must be assumed. The Cache writeback granule can be determined from maximum cache line size encoded in the Cache Size ID Registers. Values greater than 0b1001 are reserved. Arm recommends that an implementation that does not support cache write-back implements this field as 0b0001 . This applies, for example, to an implementation that supports only write-through caches.

fields.name: ERG
fields.description: Exclusives reservation granule. Log 2 of the number of words of the maximum size of the reservation granule that has been implemented for the Load-Exclusive and Store-Exclusive instructions. The use of the value 0b0000 is deprecated. The value 0b0001 and values greater than 0b1001 are reserved.

fields.name: DminLine
fields.description: Log 2 of the number of words in the smallest cache line of all the data caches and unified caches that are controlled by the PE.

fields.name: L1Ip
fields.description: Level 1 instruction cache policy. Indicates the indexing and tagging policy for the L1 instruction cache.

values.label: Reserved.
ASID-tagged Virtual Index, Virtual Tag (AIVIVT).
Virtual Index, Physical Tag (VIPT).
Physical Index, Physical Tag (PIPT).

fields.name: IminLine
fields.description: Log 2 of the number of words in the smallest cache line of all the instruction caches that are controlled by the PE.

name: CurrentEL
description: Holds the current Exception level.

values.label: EL0.
EL1.
EL2.
EL3.

name: DACR32_EL2
description: Allows access to the AArch32 DACR register from AArch64 state only. Its value has no effect on execution in AArch64 state.

fields.name: D<n>
fields.description: Domain n access permission, where n = 0 to 15. Permitted values are:

values.label: No access. Any access to the domain generates a Domain fault.
Client. Accesses are checked against the permission bits in the translation tables.
Manager. Accesses are not checked against the permission bits in the translation tables.

name: DAIF
description: Allows access to the interrupt mask bits.

values.label: Watchpoint, Breakpoint, and Software Step exceptions targeted at the current Exception level are not masked.
Watchpoint, Breakpoint, and Software Step exceptions targeted at the current Exception level are masked.
Exception not masked.
Exception masked.
Exception not masked.
Exception masked.
Exception not masked.
Exception masked.

values.label: Not implemented.

Implemented and enabled. ExternalRealmInvasiveDebugEnabled() == TRUE.

name: DBGBCR<n>_EL1
description: Holds control information for a breakpoint. Forms breakpoint n together with value register DBGBVR<n>_EL1 .

fields.name: LBNX
fields.description: Linked Breakpoint Number. For Linked address matching breakpoints, with DBGBCR<n>_EL1.LBN, specifies the index of the breakpoint linked to. For all other breakpoint types, this field is ignored and reads of the register return an UNKNOWN value.

fields.name: SSCE
fields.description:
Security State Control Extended. The fields that indicate when the breakpoint can be generated are:
HMC, PMC, SSC, and SSCE.
These fields must be considered in combination, and the values that are permitted for these fields are constrained.

fields.name: MASK
fields.description: Address Mask. Only address ranges up to 2GB can be watched using a single mask.

values.label: No mask.
Number of address bits masked.

fields.name: BT
fields.description: Breakpoint Type. With DBGBCR<n>_EL1.BT2 when implemented, specifies breakpoint type.

values.label: Unlinked instruction address match. DBGBVR<n>_EL1 is the address of an instruction.
Linked instruction address match. As 0b0000 , but linked to a breakpoint that has linking enabled.

Unlinked Context ID match. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is 1, and either the PE is executing at EL0 with HCR_EL2 .TGE set to 1 or the PE is executing at EL2, then DBGBVR<n>_EL1 .ContextID is compared against CONTEXTIDR_EL2 . Otherwise, DBGBVR<n>_EL1 .ContextID is compared against CONTEXTIDR_EL1 .
As 0b0010 , with linking enabled.
Unlinked instruction address mismatch. DBGBVR<n>_EL1 is the address of an instruction.
Linked instruction address mismatch. As 0b0100 , but linked to a breakpoint that has linking enabled.
Unlinked CONTEXTIDR_EL1 match. DBGBVR<n>_EL1 .ContextID is a Context ID compared against CONTEXTIDR_EL1 .
As 0b0110 , with linking enabled.
Unlinked VMID match. DBGBVR<n>_EL1 .VMID is a VMID compared against VTTBR_EL2 .VMID.
As 0b1000 , with linking enabled.
Unlinked VMID and Context ID match. DBGBVR<n>_EL1 .ContextID is a Context ID compared against CONTEXTIDR_EL1 , and DBGBVR<n>_EL1 .VMID is a VMID compared against VTTBR_EL2 .VMID.
As 0b1010 , with linking enabled.
Unlinked CONTEXTIDR_EL2 match. DBGBVR<n>_EL1 .ContextID2 is a Context ID compared against CONTEXTIDR_EL2 .
As 0b1100 , with linking enabled.
Unlinked Full Context ID match. DBGBVR<n>_EL1 .ContextID is compared against CONTEXTIDR_EL1 , and DBGBVR<n>_EL1 .ContextID2 is compared against CONTEXTIDR_EL2 .
As 0b1110 , with linking enabled.

fields.name: LBN
fields.description: Linked Breakpoint Number. For Linked address matching breakpoints, with DBGBCR<n>_EL1.LBNX when implemented, specifies the index of the breakpoint linked to. For all other breakpoint types, this field is ignored and reads of the register return an UNKNOWN value.

fields.name: SSC
fields.description:
Security state control. Determines the Security states under which a Breakpoint debug event for breakpoint n is generated. The fields that indicate when the breakpoint can be generated are:
HMC, PMC, SSC, and SSCE.
These fields must be considered in combination, and the values that are permitted for these fields are constrained. For more information on the operation of these fields, see 'Execution conditions for which a breakpoint generates Breakpoint exceptions' . For more information on the effect of programming the fields to a reserved set of values, see 'Reserved DBGBCR<n>_EL1.{SSC, HMC, PMC} values' .

fields.name: HMC
fields.description:
Higher mode control. Determines the debug perspective for deciding when a Breakpoint debug event for breakpoint n is generated. The fields that indicate when the breakpoint can be generated are:
HMC, PMC, SSC, and SSCE.
These fields must be considered in combination, and the values that are permitted for these fields are constrained. For more information on the operation of these fields, see 'Execution conditions for which a breakpoint generates Breakpoint exceptions' . For more information, see DBGBCR<n>_EL1.SSC.

fields.name: BAS
fields.description: Byte address select. Defines which half-words an address-matching breakpoint matches, regardless of the instruction set and Execution state. The permitted values depend on the breakpoint type. For Address match breakpoints, the permitted values are: BAS Match instruction at Constraint for debuggers 0b0011 DBGBVR<n>_EL1 Use for T32 instructions. 0b1100 DBGBVR<n>_EL1 + 2 Use for T32 instructions. 0b1111 DBGBVR<n>_EL1 Use for A64 and A32 instructions. All other values are reserved. For more information, see 'Reserved DBGBCR<n>_EL1.BAS values' . For more information on using the BAS field in address match breakpoints, see 'Using the BAS field in Address Match breakpoints' . For Context matching breakpoints, this field is RES1 and ignored.

fields.name: BT2
fields.description: Breakpoint Type 2. With DBGBCR<n>_EL1.BT, specifies breakpoint type.

values.label: As DBGBCR<n>_EL1.BT.
As DBGBCR<n>_EL1.BT, but with linking enabled. This value is only defined for the following DBGBCR<n>_EL1.BT values: 0b0000 , 0b0001 , 0b0100 , and 0b0101 . All other values are reserved.

fields.name: PMC
fields.description:
Privilege mode control. Determines the Exception level or levels at which a Breakpoint debug event for breakpoint n is generated. The fields that indicate when the breakpoint can be generated are:
HMC, PMC, SSC, and SSCE.
These fields must be considered in combination, and the values that are permitted for these fields are constrained. For more information on the operation of these fields, see 'Execution conditions for which a breakpoint generates Breakpoint exceptions' . For more information, see DBGBCR<n>_EL1.SSC.

values.label: Breakpoint n disabled.
Breakpoint n enabled.

name: DBGBVR<n>_EL1
description: Holds a virtual address, or a VMID and/or a context ID, for use in breakpoint matching. Forms breakpoint n together with control register DBGBCR<n>_EL1 .

fields.name: RESS[14:8]
fields.description: Reserved, Sign extended. Software must set all bits in this field to the same value as the most significant bit of the VA field. If all bits in this field are not the same value as the most significant bit of the VA field, then all of the following apply: It is CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE whether the PE ignores this field when comparing an address. If the breakpoint is not context-aware, it is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether the value read back in each bit of this field is a copy of the most significant bit of the VA field or the value written.

fields.name: VA[56:53]
fields.description: Extension to VA[48:2]. For more information, see VA[48:2].

fields.name: RESS[7:4]
fields.description: Extension to RESS[14:8]. For more information, see RESS[14:8].

fields.name: VA[52:49]
fields.description: Extension to VA[48:2]. For more information, see VA[48:2].

fields.name: RESS[3:0]
fields.description: Extension to RESS[14:8]. For more information, see RESS[14:8].

fields.name: VA[48:2]
fields.description: Bits[48:2] of the address value for comparison. When FEAT_LVA3 is implemented, (VA[56:53]:VA[52:49]) forms the upper part of the address value. If FEAT_LVA3 is not implemented, bits VA[56:53] are part of the RESS field. When FEAT_LVA is implemented, VA[52:49] forms the upper part of the address value. If FEAT_LVA is not implemented, bits [52:49] are part of the RESS field.

name: DBGCLAIMCLR_EL1
description: Used by software to read the values of the CLAIM tag bits, and to clear CLAIM tag bits to 0. The architecture does not define any functionality for the CLAIM tag bits. CLAIM tags are typically used for communication between the debugger and target software. Used in conjunction with the DBGCLAIMSET_EL1 register.

fields.name: CLAIM<m>
fields.description: Claim Tag Clear. Indicates the current status of Claim Tag bit <m>, and is used to clear Claim Tag bit <m> to 0.

values.label: On a read: Claim Tag bit <m> is not set. On a write: Ignored.
On a read: Claim Tag bit <m> is set. On a write: Clear Claim tag bit <m> to 0.

name: DBGCLAIMSET_EL1
description: Used by software to set the CLAIM tag bits to 1. The architecture does not define any functionality for the CLAIM tag bits. CLAIM tags are typically used for communication between the debugger and target software. Used in conjunction with the DBGCLAIMCLR_EL1 register.

fields.name: CLAIM<m>
fields.description: Claim Tag Set. Used to set Claim Tag bit <m>  to 1.

values.label: On a write: Ignored.
On a write: Set Claim Tag bit <m> to 1.

name: DBGDTRRX_EL0
description: Transfers data from an external debugger to the PE. For example, it is used by a debugger transferring commands and data to a debug target. See DBGDTR_EL0 for additional architectural mappings. It is a component of the Debug Communications Channel.

fields.name: DTRRX
fields.description: DTRRX. Reads of this register: If RXfull is 1, return the last value written to DTRRX. If RXfull is 0, return an UNKNOWN value. After the read, RXfull is cleared to 0. For the full behavior of the Debug Communications Channel, see 'The Debug Communication Channel and Instruction Transfer Register' .

name: DBGDTRTX_EL0
description: Transfers data from the PE to an external debugger. For example, it is used by a debug target to transfer data to the debugger. See DBGDTR_EL0 for additional architectural mappings. It is a component of the Debug Communication Channel.

fields.name: DTRTX
fields.description: DTRTX. Writes to this register: If TXfull is 1, DTRTX is set to an UNKNOWN value. If TXfull is 0, update the value in DTRTX. After the write, TXfull is set to 1. For the full behavior of the Debug Communications Channel, see 'The Debug Communication Channel and Instruction Transfer Register' .

name: DBGDTR_EL0
description: Transfers 64 bits of data between the PE and an external debugger. Can transfer both ways using only a single register.

fields.name: HighWord
fields.description: Writes to this register set DTRRX to the value in this field and do not change RXfull. Reads of this register: If RXfull is 1, return the last value written to DTRTX. If RXfull is 0, return an UNKNOWN value. After the read, RXfull is cleared to 0.

fields.name: LowWord
fields.description: Writes to this register set DTRTX to the value in this field and set TXfull to 1. Reads of this register: If RXfull is 1, return the last value written to DTRRX. If RXfull is 0, return an UNKNOWN value. After the read, RXfull is cleared to 0.

name: DBGPRCR_EL1
description: Controls behavior of the PE on powerdown request.

fields.name: CORENPDRQ
fields.description: Core no powerdown request. Requests emulation of powerdown. This request is typically passed to an external power controller. This means that whether a request causes power up is dependent on the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED nature of the system. The power controller must not allow the Core power domain to switch off while this bit is 1.

values.label: If the system responds to a powerdown request, it powers down Core power domain.
If the system responds to a powerdown request, it does not powerdown the Core power domain, but instead emulates a powerdown of that domain.

fields.name: CORENPDRQ
fields.description: Core no powerdown request. Requests emulation of powerdown. This request is typically passed to an external power controller. This means that whether a request causes power up is dependent on the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED nature of the system. The power controller must not allow the Core power domain to switch off while this bit is 1.

values.label: If the system responds to a powerdown request, it powers down Core power domain.
If the system responds to a powerdown request, it does not powerdown the Core power domain, but instead emulates a powerdown of that domain.

name: DBGVCR32_EL2
description: Allows access to the AArch32 register DBGVCR from AArch64 state only. Its value has no effect on execution in AArch64 state.

fields.name: NSF

fields.name: NSI
fields.description: IRQ vector catch enable in Non-secure state. The exception vector offset is 0x18 .

fields.name: NSD
fields.description: Data Abort exception vector catch enable in Non-secure state. The exception vector offset is 0x10 .

fields.name: NSP
fields.description: Prefetch Abort vector catch enable in Non-secure state. The exception vector offset is 0x0C .

fields.name: NSS
fields.description: Supervisor Call (SVC) vector catch enable in Non-secure state. The exception vector offset is 0x08 .

fields.name: NSU
fields.description: Undefined Instruction vector catch enable in Non-secure state. The exception vector offset is 0x04 .

fields.name: SF
fields.description: FIQ vector catch enable in Secure state. The exception vector offset is 0x1C .

fields.name: SI
fields.description: IRQ vector catch enable in Secure state. The exception vector offset is 0x18 .

fields.name: SD
fields.description: Data Abort exception vector catch enable in Secure state. The exception vector offset is 0x10 .

fields.name: SP
fields.description: Prefetch Abort vector catch enable in Secure state. The exception vector offset is 0x0C .

fields.name: SS
fields.description: Supervisor Call (SVC) vector catch enable in Secure state. The exception vector offset is 0x08 .

fields.name: SU
fields.description: Undefined Instruction vector catch enable in Secure state. The exception vector offset is 0x04 .

name: DBGWCR<n>_EL1
description: Holds control information for a watchpoint. Forms watchpoint n together with value register DBGWVR<n>_EL1 .

fields.name: LBNX
fields.description: Linked Breakpoint Number. For Linked data address watchpoints, with DBGWCR<n>_EL1.LBN, specifies the index of the breakpoint linked to. For all other watchpoint types, this field is ignored and reads of the register return an UNKNOWN value.

fields.name: SSCE
fields.description:
Security State Control Extended. The fields that indicate when the watchpoint can be generated are:
HMC, PAC, SSC, and SSCE.
These fields must be considered in combination, and the values that are permitted for these fields are constrained.

fields.name: MASK
fields.description: Address Mask. Only address ranges up to 2GB can be watched using a single mask.

values.label: No mask.
Number of address bits masked.

fields.name: WT2
fields.description: Watchpoint Type 2. With DBGWCR<n>_EL1.WT, specifies watchpoint type.

values.label: Watchpoint n is an address match watchpoint.
Watchpoint n is an address mismatch watchpoint.
Unlinked watchpoint.
Linked watchpoint.

fields.name: LBN
fields.description: Linked Breakpoint Number. For Linked data address watchpoints, with DBGWCR<n>_EL1.LBNX when implemented, specifies the index of the breakpoint linked to. For all other watchpoint types, this field is ignored and reads of the register return an UNKNOWN value.

fields.name: SSC
fields.description:
Security state control. Determines the Security states under which a Watchpoint debug event for watchpoint n is generated. The fields that indicate when the watchpoint can be generated are:
HMC, PAC, SSC, and SSCE.
These fields must be considered in combination, and the values that are permitted for these fields are constrained. For more information on the operation of these fields, see 'Execution conditions for which a watchpoint generates Watchpoint exceptions' . For more information on the effect of programming the fields to a reserved value, see 'Reserved DBGWCR<n>_EL1.{SSC, HMC, PAC} values' .

fields.name: HMC
fields.description:
Higher mode control. Determines the debug perspective for deciding when a Watchpoint debug event for watchpoint n is generated. The fields that indicate when the watchpoint can be generated are:
HMC, PAC, SSC, and SSCE.
These fields must be considered in combination, and the values that are permitted for these fields are constrained. For more information on the operation of these fields, see 'Execution conditions for which a watchpoint generates Watchpoint exceptions' .

fields.name: BAS
fields.description: Byte address select. Each bit of this field selects whether a byte from within the word or double-word addressed by DBGWVR<n>_EL1 is being watched. BAS Description xxxxxxx1 Match byte at DBGWVR<n>_EL1 xxxxxx1x Match byte at DBGWVR<n>_EL1 + 1 xxxxx1xx Match byte at DBGWVR<n>_EL1 + 2 xxxx1xxx Match byte at DBGWVR<n>_EL1 + 3 In cases where DBGWVR<n>_EL1 addresses a double-word: BAS Description, if DBGWVR<n>_EL1 [2] == 0 xxx1xxxx Match byte at DBGWVR<n>_EL1 + 4 xx1xxxxx Match byte at DBGWVR<n>_EL1 + 5 x1xxxxxx Match byte at DBGWVR<n>_EL1 + 6 1xxxxxxx Match byte at DBGWVR<n>_EL1 + 7 If DBGWVR<n>_EL1 [2] == 1, only BAS[3:0] are used and BAS[7:4] are ignored. Arm deprecates setting DBGWVR<n>_EL1 [2] == 1. The valid values for BAS are nonzero binary numbers all of whose set bits are contiguous. All other values are reserved and must not be used by software. See 'Reserved DBGWCR<n>_EL1.BAS values' .

fields.name: LSC
fields.description: Load/store control. This field enables watchpoint matching on the type of access being made. Possible values of this field are:

values.label: Match instructions that load from a watchpointed address.
Match instructions that store to a watchpointed address.
Match instructions that load from or store to a watchpointed address.

fields.name: PAC
fields.description:
Privilege of access control. Determines the Exception level or levels at which a Watchpoint debug event for watchpoint n is generated. The fields that indicate when the watchpoint can be generated are:
HMC, PAC, SSC, and SSCE.
These fields must be considered in combination, and the values that are permitted for these fields are constrained. For more information on the operation of these fields, see 'Execution conditions for which a watchpoint generates Watchpoint exceptions' .

values.label: Watchpoint n disabled.
Watchpoint n enabled.

name: DBGWVR<n>_EL1
description: Holds a data address value for use in watchpoint matching. Forms watchpoint n together with control register DBGWCR<n>_EL1 .

fields.name: RESS[14:8]
fields.description: Reserved, Sign extended. Software must set all bits in this field to the same value as the most significant bit of the VA field. If all bits in this field are not the same value as the most significant bit of the VA field, then all of the following apply: It is CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE whether the PE ignores this field when comparing an address. It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether the value read back in each bit of this field is a copy of the most significant bit of the VA field or the value written.

fields.name: VA[56:53]
fields.description: Extension to VA[48:2]. For more information, see VA[48:2].

fields.name: RESS[7:4]
fields.description: Extension to RESS[14:8]. For more information, see RESS[14:8].

fields.name: VA[52:49]
fields.description: Extension to VA[48:2]. For more information, see VA[48:2].

fields.name: RESS[3:0]
fields.description: Extension to RESS[14:8]. For more information, see RESS[14:8].

fields.name: VA[48:2]
fields.description: Bits[48:2] of the address value for comparison. When FEAT_LVA3 is implemented, (VA[56:53]:VA[52:49]) forms the upper part of the address value. If FEAT_LVA3 is not implemented, bits VA[56:53] are part of the RESS field. When FEAT_LVA is implemented, VA[52:49] forms the upper part of the address value. If FEAT_LVA is not implemented, bits [52:49] are part of the RESS field. Arm deprecates setting DBGWVR<n>_EL1[2] == 1.

name: DC CGDSW
long_name: Clean of Data and Allocation Tags by Set/Way
description: Clean data and Allocation Tags in data cache by set/way.

fields.name: SetWay
fields.description: Contains two fields: Way, bits[31:32-A], the number of the way to operate on. Set, bits[B-1:L], the number of the set to operate on. Bits[L-1:4] are RES0 . A = Log 2 (ASSOCIATIVITY), L = Log 2 (LINELEN), B = (L + S), S = Log 2 (NSETS). ASSOCIATIVITY, LINELEN (line length, in bytes), and NSETS (number of sets) have their usual meanings and are the values for the cache level being operated on. The values of A and S are rounded up to the next integer.

fields.name: Level
fields.description: Cache level to operate on, minus 1. For example, this field is 0 for operations on L1 cache, or 1 for operations on L2 cache.

name: DC CGDVAC
long_name: Clean of Data and Allocation Tags by VA to PoC
description: Clean data and Allocation Tags in data cache by address to Point of Coherency.

fields.name: VA
fields.description: Virtual address to use. No alignment restrictions apply to this VA.

name: DC CGDVADP
long_name: Clean of Data and Allocation Tags by VA to PoDP
description: Clean Allocation Tags and data in data cache by address to Point of Deep Persistence. If the memory system does not identify a Point of Deep Persistence, then this instruction behaves as a DC CGDVAP .

fields.name: VA
fields.description: Virtual address to use. No alignment restrictions apply to this VA.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with the NSE2 and NSE field, this field specifies the target physical address space. NSE2 NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 0b0 Reserved. 0b0 0b0 0b1 Reserved. 0b0 0b1 0b0 Reserved. 0b0 0b1 0b1 Realm. 0b1 0b0 0b0 System Agent. 0b1 0b0 0b1 NS Protected. 0b1 0b1 0b0 Reserved. 0b1 0b1 0b1 Reserved.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with the NSE field, this field specifies the target physical address space. NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 Reserved. 0b0 0b1 Reserved. 0b1 0b0 Reserved. 0b1 0b1 Realm.

fields.name: NSE
fields.description: If FEAT_RME_GDI is implemented, this field together with the NS and NSE2 fields, specifies the target physical address space. Otherwise, this field and the NS field specify the physical address space For a description of the values derived by evaluating NS, NSE, and NSE2 together, see DC CIGDPAE.NS.

fields.name: NSE2
fields.description: Together with the NS and NSE field, this field specifies the target physical address space. For a description of the values derived by evaluating NS and NSE together, see DC CIGDPAE.NS.

fields.name: PA[55:52]
fields.description: Extension to PA[51:0] if ID_AA64MMFR0_EL1 .PARange = 0111. For more information, see PA[51:0].

fields.name: PA
fields.description: Physical address to use. No alignment restrictions apply to this PA.

name: DC CIGDPAPA
long_name: Clean and Invalidate of Data and Allocation Tags by PA to PoPA
description: Clean and Invalidate data and Allocation Tags in data cache by physical address to the Point of Physical Aliasing. This instruction cleans and invalidates all copies of the Location specified in the Xt argument, irrespective of any MECID associated with the Location. Memory accesses resulting from the Clean operation use the MECID associated with the cache entry.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with the NSE2 and NSE field, this field specifies the target physical address space. NSE2 NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 0b0 When Secure state is implemented, Secure. Otherwise reserved. 0b0 0b0 0b1 Non-secure. 0b0 0b1 0b0 Root. 0b0 0b1 0b1 Realm. 0b1 0b0 0b0 System Agent. 0b1 0b0 0b1 NS Protected. 0b1 0b1 0b0 Reserved. 0b1 0b1 0b1 Reserved.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with the NSE field, this field specifies the target physical address space. NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 When Secure state is implemented, Secure. Otherwise reserved. 0b0 0b1 Non-secure. 0b1 0b0 Root. 0b1 0b1 Realm.

name: DC GBVA
long_name: Data Cache set Allocation Tag block by VA
description: Write a value to the Allocation Tags of a naturally aligned block of N bytes, where the size of N is identified in DCZID_EL0 .TBS. The Allocation Tag used is determined by the input address. This writes tags whether they are in part of a data cache or a separate tag cache.

fields.name: VA
fields.description: Virtual address to use. There is no alignment restriction on the address within the block of N bytes that is used.

name: DC GVA
long_name: Data Cache set Allocation Tag by VA
description: Write a value to the Allocation Tags of a naturally aligned block of N bytes, where the size of N is identified in DCZID_EL0 . The Allocation Tag used is determined by the input address.

fields.name: VA
fields.description: Virtual address to use. There is no alignment restriction on the address within the block of N bytes that is used.

name: DC GZVA
long_name: Data Cache set Allocation Tags and Zero by VA
description: Zero data and write a value to the Allocation Tags of a naturally aligned block of N bytes, where the size of N is identified in DCZID_EL0 .  The Allocation Tag used is determined by the input address. This instruction might have the poison-atomic property for IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED regions of physical memory.

fields.name: VA
fields.description: Virtual address to use. There is no alignment restriction on the address within the block of N bytes that is used.

name: DC ZGBVA
long_name: Data Cache Zero Allocation tag block by VA
description: Zero Allocation tag block by address. Zeroes a naturally aligned block of N tags, where the size of N is identified in DCZID_EL0 .TBS. This zeros tags whether they are in part of a data cache or a separate tag cache. This instruction might have the poison-atomic property for IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED regions of physical memory.

name: DCZID_EL0
description: Indicates the block size that is written with byte values of 0 by the DC ZVA (Data Cache Zero by Address) System instruction. If FEAT_MTE is implemented, this register indicates the granularity at which the DC GVA and DC GZVA instructions write. If FEAT_MTETC is implemented, this register indicates the granularity at which the DC GBVA and DC ZGBVA instructions write.

fields.name: TBS
fields.description: Log 2 of the block size in words written by a DC ZGBVA or DC GBVA instruction. The maximum size supported is 2KB, indicated by value 0b1001 . The minimum supported size is 16B (1 Allocation tag) indicated by the value 0b0010 .

fields.name: DZP
fields.description: Data Zero Prohibited. This field indicates whether use of DC ZVA instructions is permitted or prohibited. If FEAT_MTE is implemented, this field indicates whether use of the DC GVA and DC GZVA instructions are permitted or prohibited. If FEAT_MTETC is implemented, this field indicates whether use of the DC GBVA and DC ZGBVA instructions are permitted or prohibited.

values.label: Instructions are permitted.
Instructions are prohibited.

fields.name: BS
fields.description: Log 2 of the block size in words written by a DC ZVA instruction. The maximum size supported is 2KB, indicated by value 0b1001 . If FEAT_MTE2 is implemented, then this field determines the block size written by a DC GVA or DC GZVA instruction and the minimum size supported is 16 bytes, indicated by value 0b0010 .

name: DISR_EL1
description: Records that an SError exception has been consumed by an ESB instruction.

fields.name: A
fields.description: Set to 1 when an ESB instruction defers an asynchronous SError exception. If the implementation does not include any sources of SError exception that can be synchronized by an Error Synchronization Barrier, then this bit is RES0 .

fields.name: IDS
fields.description: Indicates the deferred SError exception type.

values.label: Deferred error uses architecturally-defined format.

fields.name: WU
fields.description: Write update. See the description of ESR_ELx .WU for an SError exception.

fields.name: AET
fields.description: Asynchronous Error Type. See the description of ESR_ELx .AET for an SError exception.

fields.name: EA
fields.description: External abort Type. See the description of ESR_ELx .EA for an SError exception.

fields.name: WnRV
fields.description: Write-not-Read Valid. See the description of ESR_ELx .WnRV for an SError exception.

fields.name: WnR
fields.description: Write-not-Read. See the description of ESR_ELx .WnR for an SError exception.

fields.name: DFSC
fields.description: Fault Status Code. See the description of ESR_ELx .DFSC for an SError exception.

name: DIT
description: Allows access to the Data Independent Timing bit.

values.label: The architecture makes no statement about the timing properties of any instructions.
The architecture requires that the execution time of a data-independent-time sequence of code must be independent of all data-independent-time values. For more information, see About PSTATE.DIT.

name: DLR_EL0
description: In Debug state, holds the address to restart from.

name: DSPSR_EL0
description: Holds the saved process state for Debug state. On entering Debug state, PSTATE information is written to this register. On exiting Debug state, values are copied from this register to PSTATE.

fields.name: UINJ
fields.description: Inject Undefined Instruction exception. Copied to PSTATE.UINJ on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: PPEND
fields.description: PMU Profiling exception pending bit. Copied to PSTATE.PPEND on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: N
fields.description: Negative Condition flag. Copied to PSTATE.N on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: Z
fields.description: Zero Condition flag. Copied to PSTATE.Z on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: C
fields.description: Carry Condition flag. Copied to PSTATE.C on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: V
fields.description: Overflow Condition flag. Copied to PSTATE.V on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: Q
fields.description: Overflow or saturation flag. Copied to PSTATE.Q on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: IT
fields.description: If-Then. Copied to PSTATE.IT on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: DIT
fields.description: Data Independent Timing. Copied to PSTATE.DIT on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: SSBS
fields.description: Speculative Store Bypass. Copied to PSTATE.SSBS on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: PAN
fields.description: Privileged Access Never. Copied to PSTATE.PAN on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: SS
fields.description: Software Step. Copied to PSTATE.SS on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: IL
fields.description: Illegal Execution state. Copied to PSTATE.IL on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: GE
fields.description: Greater than or Equal flags. Copied to PSTATE.GE on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: IT[7:2]
fields.description: This field is bits[7:2] of IT[7:0]. See IT[1:0] for the field description.

fields.name: E
fields.description: Endianness. Copied to PSTATE.E on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: A
fields.description: SError exception mask. Copied to PSTATE.A on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: I
fields.description: IRQ interrupt mask. Copied to PSTATE.I on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: F
fields.description: FIQ interrupt mask. Copied to PSTATE.F on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: T
fields.description: T32 Instruction set state. Copied to PSTATE.T on exiting Debug state.

fields.name: M[4]
fields.description: Execution state. Copied to PSTATE.nRW on exiting Debug state.

values.label: AArch32 execution state.

fields.name: M[3:0]
fields.description: AArch32 Mode. Copied to PSTATE.M[3:0] on exiting Debug state.

values.label: User.
FIQ.
IRQ.
Supervisor.
Monitor.
Abort.
Hyp.
Undefined.
System.

name: DVP RCTX
long_name: Data Value Prediction Restriction by Context
description: Data Value Prediction Restriction by Context applies to all Data Value Prediction Resources that predict execution based on information gathered within the target execution context or contexts. The prediction of the PSTATE.{N,Z,C,V} values is not considered a data value for this purpose. Data value predictions determined by the actions of code in the target execution context or contexts appearing in program order before the instruction cannot exploitatively control speculative execution occurring after the instruction is complete and synchronized. This instruction is guaranteed to be complete following a DSB that covers both read and write behavior on the same PE as executed the original restriction instruction, and a subsequent context synchronization event is required to ensure that the effect of the completion of the instructions is synchronized to the current execution. This instruction does not require the invalidation of prediction structures so long as the behavior described for completion of this instruction is met by the implementation. On some implementations the instruction is likely to take a significant number of cycles to execute. This instruction is expected to be used very rarely, such as on the roll-over of an ASID or VMID, but should not be used on every context switch.

fields.name: GVMID
fields.description: Execution of this instruction applies to all VMIDs or a specified VMID.

values.label: Applies to specified VMID for an EL0 or EL1 target execution context.
Applies to all VMIDs for an EL0 or EL1 target execution context.

fields.name: VMID
fields.description: Only applies when bit[48] is 0 and the target execution context is either: EL1. EL0 when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}. Otherwise this field is RES0 . When the instruction is executed at EL1, this field is treated as the current VMID. When the instruction is executed at EL0 and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, this field is treated as the current VMID. When the instruction is executed at EL0 and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, this field is ignored. If EL2 is not implemented or not enabled for the target Security state, this field is RES0 . If the implementation supports 16 bits of VMID, then the upper 8 bits of the VMID must be written to 0 by software when the context being affected only uses 8 bits.

fields.name: NSE
fields.description: Together with the NS field, selects the Security state. For a description of the values derived by evaluating NS and NSE together, see DVP_RCTX.NS.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with the NSE field, selects the Security state. NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 When Secure state is implemented, Secure. Otherwise reserved. 0b0 0b1 Non-secure. 0b1 0b0 Root. 0b1 0b1 Realm.

values.label: Secure state.
Non-secure state.

fields.name: EL
fields.description: Exception Level. Indicates the Exception level of the target execution context.

values.label: EL0.
EL1
EL2
EL3

fields.name: GASID
fields.description: Execution of this instruction applies to all ASIDs or a specified ASID.

values.label: Applies to specified ASID for an EL0 target execution context.
Applies to all ASIDs for an EL0 target execution context.

fields.name: ASID
fields.description: Only applies for an EL0 target execution context and when bit[16] is 0. Otherwise this field is RES0 . When the instruction is executed at EL0, this field is treated as the current ASID. If the implementation supports 16 bits of ASID, then the upper 8 bits of the ASID must be written to 0 by software when the context being affected only uses 8 bits.

name: ELR_EL1
description: When taking an exception to EL1, holds the address to return to.

fields.name: ADDR
fields.description: Return address. An exception return from EL1 using AArch64 makes ELR_EL1 become UNKNOWN .

name: ELR_EL2
description: When taking an exception to EL2, holds the address to return to.

fields.name: ADDR
fields.description: Return address. An exception return from EL2 using AArch64 makes ELR_EL2 become UNKNOWN . When EL2 is in AArch32 Execution state and an exception is taken from EL0, EL1, or EL2 to EL3 and AArch64 execution, the upper 32-bits of ELR_EL2 are either set to 0 or hold the same value that they did before AArch32 execution. Which option is adopted is determined by an implementation, and might vary dynamically within an implementation. Correspondingly software must regard the value as being an UNKNOWN choice between the two values.

name: ELR_EL3
description: When taking an exception to EL3, holds the address to return to.

fields.name: ADDR
fields.description: Return address. An exception return from EL3 using AArch64 makes ELR_EL3 become UNKNOWN .

name: ERRIDR_EL1
description: Defines the highest numbered index of the error records that can be accessed through the Error Record System registers.

fields.name: NUM
fields.description: Highest numbered index of the records that can be accessed through the Error Record System registers plus one. Zero indicates no records can be accessed through the Error Record System registers. Each implemented record is owned by a node. A node might own multiple records.

name: ERRSELR_EL1
description: Selects an error record to be accessed through the Error Record System registers.

fields.name: SEL
fields.description: Selects the error record accessed through the ERX registers.

name: ERXADDR_EL1
description: Accesses ERR<n>ADDR for the error record <n> selected by ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

fields.name: ERRnADDR
fields.description: ERXADDR_EL1 accesses ERR<n>ADDR , where <n> is the value in ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

name: ERXCTLR_EL1
description: Accesses ERR<n>CTLR for the error record <n> selected by ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

fields.name: ERRnCTLR
fields.description: ERXCTLR_EL1 accesses ERR<n>CTLR , where <n> is the value in ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

name: ERXFR_EL1
description: Accesses ERR<n>FR for the error record <n> selected by ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

fields.name: ERRnFR
fields.description: ERXFR_EL1 accesses ERR<n>FR , where <n> is the value in ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

name: ERXGSR_EL1
long_name: Selected Error Record Group Status Register
description: Shows the status for the records in a group of error records. Accesses ERRGSR for the group of error records <n> selected by ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL[15:6].

fields.name: S<q>
fields.description: The status for error record <m>, where m = q + (UInt( ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL[15:6])×64). A read-only copy of ERR<m>STATUS .V.

values.label: No error.
One or more errors.

name: ERXMISC0_EL1
description: Accesses ERR<n>MISC0 for the error record <n> selected by ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

fields.name: ERRnMISC0
fields.description: ERXMISC0_EL1 accesses ERR<n>MISC0 , where <n> is the value in ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

name: ERXMISC1_EL1
description: Accesses ERR<n>MISC1 for the error record <n> selected by ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

fields.name: ERRnMISC1
fields.description: ERXMISC1_EL1 accesses ERR<n>MISC1 , where <n> is the value in ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

name: ERXMISC2_EL1
description: Accesses ERR<n>MISC2 for the error record <n> selected by ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

fields.name: ERRnMISC2
fields.description: ERXMISC2_EL1 accesses ERR<n>MISC2 , where <n> is the value in ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

name: ERXMISC3_EL1
description: Accesses ERR<n>MISC3 for the error record <n> selected by ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

fields.name: ERRnMISC3
fields.description: ERXMISC3_EL1 accesses ERR<n>MISC3 , where <n> is the value in ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

name: ERXPFGCDN_EL1
description: Accesses ERR<n>PFGCDN for the error record <n> selected by ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

fields.name: ERRnPFGCDN
fields.description: ERXPFGCDN_EL1 accesses ERR<n>PFGCDN , where <n> is the value in ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

name: ERXPFGCTL_EL1
description: Accesses ERR<n>PFGCTL for the error record <n> selected by ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

fields.name: ERRnPFGCTL
fields.description: ERXPFGCTL_EL1 accesses ERR<n>PFGCTL , where <n> is the value in ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

name: ERXPFGF_EL1
description: Accesses ERR<n>PFGF for the error record <n> selected by ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

fields.name: ERRnPFGF
fields.description: ERXPFGF_EL1 accesses ERR<n>PFGF , where <n> is the value in ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

name: ERXSTATUS_EL1
long_name: Selected Error Record Primary Status Register
description: Accesses ERR<n>STATUS for the error record <n> selected by ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

fields.name: ERRnSTATUS
fields.description: ERXSTATUS_EL1 accesses ERR<n>STATUS , where <n> is the value in ERRSELR_EL1 .SEL.

name: ESR_EL1
description: Holds syndrome information for an exception taken to EL1.

fields.name: ISS2
fields.description: ISS2 encoding for an exception, the bit assignments are:

fields.name: EC
fields.description: Exception Class. Indicates the reason for the exception that this register holds information about. For each EC value, the table references a subsection that gives information about: The cause of the exception, for example the configuration required to enable the trap. The encoding of the associated ISS. Possible values of the EC field are:

values.label: Unknown reason.
Trapped WF* instruction execution. Conditional WF* instructions that fail their condition code check do not cause an exception.
Trapped MCR or MRC access with (coproc== 0b1111 ) that is not reported using EC value 0b000000 .
Trapped MCRR or MRRC access with (coproc== 0b1111 ) that is not reported using EC value 0b000000 .
Trapped MCR or MRC access with (coproc== 0b1110 ).
Trapped LDC or STC access. The only architected uses of these instruction are: An STC to write data to memory from DBGDTRRXint . An LDC to read data from memory to DBGDTRTXint .

Access to SME, SVE, Advanced SIMD or floating-point functionality trapped by CPACR_EL1 .FPEN, CPTR_EL2 .FPEN, CPTR_EL2 .TFP, or CPTR_EL3 .TFP control. Excludes exceptions resulting from CPACR_EL1 when the value of HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, or because SVE or Advanced SIMD and floating-point are not implemented. These are reported with EC value 0b000000 .
Trapped use of a Pointer authentication instruction.
Trapped execution of any instruction not covered by other EC values.
Trapped MRRC access with (coproc== 0b1110 ).
Branch Target Exception.
Illegal Execution state.
SVC instruction execution in AArch32 state.
Trapped MSRR, MRRS or System instruction execution in AArch64 state, that is not reported using EC value 0b000000 .
SVC instruction execution in AArch64 state.

Trapped MSR, MRS or System instruction execution in AArch64 state, that is not reported using EC values 0b000000 , 0b000001 , or 0b000111 . This includes all instructions that cause exceptions that are part of the encoding space defined in 'System instruction class encoding overview' , except for those exceptions reported using EC values 0b000000 , 0b000001 , or 0b000111 .
Access to SVE functionality trapped as a result of CPACR_EL1 .ZEN, CPTR_EL2 .ZEN, CPTR_EL2 .TZ, or CPTR_EL3 .EZ, that is not reported using EC value 0b000000 .
Trapped ERET, ERETAA, or ERETAB instruction execution.
Exception from a PAC Fail

Access to SME functionality trapped as a result of CPACR_EL1 .SMEN, CPTR_EL2 .SMEN, CPTR_EL2 .TSM, CPTR_EL3 .ESM, or an attempted execution of an instruction that is illegal because of the value of PSTATE.SM or PSTATE.ZA, that is not reported using EC value 0b000000 .

Instruction Abort from a lower Exception level. Used for MMU faults generated by instruction accesses and synchronous External aborts, including synchronous parity or ECC errors. Not used for debug-related exceptions.

Instruction Abort taken without a change in Exception level. Used for MMU faults generated by instruction accesses and synchronous External aborts, including synchronous parity or ECC errors. Not used for debug-related exceptions.
PC alignment fault exception.

Data Abort exception from a lower Exception level. Used for MMU faults generated by data accesses, alignment faults other than those caused by Stack Pointer misalignment, and synchronous External aborts, including synchronous parity or ECC errors. Not used for debug-related exceptions.

Data Abort exception taken without a change in Exception level. Used for MMU faults generated by data accesses, alignment faults other than those caused by Stack Pointer misalignment, and synchronous External aborts, including synchronous parity or ECC errors. Not used for debug-related exceptions.
SP alignment fault exception.
Memory Operation Exception.

Trapped floating-point exception taken from AArch32 state. This EC value is valid if the implementation supports trapping of floating-point exceptions, otherwise it is reserved. Whether a floating-point implementation supports trapping of floating-point exceptions is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .

Trapped floating-point exception taken from AArch64 state. This EC value is valid if the implementation supports trapping of floating-point exceptions, otherwise it is reserved. Whether a floating-point implementation supports trapping of floating-point exceptions is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .
GCS exception.
SError exception.
Breakpoint exception from a lower Exception level.
Breakpoint exception taken without a change in Exception level.
Software Step exception from a lower Exception level.
Software Step exception taken without a change in Exception level.
Watchpoint exception from a lower Exception level.
Watchpoint exception taken without a change in Exception level.
BKPT instruction execution in AArch32 state.
BRK instruction execution in AArch64 state.
Profiling exception

fields.name: IL
fields.description: Instruction Length for synchronous exceptions. Possible values of this bit are:

values.label: 16-bit instruction trapped.

32-bit instruction trapped. This value is also used when the exception is one of the following: An SError exception. An Instruction Abort exception. A PC alignment fault exception. An SP alignment fault exception. A Data Abort exception for which the value of the ISV bit is 0. An Illegal Execution state exception. Any debug exception except for Breakpoint instruction exceptions. For Breakpoint instruction exceptions, this bit has its standard meaning: 0b0 : 16-bit T32 BKPT instruction. 0b1 : 32-bit A32 BKPT instruction or A64 BRK instruction. An exception reported using EC value 0b000000 .

fields.name: ISS
fields.description: Instruction Specific Syndrome. Architecturally, this field can be defined independently for each defined Exception class. However, in practice, some ISS encodings are used for more than one Exception class. Typically, an ISS encoding has a number of subfields. When an ISS subfield holds a register number, the value returned in that field is the AArch64 view of the register number. For an exception taken from AArch32 state, see 'Mapping of the general-purpose registers between the Execution states' . If the AArch32 register descriptor is 0b1111 , then: If the instruction that generated the exception was not UNPREDICTABLE , the field takes the value 0b11111 . If the instruction that generated the exception was UNPREDICTABLE , the field takes an UNKNOWN value that must be either: The AArch64 view of the register number of a register that might have been used at the Exception level from which the exception was taken. The value 0b11111 .

name: ESR_EL2
description: Holds syndrome information for an exception taken to EL2.

fields.name: ISS2
fields.description: ISS2 encoding for an exception, the bit assignments are:

fields.name: EC
fields.description: Exception Class. Indicates the reason for the exception that this register holds information about. For each EC value, the table references a subsection that gives information about: The cause of the exception, for example the configuration required to enable the trap. The encoding of the associated ISS. Possible values of the EC field are:

values.label: Unknown reason.
Trapped WF* instruction execution. Conditional WF* instructions that fail their condition code check do not cause an exception.
Trapped MCR or MRC access with (coproc== 0b1111 ) that is not reported using EC value 0b000000 .
Trapped MCRR or MRRC access with (coproc== 0b1111 ) that is not reported using EC value 0b000000 .
Trapped MCR or MRC access with (coproc== 0b1110 ).
Trapped LDC or STC access. The only architected uses of these instruction are: An STC to write data to memory from DBGDTRRXint . An LDC to read data from memory to DBGDTRTXint .

Access to SME, SVE, Advanced SIMD or floating-point functionality trapped by CPACR_EL1 .FPEN, CPTR_EL2 .FPEN, CPTR_EL2 .TFP, or CPTR_EL3 .TFP control. Excludes exceptions resulting from CPACR_EL1 when the value of HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, or because SVE or Advanced SIMD and floating-point are not implemented. These are reported with EC value 0b000000 .
Trapped VMRS access, from ID group trap, that is not reported using EC value 0b000111 .
Trapped use of a Pointer authentication instruction.
Trapped execution of any instruction not covered by other EC values.
Trapped MRRC access with (coproc== 0b1110 ).
Branch Target Exception.
Illegal Execution state.
SVC instruction execution in AArch32 state. This is reported in ESR_EL2 only when the exception is generated because the value of HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1.
HVC instruction execution in AArch32 state, when HVC is not disabled.
SMC instruction execution in AArch32 state, when SMC is not disabled. This is reported in ESR_EL2 only when the exception is generated because the value of HCR_EL2 .TSC is 1.
Trapped MSRR, MRRS or System instruction execution in AArch64 state, that is not reported using EC value 0b000000 .
SVC instruction execution in AArch64 state.
HVC instruction execution in AArch64 state, when HVC is not disabled.
SMC instruction execution in AArch64 state, when SMC is not disabled. This is reported in ESR_EL2 only when the exception is generated because the value of HCR_EL2 .TSC is 1.

Trapped MSR, MRS or System instruction execution in AArch64 state, that is not reported using EC values 0b000000 , 0b000001 or 0b000111 . This includes all instructions that cause exceptions that are part of the encoding space defined in 'System instruction class encoding overview' , except for those exceptions reported using EC values 0b000000 , 0b000001 , or 0b000111 .
Access to SVE functionality trapped as a result of CPACR_EL1 .ZEN, CPTR_EL2 .ZEN, CPTR_EL2 .TZ, or CPTR_EL3 .EZ, that is not reported using EC value 0b000000 .
Trapped ERET, ERETAA, or ERETAB instruction execution.
Exception from a PAC Fail

Access to SME functionality trapped as a result of CPACR_EL1 .SMEN, CPTR_EL2 .SMEN, CPTR_EL2 .TSM, CPTR_EL3 .ESM, or an attempted execution of an instruction that is illegal because of the value of PSTATE.SM or PSTATE.ZA, that is not reported using EC value 0b000000 .

Instruction Abort from a lower Exception level. Used for MMU faults generated by instruction accesses and synchronous External aborts, including synchronous parity or ECC errors. Not used for debug-related exceptions.

Instruction Abort taken without a change in Exception level. Used for MMU faults generated by instruction accesses and synchronous External aborts, including synchronous parity or ECC errors. Not used for debug-related exceptions.
PC alignment fault exception.

Data Abort exception from a lower Exception level, excluding Data Abort exceptions taken to EL2 as a result of accesses generated associated with VNCR_EL2 as part of nested virtualization support. These Data Abort exceptions might be generated from Exception levels in any Execution state. Used for MMU faults generated by data accesses, alignment faults other than those caused by Stack Pointer misalignment, and synchronous External aborts, including synchronous parity or ECC errors. Not used for debug-related exceptions.

Data Abort exception without a change in Exception level, or Data Abort exceptions taken to EL2 as a result of accesses generated associated with VNCR_EL2 as part of nested virtualization support. Used for MMU faults generated by data accesses, alignment faults other than those caused by Stack Pointer misalignment, and synchronous External aborts, including synchronous parity or ECC errors. Not used for debug-related exceptions.
SP alignment fault exception.
Memory Operation Exception.

Trapped floating-point exception taken from AArch32 state. This EC value is valid if the implementation supports trapping of floating-point exceptions, otherwise it is reserved. Whether a floating-point implementation supports trapping of floating-point exceptions is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .

Trapped floating-point exception taken from AArch64 state. This EC value is valid if the implementation supports trapping of floating-point exceptions, otherwise it is reserved. Whether a floating-point implementation supports trapping of floating-point exceptions is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .
GCS exception.
SError exception.
Breakpoint exception from a lower Exception level.
Breakpoint exception taken without a change in Exception level.
Software Step exception from a lower Exception level.
Software Step exception taken without a change in Exception level.

Watchpoint from a lower Exception level, excluding Watchpoint Exceptions taken to EL2 as a result of accesses generated associated with VNCR_EL2 as part of nested virtualization support. These Watchpoint Exceptions might be generated from Exception levels using any Execution state.
Watchpoint exceptions without a change in Exception level, or Watchpoint exceptions taken to EL2 as a result of accesses generated associated with VNCR_EL2 as part of nested virtualization support.
BKPT instruction execution in AArch32 state.

Vector Catch exception from AArch32 state. The only case where a Vector Catch exception is taken to an Exception level that is using AArch64 is when the exception is routed to EL2 and EL2 is using AArch64.
BRK instruction execution in AArch64 state.
Profiling exception

Any debug exception except for Breakpoint instruction exceptions. For Breakpoint instruction exceptions, this bit has its standard meaning: 0b0 : 16-bit T32 BKPT instruction. 0b1 : 32-bit A32 BKPT instruction or A64 BRK instruction. For each EC value, the table references a subsection that gives information about: The cause of the exception, for example the configuration required to enable the trap.
