name: ICC_ASGI1R_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Alias Software Generated Interrupt Group 1 Register
description: Generates Group 1 SGIs for the Security state that is not the current Security state.

fields.name: Aff3
fields.description: The affinity 3 value of the affinity path of the cluster for which SGI interrupts will be generated. If the IRM bit is 1, this field is RES0 .

fields.name: RS
fields.description: RangeSelector Controls which group of 16 values is represented by the TargetList field. TargetList[n] represents aff0 value ((RS * 16) + n). When ICC_CTLR_EL1 .RSS==0, RS is RES0 . When ICC_CTLR_EL1 .RSS==1 and GICD_TYPER .RSS==0, writing this register with RS != 0 is a CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE choice of: The write is ignored. The RS field is treated as 0.

fields.name: IRM
fields.description: Interrupt Routing Mode. Determines how the generated interrupts are distributed to PEs. Possible values are:

values.label: Interrupts routed to the PEs specified by Aff3.Aff2.Aff1.<target list>.
Interrupts routed to all PEs in the system, excluding "self".

fields.name: Aff2
fields.description: The affinity 2 value of the affinity path of the cluster for which SGI interrupts will be generated. If the IRM bit is 1, this field is RES0 .

fields.name: Aff1
fields.description: The affinity 1 value of the affinity path of the cluster for which SGI interrupts will be generated. If the IRM bit is 1, this field is RES0 .

fields.name: TargetList
fields.description:
Target List. The set of PEs for which SGI interrupts will be generated. Each bit corresponds to the PE within a cluster with an Affinity 0 value equal to the bit number. If SRE is set only for EL3, software executing at EL3 might use the System register interface to generate SGIs.
Therefore, the Distributor must always be able to receive and acknowledge Generate SGI packets received from CPU interface regardless of the ARE settings for a Security state.
However, the Distributor might discard such packets. If the IRM bit is 1, this field is RES0 .

name: ICC_BPR0_EL1
description: Defines the point at which the priority value fields split into two parts, the group priority field and the subpriority field. The group priority field determines Group 0 interrupt preemption.

fields.name: BinaryPoint
fields.description: The value of this field controls how the 8-bit interrupt priority field is split into a group priority field, that determines interrupt preemption, and a subpriority field. This is done as follows: Binary point value Group priority field Subpriority field Field with binary point 0 [7:1] [0] ggggggg.s 1 [7:2] [1:0] gggggg.ss 2 [7:3] [2:0] ggggg.sss 3 [7:4] [3:0] gggg.ssss 4 [7:5] [4:0] ggg.sssss 5 [7:6] [5:0] gg.ssssss 6 [7] [6:0] g.sssssss 7 No preemption [7:0] .ssssssss

name: ICC_BPR1_EL1
description: Defines the point at which the priority value fields split into two parts, the group priority field and the subpriority field. The group priority field determines Group 1 interrupt preemption.

fields.name: BinaryPoint
fields.description: If the GIC is configured to use separate binary point fields for Group 0 and Group 1 interrupts, the value of this field controls how the 8-bit interrupt priority field is split into a group priority field, that determines interrupt preemption, and a subpriority field. For more information about priorities, see 'Priority grouping' in ARM® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 3.0 and version 4.0 (ARM IHI 0069) . The minimum value of the Non-secure copy of this register is the minimum value of ICC_BPR0_EL1 + 1. The minimum value of the Secure copy of this register is the minimum value of ICC_BPR0_EL1 . If EL3 is implemented and ICC_CTLR_EL3 .CBPR_EL1S is 1: Accesses to this register from Secure EL2 access the state of ICC_BPR0_EL1 . Accesses to this register from Secure EL1: When SCR_EL3 .EEL2 is 1 and HCR_EL2 .IMO is 1, access the state of ICV_BPR1_EL1 . Otherwise, access the state of ICC_BPR0_EL1 . If EL3 is implemented and ICC_CTLR_EL3 .CBPR_EL1NS is 1, Non-secure accesses to this register at EL1 and EL2 behave as follows, depending on the values of HCR_EL2 .IMO and SCR_EL3 .IRQ: HCR_EL2.IMO SCR_EL3.IRQ Behavior 0b0 0b0 Non-secure EL1 and EL2 reads return ICC_BPR0_EL1 + 1 saturated to 0b111 . Non-secure EL1 and EL2 writes are ignored. 0b0 0b1 Non-secure EL1 and EL2 accesses trap to EL3. 0b1 0b0 Non-secure EL1 accesses affect virtual interrupts. Non-secure EL2 reads return ICC_BPR0_EL1 + 1 saturated to 0b111 . Non-secure EL2 writes are ignored. 0b1 0b1 Non-secure EL1 accesses affect virtual interrupts. Non-secure EL2 accesses trap to EL3. If EL3 is not implemented and ICC_CTLR_EL1 .CBPR is 1, Non-secure accesses to this register at EL1 and EL2 behave as follows, depending on the values of HCR_EL2 .IMO: HCR_EL2.IMO Behavior 0b0 Non-secure EL1 and EL2 reads return ICC_BPR0_EL1 + 1 saturated to 0b111 . Non-secure EL1 and EL2 writes are ignored. 0b1 Non-secure EL1 accesses affect virtual interrupts. Non-secure EL2 reads return ICC_BPR0_EL1 + 1 saturated to 0b111 . Non-secure EL2 writes are ignored.

name: ICC_CR0_EL1
description: Controls behavior of the physical CPU interface for the Non-secure, Realm, and Secure Interrupt Domains.

fields.name: PID
fields.description: Preemptive Interrupt Domain. Indicates whether the Interrupt Domain associated with the Security state selected by SCR_EL3.{NSE,NS} is the Preemptive Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: IPPT
fields.description: Interrupt Preemptive Priority Threshold value for the Preemptive Interrupt Domain. ICC_CR0_EL1 .IPPT is a 6 bits field to ensure it can be strictly higher than the interrupt priority, which is a 5-bit unsigned value. See 'Preemptive interrupts' in Arm® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 5 (ARM AES 0070) for more information.

fields.name: LINK_IDLE
fields.description: Whether the link between the CPU interface and the IRI is in the process of connecting or disconnecting.

values.label: The link between the CPU interface and the IRI is in the process of connecting or disconnecting.
The link between the CPU interface and the IRI is not in the process of connecting or disconnecting.

fields.name: LINK
fields.description: Whether the link between the CPU interface and the IRI is connected.

values.label: The link between the CPU interface and the IRI is disconnected.
The link between the CPU interface and the IRI is connected.

fields.name: EN
fields.description: Enable interrupts for the Interrupt Domain. When this field is 0, there is no HPPI of Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.

values.label: Disabled.
Enabled.

name: ICC_CR0_EL3
description: Controls behavior of the CPU interface in the EL3 Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: PID
fields.description: Preemptive Interrupt Domain. The PE behaves as if there is no Preemptive Interrupt Domain when any of the following are true: This field is set to 0b10 . This field is set to an Interrupt Domain that is not implemented.

values.label: Secure Interrupt Domain
Non-secure Interrupt Domain
None
Realm Interrupt Domain

fields.name: LINK_IDLE
fields.description: Whether the link between the CPU interface and the IRI is in the process of connecting or disconnecting.

values.label: The link between the CPU interface and the IRI is in the process of connecting or disconnecting.
The link between the CPU interface and the IRI is not in the process of connecting or disconnecting.

fields.name: LINK
fields.description: Whether the link between the CPU interface and the IRI is connected.

values.label: The link between the CPU interface and the IRI is disconnected.
The link between the CPU interface and the IRI is connected.

fields.name: EN
fields.description: Enable interrupts for the EL3 Interrupt Domain. When this field is set to 0, the PE behaves as if there is no HPPI of Sufficient priority in the EL3 Interrupt Domain.

values.label: Disabled.
Enabled.

name: ICC_CTLR_EL1
description: Controls aspects of the behavior of the GIC CPU interface and provides information about the features implemented.

values.label: CPU interface does not support INTIDs in the range 1024..8191. Behavior is UNPREDICTABLE if the IRI delivers an interrupt in the range 1024 to 8191 to the CPU interface. Arm strongly recommends that the IRI is not configured to deliver interrupts in this range to a PE that does not support them.
CPU interface supports INTIDs in the range 1024..8191 All INTIDs in the range 1024..8191 are treated as requiring deactivation.

fields.name: RSS
fields.description: Range Selector Support. Possible values are:

values.label: Targeted SGIs with affinity level 0 values of 0 - 15 are supported.
Targeted SGIs with affinity level 0 values of 0 - 255 are supported.

fields.name: A3V
fields.description: Affinity 3 Valid. Read-only and writes are ignored. Possible values are:

values.label: The CPU interface logic only supports zero values of Affinity 3 in SGI generation System registers.
The CPU interface logic supports nonzero values of Affinity 3 in SGI generation System registers.

fields.name: SEIS
fields.description: SEI Support. Read-only and writes are ignored. Indicates whether the CPU interface supports local generation of SEIs:

values.label: The CPU interface logic does not support local generation of SEIs.
The CPU interface logic supports local generation of SEIs.

fields.name: IDbits
fields.description: Identifier bits. Read-only and writes are ignored. The number of physical interrupt identifier bits supported:

values.label: 16 bits.
24 bits.

fields.name: PRIbits
fields.description: Priority bits. Read-only and writes are ignored. The number of priority bits implemented, minus one. An implementation that supports two Security states must implement at least 32 levels of physical priority (5 priority bits). An implementation that supports only a single Security state must implement at least 16 levels of physical priority (4 priority bits). This field always returns the number of priority bits implemented, regardless of the Security state of the access or the value of GICD_CTLR .DS. For physical accesses, this field determines the minimum value of ICC_BPR0_EL1 . If EL3 is implemented, physical accesses return the value from ICC_CTLR_EL3 .PRIbits. If EL3 is not implemented, physical accesses return the value from this field.

fields.name: PMHE
fields.description: Priority Mask Hint Enable. Controls whether the priority mask register is used as a hint for interrupt distribution:

values.label: Disables use of ICC_PMR_EL1 as a hint for interrupt distribution.
Enables use of ICC_PMR_EL1 as a hint for interrupt distribution.

fields.name: EOImode
fields.description: EOI mode for the current Security state. Controls whether a write to an End of Interrupt register also deactivates the interrupt:

values.label: ICC_EOIR0_EL1 and ICC_EOIR1_EL1 provide both priority drop and interrupt deactivation functionality. Accesses to ICC_DIR_EL1 are UNPREDICTABLE .
ICC_EOIR0_EL1 and ICC_EOIR1_EL1 provide priority drop functionality only. ICC_DIR_EL1 provides interrupt deactivation functionality.

fields.name: CBPR
fields.description: Common Binary Point Register. Controls whether the same register is used for interrupt preemption of both Group 0 and Group 1 interrupts:

values.label: ICC_BPR0_EL1 determines the preemption group for Group 0 interrupts only. ICC_BPR1_EL1 determines the preemption group for Group 1 interrupts.
ICC_BPR0_EL1 determines the preemption group for both Group 0 and Group 1 interrupts.

name: ICC_CTLR_EL3
description: Controls aspects of the behavior of the GIC CPU interface and provides information about the features implemented.

values.label: CPU interface does not support INTIDs in the range 1024..8191. Behavior is UNPREDICTABLE if the IRI delivers an interrupt in the range 1024 to 8191 to the CPU interface. Arm strongly recommends that the IRI is not configured to deliver interrupts in this range to a PE that does not support them.
CPU interface supports INTIDs in the range 1024..8191 All INTIDs in the range 1024..8191 are treated as requiring deactivation.
Targeted SGIs with affinity level 0 values of 0-15 are supported.
Targeted SGIs with affinity level 0 values of 0-255 are supported.

fields.name: nDS
fields.description: Disable Security not supported. Read-only and writes are ignored.

values.label: The CPU interface logic supports disabling of security.
The CPU interface logic does not support disabling of security, and requires that security is not disabled.

fields.name: A3V
fields.description: Affinity 3 Valid. Read-only and writes are ignored.

values.label: The CPU interface logic does not support nonzero values of the Aff3 field in SGI generation System registers.
The CPU interface logic supports nonzero values of the Aff3 field in SGI generation System registers.

fields.name: SEIS
fields.description: SEI Support. Read-only and writes are ignored. Indicates whether the CPU interface supports generation of SEIs:

values.label: The CPU interface logic does not support generation of SEIs.
The CPU interface logic supports generation of SEIs.

fields.name: IDbits
fields.description: Identifier bits. Read-only and writes are ignored. Indicates the number of physical interrupt identifier bits supported.

values.label: 16 bits.
24 bits.

fields.name: PRIbits
fields.description: Priority bits. Read-only and writes are ignored. The number of priority bits implemented, minus one. An implementation that supports two Security states must implement at least 32 levels of physical priority (5 priority bits). An implementation that supports only a single Security state must implement at least 16 levels of physical priority (4 priority bits). This field always returns the number of priority bits implemented, regardless of the value of SCR_EL3 .NS or the value of GICD_CTLR .DS. The division between group priority and subpriority is defined in the binary point registers ICC_BPR0_EL1 and ICC_BPR1_EL1 . This field determines the minimum value of ICC_BPR0_EL1.

values.label: Disables use of the priority mask register as a hint for interrupt distribution.
Enables use of the priority mask register as a hint for interrupt distribution.

fields.name: RM
fields.description: Routing Modifier. This bit controls whether EL3 can acknowledge, or observe as the Highest Priority Pending Interrupt, Secure Group 0 and Non-secure Group 1 interrupts.

values.label: Secure Group 0 and Non-secure Group 1 interrupts can be acknowledged and observed as the highest priority interrupt at EL3.

Secure Group 0 and Non-secure Group 1 interrupts cannot be acknowledged and observed as the highest priority interrupt at EL3. Secure Group 0 interrupts return a special INTID value of 1020. This affects accesses to ICC_IAR0_EL1 and ICC_HPPIR0_EL1 . Non-secure Group 1 interrupts return a special INTID value of 1021. This affects accesses to ICC_IAR1_EL1 and ICC_HPPIR1_EL1 .

fields.name: EOImode_EL1NS
fields.description: EOI mode for interrupts handled at Non-secure EL1 and EL2. Controls whether a write to an End of Interrupt register also deactivates the interrupt.

values.label: ICC_EOIR0_EL1 and ICC_EOIR1_EL1 provide both priority drop and interrupt deactivation functionality. Accesses to ICC_DIR_EL1 are UNPREDICTABLE .
ICC_EOIR0_EL1 and ICC_EOIR1_EL1 provide priority drop functionality only. ICC_DIR_EL1 provides interrupt deactivation functionality.

fields.name: EOImode_EL1S
fields.description: EOI mode for interrupts handled at Secure EL1 and EL2. Controls whether a write to an End of Interrupt register also deactivates the interrupt.

values.label: ICC_EOIR0_EL1 and ICC_EOIR1_EL1 provide both priority drop and interrupt deactivation functionality. Accesses to ICC_DIR_EL1 are UNPREDICTABLE .
ICC_EOIR0_EL1 and ICC_EOIR1_EL1 provide priority drop functionality only. ICC_DIR_EL1 provides interrupt deactivation functionality.

fields.name: EOImode_EL3
fields.description: EOI mode for interrupts handled at EL3. Controls whether a write to an End of Interrupt register also deactivates the interrupt.

values.label: ICC_EOIR0_EL1 and ICC_EOIR1_EL1 provide both priority drop and interrupt deactivation functionality. Accesses to ICC_DIR_EL1 are UNPREDICTABLE .
ICC_EOIR0_EL1 and ICC_EOIR1_EL1 provide priority drop functionality only. ICC_DIR_EL1 provides interrupt deactivation functionality.

fields.name: CBPR_EL1NS
fields.description: Common Binary Point Register, EL1 Non-secure. Controls whether the same register is used for interrupt preemption of both Group 0 and Group 1 Non-secure interrupts at EL1 and EL2.

values.label: ICC_BPR0_EL1 determines the preemption group for Group 0 interrupts only. ICC_BPR1_EL1 determines the preemption group for Non-secure Group 1 interrupts.
ICC_BPR0_EL1 determines the preemption group for Group 0 interrupts and Non-secure Group 1 interrupts. Non-secure accesses to GICC_BPR and ICC_BPR1_EL1 access the state of ICC_BPR0_EL1 .

fields.name: CBPR_EL1S
fields.description: Common Binary Point Register, EL1 Secure. Controls whether the same register is used for interrupt preemption of both Group 0 and Group 1 Secure interrupts at EL1 and EL2.

values.label: ICC_BPR0_EL1 determines the preemption group for Group 0 interrupts only. ICC_BPR1_EL1 determines the preemption group for Secure Group 1 interrupts.
ICC_BPR0_EL1 determines the preemption group for Group 0 interrupts and Secure Group 1 interrupts. Secure EL1 and Secure EL2 accesses to ICC_BPR1_EL1 access the state of ICC_BPR0_EL1 .

name: ICC_DIR_EL1
description: When interrupt priority drop is separated from interrupt deactivation, a write to this register deactivates the specified interrupt.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID of the interrupt to be deactivated. This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICC_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits and ICC_CTLR_EL3 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 .

name: ICC_DOMHPPIR_EL3
long_name: Interrupt Controller Domain Highest Priority Pending Interrupt Register
description: Reports whether there are HPPIs for non-EL3 Interrupt Domains.

fields.name: P_HPPI
fields.description: Preemptive HPPI for the Preemptive Interrupt Domain valid bit.

values.label: There is no Preemptive HPPI of Sufficient priority for the Preemptive Interrupt Domain.
There is a Preemptive HPPI of Sufficient priority for the Preemptive Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: RL_HPPI
fields.description: HPPI for Realm Interrupt Domain valid bit.

values.label: There is no HPPI of Sufficient priority for Realm Interrupt Domain.
There is an HPPI of Sufficient priority for Realm Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: S_HPPI
fields.description: HPPI for Secure Interrupt Domain valid bit.

values.label: There is no HPPI of Sufficient priority for Secure Interrupt Domain.
There is an HPPI of Sufficient priority for Secure Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: NS_HPPI
fields.description: HPPI for Non-secure Interrupt Domain valid bit.

values.label: There is no HPPI of Sufficient priority for Non-secure Interrupt Domain.
There is an HPPI of Sufficient priority for Non-secure Interrupt Domain.

name: ICC_EOIR0_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller End Of Interrupt Register 0
description: A PE writes to this register to inform the CPU interface that it has completed the processing of the specified Group 0 interrupt.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID from the corresponding ICC_IAR0_EL1 access. This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICC_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits and ICC_CTLR_EL3 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 . If the EOImode bit for the current Exception level and Security state is 0, a write to this register drops the priority for the interrupt, and also deactivates the interrupt. If the EOImode bit for the current Exception level and Security state is 1, a write to this register only drops the priority for the interrupt. Software must write to ICC_DIR_EL1 to deactivate the interrupt. The EOImode bit for the current Exception level and Security state is determined as follows: If EL3 is not implemented, the appropriate bit is ICC_CTLR_EL1 .EOIMode. If EL3 is implemented and the software is executing at EL3, the appropriate bit is ICC_CTLR_EL3 .EOImode_EL3. If EL3 is implemented and the software is not executing at EL3, the bit depends on the current Security state: If the software is executing in Secure state, the bit is ICC_CTLR_EL3 .EOImode_EL1S. If the software is executing in Non-secure state, the bit is ICC_CTLR_EL3 .EOImode_EL1NS.

name: ICC_EOIR1_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller End Of Interrupt Register 1
description: A PE writes to this register to inform the CPU interface that it has completed the processing of the specified Group 1 interrupt.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID from the corresponding ICC_IAR1_EL1 access. This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICC_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits and ICC_CTLR_EL3 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 . If the EOImode bit for the current Exception level and Security state is 0, a write to this register drops the priority for the interrupt, and also deactivates the interrupt. If the EOImode bit for the current Exception level and Security state is 1, a write to this register only drops the priority for the interrupt. Software must write to ICC_DIR_EL1 to deactivate the interrupt. The EOImode bit for the current Exception level and Security state is determined as follows: If EL3 is not implemented, the appropriate bit is ICC_CTLR_EL1 .EOIMode. If EL3 is implemented and the software is executing at EL3, the appropriate bit is ICC_CTLR_EL3 .EOImode_EL3. If EL3 is implemented and the software is not executing at EL3, the bit depends on the current Security state: If the software is executing in Secure state, the bit is ICC_CTLR_EL3 .EOImode_EL1S. If the software is executing in Non-secure state, the bit is ICC_CTLR_EL3 .EOImode_EL1NS.

name: ICC_HAPR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Physical Highest Active Priority Register
description: Reports the running priority of the Current Physical Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: PRIORITY
fields.description: The running priority for the Current Physical Interrupt Domain. If there are no active priorities on the CPU interface in the applicable Interrupt Domain, or all active priorities have undergone a priority drop, the value returned is the Idle priority.

name: ICC_HPPIR0_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Highest Priority Pending Interrupt Register 0
description: Indicates the highest priority pending Group 0 interrupt on the CPU interface.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID of the highest priority pending interrupt, if that interrupt is observable at the current Security state and Exception level. If the highest priority pending interrupt is not observable, this field contains a special INTID to indicate the reason. These special INTIDs can be one of: 1020, 1021, or 1023. For more information, see 'Special INTIDs' in ARM® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 3.0 and version 4.0 (ARM IHI 0069) . This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICC_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits and ICC_CTLR_EL3 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 .

name: ICC_HPPIR1_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Highest Priority Pending Interrupt Register 1
description: Indicates the highest priority pending Group 1 interrupt on the CPU interface.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID of the highest priority pending interrupt, if that interrupt is observable at the current Security state and Exception level. If the highest priority pending interrupt is not observable, this field contains a special INTID to indicate the reason. These special INTIDs can be one of: 1020, 1021, or 1023. For more information, see 'Special INTIDs' in ARM® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 3.0 and version 4.0 (ARM IHI 0069) . This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICC_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits and ICC_CTLR_EL3 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 .

name: ICC_HPPIR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Physical Highest Priority Pending Interrupt Register (EL1)
description: Reports the HPPI for the Physical Interrupt Domain associated with the Security state selected by SCR_EL3.{NS, NSE}.

fields.name: HPPIV
fields.description: HPPI valid. There is an HPPI with Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.

values.label: Invalid: There is no HPPI with Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.
VALID: There is an HPPI with Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: TYPE
fields.description: The encoding of this field depends on the value of HPPIV as described below: If ICC_HPPIR_EL1.HPPIV is 0, TYPE is RES0 . If ICC_HPPIR_EL1.HPPIV is 1, TYPE specifies the Type of the interrupt.

values.label: PPI
LPI
SPI

fields.name: ID
fields.description: The encoding of this field depends on the value of HPPIV as described below: If ICC_HPPIR_EL1.HPPIV is 0, ID is RES0 . If ICC_HPPIR_EL1.HPPIV is 1, ID specifies the interrupt ID.

name: ICC_HPPIR_EL3
long_name: Interrupt Controller Physical Highest Priority Pending Interrupt Register (EL3)
description: Reports the HPPI for the EL3 Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: HPPIV
fields.description: HPPI valid. There is an HPPI with Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.

values.label: Invalid: There is no HPPI with Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.
VALID: There is an HPPI with Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: TYPE
fields.description: The encoding of this field depends on the value of HPPIV as described below: If ICC_HPPIR_EL3.HPPIV is 0, TYPE is RES0 . If ICC_HPPIR_EL3.HPPIV is 1, TYPE specifies the Type of the interrupt.

values.label: PPI
LPI
SPI

fields.name: ID
fields.description: The encoding of this field depends on the value of HPPIV as described below: If ICC_HPPIR_EL3.HPPIV is 0, ID is RES0 . If ICC_HPPIR_EL3.HPPIV is 1, ID specifies the interrupt ID.

name: ICC_IAFFIDR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller PE Interrupt Affinity ID Register
description: Reports the PE interrupt Affinity ID for the PE.

name: ICC_IAR0_EL1
description: The PE reads this register to obtain the INTID of the signaled Group 0 interrupt. This read acts as an acknowledge for the interrupt.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID of the signaled interrupt. This is the INTID of the highest priority pending interrupt, if that interrupt is of sufficient priority for it to be signaled to the PE, and if it can be acknowledged at the current Security state and Exception level. If the highest priority pending interrupt is not observable, this field contains a special INTID to indicate the reason. For more information, see 'Special INTIDs' in ARM® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 3.0 and version 4.0 (ARM IHI 0069) . This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICC_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits and ICC_CTLR_EL3 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 .

name: ICC_IAR1_EL1
description: The PE reads this register to obtain the INTID of the signaled Group 1 interrupt. This read acts as an acknowledge for the interrupt.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID of the signaled interrupt. This is the INTID of the highest priority pending interrupt, if that interrupt is of sufficient priority for it to be signaled to the PE, and if it can be acknowledged at the current Security state and Exception level. If the highest priority pending interrupt is not observable, this field contains a special INTID to indicate the reason. For more information, see 'Special INTIDs' in ARM® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 3.0 and version 4.0 (ARM IHI 0069) . This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICC_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits and ICC_CTLR_EL3 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 .

name: ICC_ICSR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Interrupt Configuration and State Register
description: Reports the configuration and state of the INTID specified to a previous GIC request interrupt configuration instruction.

fields.name: IAFFID
fields.description:
The interrupt Affinity value. The IRS may support fewer than 16 bits of IAFFID.
Upper bits not implemented by the IRS are returned as zero. When IRM is 1, this field is IMPSPEC.

fields.name: HM
fields.description: The Handling mode of the interrupt.

values.label: Edge-triggered
Level-sensitive
Inactive
Active
The interrupt Routing mode is Targeted.
The interrupt Routing mode is 1ofN.
Not pending
Pending
Disabled
Enabled

fields.name: F
fields.description: Indicates whether the IRS returned valid data.

values.label: Request completed successfully.
Request did not complete successfully.

name: ICC_IDR0_EL1
description: Contains information about features implemented by the GICv5 CPU interface.

fields.name: GCIE_LEGACY
fields.description: Indicates support for legacy GICv3.3 virtual CPU interface.

values.label: Legacy GICv3.3 virtual CPU interface is not implemented
Legacy GICv3.3 virtual CPU interface is implemented

fields.name: PRI_BITS
fields.description: The number of priority bits implemented, minus one. The number of supported priority bits is 5.

fields.name: ID_BITS
fields.description: Identifier bits. The number of interrupt identifier bits supported.

values.label: 16 bits
24 bits

name: ICC_IGRPEN0_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Interrupt Group 0 Enable Register
description: Controls whether Group 0 interrupts are enabled or not.

values.label: Group 0 interrupts are disabled.
Group 0 interrupts are enabled.

name: ICC_IGRPEN1_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Interrupt Group 1 Enable Register
description: Controls whether Group 1 interrupts are enabled for the current Security state.

fields.name: Enable
fields.description: Enables Group 1 interrupts for the current Security state.

values.label: Group 1 interrupts are disabled for the current Security state.
Group 1 interrupts are enabled for the current Security state.

name: ICC_IGRPEN1_EL3
long_name: Interrupt Controller Interrupt Group 1 Enable Register (EL3)
description: Controls whether Group 1 interrupts are enabled or not.

fields.name: EnableGrp1S
fields.description: Enables Group 1 interrupts for the Secure state.

values.label: Secure Group 1 interrupts are disabled.
Secure Group 1 interrupts are enabled.

fields.name: EnableGrp1NS
fields.description: Enables Group 1 interrupts for the Non-secure state.

values.label: Non-secure Group 1 interrupts are disabled.
Non-secure Group 1 interrupts are enabled.

name: ICC_NMIAR1_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Non-maskable Interrupt Acknowledge Register 1
description: The PE reads this register to obtain the INTID of the signaled Group 1 non-maskable interrupt. This read acts as an acknowledge for the interrupt.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID of the signaled interrupt. This is the INTID of the highest priority pending interrupt, if that interrupt has the Non-maskable property and is of sufficient priority for it to be signaled to the PE, and if it can be acknowledged at the current Security state and Exception level. If the highest priority pending interrupt is not observable, this field contains a special INTID to indicate the reason. For more information, see 'Special INTIDs' in ARM® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 3.0 and version 4.0 (ARM IHI 0069) . This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICC_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits and ICC_CTLR_EL3 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 .

name: ICC_PCR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Physical Interrupt Priority Control Register (EL1)
description: Controls the Physical priority mask for the Non-secure, Realm, and Secure Interrupt Domains.

fields.name: PRIORITY
fields.description: The priority mask for the Interrupt Domain.

name: ICC_PCR_EL3
long_name: Interrupt Controller Interrupt Priority Control Register (EL3)
description: Controls the Physical priority mask for the physical CPU interface for the EL3 Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: PRIORITY
fields.description: The priority mask for the EL3 Interrupt Domain.

name: ICC_PMR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Interrupt Priority Mask Register
description: Provides an interrupt priority filter. Only interrupts with a higher priority than the value in this register are signaled to the PE. Writes to this register must be high performance and must ensure that no interrupt of lower priority than the written value occurs after the write, without requiring an ISB or an exception boundary.

fields.name: Priority
fields.description: The priority mask level for the CPU interface. If the priority of an interrupt is higher than the value indicated by this field, the interface signals the interrupt to the PE. The possible priority field values are as follows: Implemented priority bits Possible priority field values Number of priority levels [7:0] 0x00 - 0xFF (0-255), all values 256 [7:1] 0x00 - 0xFE (0-254), even values only 128 [7:2] 0x00 - 0xFC (0-252), in steps of 4 64 [7:3] 0x00 - 0xF8 (0-248), in steps of 8 32 [7:4] 0x00 - 0xF0 (0-240), in steps of 16 16 Unimplemented priority bits are RAZ/WI.

Attempts to deactivate the PPI by executing a GIC xDDI System instruction are treated as NOPs.

description: Set Active state for physical PPIs.

fields.name: ACTIVE<x>
fields.description:
Configures whether PPIs are Active. Reads return the Active state of the INTID. Writing 1 sets the Active state of the INTID.
Writing 0 has no effect.

values.label: Inactive
Active

name: ICC_PPI_SPENDR<n>_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Physical PPI Set Pending State Registers
description: Set pending state for Physical PPIs.

fields.name: PEND<x>
fields.description:
Controls the Pending state of PPIs. Reads return the current state of the INTIDs. Writing 1 to a field sets the Pending state of the corresponding INTID.
Writing 0 has no effect.

values.label: Not pending
Pending

name: ICC_RPR_EL1
description: Indicates the Running priority of the CPU interface.

fields.name: NMI
fields.description: Indicates whether the running priority is from a NMI.

values.label: When GICD_CTLR .DS==1, there are no Active NMIs, or all Active NMIs have undergone priority drop. When GICD_CTLR .DS==0: For Non-secure and Realm reads, there are no Active Non-secure Group 1 NMIs, or all Active Non-secure Group 1 NMIs have undergone priority drop. For Secure and Root reads, there are no Active Secure Group 1 NMIs, or all Active Secure Group 1 NMIs have undergone priority drop.

When GICD_CTLR .DS==1, there is an Active NMI. When GICD_CTLR .DS==0: For Non-secure and Realm reads, there is an Active Non-secure Group 1 NMI. For Secure and Root reads, there is an Active Secure Group 1 NMI.

fields.name: NMI_NS
fields.description: Indicates whether the running priority is from a Non-secure Group 1 NMI.

values.label: There are no Active Non-secure Group 1 NMIs, or all Active Non-secure Group 1 NMIs have undergone priority drop.
There is an Active Non-secure Group 1 NMI which has not undergone priority drop.

fields.name: Priority
fields.description: The current running priority on the CPU interface. This is the group priority of the current active interrupt. The group priority of a Secure NMI, or NMI when GICD_CTLR.DS is 1, is 0x00 . The group priority of a Non-secure NMI is 0x80 , saturated to 0x00 for Non-secure reads. If there are no active interrupts on the CPU interface, or all active interrupts have undergone a priority drop, the value returned is the Idle priority. The priority returned is the group priority as if the BPR for the current Exception level and Security state was set to the minimum value of BPR for the number of implemented priority bits. If 8 bits of priority are implemented the group priority is bits[7:1] of the priority.

name: ICC_SGI0R_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Software Generated Interrupt Group 0 Register
description: Generates Secure Group 0 SGIs.

fields.name: Aff3
fields.description: The affinity 3 value of the affinity path of the cluster for which SGI interrupts will be generated. If the IRM bit is 1, this field is RES0 .

fields.name: RS
fields.description: RangeSelector Controls which group of 16 values is represented by the TargetList field. TargetList[n] represents aff0 value ((RS * 16) + n). When ICC_CTLR_EL1 .RSS==0, RS is RES0 . When ICC_CTLR_EL1 .RSS==1 and GICD_TYPER .RSS==0, writing this register with RS != 0 is a CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE choice of: The write is ignored. The RS field is treated as 0.

fields.name: IRM
fields.description: Interrupt Routing Mode. Determines how the generated interrupts are distributed to PEs. Possible values are:

values.label: Interrupts routed to the PEs specified by Aff3.Aff2.Aff1.<target list>.
Interrupts routed to all PEs in the system, excluding "self".

fields.name: Aff2
fields.description: The affinity 2 value of the affinity path of the cluster for which SGI interrupts will be generated. If the IRM bit is 1, this field is RES0 .

fields.name: Aff1
fields.description: The affinity 1 value of the affinity path of the cluster for which SGI interrupts will be generated. If the IRM bit is 1, this field is RES0 .

fields.name: TargetList
fields.description: Target List. The set of PEs for which SGI interrupts will be generated. Each bit corresponds to the PE within a cluster with an Affinity 0 value equal to the bit number. If SRE is set only for EL3, software executing at EL3 might use the System register interface to generate SGIs. Therefore, the Distributor must always be able to receive and acknowledge Generate SGI packets received from CPU interface regardless of the ARE settings for a Security state. However, the Distributor might discard such packets. If the IRM bit is 1, this field is RES0 .

name: ICC_SGI1R_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Software Generated Interrupt Group 1 Register
description: Generates Group 1 SGIs for the current Security state.

fields.name: Aff3
fields.description: The affinity 3 value of the affinity path of the cluster for which SGI interrupts will be generated. If the IRM bit is 1, this field is RES0 .

fields.name: RS
fields.description: RangeSelector Controls which group of 16 values is represented by the TargetList field. TargetList[n] represents aff0 value ((RS * 16) + n). When ICC_CTLR_EL1 .RSS==0, RS is RES0 . When ICC_CTLR_EL1 .RSS==1 and GICD_TYPER .RSS==0, writing this register with RS != 0 is a CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE choice of: The write is ignored. The RS field is treated as 0.

fields.name: IRM
fields.description: Interrupt Routing Mode. Determines how the generated interrupts are distributed to PEs. Possible values are:

values.label: Interrupts routed to the PEs specified by Aff3.Aff2.Aff1.<target list>.
Interrupts routed to all PEs in the system, excluding "self".

fields.name: Aff2
fields.description: The affinity 2 value of the affinity path of the cluster for which SGI interrupts will be generated. If the IRM bit is 1, this field is RES0 .

fields.name: Aff1
fields.description: The affinity 1 value of the affinity path of the cluster for which SGI interrupts will be generated. If the IRM bit is 1, this field is RES0 .

fields.name: TargetList
fields.description: Target List. The set of PEs for which SGI interrupts will be generated. Each bit corresponds to the PE within a cluster with an Affinity 0 value equal to the bit number. If SRE is set only for EL3, software executing at EL3 might use the System register interface to generate SGIs. Therefore, the Distributor must always be able to receive and acknowledge Generate SGI packets received from CPU interface regardless of the ARE settings for a Security state. However, the Distributor might discard such packets. If the IRM bit is 1, this field is RES0 .

name: ICC_SRE_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller System Register Enable Register (EL1)
description: Controls whether the System register interface or the memory-mapped interface to the GIC CPU interface is used for EL1.

values.label: IRQ bypass enabled.
IRQ bypass disabled.
FIQ bypass enabled.
FIQ bypass disabled.
The memory-mapped interface must be used. Access at EL1 to any ICC_* System register other than ICC_SRE_EL1 is trapped to EL1.
The System register interface for the current Security state is enabled.

name: ICC_SRE_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller System Register Enable Register (EL2)
description: Controls whether the System register interface or the memory-mapped interface to the GIC CPU interface is used for EL2.

fields.name: Enable
fields.description: Enable. Enables lower Exception level access to ICC_SRE_EL1 .

values.label: When EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, EL1 accesses to ICC_SRE_EL1 trap to EL2.
EL1 accesses to ICC_SRE_EL1 do not trap to EL2.
IRQ bypass enabled.
IRQ bypass disabled.
FIQ bypass enabled.
FIQ bypass disabled.
The memory-mapped interface must be used. Access at EL2 to any ICH_* or ICC_* register other than ICC_SRE_EL1 or ICC_SRE_EL2, is trapped to EL2.
The System register interface to the ICH_* registers and the EL1 and EL2 ICC_* registers is enabled for EL2.

name: ICC_SRE_EL3
long_name: Interrupt Controller System Register Enable Register (EL3)
description: Controls whether the System register interface or the memory-mapped interface to the GIC CPU interface is used for EL3.

fields.name: Enable
fields.description: Enable. Enables lower Exception level access to ICC_SRE_EL1 and ICC_SRE_EL2 .

values.label: EL1 accesses to ICC_SRE_EL1 trap to EL3, unless these accesses are trapped to EL2 as a result of ICC_SRE_EL2 .Enable == 0. EL2 accesses to ICC_SRE_EL1 and ICC_SRE_EL2 trap to EL3.
EL1 accesses to ICC_SRE_EL1 do not trap to EL3. EL2 accesses to ICC_SRE_EL1 and ICC_SRE_EL2 do not trap to EL3.
IRQ bypass enabled.
IRQ bypass disabled.
FIQ bypass enabled.
FIQ bypass disabled.
The memory-mapped interface must be used. Access at EL3 to any ICH_* or ICC_* register other than ICC_SRE_EL1 , ICC_SRE_EL2 , or ICC_SRE_EL3 is trapped to EL3
The System register interface to the ICH_* registers and the EL1, EL2, and EL3 ICC_* registers is enabled for EL3.

name: ICH_AP0R<n>_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller Hyp Active Priorities Group 0 Registers
description: Provides information about Group 0 virtual active priorities for EL2.

fields.name: P<x>
fields.description: Provides the access to the virtual active priorities for Group 0 interrupts. Possible values of each bit are:

values.label: There is no Group 0 interrupt active with this priority level, or all active Group 0 interrupts with this priority level have undergone priority-drop.
There is a Group 0 interrupt active with this priority level which has not undergone priority drop.

name: ICH_AP1R<n>_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller Hyp Active Priorities Group 1 Registers
description: Provides information about Group 1 virtual active priorities for EL2.

fields.name: NMI
fields.description: Indicates whether the running virtual priority is from a NMI.

values.label: There is no active Group 1 NMI, or all active Group 1 NMIs have undergone priority drop.
There is an active Group 1 NMI.

fields.name: P<x>
fields.description: Group 1 interrupt active priorities. Possible values of each bit are:

values.label: There is no Group 1 interrupt active with this priority level, or all active Group 1 interrupts with this priority level have undergone priority-drop.
There is a Group 1 interrupt active with this priority level which has not undergone priority drop.

name: ICH_APR_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller Active Virtual Priorities Register
description: Records active priorities for the Virtual Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: P<x>
fields.description: Provides access to the active virtual priorities.

values.label: Priority not active
Priority active

name: ICH_CONTEXTR_EL2
description: Selects the current resident VPE.

fields.name: V
fields.description: Indicates whether a VPE is currently resident. If EL2 is not enabled in the Security state identified by SCR_EL3.{NSE,NS}, the Effective value of this field is 0.

values.label: No VPE is resident.
A VPE is resident.

fields.name: F
fields.description: Indicates whether the last write that set V to 1 succeeded in making a VPE resident.

values.label: Success
Fault

fields.name: IRICHPPIDIS
fields.description: Specifies whether the candidate HPPI presented by the IRI for the resident VPE is considered when selecting the HPPI for the Virtual Interrupt Domain. When V is 0, this field is IGNORED.

values.label: The CPU interface considers both the virtual PPIs and the candidate HPPI presented by the IRI when determining the HPPI for the Virtual Interrupt Domain.
The CPU interface only considers the virtual PPIs when determining the HPPI for the Virtual Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: DB
fields.description: Specifies whether a doorbell interrupt is requested when making a VPE non-resident.

values.label: No doorbell requested.
Doorbell requested.

fields.name: VPE
fields.description: When V is 1, identifies the resident VPE. When V is 0, this field is IGNORED. This field has no effect if EL2 is not enabled in the Security state identified by SCR_EL3.{NSE,NS}.

fields.name: VM
fields.description: When V is 1, identifies the resident VM. When V is 0, this field is IGNORED. This field has no effect if EL2 is not enabled in the Security state identified by SCR_EL3.{NSE,NS}.

name: ICH_EISR_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller End of Interrupt Status Register
description: Indicates which List registers have outstanding EOI maintenance interrupts.

fields.name: Status<n>
fields.description: EOI maintenance interrupt status bit for List register <n>:

values.label: List register <n>, ICH_LR<n>_EL2 , does not have an EOI maintenance interrupt.
List register <n>, ICH_LR<n>_EL2 , has an EOI maintenance interrupt that has not been handled.

name: ICH_ELRSR_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller Empty List Register Status Register
description: These registers can be used to locate a usable List register when the hypervisor is delivering an interrupt to a VM.

fields.name: Status<n>
fields.description: Status bit for List register <n>, ICH_LR<n>_EL2 :

values.label: List register ICH_LR<n>_EL2 , if implemented, contains a valid interrupt. Using this List register can result in overwriting a valid interrupt.
List register ICH_LR<n>_EL2 does not contain a valid interrupt. The List register is empty and can be used without overwriting a valid interrupt or losing an EOI maintenance interrupt.

name: ICH_HCR_EL2
description: Controls the environment for the virtual CPU interface.

fields.name: EOIcount
fields.description: This field is incremented whenever a successful write to a virtual EOIR or DIR register would have resulted in a virtual interrupt deactivation. That is either: A virtual write to EOIR with a valid interrupt identifier that is not in the LPI range (that is < 8192) when EOI mode is zero and no List Register was found. A virtual write to DIR with a valid interrupt identifier that is not in the LPI range (that is < 8192) when EOI mode is one and no List Register was found. This allows software to manage more active interrupts than there are implemented List Registers. It is CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE whether a virtual write to EOIR that does not clear a bit in the Active Priorities registers ( ICH_AP0R<n>_EL2 / ICH_AP1R<n>_EL2 ) increments EOIcount. Permitted behaviors are: Increment EOIcount. Leave EOIcount unchanged.

values.label: This control has no effect on the signaling of virtual interrupts.
Virtual interrupts received via direct-injection are not presented to the virtual CPU interface and not considered when determining the highest priority pending virtual interrupt.

fields.name: TDIR
fields.description: Trap EL1 writes to ICC_DIR_EL1 and ICV_DIR_EL1 .

values.label: EL1 writes of ICC_DIR_EL1 and ICV_DIR_EL1 are not trapped to EL2, unless trapped by other mechanisms.
EL1 writes of ICV_DIR_EL1 are trapped to EL2. It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether writes of ICC_DIR_EL1 are trapped. Not trapping ICC_DIR_EL1 writes is DEPRECATED.

fields.name: TSEI
fields.description: Trap all locally generated SEIs. This bit allows the hypervisor to intercept locally generated SEIs that would otherwise be taken at EL1.

values.label: Locally generated SEIs do not cause a trap to EL2.
Locally generated SEIs trap to EL2.

fields.name: TALL1
fields.description: Trap all EL1 accesses to ICC_* and ICV_* System registers for Group 1 interrupts to EL2.

values.label: EL1 accesses to ICC_* and ICV_* registers for Group 1 interrupts proceed as normal.
EL1 accesses to ICC_* and ICV_* registers for Group 1 interrupts trap to EL2.

fields.name: TALL0
fields.description: Trap all EL1 accesses to ICC_* and ICV_* System registers for Group 0 interrupts to EL2.

values.label: EL1 accesses to ICC_* and ICV_* registers for Group 0 interrupts proceed as normal.
EL1 accesses to ICC_* and ICV_* registers for Group 0 interrupts trap to EL2.

fields.name: TC
fields.description: Trap all EL1 accesses to System registers that are common to Group 0 and Group 1 to EL2.

values.label: EL1 accesses to common registers proceed as normal.
EL1 accesses to common registers trap to EL2.

fields.name: vSGIEOICount
fields.description: Controls whether deactivation of virtual SGIs can increment ICH_HCR_EL2.EOIcount

values.label: Deactivation of virtual SGIs can increment ICH_HCR_EL2.EOIcount.
Deactivation of virtual SGIs does not increment ICH_HCR_EL2.EOIcount.

fields.name: VGrp1DIE
fields.description: VM Group 1 Disabled Interrupt Enable. Enables the signaling of a maintenance interrupt while signaling of Group 1 interrupts from the virtual CPU interface to the connected vPE is disabled:

values.label: Maintenance interrupt disabled.
Maintenance interrupt signaled when ICH_VMCR_EL2 .VENG1 is 0.

fields.name: VGrp1EIE
fields.description: VM Group 1 Enabled Interrupt Enable. Enables the signaling of a maintenance interrupt while signaling of Group 1 interrupts from the virtual CPU interface to the connected vPE is enabled:

values.label: Maintenance interrupt disabled.
Maintenance interrupt signaled when ICH_VMCR_EL2 .VENG1 is 1.

fields.name: VGrp0DIE
fields.description: VM Group 0 Disabled Interrupt Enable. Enables the signaling of a maintenance interrupt while signaling of Group 0 interrupts from the virtual CPU interface to the connected vPE is disabled:

values.label: Maintenance interrupt disabled.
Maintenance interrupt signaled when ICH_VMCR_EL2 .VENG0 is 0.

fields.name: VGrp0EIE
fields.description: VM Group 0 Enabled Interrupt Enable. Enables the signaling of a maintenance interrupt while signaling of Group 0 interrupts from the virtual CPU interface to the connected vPE is enabled:

values.label: Maintenance interrupt disabled.
Maintenance interrupt signaled when ICH_VMCR_EL2 .VENG0 is 1.

fields.name: NPIE
fields.description: No Pending Interrupt Enable. Enables the signaling of a maintenance interrupt when there are no List registers with the State field set to 0b01 (pending):

values.label: Maintenance interrupt disabled.
Maintenance interrupt signaled while the List registers contain no interrupts in the pending state.

fields.name: LRENPIE
fields.description: List Register Entry Not Present Interrupt Enable. Enables the signaling of a maintenance interrupt while the virtual CPU interface does not have a corresponding valid List register entry for an EOI request:

values.label: Maintenance interrupt disabled.
Maintenance interrupt is asserted while the EOIcount field is not 0.

fields.name: UIE
fields.description: Underflow Interrupt Enable. Enables the signaling of a maintenance interrupt when the List registers are empty, or hold only one valid entry:

values.label: Maintenance interrupt disabled.
Maintenance interrupt is asserted if none, or only one, of the List register entries is marked as a valid interrupt.

fields.name: En
fields.description: Enable. Global enable bit for the virtual CPU interface:

values.label: Virtual CPU interface operation disabled.
Virtual CPU interface operation enabled.

fields.name: GICRCDNMIA
fields.description: Trap execution of GICR CDNMIA at EL1 to EL2.

values.label: If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, then execution of GICR CDNMIA at EL1 is trapped to EL2 and reported with EC syndrome value 0x18 , unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
Execution of GICR CDNMIA is not trapped by this mechanism.

description: Provides controls for traps of MSR writes of GIC System registers.

fields.name: ICC_PPI_ACTIVERn_EL1
fields.description: Trap MSR writes of ICC_PPI_CACTIVER<n>_EL1, ICC_PPI_SACTIVER<n>_EL1, ICV_PPI_CACTIVER<n>_EL1 and ICV_PPI_SACTIVER<n>_EL1 at EL1 using AArch64 to EL2.

values.label: If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, then MSR writes of the specified registers at EL1 using AArch64 are trapped to EL2 and reported with EC syndrome value 0x18 , unless the read generates a higher priority exception.
MSR writes of the specified registers are not trapped by this mechanism.
