name: ICH_HPPIR_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller Hypervisor Highest Priority Pending Interrupt Register
description: Reports the HPPI for the Virtual Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: HPPIV
fields.description: HPPI valid. There is an HPPI with Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.

values.label: Invalid: There is no HPPI with Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.
VALID: There is an HPPI with Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: TYPE
fields.description: The encoding of this field depends on the value of HPPIV as described below: If ICH_HPPIR_EL2.HPPIV is 0, TYPE is RES0 . If ICH_HPPIR_EL2.HPPIV is 1, TYPE specifies the Type of the interrupt.

values.label: PPI
LPI
SPI

fields.name: ID
fields.description: The encoding of this field depends on the value of HPPIV as described below: If ICH_HPPIR_EL2.HPPIV is 0, ID is RES0 . If ICH_HPPIR_EL2.HPPIV is 1, ID specifies the interrupt ID.

name: ICH_LR<n>_EL2
description: Provides interrupt context information for the virtual CPU interface.

values.label: Invalid (Inactive).
Pending.
Active.
Pending and active.

fields.name: HW
fields.description: Indicates whether this virtual interrupt maps directly to a hardware interrupt, meaning that it corresponds to a physical interrupt. Deactivation of the virtual interrupt also causes the deactivation of the physical interrupt with the ID that the pINTID field indicates.

values.label: The interrupt is triggered entirely by software. No notification is sent to the Distributor when the virtual interrupt is deactivated.

If FEAT_GICv3 is implemented, all of the following are true: The interrupt maps directly to a hardware interrupt. A deactivate interrupt request is sent to the Distributor when the virtual interrupt is deactivated, using the pINTID field from this register to indicate the physical interrupt ID. If ICH_VMCR_EL2 .VEOIM is 0, this request corresponds to a write to ICC_EOIR0_EL1 or ICC_EOIR1_EL1 . Otherwise, it corresponds to a write to ICC_DIR_EL1 . If FEAT_GCIE_LEGACY is implemented, the virtual interrupt also deactivates the physical interrupt specified in pINTID.

fields.name: Group
fields.description: Indicates the group for this virtual interrupt.

values.label: This is a Group 0 virtual interrupt. ICH_VMCR_EL2 .VFIQEn determines whether it is signaled as a virtual IRQ or as a virtual FIQ, and ICH_VMCR_EL2 .VENG0 enables signaling of this interrupt to the virtual machine.

This is a Group 1 virtual interrupt, signaled as a virtual IRQ. ICH_VMCR_EL2 .VENG1 enables the signaling of this interrupt to the virtual machine. If ICH_VMCR_EL2 .VCBPR is 0, then ICC_BPR1_EL1 determines if a pending Group 1 interrupt has sufficient priority to preempt current execution. Otherwise, ICH_LR<n>_EL2 determines preemption.

fields.name: NMI
fields.description: Indicates whether the virtual priority has the non-maskable property.

values.label: vINTID does not have the non-maskable interrupt property.
vINTID has the non-maskable interrupt property.

fields.name: Priority
fields.description: The priority of this interrupt. It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED how many bits of priority are implemented, though at least five bits must be implemented. Unimplemented bits are RES0 and start from bit[48] up to bit[50]. The number of implemented bits can be discovered from ICH_VTR_EL2 .PRIbits. When ICH_LR<n>_EL2.NMI is set to 1, this field is RES0 and the virtual interrupt's priority is treated as 0x00 . If FEAT_GCIE_LEGACY is implemented, 5 bits of priority are implemented meaning that bits[50:48] are RES0 .

fields.name: pINTID
fields.description: Physical PPI INTID, for hardware interrupts. When ICH_LR<n>_EL2.HW is 0 (there is no corresponding physical interrupt), this field has the following meaning: Bits[44:42]: RES0 . Bit[41]: EOI. If this bit is 1, then when the interrupt identified by vINTID is deactivated, a maintenance interrupt is asserted. Bits[40:32]: RES0 . When ICH_LR<n>_EL2.HW is 1 (there is a corresponding physical interrupt): This field specifies the ID of a physical PPI in the Current Physical Interrupt Domain. This field is only required to implement enough bits to hold a valid value for the implemented physical PPIs. Any unused higher order bits are RES0 . If pINTID specifies an unreachable PPI, no physical interrupt is deactivated.

fields.name: pINTID
fields.description: Physical INTID, for hardware interrupts. When ICH_LR<n>_EL2.HW is 0 (there is no corresponding physical interrupt), this field has the following meaning: Bits[44:42]: RES0 . Bit[41]: EOI. If this bit is 1, then when the interrupt identified by vINTID is deactivated, a maintenance interrupt is asserted. Bits[40:32]: RES0 . When ICH_LR<n>_EL2.HW is 1 (there is a corresponding physical interrupt): This field indicates the physical INTID. This field is only required to implement enough bits to hold a valid value for the implemented INTID size. Any unused higher order bits are RES0 . When ICC_CTLR_EL1 .ExtRange is 0, then bits[44:42] of this field are RES0 . If the value of pINTID is not a valid INTID, behavior is UNPREDICTABLE . If the value of pINTID indicates a PPI, this field applies to the PPI associated with this same physical PE ID as the virtual CPU interface requesting the deactivation. A hardware physical identifier is only required in List Registers for interrupts that require deactivation. This means only 13 bits of Physical INTID are required, regardless of the number specified by ICC_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits.

fields.name: vINTID
fields.description: Virtual INTID of the interrupt. If the value of vINTID is 1020-1023 and ICH_LR<n>_EL2.State!= 0b00 (Inactive), behavior is UNPREDICTABLE . Behavior is UNPREDICTABLE if two or more List Registers specify the same vINTID when: ICH_LR<n>_EL2.State == 0b01 . ICH_LR<n>_EL2.State == 0b10 . ICH_LR<n>_EL2.State == 0b11 . It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED how many bits are implemented, though at least 16 bits must be implemented. Unimplemented bits are RES0 . The number of implemented bits can be discovered from ICH_VTR_EL2 .IDbits. When ICC_SRE_EL1 .SRE == 0, specifying a vINTID in the LPI range is UNPREDICTABLE When a VM is using memory-mapped access to the GIC, software must ensure that the correct source PE ID is provided in bits[12:10].

name: ICH_MISR_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller Maintenance Interrupt State Register
description: Indicates which maintenance interrupts are asserted.

values.label: vPE Group 1 Disabled maintenance interrupt not asserted.
vPE Group 1 Disabled maintenance interrupt asserted.
vPE Group 1 Enabled maintenance interrupt not asserted.
vPE Group 1 Enabled maintenance interrupt asserted.
vPE Group 0 Disabled maintenance interrupt not asserted.
vPE Group 0 Disabled maintenance interrupt asserted.
vPE Group 0 Enabled maintenance interrupt not asserted.
vPE Group 0 Enabled maintenance interrupt asserted.
No Pending maintenance interrupt not asserted.
No Pending maintenance interrupt asserted.
List Register Entry Not Present maintenance interrupt not asserted.
List Register Entry Not Present maintenance interrupt asserted.
Underflow maintenance interrupt not asserted.
Underflow maintenance interrupt asserted.
End Of Interrupt maintenance interrupt not asserted.
End Of Interrupt maintenance interrupt asserted.

name: ICH_PPI_ACTIVER<n>_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Interrupt Active Registers
description: Access to Active state for virtual PPIs.

fields.name: ACTIVE<x>
fields.description: Accesses the Active state of the virtual PPI.

values.label: Inactive
Active

name: ICH_PPI_DVIR<n>_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller PPI Direct-inject Virtual Interrupt Registers
description: Controls whether Pending physical PPIs are directly injected as virtual PPIs to the Virtual Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: DVI<x>
fields.description: Controls whether the physical PPI Pending state is directly injected to a virtual PPI in the Virtual Interrupt Domain.

values.label: Physical PPI <x> is not directly injected as a virtual PPI.
Physical PPI <x> is directly injected as a virtual PPI.

name: ICH_PPI_ENABLER<n>_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Interrupt Enable Registers
description: Access to Enable state for virtual PPIs.

name: ICH_PPI_PENDR<n>_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Interrupt Pending State Registers
description: Pending state for virtual PPIs.

fields.name: PEND<x>
fields.description: Pend<x> accesses the Pending state of virtual PPI <x>.

values.label: Not pending
Pending

name: ICH_PPI_PRIORITYR<n>_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Interrupt Priority Registers
description: Priority of virtual PPIs.

fields.name: PRIORITY<x>
fields.description: Alias of equivalent ICV_PPI_PRIORITY<n>_EL1 field. Fields corresponding to unimplemented INTIDs are RES0 .

fields.name: PRIORITY7
fields.description: Alias of equivalent ICV_PPI_PRIORITY<n>_EL1 field. Fields corresponding to unimplemented INTIDs are RES0 .

fields.name: PRIORITY6
fields.description: Alias of equivalent ICV_PPI_PRIORITY<n>_EL1 field. Fields corresponding to unimplemented INTIDs are RES0 .

fields.name: PRIORITY5
fields.description: Alias of equivalent ICV_PPI_PRIORITY<n>_EL1 field. Fields corresponding to unimplemented INTIDs are RES0 .

fields.name: PRIORITY4
fields.description: Alias of equivalent ICV_PPI_PRIORITY<n>_EL1 field. Fields corresponding to unimplemented INTIDs are RES0 .

fields.name: PRIORITY3
fields.description: Alias of equivalent ICV_PPI_PRIORITY<n>_EL1 field. Fields corresponding to unimplemented INTIDs are RES0 .

fields.name: PRIORITY2
fields.description: Alias of equivalent ICV_PPI_PRIORITY<n>_EL1 field. Fields corresponding to unimplemented INTIDs are RES0 .

fields.name: PRIORITY1
fields.description: Alias of equivalent ICV_PPI_PRIORITY<n>_EL1 field. Fields corresponding to unimplemented INTIDs are RES0 .

fields.name: PRIORITY0
fields.description: Alias of equivalent ICV_PPI_PRIORITY<n>_EL1 field. Fields corresponding to unimplemented INTIDs are RES0 .

name: ICH_VCTLR_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual CPU interface Control Register
description: Controls behavior of the Virtual CPU interface.

fields.name: V3
fields.description: Enable bit for Legacy operation. If EL2 is not enabled in the Security state identified by SCR_EL3.{NSE,NS}, the Effective value of this field is 0. When the Effective value of this field is 0, the Effective value of ICH_HCR_EL2.En is 0. See 'Legacy virtual CPU interface' for more information.

values.label: Legacy operation disabled.
Legacy operation enabled.

fields.name: EN
fields.description: Enable interrupts for the Virtual Interrupt Domain. If EL2 is not enabled in the Security state identified by SCR_EL3.{NSE,NS}, the Effective value of this field is 0.

values.label: Disabled.
Enabled.

name: ICH_VMCR_EL2
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Machine Control Register
description: Enables the hypervisor to save and restore the virtual machine view of the GIC state.

fields.name: VPMR
fields.description: Virtual priority mask. This field is an alias of ICV_PCR_EL1.Priority.

fields.name: EN
fields.description: Enables interrupts for the Virtual Interrupt Domain. When this field is 0, there is no HPPI of Sufficient priority for the Virtual Interrupt Domain. This field is an alias of ICV_CR0_EL1.EN.

name: ICH_VTR_EL2
description: Reports supported GIC virtualization features.

fields.name: PRIbits
fields.description: Priority bits. Indicates the number of virtual priority bits implemented, minus one. An implementation must implement at least 32 levels of virtual priority (5 priority bits). This field is an alias of ICV_CTLR_EL1 .PRIbits. If FEAT_GCIE_LEGACY is implemented, this field returns 4 (5 bits of priority).

values.label: The number of virtual priority bits implemented, minus one.

fields.name: PREbits
fields.description: Preemption bits. Indicates the number of virtual preemption bits implemented, minus one. An implementation must implement at least 32 levels of virtual preemption priority (5 preemption bits). The value of this field must be less than or equal to the value of ICH_VTR_EL2.PRIbits. This field determines the minimum value of ICH_VMCR_EL2 .VBPR0. If FEAT_GCIE_LEGACY is implemented, this field returns 4 (5 bits of preemption).

values.label: The number of virtual preemption bits implemented, minus one.

fields.name: IDbits
fields.description: The number of virtual interrupt identifier bits supported:

values.label: 16 bits.
24 bits.

fields.name: SEIS
fields.description: SEI Support. The virtual CPU interface does not support generation of SEIs.

fields.name: SEIS
fields.description: SEI Support. Indicates whether the virtual CPU interface supports generation of SEIs:

values.label: The virtual CPU interface logic does not support generation of SEIs.
The virtual CPU interface logic supports generation of SEIs.
The virtual CPU interface logic only supports zero values of Affinity 3 in SGI generation System registers.
The virtual CPU interface logic supports nonzero values of Affinity 3 in SGI generation System registers.

fields.name: nV4
fields.description: Direct injection of virtual interrupts not supported.

fields.name: nV4
fields.description: Direct injection of virtual interrupts not supported.

values.label: The CPU interface logic supports direct injection of virtual interrupts.
The CPU interface logic does not support direct injection of virtual interrupts.

fields.name: TDS
fields.description: Separate trapping of EL1 writes to ICV_DIR_EL1 supported.

values.label: Implementation does not support ICH_HCR_EL2 .TDIR.
Implementation supports ICH_HCR_EL2 .TDIR.

fields.name: TDS
fields.description: Separate trapping of EL1 writes to ICV_DIR_EL1 supported.

values.label: Masking of Directly-injected Virtual Interrupts not supported.
Masking of Directly-injected Virtual Interrupts is supported.

fields.name: ListRegs
fields.description: List Registers. Indicates the number of List registers implemented, minus one.

values.label: The number of List registers implemented, minus one.

name: ICV_AP0R<n>_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Active Priorities Group 0 Registers
description: Provides information about virtual Group 0 active priorities.

name: ICV_AP1R<n>_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Active Priorities Group 1 Registers
description: Provides information about virtual Group 1 active priorities.

fields.name: NMI
fields.description: Indicates whether the running priority is from a NMI.

values.label: There is no active Group 1 NMI, or all active Group 1 NMIs have undergone priority-drop.
There is an active Group 1 NMI.

name: ICV_APR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Active Priorities Register
description: Records active priorities for the Virtual CPU interface.

fields.name: P<x>
fields.description: Provides access to the active priorities.

values.label: Priority not active
Priority active

name: ICV_BPR0_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Binary Point Register 0
description: Defines the point at which the priority value fields split into two parts, the group priority field and the subpriority field. The group priority field determines virtual Group 0 interrupt preemption.

fields.name: BinaryPoint
fields.description: The value of this field controls how the 8-bit interrupt priority field is split into a group priority field, that determines interrupt preemption, and a subpriority field. This is done as follows: Binary point value Group priority field Subpriority field Field with binary point 0 [7:1] [0] ggggggg.s 1 [7:2] [1:0] gggggg.ss 2 [7:3] [2:0] ggggg.sss 3 [7:4] [3:0] gggg.ssss 4 [7:5] [4:0] ggg.sssss 5 [7:6] [5:0] gg.ssssss 6 [7] [6:0] g.sssssss 7 No preemption [7:0] .ssssssss

name: ICV_BPR1_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Binary Point Register 1
description: Defines the point at which the priority value fields split into two parts, the group priority field and the subpriority field. The group priority field determines virtual Group 1 interrupt preemption.

fields.name: BinaryPoint
fields.description: If the GIC is configured to use separate binary point fields for Group 0 and Group 1 interrupts, the value of this field controls how the 8-bit interrupt priority field is split into a group priority field, that determines interrupt preemption, and a subpriority field. For more information about priorities, see 'Priority grouping' in ARM® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 3.0 and version 4.0 (ARM IHI 0069) . An attempt to program this field to a value less than the minimum value sets the field to the minimum value. If ICV_CTLR_EL1 .CBPR is set to 1, Non-secure EL1 reads return ICV_BPR0_EL1 + 1 saturated to 0b111 . Non-secure EL1 writes are ignored. If ICV_CTLR_EL1 .CBPR is set to 1, Secure EL1 reads return ICV_BPR0_EL1 . Secure EL1 writes modify ICV_BPR0_EL1 .

name: ICV_CR0_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller EL1 Virtual Control Register
description: Controls behavior of the CPU interface in the Virtual Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: LINK_IDLE
fields.description: Whether the link between the CPU interface and the IRI is in the process of connecting or disconnecting.

values.label: The link between the CPU interface and the IRI is in the process of connecting or disconnecting.
The link between the CPU interface and the IRI is not in the process of connecting or disconnecting.

fields.name: LINK
fields.description: Whether the link between the CPU interface and the IRI is connected.

values.label: The link between the CPU interface and the IRI is disconnected.
The link between the CPU interface and the IRI is connected.

fields.name: EN
fields.description: Enable interrupts for the Interrupt Domain. When this field is 0, there is no HPPI of Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.

values.label: Disabled.
Enabled.

name: ICV_CTLR_EL1
description: Controls aspects of the behavior of the GIC virtual CPU interface and provides information about the features implemented.

values.label: CPU interface does not support INTIDs in the range 1024..8191. Behavior is UNPREDICTABLE if the IRI delivers an interrupt in the range 1024 to 8191 to the CPU interface. Arm strongly recommends that the IRI is not configured to deliver interrupts in this range to a PE that does not support them.
CPU interface supports INTIDs in the range 1024..8191 All INTIDs in the range 1024..8191 are treated as requiring deactivation.
Targeted SGIs with affinity level 0 values of 0 - 15 are supported.
Targeted SGIs with affinity level 0 values of 0 - 255 are supported.

fields.name: A3V
fields.description: Affinity 3 Valid. Read-only and writes are ignored.

values.label: The virtual CPU interface logic only supports zero values of Affinity 3 in SGI generation System registers.
The virtual CPU interface logic supports nonzero values of Affinity 3 in SGI generation System registers.

fields.name: SEIS
fields.description: SEI Support. Read-only and writes are ignored. Indicates whether the virtual CPU interface supports local generation of SEIs.

values.label: The virtual CPU interface logic does not support local generation of SEIs.
The virtual CPU interface logic supports local generation of SEIs.

fields.name: IDbits
fields.description: Identifier bits. Read-only and writes are ignored. Indicates the number of virtual interrupt identifier bits supported.

values.label: 16 bits.
24 bits.

fields.name: PRIbits
fields.description: Indicates the number of virtual priority bits implemented. An implementation must implement at least 32 levels of virtual priority (5 priority bits). The division between group priority and subpriority is defined in the binary point registers ICV_BPR0_EL1 and ICV_BPR1_EL1 .

values.label: The number of virtual priority bits implemented, minus one.

fields.name: EOImode
fields.description: Virtual EOI mode. Controls whether a write to an End of Interrupt register also deactivates the virtual interrupt:

values.label: ICV_EOIR0_EL1 and ICV_EOIR1_EL1 provide both priority drop and interrupt deactivation functionality. Accesses to ICV_DIR_EL1 are UNPREDICTABLE .
ICV_EOIR0_EL1 and ICV_EOIR1_EL1 provide priority drop functionality only. ICV_DIR_EL1 provides interrupt deactivation functionality.

fields.name: CBPR
fields.description: Common Binary Point Register. Controls whether the same register is used for interrupt preemption of both virtual Group 0 and virtual Group 1 interrupts:

values.label: ICV_BPR1_EL1 determines the preemption group for virtual Group 1 interrupts.

Non-secure reads of ICV_BPR1_EL1 return ICV_BPR0_EL1 plus one, saturated to 0b111 . Non-secure writes to ICV_BPR1_EL1 are ignored. Secure reads of ICV_BPR1_EL1 return ICV_BPR0_EL1 . Secure writes of ICV_BPR1_EL1 modify ICV_BPR0_EL1 .

name: ICV_DIR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Deactivate Virtual Interrupt Register
description: When interrupt priority drop is separated from interrupt deactivation, a write to this register deactivates the specified virtual interrupt.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID of the virtual interrupt to be deactivated. This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICV_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 .

name: ICV_EOIR0_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual End Of Interrupt Register 0
description: A PE writes to this register to inform the CPU interface that it has completed the processing of the specified virtual Group 0 interrupt.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID from the corresponding ICV_IAR0_EL1 access. This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICV_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 . If the ICV_CTLR_EL1 .EOImode bit is 0, a write to this register drops the priority for the virtual interrupt, and also deactivates the virtual interrupt. If the ICV_CTLR_EL1 .EOImode bit is 1, a write to this register only drops the priority for the virtual interrupt. Software must write to ICV_DIR_EL1 to deactivate the virtual interrupt.

name: ICV_EOIR1_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual End Of Interrupt Register 1
description: A PE writes to this register to inform the CPU interface that it has completed the processing of the specified virtual Group 1 interrupt.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID from the corresponding ICV_IAR1_EL1 access. This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICV_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 . If the ICV_CTLR_EL1 .EOImode bit is 0, a write to this register drops the priority for the virtual interrupt, and also deactivates the virtual interrupt. If the ICV_CTLR_EL1 .EOImode bit is 1, a write to this register only drops the priority for the virtual interrupt. Software must write to ICV_DIR_EL1 to deactivate the virtual interrupt.

name: ICV_HAPR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Highest Active Priority Register
description: Reports the running priority of the Virtual Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: PRIORITY
fields.description: The running priority for the Virtual Interrupt Domain. If there are no active priorities on the CPU interface in the applicable Interrupt Domain, or all active priorities have undergone a priority drop, the value returned is the Idle priority.

name: ICV_HPPIR0_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Highest Priority Pending Interrupt Register 0
description: Indicates the highest priority pending virtual Group 0 interrupt on the virtual CPU interface.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID of the highest priority pending virtual interrupt. If the highest priority pending interrupt is not observable, this field contains a special INTID to indicate the reason. This special INTID can take the value 1023 only. For more information, see 'Special INTIDs' in ARM® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 3.0 and version 4.0 (ARM IHI 0069) . This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICV_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 .

name: ICV_HPPIR1_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Highest Priority Pending Interrupt Register 1
description: Indicates the highest priority pending virtual Group 1 interrupt on the virtual CPU interface.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID of the highest priority pending virtual interrupt. If the highest priority pending interrupt is not observable, this field contains a special INTID to indicate the reason. This special INTID can take the value 1023 only. For more information, see 'Special INTIDs' in ARM® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 3.0 and version 4.0 (ARM IHI 0069) . This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICV_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 .

name: ICV_HPPIR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Highest Priority Pending Interrupt Register
description: Reports the HPPI for the Virtual Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: HPPIV
fields.description: HPPI valid. There is an HPPI with Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.

values.label: Invalid: There is no HPPI with Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.
VALID: There is an HPPI with Sufficient priority for the Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: TYPE
fields.description: The encoding of this field depends on the value of HPPIV as described below: If ICV_HPPIR_EL1.HPPIV is 0, TYPE is RES0 . If ICV_HPPIR_EL1.HPPIV is 1, TYPE specifies the Type of the interrupt.

values.label: PPI
LPI
SPI

fields.name: ID
fields.description: The encoding of this field depends on the value of HPPIV as described below: If ICV_HPPIR_EL1.HPPIV is 0, ID is RES0 . If ICV_HPPIR_EL1.HPPIV is 1, ID specifies the interrupt ID.

name: ICV_IAR0_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Interrupt Acknowledge Register 0
description: The PE reads this register to obtain the INTID of the signaled virtual Group 0 interrupt. This read acts as an acknowledge for the interrupt.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID of the signaled virtual interrupt. This is the INTID of the highest priority pending virtual interrupt, if that interrupt is of sufficient priority for it to be signaled to the PE, and if it can be acknowledged. If the highest priority pending interrupt is not observable, this field contains a special INTID to indicate the reason. For more information, see 'Special INTIDs' in ARM® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 3.0 and version 4.0 (ARM IHI 0069) . This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICV_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 .

name: ICV_IAR1_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Interrupt Acknowledge Register 1
description: The PE reads this register to obtain the INTID of the signaled virtual Group 1 interrupt. This read acts as an acknowledge for the interrupt.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID of the signaled virtual interrupt. This is the INTID of the highest priority pending virtual interrupt, if that interrupt is of sufficient priority for it to be signaled to the PE, and if it can be acknowledged. If the highest priority pending interrupt is not observable, this field contains a special INTID to indicate the reason. For more information, see 'Special INTIDs' in ARM® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 3.0 and version 4.0 (ARM IHI 0069) . This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICV_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 .

name: ICV_IGRPEN0_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Interrupt Group 0 Enable Register
description: Controls whether virtual Group 0 interrupts are enabled or not.

values.label: Virtual Group 0 interrupts are disabled.
Virtual Group 0 interrupts are enabled.

name: ICV_IGRPEN1_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Interrupt Group 1 Enable Register
description: Controls whether virtual Group 1 interrupts are enabled for the current Security state.

values.label: Virtual Group 1 interrupts are disabled.
Virtual Group 1 interrupts are enabled.

name: ICV_NMIAR1_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Non-maskable Interrupt Acknowledge Register 1
description: The PE reads this register to obtain the INTID of the signaled virtual Group 1 interrupt. This read acts as an acknowledge for the interrupt.

fields.name: INTID
fields.description: The INTID of the signaled virtual interrupt. This is the INTID of the highest priority pending virtual interrupt, if that virtual interrupt has the Non-maskable property and is of sufficient priority for it to be signaled to the PE, and if it can be acknowledged at the current Security state and Exception level. If the highest priority pending interrupt is not observable, this field contains a special INTID to indicate the reason. For more information, see 'Special INTIDs' in ARM® Generic Interrupt Controller Architecture Specification, GIC architecture version 3.0 and version 4.0 (ARM IHI 0069) . This field has either 16 or 24 bits implemented. The number of implemented bits can be found in ICV_CTLR_EL1 .IDbits. If only 16 bits are implemented, bits [23:16] of this register are RES0 .

name: ICV_PCR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Interrupt Priority Control Register
description: Controls the Virtual priority mask for the Virtual Interrupt Domain.

fields.name: PRIORITY
fields.description: The priority mask for the Interrupt Domain.

name: ICV_PMR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Interrupt Priority Mask Register
description: Provides a virtual interrupt priority filter. Only virtual interrupts with a higher priority than the value in this register are signaled to the PE.

fields.name: Priority
fields.description: The priority mask level for the virtual CPU interface. If the priority of a virtual interrupt is higher than the value indicated by this field, the interface signals the virtual interrupt to the PE. The possible priority field values are as follows: Implemented priority bits Possible priority field values Number of priority levels [7:0] 0x00 - 0xFF (0-255), all values 256 [7:1] 0x00 - 0xFE (0-254), even values only 128 [7:2] 0x00 - 0xFC (0-252), in steps of 4 64 [7:3] 0x00 - 0xF8 (0-248), in steps of 8 32 [7:4] 0x00 - 0xF0 (0-240), in steps of 16 16 Unimplemented priority bits are RAZ/WI.

name: ICV_PPI_CACTIVER<n>_EL1

Writing 0 has no effect.

name: ICV_RPR_EL1
long_name: Interrupt Controller Virtual Running Priority Register
description: Indicates the Running priority of the virtual CPU interface.

fields.name: NMI
fields.description: Indicates whether the running priority is from a NMI.

values.label: There is no active Group 1 NMI, or all active Group 1 NMIs have undergone priority drop.
There is an active Group 1 NMI.

fields.name: Priority
fields.description: The current running priority on the virtual CPU interface. This is the group priority of the current active virtual interrupt. If there are no active interrupts on the virtual CPU interface, or all active interrupts have undergone a priority drop, the value returned is the Idle priority. The priority returned is the group priority as if the BPR for the current Exception level and Security state was set to the minimum value of BPR for the number of implemented priority bits. If 8 bits of priority are implemented the group priority is bits[7:1] of the priority.

name: ID_AA64AFR0_EL1

description: Reserved for future expansion of information about the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED features of the PE in AArch64 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers, see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

name: ID_AA64DFR0_EL1

fields.name: HPMN0
fields.description: Zero PMU event counters for a Guest operating system.

values.label: Setting MDCR_EL2 .HPMN to zero has CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE behavior.
Setting MDCR_EL2 .HPMN to zero has defined behavior.
Trace Buffer External Mode not implemented.
Trace Buffer External Mode implemented.
Branch Record Buffer Extension not implemented.
Branch Record Buffer Extension implemented.
As 0b0001 , and adds support for branch recording at EL3.
FEAT_MTPMU not implemented. If FEAT_PMUv3 is implemented, it is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .MT and PMEVTYPER<n> .MT are read/write or RES0 .
FEAT_MTPMU and FEAT_PMUv3 implemented. PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .MT and PMEVTYPER<n> .MT are read/write. When FEAT_MTPMU is disabled, the Effective values of PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .MT and PMEVTYPER<n> .MT are 0.
FEAT_MTPMU not implemented. If FEAT_PMUv3 is implemented, PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .MT and PMEVTYPER<n> .MT are RES0 .
Trace Buffer Extension not implemented.
Trace Buffer Extension implemented.

As 0b0001 , and adds: If EL2 and FEAT_FGT are implemented, a fine-grained trap on the TSB CSYNC instruction. If EL2 is implemented, an EL2 control to override TRBLIMITR_EL1 .nVM. The TRBE Profiling exception extension, FEAT_TRBE_EXC .
Armv8.4 Self-hosted Trace Extension not implemented.
Armv8.4 Self-hosted Trace Extension implemented.
OS Double Lock implemented. OSDLR_EL1 is RW.
OS Double Lock not implemented. OSDLR_EL1 is RAZ/WI.
Statistical Profiling Extension not implemented.
Statistical Profiling Extension implemented.
As 0b0001 , and adds: Support for the Events packet Alignment flag. If FEAT_SVE is implemented, support for the Scalable Vector extensions to Statistical Profiling.

As 0b0010 , and adds: Discard mode. Extended event filtering, including the PMSNEVFR_EL1 System register. Support for the OPTIONAL previous branch target Address packet. If FEAT_PMUv3 is implemented, controls to freeze the PMU event counters after an SPE buffer management event occurs. If FEAT_PMUv3 is implemented, the SAMPLE_FEED_BR, SAMPLE_FEED_EVENT, SAMPLE_FEED_LAT, SAMPLE_FEED_LD, SAMPLE_FEED_OP, and SAMPLE_FEED_ST PMU events.

As 0b0011 , and adds: If FEAT_MOPS is implemented, Operation Type packet encodings for Memory Copy and Set operations. If FEAT_MTE is implemented, Operation Type packet encodings for loads and stores of Allocation Tags.

As 0b0100 , and adds: Support for the Events packet Level 2 Data cache access, Level 2 Data cache miss, Cached data modified, Recently fetched cache line, and Cache snoop flags. Support for Data Source filtering.

As 0b0101 , and adds: If EL2 and FEAT_FGT are implemented, a fine-grained trap on the PSB CSYNC instruction. The SPE Profiling exception extension, FEAT_SPE_EXC . The Statistical Profiling physical addressing mode extension, FEAT_SPE_nVM .
The number of context-aware breakpoints, minus 1.

If ID_AA64DFR1_EL1 .CTX_CMPs is zero, then 16 context-aware breakpoints are implemented. Otherwise, 16 or more context-aware breakpoints are implemented and ID_AA64DFR1_EL1 .CTX_CMPs indicates how many.
Synchronous-exception-based event profiling not implemented.
Synchronous-exception-based event profiling implemented.
The number of watchpoints, minus 1.
PMU snapshot extension not implemented.
PMU snapshot extension implemented.

fields.name: BRPs
fields.description: Index of the highest numbered context-aware breakpoint. Identifies which of the implemented breakpoints are context-aware breakpoints.

values.label: Index of the highest numbered context-aware breakpoint.

If ID_AA64DFR0_EL1.CTX_CMPs and ID_AA64DFR1_EL1 .CTX_CMPs indicate that 16 or fewer breakpoints are context-aware, then the index of the highest context-aware breakpoint is 15. Otherwise, the context-aware breakpoints are the lowest numbered breakpoints.

fields.name: PMUVer
fields.description: Performance Monitors Extension version. This field does not follow the standard ID scheme, but uses the alternative ID scheme described in 'Alternative ID scheme used for the Performance Monitors Extension version' .

values.label: Performance Monitors Extension not implemented.
Performance Monitors Extension, PMUv3 implemented.
PMUv3 for Armv8.1. As 0b0001 , and adds support for: Extended 16-bit PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .evtCount field. If EL2 is implemented, the MDCR_EL2 .HPMD control.
PMUv3 for Armv8.4. As 0b0100 , and adds support for the PMMIR_EL1 register.
PMUv3 for Armv8.5. As 0b0101 , and adds support for: 64-bit event counters. If EL2 is implemented, the MDCR_EL2 .HCCD control. If EL3 is implemented, the MDCR_EL3 .SCCD control.
PMUv3 for Armv8.7. As 0b0110 , and adds support for: The PMCR_EL0 .FZO and, if EL2 is implemented, MDCR_EL2 .HPMFZO controls. If EL3 is implemented, the MDCR_EL3 .{MPMX,MCCD} controls.

PMUv3 for Armv8.8. As 0b0111 , and: Extends the Common event number space to include 0x0040 to 0x00BF and 0x4040 to 0x40BF . Removes the CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE behaviors if a reserved or unimplemented PMU event number is selected.

PMUv3 for Armv8.9. As 0b1000 , and: Updates the definitions of existing PMU events. Adds support for the PMUSERENR_EL0 .UEN control and the PMUACR_EL1 register. Adds support for the EDECR .PME control.
IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED form of performance monitors supported, PMUv3 not supported. Arm does not recommend this value for new implementations.

fields.name: F8CVT
fields.description: Indicates support for the following instructions: The Advanced SIMD floating-point scaling instruction FSCALE . As 0b0010 , and adds support for hardware update of the Access flag for Table descriptors.
As 0b0011 , and adds support for hardware tracking of Dirty state Structure.

name: ID_AA64MMFR2_EL1
description: Provides information about the implemented memory model and memory management support in AArch64 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers, see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: E0PD
fields.description: Indicates support for the E0PD mechanism.

values.label: E0PDx mechanism is not implemented.
E0PDx mechanism is implemented.

fields.name: EVT
fields.description: Enhanced Virtualization Traps. If EL2 is implemented, indicates support for the HCR_EL2 .{TTLBOS, TTLBIS, TOCU, TICAB, TID4} traps.

values.label: HCR_EL2 .{TTLBOS, TTLBIS, TOCU, TICAB, TID4} traps are not supported.
HCR_EL2 .{TOCU, TICAB, TID4} traps are supported.
As 0b0001 , and HCR_EL2 .{TTLBOS, TTLBIS} traps are supported.

fields.name: BBM
fields.description: Allows identification of the requirements of the hardware to have break-before-make sequences when changing block or table size for a translation.

values.label: Break-before-make sequence must be used.
Level 1 support for changing block size is supported.
Level 2 support for changing block size is supported.
Level 3 support for changing block size is supported.

fields.name: TTL
fields.description: Indicates support for TTL field in address operations.

values.label: TLB maintenance instructions by address have bits[47:44] as RES0 .
TLB maintenance instructions by address have bits[47:44] holding the TTL field.
HCR_EL2 .FWB bit is not supported.
HCR_EL2 .FWB is supported.

fields.name: IDS
fields.description: Indicates the EC syndrome value in ESR_ELx .EC that is reported if an exception is generated by a read access to the feature ID space.

values.label: An exception which is generated by a read access to the feature ID space, other than a trap caused by HCR_EL2 .TIDx, SCTLR_EL1 .UCT, or SCTLR_EL2 .UCT is reported by ESR_ELx .EC == 0x0 .
All exceptions generated by an AArch64 read access to the feature ID space are reported by ESR_ELx .EC == 0x18 .
As 0b0001 and introduces support for trapping ID register accesses to EL3.

fields.name: AT
fields.description: Identifies support for unaligned single-copy atomicity and atomic functions.

values.label: Unaligned single-copy atomicity and atomic functions are not supported.
Unaligned single-copy atomicity and atomic functions with a 16-byte address range aligned to 16-bytes are supported.

fields.name: ST
fields.description: Identifies support for small translation tables.

values.label: The maximum value of the TCR_ELx.{T0SZ,T1SZ} and VTCR_EL2 .T0SZ fields is 39.
The maximum value of the TCR_ELx.{T0SZ,T1SZ} and VTCR_EL2 .T0SZ fields is 48 for 4KB and 16KB granules, and 47 for 64KB granules.

fields.name: NV
fields.description: Nested Virtualization. If EL2 is implemented, indicates support for the use of nested virtualization.

values.label: If ID_AA64MMFR4_EL1 .NV_frac != 0b0000 , support for nested virtualization is described in ID_AA64MMFR4_EL1 .NV_frac. Otherwise, nested virtualization is not supported.
The HCR_EL2 .{AT, NV1, NV} bits are implemented.
The VNCR_EL2 register and the HCR_EL2 .{NV2, AT, NV1, NV} bits are implemented.

fields.name: CCIDX
fields.description: Support for the use of revised CCSIDR_EL1 register format.

values.label: 32-bit format implemented for all levels of the CCSIDR_EL1 .
64-bit format implemented for all levels of the CCSIDR_EL1 .

fields.name: VARange
fields.description: Indicates support for a larger virtual address.

values.label: VMSAv8-64 supports 48-bit VAs.
VMSAv8-64 supports 52-bit VAs when using the 64KB translation granule. The size for other translation granules is not defined by this field.
VMSAv9-128 supports 56-bit VAs.

fields.name: IESB
fields.description: Indicates support for the IESB bit in the SCTLR_ELx registers.

values.label: IESB bit in the SCTLR_ELx registers is not supported.
IESB bit in the SCTLR_ELx registers is supported.

fields.name: LSM

values.label: LSMAOE and nTLSMD bits not supported.
LSMAOE and nTLSMD bits supported.
UAO not supported.
UAO supported.

fields.name: CnP

values.label: Common not Private translations not supported.
Common not Private translations supported.

name: ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1
description: Provides information about the implemented memory model and memory management support in AArch64 state.

fields.name: Spec_FPACC
fields.description: Speculative behavior in the event of a PAC authentication failure in an implementation that includes FEAT_FPACCOMBINE .

values.label: The implementation does not disclose whether the speculative use of pointers processed by a PAC Authentication is materially different in terms of the impact on cached microarchitectural state between passing and failing of the PAC Authentication.

The speculative use of pointers processed by a PAC Authentication is not materially different in terms of the impact on cached microarchitectural state between passing and failing of the PAC Authentication.

fields.name: ADERR
fields.description: Asynchronous Device error exceptions. With ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1.SDERR, describes the PE behavior for External aborts signaled on Device memory loads.

values.label: If FEAT_RASv2 is not implemented and ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1.SDERR is 0b0000 , then the behavior is not described. Otherwise, the behavior is described by ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1.SDERR.
All External aborts on Device memory loads are handled asynchronously.

FEAT_ADERR is implemented. SCTLR2_ELx.EnADERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSDERR are implemented. If FEAT_ANERR is also implemented, then software should ensure that all the following apply: SCTLR2_ELx.EnADERR is set to the value of SCTLR2_ELx.EnANERR. HCRX_EL2 .EnSDERR is set to the value of HCRX_EL2 .EnSNERR.

FEAT_ADERR is implemented. SCTLR2_ELx.EnADERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSDERR are implemented. If FEAT_ANERR is also implemented, then SCTLR2_ELx.EnADERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSDERR operate independently of SCTLR2_ELx.EnANERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSNERR.

fields.name: SDERR
fields.description: Synchronous Device error exceptions. With ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1.ADERR, describes the PE behavior for External aborts signaled on Device memory loads.

values.label: If FEAT_RASv2 is not implemented and ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1.ADERR is 0b0000 , then the behavior is not described. Otherwise, the behavior is described by ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1.ADERR.
All External aborts on Device memory loads are handled synchronously.

FEAT_ADERR is implemented. SCTLR2_ELx.EnADERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSDERR are implemented. If FEAT_ANERR is also implemented, then software should ensure that all the following apply: SCTLR2_ELx.EnADERR is set to the value of SCTLR2_ELx.EnANERR. HCRX_EL2 .EnSDERR is set to the value of HCRX_EL2 .EnSNERR.

FEAT_ADERR is implemented. SCTLR2_ELx.EnADERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSDERR are implemented. If FEAT_ANERR is also implemented, then SCTLR2_ELx.EnADERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSDERR operate independently of SCTLR2_ELx.EnANERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSNERR.

fields.name: ANERR
fields.description: Asynchronous Normal error exceptions. With ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1.SNERR, describes the PE behavior for External aborts signaled on Normal memory loads.

values.label: If FEAT_RASv2 is not implemented and ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1.SNERR is 0b0000 , then the behavior is not described. Otherwise, the behavior is described by ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1.SNERR.
All External aborts on Normal memory loads are handled asynchronously.

FEAT_ANERR is implemented. SCTLR2_ELx.EnANERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSNERR are implemented. If FEAT_ADERR is also implemented, then software should ensure that all the following apply: SCTLR2_ELx.EnANERR is set to the value of SCTLR2_ELx.EnADERR. HCRX_EL2 .EnSNERR is set to the value of HCRX_EL2 .EnSDERR.

FEAT_ANERR is implemented. SCTLR2_ELx.EnANERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSNERR are implemented. If FEAT_ADERR is also implemented, then SCTLR2_ELx.EnANERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSNERR operate independently of SCTLR2_ELx.EnADERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSDERR.

fields.name: SNERR
fields.description: Synchronous Normal error exceptions. With ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1.ANERR, describes the PE behavior for External aborts signaled on Normal memory loads.

values.label: If FEAT_RASv2 is not implemented and ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1.ANERR is 0b0000 , then the behavior is not described. Otherwise, the behavior is described by ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1.ANERR.
All External aborts on Normal memory loads are handled synchronously.

FEAT_ANERR is implemented. SCTLR2_ELx.EnANERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSNERR are implemented. If FEAT_ADERR is also implemented, then software should ensure that all the following apply: SCTLR2_ELx.EnANERR is set to the value of SCTLR2_ELx.EnADERR. HCRX_EL2 .EnSNERR is set to the value of HCRX_EL2 .EnSDERR.

FEAT_ANERR is implemented. SCTLR2_ELx.EnANERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSNERR are implemented. If FEAT_ADERR is also implemented, then SCTLR2_ELx.EnANERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSNERR operate independently of SCTLR2_ELx.EnADERR and HCRX_EL2 .EnSDERR.

description: Provides additional information about implemented memory model and memory management support in AArch64.

values.label: SCR2_EL3 is not supported.
SCR2_EL3 is supported.

fields.name: SRMASK
fields.description: Indicates support for bitwise write masks for the following registers: ACTLR_EL1 . CPACR_EL1 . SCTLR_EL1 . SCTLR2_EL1 . TCR_EL1 . TCR2_EL1 . ACTLR_EL2 . CPTR_EL2 . SCTLR_EL2 . SCTLR2_EL2 . TCR_EL2 . TCR2_EL2 . When FEAT_SRMASK2 is implemented, indicates support for bitwise write masks for the following registers: HCR_EL2 and HCRX_EL2 . If FEAT_NV3 is implemented, indicates support for the following: The NVHCRX_EL2 register. Bitwise masks for NVHCRX_EL2 and NVHCR_EL2 .

FEAT_E2H0 is not implemented.

FEAT_NV and FEAT_NV2 are implemented, but all of the following apply: ID_AA64MMFR2_EL1 .NV is 0b0000 . Programming HCR_EL2 .{NV, NV2} to {1, 0} behaves as {1, 1}.

As 0b0010 and adds support for FEAT_NV3 .

fields.name: FGWTE3
fields.description: Indicates support for Fine Grained Write Trap EL3 feature.

values.label: Fine Grained Write Trap EL3 is not supported.
Fine Grained Write Trap EL3 is supported.

fields.name: HACDBS

values.label: Hardware accelerator for cleaning Dirty state is not supported.
Hardware accelerator for cleaning Dirty state is supported.

fields.name: ASID2
fields.description: Indicates support for concurrent use of two ASIDs.

values.label: FEAT_ASID2 is not implemented.
FEAT_ASID2 is implemented.

fields.name: EIESB
fields.description: Early Implicit Error Synchronization event. Indicates whether the implicit Error synchronization event inserted on taking an exception to ELx when SCTLR_ELx.IESB is 1 is inserted before or after the exception is taken.

values.label: FEAT_IESB is implemented. When SError exceptions are routed to EL3, and either FEAT_DoubleFault is not implemented or the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .NMEA is 1, an implicit Error synchronization event might be inserted after an exception is taken to EL3.
FEAT_IESB is not implemented, or behavior is not described.

FEAT_IESB is implemented. When SError exceptions are routed to EL3, and either FEAT_DoubleFault is not implemented or the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .NMEA is 1, an implicit Error synchronization event is inserted before an exception taken to EL3.

As 0b0001 , and also: When SError exceptions are routed to EL1, and either FEAT_DoubleFault2 is not implemented or the Effective value of SCTLR2_EL1 .NMEA is 1, an implicit Error synchronization event is inserted before an exception taken to EL1. When SError exceptions are routed to EL2, and either FEAT_DoubleFault2 is not implemented or the Effective value of SCTLR2_EL2 .NMEA is 1, an implicit Error synchronization event is inserted before an exception taken to EL2.

fields.name: PoPS
fields.description: Support for the clean and invalidate to the Point of Physical Storage instructions: DC CIVAPS . If FEAT_MTE2 is implemented, DC CIGDVAPS .

values.label: The System instructions to clean and invalidate to the Point of Physical Storage are not implemented.
The specified System instructions to clean and invalidate to the Point of Physical Storage are implemented.

name: ID_AA64PFR0_EL1
description: Provides additional information about implemented PE features in AArch64 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers, see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

values.label: This PE does not disclose whether data loaded or read from a register under speculation where the data load or register read would not be permitted architecturally, can be used by instructions newer than the load or register read in a manner that allows the value of the inaccessible data to be recovered by code architecturally executed.

Data loaded or read from a register under speculation where the data load or register read would not be permitted architecturally, cannot be used by instructions newer than the load or register read in a manner that allows the value of the inaccessible data to be recovered by code architecturally executed.

fields.name: CSV2
fields.description: Speculative use of out of context prediction resources.

values.label: The implementation does not disclose whether FEAT_CSV2 is implemented.
FEAT_CSV2 is implemented, but FEAT_CSV2_2 and FEAT_CSV2_3 are not implemented. ID_AA64PFR1_EL1 .CSV2_frac determines whether either or both of FEAT_CSV2_1p1 or FEAT_CSV2_1p2 are implemented.
FEAT_CSV2_2 is implemented, but FEAT_CSV2_3 is not implemented.
FEAT_CSV2_3 is implemented.
Realm Management Extension not implemented.
RMEv1 is implemented.

As 0b0010 , and adds support for the GPC3 Extension.
AArch64 does not guarantee constant execution time of any instructions.
AArch64 provides the PSTATE .DIT mechanism to guarantee constant execution time of certain instructions.
As 0b0001 and adds support for FEAT_FDIT .

fields.description: Indicates support for Activity Monitors Extension.

values.label: Activity Monitors Extension is not implemented.
FEAT_AMUv1 is implemented.

fields.name: MPAM
fields.description: Indicates the major version number of support for the MPAM Extension.

values.label: The major version number of the MPAM extension is 0.
The major version number of the MPAM extension is 1.
Secure EL2 is not implemented.
Secure EL2 is implemented.

SVE architectural state and programmers' model are implemented.
No RAS Extension.
Support for the Reliability, Availability, and Serviceability Extension is implemented. The ESB instruction and the Error synchronization event are supported.

As 0b0001 , and adds support for: If EL3 is implemented, FEAT_DoubleFault . Additional ERXMISC<m>_EL1 System registers. Additional System registers ERXPFGCDN_EL1 , ERXPFGCTL_EL1 , and ERXPFGF_EL1 , and the SCR_EL3 .FIEN and HCR_EL2 .FIEN trap controls, to support the optional RAS Common Fault Injection Model Extension. Error records accessed through System registers conform to RAS System Architecture v1.1, which includes simplifications to ERR<n>STATUS and support for the optional RAS Timestamp and RAS Common Fault Injection Model Extensions.

As 0b0010 and adds support for: The error group status register, ERXGSR_EL1 . The SCR_EL3 .TWERR write trap control for error record System registers. Additional fields in ESR_ELx .ISS for error exceptions. Error records accessed through System registers conform to either RAS System Architecture v1.1 or RAS System Architecture v2.
GIC CPU interface system registers not implemented.
System register interface to versions 3.0 and 4.0 of the GIC CPU interface is supported.
System register interface to version 4.1 of the GIC CPU interface is supported.

Advanced SIMD is implemented, including support for the following SISD and SIMD operations: Integer byte, halfword, word and doubleword element operations. Single-precision and double-precision floating-point arithmetic. Conversions between single-precision and half-precision data types, and double-precision and half-precision data types.
As for 0b0000 , and also includes support for half-precision floating-point arithmetic.
Advanced SIMD is not implemented.

Floating-point is implemented, and includes support for: Single-precision and double-precision floating-point types. Conversions between single-precision and half-precision data types, and double-precision and half-precision data types.
As for 0b0000 , and also includes support for half-precision floating-point arithmetic.
Floating-point is not implemented.
EL3 is not implemented.
EL3 can be executed in AArch64 state only.
EL3 can be executed in either AArch64 or AArch32 state.
EL2 is not implemented.

EL2 can be executed in either AArch64 or AArch32 state.
EL1 can be executed in AArch64 state only.
EL1 can be executed in either AArch64 or AArch32 state.
EL0 can be executed in AArch64 state only.
EL0 can be executed in either AArch64 or AArch32 state.

name: ID_AA64PFR1_EL1
description: Provides additional information about implemented PE features in AArch64 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers, see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .
