values.label: Allocation tag access permissions are not supported.
Allocation tag access permissions are supported. NoTagAccess is supported at stage 2 of translation only.

name: ID_AA64SMFR0_EL1
description: Provides information about the implemented features of the AArch64 Scalable Matrix Extension. The fields in this register do not follow the standard ID scheme. See Alternative ID scheme used for ID_AA64SMFR0_EL1 and ID_AA64FPFR0_EL1 .

fields.name: FA64
fields.description: Indicates support at each Exception Level for execution of the full AArch64 Advanced SIMD and SVE instruction sets when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode.

values.label: Only those AArch64 instructions defined as being legal can be executed in streaming SVE mode.
All implemented AArch64 instructions are legal for execution in Streaming SVE mode, when enabled by SMCR_EL1 .FA64, SMCR_EL2 .FA64, and SMCR_EL3 .FA64.

fields.name: LUT6
fields.description: Indicates support for SME lookup table instructions with 6-bit indices.

values.label: SME LUTI6 instructions with 6-bit indices are not implemented.
SME LUTI6 instructions with 6-bit indices are implemented.

fields.name: LUTv2
fields.description: Indicates support for the following additional variants of SME2 lookup table LUTI4 and MOVT instructions: A LUTI4 instruction with 8-bit result elements, two consecutively numbered source vectors, and four consecutively numbered destination vectors. If FEAT_SME2p1 is implemented, a LUTI4 instruction with 8-bit result elements, two consecutively numbered source vectors, and four destination vectors with strided register numbering. A MOVT instruction that copies a single Z source vector to ZT0.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.
The mandatory SME instructions are implemented.
As 0b0000 , and adds the mandatory SME2 instructions.
As 0b0001 , and adds the mandatory SME2.1 instructions.
As 0b0010 , and adds the mandatory SME2.2 instructions.
As 0b0011 , and adds the mandatory SME2.3 instructions.

fields.name: I16I64
fields.description: Indicates support for the following SME instructions that accumulate into 64-bit integer elements in the ZA array: The variants of the ADDHA , ADDVA , SMOPA , SMOPS , SUMOPA , SUMOPS , UMOPA , UMOPS , USMOPA , and USMOPS instructions that accumulate into 64-bit integer tiles. When FEAT_SME2 is implemented, the variants of the ADD , ADDA , SDOT , SMLALL , SMLSLL , SUB , SUBA , SVDOT , UDOT , UMLALL , UMLSLL , and UVDOT instructions that accumulate into 64-bit integer elements in ZA array vectors.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: F64F64
fields.description: Indicates support for the following SME instructions that accumulate into double-precision floating-point elements in the ZA array: The variants of the FMOPA and FMOPS instructions that accumulate into double-precision tiles. When FEAT_SME2 is implemented, the variants of the FADD , FMLA , FMLS , and FSUB instructions that accumulate into double-precision elements in ZA array vectors.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented .

fields.name: I16I32
fields.description: Indicates support for SME2 SMOPA (2-way), SMOPS (2-way), UMOPA (2-way), and UMOPS (2-way) instructions that accumulate 16-bit outer products into 32-bit integer tiles.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: B16B16
fields.description: Indicates support for the SME ZA-targeting non-widening BFloat16 BFADD , BFMLA , BFMLS , BFMOPA , BFMOPS , and BFSUB instructions with BFloat16 operands and results.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: F16F16
fields.description: Indicates support for the following SME2 half-precision floating-point instructions: FMOPA and FMOPS instructions that accumulate half-precision outer-products into half-precision tiles. Multi-vector FADD , FMLA , FMLS , and FSUB instructions with half-precision operands and results. Multi-vector FCVT and FCVTL instructions that convert half-precision inputs to single-precision results.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: F8F16
fields.description: Indicates support for the following SME2 instructions: The ZA-targeting FP8 instructions FDOT (2-way), FMLAL , FMOPA (2-way), and FVDOT that accumulate into half-precision floating-point elements. ZA-targeting non-widening half-precision FADD and FSUB instructions.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: F8F32
fields.description: Indicates support for the SME2 ZA-targeting FP8 FDOT (4-way), FMLALL , FMOPA (4-way), FVDOTB , and FVDOTT instructions that accumulate into single-precision floating-point elements.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: I8I32
fields.description: Indicates support for SME SMOPA , SMOPS , SUMOPA , SUMOPS , UMOPA , UMOPS , USMOPA , and USMOPS instructions that accumulate 8-bit outer products into 32-bit tiles

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: F16F32
fields.description: Indicates support for SME FMOPA and FMOPS instructions that accumulate half-precision floating-point outer products into single-precision floating-point tiles.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: B16F32
fields.description: Indicates support for SME BFMOPA and BFMOPS instructions that accumulate BFloat16 outer products into single-precision floating-point tiles.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: BI32I32
fields.description: Indicates support for SME BMOPA and BMOPS instructions that accumulate thirty-two 1-bit binary outer products into 32-bit integer tiles.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: F32F32
fields.description: Indicates support for SME FMOPA and FMOPS instructions that accumulate single-precision floating-point outer products into single-precision floating-point tiles.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: SF8FMA
fields.description: Indicates support for the SVE2 FP8 to single-precision and half-precision multiply-accumulate FMLALB , FMLALT , FMLALLBB , FMLALLBT , FMLALLTB , and FMLALLTT instructions when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode.

values.label: This control has no effect on Streaming SVE mode behavior.
The specified instructions are supported in Streaming SVE mode.

fields.name: SF8DP4
fields.description: Indicates support for the SVE2 FP8 to single-precision 4-way dot product FDOT (4-way) instructions when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode.

values.label: This control has no effect on Streaming SVE mode behavior.
The specified instructions are supported in Streaming SVE mode.

fields.name: SF8DP2
fields.description: Indicates support for the SVE2 FP8 to half-precision 2-way dot product FDOT (2-way) instructions when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode.

values.label: This control has no effect on Streaming SVE mode behavior.
The specified instructions are supported in Streaming SVE mode.

fields.name: SBitPerm
fields.description: Indicates support for the SVE bit permute instructions identified as implemented by ID_AA64ZFR0_EL1 .BitPerm, when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode.

values.label: This control has no effect on Streaming SVE mode behavior.
The specified instructions are implemented when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode.

fields.name: AES
fields.description: Indicates support for the SVE AES and 128-bit polynomial multiply long instructions identified as implemented by ID_AA64ZFR0_EL1 .AES, when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode.

values.label: This control has no effect on Streaming SVE mode behavior.
The specified instructions are supported when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode.

fields.name: SFEXPA
fields.description: Indicates support for the SVE FEXPA instruction when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode.

values.label: This control has no effect on Streaming SVE mode behavior.
The specified instruction is supported when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode.

fields.name: STMOP
fields.description: Indicates support for the following SME Structured sparsity outer product instructions: BFTMOPA (non-widening), if FEAT_SME_B16B16 is implemented. BFTMOPA (widening, BF16 to FP32). FTMOPA (non-widening, FP16), if FEAT_SME_F16F16 is implemented. FTMOPA (non-widening, FP32). FTMOPA (widening, 2-way, FP16 to FP32). FTMOPA (widening, 2-way, FP8 to FP16), if FEAT_SME_F8F16 is implemented. FTMOPA (widening, 4-way, FP8 to FP32) if FEAT_SME_F8F32 is implemented. STMOPA , SUTMOPA , USTMOPA , UTMOPA (4-way, Int8 to Int32). STMOPA , UTMOPA (2-way, Int16 to Int32).

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: SMOP4
fields.description: Indicates support for the following SME Quarter-tile outer product instructions: BFMOP4A , BFMOP4S (non-widening, BF16), if FEAT_SME_B16B16 is implemented. BFMOP4A , BFMOP4S (widening, 2-way, BF16 to FP32). FMOP4A , FMOP4S (non-widening, FP16), if FEAT_SME_F16F16 is implemented. FMOP4A , FMOP4S (non-widening, FP32). FMOP4A , FMOP4S (non-widening, FP64), if FEAT_SME_F64F64 is implemented. FMOP4A , FMOP4S (widening, 2-way, FP16 to FP32). FMOP4A (widening, 2-way, FP8 to FP16), if FEAT_SME_F8F16 is implemented. FMOP4A (widening, 4-way, FP8 to FP32) instruction, if FEAT_SME_F8F32 is implemented. SMOP4A , SMOP4S , SUMOP4A , SUMOP4S , UMOP4A , UMOP4S , USMOP4A , USMOP4S (4-way, Int8 to Int32). SMOP4A , SMOP4S , SUMOP4A , SUMOP4S , UMOP4A , UMOP4S , USMOP4A , USMOP4S (4-way, Int16 to Int64), if FEAT_SME_I16I64 is implemented. SMOP4A , SMOP4S , UMOP4A , UMOP4S (2-way, Int16 to Int32).

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented by this control.
The specified instructions are implemented.

name: ID_AA64ZFR0_EL1
description: Provides additional information about the implemented features of the AArch64 Scalable Vector Extension instruction set, when FEAT_SVE or FEAT_SME is implemented. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers, see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: F64MM
fields.description: Indicates support for the SVE double-precision floating-point matrix multiply-accumulate instruction FMMLA , the LD1RO* instructions, the 128-bit element variants of the SVE TRN1 , TRN2 , UZP1 , UZP2 , ZIP1 , and ZIP2 instructions.

values.label: This field does not indicate support for the specified instructions.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: F32MM
fields.description: Indicates support for the SVE single-precision floating-point matrix multiply-accumulate instruction FMMLA .

values.label: This field does not indicate support for the specified instruction.
The specified instruction is implemented.

fields.name: F16MM
fields.description: Indicates support for the SVE half-precision floating-point matrix multiply-accumulate to single-precision instruction FMMLA (widening, FP16 to FP32).

values.label: This field does not indicate support for the specified instruction.
The specified instruction is implemented.

fields.name: I8MM
fields.description: Indicates support for the following SVE 8-bit integer sum-of-products and accumulate to 32-bit integer instructions SMMLA , SUDOT , UMMLA , USMMLA , and USDOT .

values.label: This field does not indicate support for the specified instructions.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: SM4
fields.description: Indicates support for SVE SM4 instructions.

values.label: This field does not indicate support for the specified instructions.
SVE SM4E and SM4EKEY instructions are implemented.

fields.name: SHA3
fields.description: Indicates support for the SVE SHA3 instructions.

values.label: This field does not indicate support for the specified instructions.
SVE RAX1 instruction is implemented.

fields.name: B16B16
fields.description: Indicates support for SVE non-widening BFloat16 instructions and SME multi-vector Z-targeting non-widening BFloat16 instructions.

values.label: This field does not indicate support for the specified instructions.

The following non-widening BFloat16 instructions are implemented: SVE instructions: BFADD , BFCLAMP , BFMAX , BFMAXNM , BFMIN , BFMINNM , BFMLA , BFMLS , BFMUL , and BFSUB . If FEAT_SME2 is implemented, SME multi-vector Z-targeting instructions: BFCLAMP , BFMAX , BFMAXNM , BFMIN , and BFMINNM .
As 0b0001 , and adds the following non-widening BFloat16 instructions: SVE instruction BFSCALE . If FEAT_SME2 is implemented, SME multi-vector Z-targeting instructions BFMUL and BFSCALE .
As 0b0010 , and adds the SVE BFloat16 matrix multiply-accumulate instruction BFMMLA .

fields.name: BF16
fields.description: Indicates support for SVE BFloat16 instructions.

values.label: SVE BFloat16 instructions are not implemented.
SVE BFCVT , BFCVTNT , BFDOT , BFMLALB , BFMLALT , and BFMMLA instructions are implemented.
As 0b0001 , but the FPCR .EBF field is also supported.

fields.name: BitPerm
fields.description: Indicates support for the SVE bit permute instructions BDEP , BEXT , and BGRP .

values.label: This field does not indicate support for the specified instructions.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: EltPerm
fields.description: If FEAT_SVE2p2 is implemented, the following SVE instructions are implemented when the PE is not in Streaming SVE mode: 8-bit and 16-bit element COMPACT . 8-bit, 16-bit, 32-bit, and 64-bit element EXPAND . If FEAT_SME2p2 is implemented, the following SVE instructions are implemented when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode: 8-bit, 16-bit, 32-bit, and 64-bit element COMPACT and EXPAND .

values.label: This field does not indicate support for the specified instructions.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: AES
fields.description: Indicates support for SVE Advanced Encryption Standard instructions and 128-bit polynomial multiply long instructions.

values.label: This field does not indicate support for the specified instructions.

The following instructions are implemented: SVE single-vector AESD , AESE , AESIMC , and AESMC instructions. The 128-bit destination element variant of the SVE single-vector PMULLB and PMULLT instructions.
As 0b0010 , and adds the following SVE instructions: Multi-vector AESD , AESDIMC , AESE and AESEMC instructions. Multi-vector 128-bit destination element PMULL and PMLAL instructions.
The SVE instructions are implemented.
As 0b0000 , and adds the mandatory SVE2 instructions.
As 0b0001 , and adds the mandatory SVE2.1 instructions.
As 0b0010 , and adds the mandatory SVE2.2 instructions.
As 0b0011 , and adds the mandatory SVE2.3 instructions.

name: ID_AFR0_EL1
description: Provides information about the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED features of the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with the Main ID Register, MIDR_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

name: ID_DFR0_EL1
description: Provides top-level information about the debug system in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with the Main ID Register, MIDR_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

values.label: Armv8.4 Self-hosted Trace Extension not implemented.
Armv8.4 Self-hosted Trace Extension implemented.

fields.name: PerfMon
fields.description: Performance Monitors Extension version. This field does not follow the standard ID scheme, but uses the alternative ID scheme described in 'Alternative ID scheme used for the Performance Monitors Extension version'

values.label: Performance Monitors Extension not implemented.
Performance Monitors Extension, PMUv1 implemented.
Performance Monitors Extension, PMUv2 implemented.
Performance Monitors Extension, PMUv3 implemented.
PMUv3 for Armv8.1. As 0b0011 , and adds support for: Extended 16-bit PMEVTYPER<n> .evtCount field. If EL2 is implemented, the HDCR .HPMD control.
PMUv3 for Armv8.4. As 0b0100 , and adds support for the PMMIR register.
PMUv3 for Armv8.5. As 0b0101 , and adds support for: 64-bit event counters. If EL2 is implemented, the HDCR .HCCD control. If EL3 is implemented, the MDCR_EL3 .SCCD control.
PMUv3 for Armv8.7. As 0b0110 , and adds support for: The PMCR .FZO and, if EL2 is implemented, HDCR .HPMFZO controls. If EL3 is implemented, the MDCR_EL3 .{MPMX,MCCD} controls.

PMUv3 for Armv8.8. As 0b0111 , and: Extends the Common event number space to include 0x0040 to 0x00BF and 0x4040 to 0x40BF . Removes the CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE behaviors if a reserved or unimplemented PMU event number is selected.
PMUv3 for Armv8.9. As 0b1000 , and: Updates the definitions of existing PMU events. Adds support for the EDECR .PME control.
IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED form of performance monitors supported, PMUv3 not supported. Arm does not recommend this value for new implementations.

fields.name: MProfDbg
fields.description: M-profile Debug. Support for memory-mapped debug model for M-profile processors.

values.label: Not supported.
Support for M-profile Debug architecture, with memory-mapped access.

fields.name: MMapTrc
fields.description: Memory-mapped Trace. Support for memory-mapped trace model.

values.label: Not supported.
Support for Arm trace architecture, with memory-mapped access.

fields.name: CopTrc
fields.description: Support for System registers-based trace model, using registers in the coproc == 0b1110 encoding space.

values.label: Not supported.
Support for Arm trace architecture, with System registers access.

fields.name: MMapDbg
fields.description: Memory-mapped Debug. Support for Armv7 memory-mapped debug model for A and R-profile processors.

values.label: Not supported.
Support for Armv7, v7 Debug architecture, with memory-mapped access.
Support for Armv7, v7.1 Debug architecture, with memory-mapped access.

fields.name: CopSDbg
fields.description: Support for a System registers-based Secure debug model, using registers in the coproc = 0b1110 encoding space, for an A-profile processor that includes EL3. If EL3 is not implemented and the implemented Security state is Non-secure state, this field is RES0 . Otherwise, this field reads the same as bits [3:0].

fields.name: CopDbg
fields.description: Debug architecture version. Indicates presence of Armv8 debug architecture.

values.label: Not supported.
Armv6, v6 Debug architecture, with System registers access.
Armv6, v6.1 Debug architecture, with System registers access.
Armv7, v7 Debug architecture, with System registers access.
Armv7, v7.1 Debug architecture, with System registers access.
Armv8 debug architecture.
Armv8.1 debug architecture, FEAT_Debugv8p1 .
Armv8.2 debug architecture, FEAT_Debugv8p2 .
Armv8.4 debug architecture, FEAT_Debugv8p4 .
Armv8.8 debug architecture, FEAT_Debugv8p8 .
Armv8.9 debug architecture, FEAT_Debugv8p9 .

name: ID_DFR1_EL1
description: Provides top-level information about the debug system in AArch32. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: HPMN0
fields.description: Zero PMU event counters for a Guest operating system.

values.label: Setting HDCR .HPMN to zero has CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE behavior.
Setting HDCR .HPMN to zero has defined behavior.
FEAT_MTPMU not implemented. If FEAT_PMUv3 is implemented, it is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether PMEVTYPER<n> .MT are read/write or RES0 .
FEAT_MTPMU and FEAT_PMUv3 implemented. PMEVTYPER<n> .MT are read/write. When FEAT_MTPMU is disabled, the Effective values of PMEVTYPER<n> .MT are 0.
FEAT_MTPMU not implemented. If FEAT_PMUv3 is implemented, PMEVTYPER<n> .MT are RES0 .

name: ID_ISAR0_EL1
description: Provides information about the instruction sets implemented by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_ISAR1_EL1 , ID_ISAR2_EL1 , ID_ISAR3_EL1 , ID_ISAR4_EL1 , and ID_ISAR5_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

values.label: None implemented.
Adds SDIV and UDIV in the T32 instruction set.
As for 0b0001 , and adds SDIV and UDIV in the A32 instruction set.
None implemented.
Adds BKPT .

fields.name: Coproc
fields.description: Indicates the implemented System register access instructions.

values.label: None implemented, except for instructions separately attributed by the architecture to provide access to AArch32 System registers and System instructions.
Adds generic CDP , LDC , MCR , MRC , and STC .
As for 0b0001 , and adds generic CDP2 , LDC2 , MCR2 , MRC2 , and STC2 .
As for 0b0010 , and adds generic MCRR and MRRC .
As for 0b0011 , and adds generic MCRR2 and MRRC2 .

fields.name: CmpBranch
fields.description: Indicates the implemented combined Compare and Branch instructions in the T32 instruction set.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds CBNZ and CBZ .

fields.name: BitField
fields.description: Indicates support for the following BitField instructions BFC , BFI , SBFX , and UBFX .

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: BitCount
fields.description: Indicates the implemented Bit Counting instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds CLZ .

fields.name: Swap
fields.description: Indicates the implemented Swap instructions in the A32 instruction set.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds SWP and SWPB .

name: ID_ISAR1_EL1
description: Provides information about the instruction sets implemented by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_ISAR0_EL1 , ID_ISAR2_EL1 , ID_ISAR3_EL1 , ID_ISAR4_EL1 , and ID_ISAR5_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: Jazelle
fields.description: Indicates the implemented Jazelle extension instructions.

values.label: No support for Jazelle.
Adds the BXJ instruction and the J bit in the PSR. This setting might indicate a trivial implementation of the Jazelle extension.
None implemented.
Adds the BX instruction, and the T bit in the PSR.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the BLX instruction. PC loads have BX-like behavior.
As for 0b0010 , and guarantees that data-processing instructions in the A32 instruction set with the PC as the destination and the S bit clear have BX-like behavior.

fields.name: Immediate
fields.description: Indicates support for the following data-processing instructions with long immediates: The MOVT instruction. The MOV instruction encodings with zero-extended 16-bit immediates. The T32 ADD and SUB instruction encodings with zero-extended 12-bit immediates, and the other ADD , ADR , and SUB encodings cross-referenced by the pseudocode for those encodings.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: IfThen
fields.description: Indicates the implemented If-Then instructions in the T32 instruction set.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the IT instructions, and the IT bits in the PSRs.
No scalar sign-extend or zero-extend instructions are implemented, where scalar instructions means non-Advanced SIMD instructions.
Adds the SXTB , SXTH , UXTB , and UXTH instructions.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the SXTB16 , SXTAB , SXTAB16 , SXTAH , UXTB16 , UXTAB , UXTAB16 , and UXTAH instructions.

fields.name: Except_AR
fields.description: Indicates the implemented A and R-profile exception-handling instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the SRS and RFE instructions, and the A and R-profile forms of the CPS instruction.

fields.name: Except
fields.description: Indicates the implemented exception-handling instructions in the A32 instruction set.

values.label: Not implemented. This indicates that the User bank and Exception return forms of the LDM and STM instructions are not implemented.
Adds the LDM (exception return), LDM (user registers), and STM (user registers) instruction versions.
None implemented.
Adds the SETEND instruction, and the E bit in the PSRs.

name: ID_ISAR2_EL1
description: Provides information about the instruction sets implemented by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_ISAR0_EL1 , ID_ISAR1_EL1 , ID_ISAR3_EL1 , ID_ISAR4_EL1 , and ID_ISAR5_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the REV, REV16, and REVSH instructions.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the RBIT instruction.

fields.name: PSR_AR
fields.description: Indicates the implemented A and R-profile instructions to manipulate the PSR.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the MRS and MSR instructions, and the exception return forms of data-processing instructions.

fields.name: MultU
fields.description: Indicates the implemented advanced unsigned Multiply instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the UMULL and UMLAL instructions.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the UMAAL instruction.

fields.name: MultS
fields.description: Indicates the implemented advanced signed Multiply instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the SMULL and SMLAL instructions.

As for 0b0001 , and adds the SMLABB, SMLABT, SMLALBB, SMLALBT, SMLALTB, SMLALTT, SMLATB, SMLATT, SMLAWB, SMLAWT, SMULBB, SMULBT, SMULTB, SMULTT, SMULWB, and SMULWT instructions. Also adds the Q bit in the PSRs.
As for 0b0010 , and adds the SMLAD, SMLADX, SMLALD, SMLALDX, SMLSD, SMLSDX, SMLSLD, SMLSLDX, SMMLA, SMMLAR, SMMLS, SMMLSR, SMMUL, SMMULR, SMUAD, SMUADX, SMUSD, and SMUSDX instructions.

fields.name: Mult
fields.description: Indicates the implemented additional Multiply instructions.

values.label: No additional instructions implemented. This means only MUL is implemented.
Adds the MLA instruction.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the MLS instruction.

fields.name: MultiAccessInt
fields.description: Indicates the support for interruptible multi-access instructions.

values.label: No support. This means the LDM and STM instructions are not interruptible.
LDM and STM instructions are restartable.
LDM and STM instructions are continuable.

fields.name: MemHint
fields.description: Indicates the implemented Memory Hint instructions.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the PLD instruction.
Adds the PLD instruction. ( 0b0001 and 0b0010 have identical effects.)
As for 0b0001 (or 0b0010 ), and adds the PLI instruction.
As for 0b0011 , and adds the PLDW instruction.

fields.name: LoadStore
fields.description: Indicates the implemented additional load/store instructions.

values.label: No additional load/store instructions implemented.
Adds the LDRD and STRD instructions.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the Load Acquire (LDAB, LDAH, LDA, LDAEXB, LDAEXH, LDAEX, LDAEXD) and Store Release (STLB, STLH, STL, STLEXB, STLEXH, STLEX, STLEXD) instructions.

name: ID_ISAR3_EL1
description: Provides information about the instruction sets implemented by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_ISAR0_EL1 , ID_ISAR1_EL1 , ID_ISAR2_EL1 , ID_ISAR4_EL1 , and ID_ISAR5_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the ENTERX and LEAVEX instructions, and modifies the load behavior to include null checking.

fields.name: TrueNOP
fields.description: Indicates the implemented true NOP instructions.

values.label: None implemented. This means there are no NOP instructions that do not have any register dependencies.
Adds true NOP instructions in both the T32 and A32 instruction sets. This also permits additional NOP-compatible hints.

fields.name: T32Copy
fields.description: Indicates the support for T32 non flag-setting MOV instructions.

values.label: Not supported. This means that in the T32 instruction set, encoding T1 of the MOV (register) instruction does not support a copy from a low register to a low register.
Adds support for T32 instruction set encoding T1 of the MOV (register) instruction, copying from a low register to a low register.

fields.name: TabBranch
fields.description: Indicates the implemented Table Branch instructions in the T32 instruction set.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the TBB and TBH instructions.

fields.name: SynchPrim
fields.description: Used in conjunction with ID_ISAR4.SynchPrim_frac to indicate the implemented Synchronization Primitive instructions.

values.label: If SynchPrim_frac == 0b0000 , no Synchronization Primitives implemented.
If SynchPrim_frac == 0b0000 , adds the LDREX and STREX instructions. If SynchPrim_frac == 0b0011 , also adds the CLREX, LDREXB, STREXB, and STREXH instructions.
If SynchPrim_frac == 0b0000 , as for [ 0b0001 , 0b0011 ] and also adds the LDREXD and STREXD instructions.
Not implemented.
Adds the SVC instruction.
None implemented.
Adds the SSAT and USAT instructions, and the Q bit in the PSRs.

As for 0b0001 , and adds the PKHBT, PKHTB, QADD16, QADD8, QASX, QSUB16, QSUB8, QSAX, SADD16, SADD8, SASX, SEL, SHADD16, SHADD8, SHASX, SHSUB16, SHSUB8, SHSAX, SSAT16, SSUB16, SSUB8, SSAX, SXTAB16, SXTB16, UADD16, UADD8, UASX, UHADD16, UHADD8, UHASX, UHSUB16, UHSUB8, UHSAX, UQADD16, UQADD8, UQASX, UQSUB16, UQSUB8, UQSAX, USAD8, USADA8, USAT16, USUB16, USUB8, USAX, UXTAB16, and UXTB16 instructions. Also adds support for the GE[3:0] bits in the PSRs.
None implemented. This means no non-Advanced SIMD saturate instructions are implemented.
Adds the QADD, QDADD, QDSUB, and QSUB instructions, and the Q bit in the PSRs.

name: ID_ISAR4_EL1
description: Provides information about the instruction sets implemented by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_ISAR0_EL1 , ID_ISAR1_EL1 , ID_ISAR2_EL1 , ID_ISAR3_EL1 , and ID_ISAR5_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers, see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: SWP_frac
fields.description: Indicates support for the memory system locking the bus for SWP or SWPB instructions.

values.label: SWP or SWPB instructions not implemented.

SWP or SWPB implemented but only in a uniprocessor context. SWP and SWPB do not guarantee whether memory accesses from other Requesters can come between the load memory access and the store memory access of the SWP or SWPB.

fields.name: PSR_M
fields.description: Indicates the implemented M-profile instructions to modify the PSRs.

values.label: None implemented.
Adds the M-profile forms of the CPS, MRS, and MSR instructions.

fields.name: SynchPrim_frac
fields.description: Used in conjunction with ID_ISAR3 .SynchPrim to indicate the implemented Synchronization Primitive instructions.

values.label: If SynchPrim == 0b0000 , no Synchronization Primitives implemented. If SynchPrim == 0b0001 , adds the LDREX and STREX instructions. If SynchPrim == 0b0010 , also adds the CLREX, LDREXB, LDREXH, STREXB, STREXH, LDREXD, and STREXD instructions.
If SynchPrim == 0b0001 , adds the LDREX, STREX, CLREX, LDREXB, LDREXH, STREXB, and STREXH instructions.

fields.name: Barrier
fields.description: Indicates the implemented Barrier instructions in the T32 and A32 instruction sets.

values.label: None implemented. Barrier operations are provided only as System instructions in the (coproc== 0b1111 ) encoding space.
Adds the DMB, DSB, and ISB barrier instructions.
None implemented.
Adds the SMC instruction.

fields.name: Writeback
fields.description: Indicates the support for Writeback addressing modes.

values.label: Basic support. Only the LDM, STM, PUSH, POP, SRS, and RFE instructions support writeback addressing modes. These instructions support all of their writeback addressing modes.
Adds support for all of the writeback addressing modes.

fields.name: WithShifts
fields.description: Indicates the support for instructions with shifts.

values.label: Nonzero shifts supported only in MOV and shift instructions.
Adds support for shifts of loads and stores over the range LSL 0-3.
As for 0b0001 , and adds support for other constant shift options, both on load/store and other instructions.
As for 0b0011 , and adds support for register-controlled shift options.
None implemented. No T variant instructions are implemented.
Adds the LDRBT, LDRT, STRBT, and STRT instructions.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the LDRHT, LDRSBT, LDRSHT, and STRHT instructions.

name: ID_ISAR5_EL1
description: Provides information about the instruction sets implemented by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_ISAR0_EL1 , ID_ISAR1_EL1 , ID_ISAR2_EL1 , ID_ISAR3_EL1 , and ID_ISAR4_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: VCMA
fields.description: Indicates AArch32 support for complex number addition and multiplication where numbers are stored in vectors.

values.label: The VCMLA and VCADD instructions are not implemented in AArch32.
The VCMLA and VCADD instructions are implemented in AArch32.

fields.name: RDM
fields.description: Indicates whether the VQRDMLAH and VQRDMLSH instructions are implemented in AArch32 state.

values.label: No VQRDMLAH and VQRDMLSH instructions implemented.
VQRDMLAH and VQRDMLSH instructions implemented.

fields.name: CRC32
fields.description: Indicates whether the CRC32 instructions CRC32B , CRC32H , CRC32W , CRC32CB , CRC32CH , and CRC32CW are implemented in AArch32 state.

values.label: CRC32 instructions are not implemented.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: SHA2
fields.description: Indicates whether the SHA2 instructions SHA256H , SHA256H2 , SHA256SU0 , and SHA256SU1 are implemented in AArch32 state.

values.label: No SHA2 instructions implemented.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: SHA1
fields.description: Indicates whether the SHA1 instructions SHA1C , SHA1P , SHA1M , SHA1H , SHA1SU0 , and SHA1SU1 are implemented in AArch32 state.

values.label: No SHA1 instructions implemented.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: AES
fields.description: Indicates whether the AES instructions are implemented in AArch32 state.

values.label: No AES instructions implemented.
AESE , AESD , AESMC , and AESIMC implemented.
As for 0b0001 , plus VMULL (polynomial) instructions operating on 64-bit data quantities.

fields.name: SEVL
fields.description: Indicates whether the SEVL instruction is implemented in AArch32 state.

values.label: SEVL is implemented as a NOP.
SEVL is implemented as Send Event Local.

name: ID_ISAR6_EL1
description: Provides information about the instruction sets implemented by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_ISAR0_EL1 , ID_ISAR1_EL1 , ID_ISAR2_EL1 , ID_ISAR3_EL1 , ID_ISAR4_EL1 and ID_ISAR5_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: CLRBHB
fields.description: Indicates support for the CLRBHB instruction in AArch32 state.

values.label: CLRBHB instruction is not implemented.
CLRBHB instruction is implemented.

fields.name: I8MM
fields.description: Indicates support for the following Advanced SIMD Int8 matrix multiplication instructions VSMMLA , VSUDOT , VUMMLA , VUSMMLA , and VUSDOT in AArch32 state.

values.label: Int8 matrix multiplication instructions are not implemented.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: BF16
fields.description: Indicates support for the following Advanced SIMD and floating-point BFloat16 instructions VCVT , VCVTB , VCVTT , VDOT , VFMAB , VFMAT , and VMMLA instructions with BF16 operand or result types in AArch32 state.

values.label: BFloat16 instructions are not implemented.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: SPECRES
fields.description: Indicates support for prediction invalidation instructions in AArch32 state.

values.label: Prediction invalidation instructions are not implemented.
CFPRCTX , DVPRCTX , and CPPRCTX instructions are implemented.
As 0b0001 , and COSPRCTX instruction is implemented.

fields.name: SB
fields.description: Indicates support for the SB instruction in AArch32 state.

values.label: SB instruction is not implemented.
SB instruction is implemented.

fields.name: FHM
fields.description: Indicates support for the following Advanced SIMD and floating-point VFMAL and VFMSL instructions in AArch32 state.

values.label: The specified instructions are not implemented.
The specified instructions are implemented.

fields.name: DP
fields.description: Indicates support for dot product instructions in AArch32 state.

values.label: Dot product instructions are not implemented.
VUDOT and VSDOT instructions are implemented.

fields.name: JSCVT
fields.description: Indicates support for the VJCVT instruction in AArch32 state.

values.label: The VJCVT instruction is not implemented.
The VJCVT instruction is implemented.

name: ID_MMFR0_EL1
description: Provides information about the implemented memory model and memory management support in AArch32 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: InnerShr
fields.description: Innermost Shareability. Indicates the innermost shareability domain implemented.

values.label: Implemented as Non-cacheable.
Implemented with hardware coherency support.
Shareability ignored.

fields.name: FCSE
fields.description: Indicates whether the implementation includes the FCSE.

values.label: Not supported.
Support for FCSE.

fields.name: AuxReg
fields.description: Auxiliary Registers. Indicates support for Auxiliary registers.

values.label: None supported.
Support for Auxiliary Control Register only.
Support for Auxiliary Fault Status Registers ( AIFSR and ADFSR ) and Auxiliary Control Register.

fields.name: TCM
fields.description: Indicates support for TCMs and associated DMAs.

values.label: Not supported.
Support is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .
Support for TCM only, Armv6 implementation.
Support for TCM and DMA, Armv6 implementation.

fields.name: ShareLvl
fields.description: Shareability Levels. Indicates the number of shareability levels implemented.

values.label: One level of shareability implemented.
Two levels of shareability implemented.

fields.name: OuterShr
fields.description: Outermost Shareability. Indicates the outermost shareability domain implemented.

values.label: Implemented as Non-cacheable.
Implemented with hardware coherency support.
Shareability ignored.
Not supported.
Support for IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED PMSA.
Support for PMSAv6, with a Cache Type Register implemented.
Support for PMSAv7, with support for memory subsections. Armv7-R profile.
Not supported.
Support for IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED VMSA.
Support for VMSAv6, with Cache and TLB Type Registers implemented.
Support for VMSAv7, with support for remapping and the Access flag. Armv7-A profile.
As for 0b0011 , and adds support for the PXN bit in the Short-descriptor translation table format descriptors.
As for 0b0100 , and adds support for the Long-descriptor translation table format.

name: ID_MMFR1_EL1
description: Provides information about the implemented memory model and memory management support in AArch32 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: BPred
fields.description: Branch Predictor. Indicates branch predictor management requirements.

values.label: No branch predictor, or no MMU present. Implies a fixed MPU configuration.

Branch predictor requires flushing on: Enabling or disabling a stage of address translation. Writing new data to instruction locations. Writing new mappings to the translation tables. Changes to the TTBR0 , TTBR1 , or TTBCR registers. Changes to the ContextID or ASID, or to the FCSE ProcessID if this is supported.

Branch predictor requires flushing on: Enabling or disabling a stage of address translation. Writing new data to instruction locations. Writing new mappings to the translation tables. Any change to the TTBR0 , TTBR1 , or TTBCR registers without a change to the corresponding ContextID or ASID, or FCSE ProcessID if this is supported.
Branch predictor requires flushing only on writing new data to instruction locations.
For execution correctness, branch predictor requires no flushing at any time.

fields.name: L1TstCln
fields.description: Level 1 cache Test and Clean. Indicates the supported Level 1 data cache test and clean operations, for Harvard or unified cache implementations.

values.label: None supported.
Supported Level 1 data cache test and clean operations are: Test and clean data cache.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Test, clean, and invalidate data cache.

fields.name: L1Uni
fields.description: Level 1 Unified cache. Indicates the supported entire Level 1 cache maintenance operations for a unified cache implementation.

values.label: None supported.
Supported entire Level 1 cache operations are: Invalidate cache, including branch predictor if appropriate. Invalidate branch predictor, if appropriate.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Clean cache, using a recursive model that uses the cache dirty status bit. Clean and invalidate cache, using a recursive model that uses the cache dirty status bit.

fields.name: L1Hvd
fields.description: Level 1 Harvard cache. Indicates the supported entire Level 1 cache maintenance operations for a Harvard cache implementation.

values.label: None supported.
Supported entire Level 1 cache operations are: Invalidate instruction cache, including branch predictor if appropriate. Invalidate branch predictor, if appropriate.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Invalidate data cache. Invalidate data cache and instruction cache, including branch predictor if appropriate.

As for 0b0010 , and adds: Clean data cache, using a recursive model that uses the cache dirty status bit. Clean and invalidate data cache, using a recursive model that uses the cache dirty status bit.

fields.name: L1UniSW
fields.description: Level 1 Unified cache by Set/Way. Indicates the supported Level 1 cache line maintenance operations by set/way, for a unified cache implementation.

values.label: None supported.
Supported Level 1 unified cache line maintenance operations by set/way are: Clean cache line by set/way.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Clean and invalidate cache line by set/way.
As for 0b0010 , and adds: Invalidate cache line by set/way.

fields.name: L1HvdSW
fields.description: Level 1 Harvard cache by Set/Way. Indicates the supported Level 1 cache line maintenance operations by set/way, for a Harvard cache implementation.

values.label: None supported.
Supported Level 1 Harvard cache line maintenance operations by set/way are: Clean data cache line by set/way. Clean and invalidate data cache line by set/way.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Invalidate data cache line by set/way.
As for 0b0010 , and adds: Invalidate instruction cache line by set/way.

fields.name: L1UniVA
fields.description: Level 1 Unified cache by Virtual Address. Indicates the supported Level 1 cache line maintenance operations by VA, for a unified cache implementation.

values.label: None supported.
Supported Level 1 unified cache line maintenance operations by VA are: Clean cache line by VA. Invalidate cache line by VA. Clean and invalidate cache line by VA.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Invalidate branch predictor by VA, if branch predictor is implemented.

fields.name: L1HvdVA
fields.description: Level 1 Harvard cache by Virtual Address. Indicates the supported Level 1 cache line maintenance operations by VA, for a Harvard cache implementation.

values.label: None supported.

Supported Level 1 Harvard cache line maintenance operations by VA are: Clean data cache line by VA. Invalidate data cache line by VA. Clean and invalidate data cache line by VA. Clean instruction cache line by VA.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Invalidate branch predictor by VA, if branch predictor is implemented.

name: ID_MMFR2_EL1
description: Provides information about the implemented memory model and memory management support in AArch32 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: HWAccFlg
fields.description: Hardware Access Flag. In earlier versions of the Arm Architecture, this field indicates support for a Hardware Access flag, as part of the VMSAv7 implementation.

values.label: Not supported.
Support for VMSAv7 Access flag, updated in hardware.

fields.name: WFIStall
fields.description: Wait For Interrupt Stall. Indicates the support for Wait For Interrupt (WFI) stalling.

values.label: Not supported.
Support for WFI stalling.

fields.name: MemBarr
fields.description: Memory Barrier. Indicates the supported memory barrier System instructions in the (coproc== 0b1111 ) encoding space:

values.label: None supported.
Supported memory barrier System instructions are: Data Synchronization Barrier (DSB).
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Instruction Synchronization Barrier (ISB). Data Memory Barrier (DMB).

fields.name: UniTLB
fields.description: Unified TLB. Indicates the supported TLB maintenance operations, for a unified TLB implementation.

values.label: Not supported.
Supported unified TLB maintenance operations are: Invalidate all entries in the TLB. Invalidate TLB entry by VA.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Invalidate TLB entries by ASID match.
As for 0b0010 , and adds: Invalidate instruction TLB and data TLB entries by VA All ASID. This is a shared unified TLB operation.
As for 0b0011 , and adds: Invalidate Hyp mode unified TLB entry by VA. Invalidate entire Non-secure PL1&0 unified TLB. Invalidate entire Hyp mode unified TLB.
As for 0b0100 , and adds the following operations: TLBIMVALIS , TLBIMVAALIS , TLBIMVALHIS , TLBIMVAL , TLBIMVAAL , TLBIMVALH .
As for 0b0101 , and adds the following operations: TLBIIPAS2IS , TLBIIPAS2LIS , TLBIIPAS2 , TLBIIPAS2L .

fields.name: HvdTLB
fields.description: If the Unified TLB field (UniTLB, bits [19:16]) is not 0000, then the meaning of this field is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED . Arm deprecates the use of this field by software.

fields.name: L1HvdRng
fields.description: Level 1 Harvard cache Range. Indicates the supported Level 1 cache maintenance range operations, for a Harvard cache implementation.

values.label: Not supported.

Supported Level 1 Harvard cache maintenance range operations are: Invalidate data cache range by VA. Invalidate instruction cache range by VA. Clean data cache range by VA. Clean and invalidate data cache range by VA.

fields.name: L1HvdBG
fields.description: Level 1 Harvard cache Background fetch. Indicates the supported Level 1 cache background fetch operations, for a Harvard cache implementation. When supported, background fetch operations are non-blocking operations.

values.label: Not supported.
Supported Level 1 Harvard cache background fetch operations are: Fetch instruction cache range by VA. Fetch data cache range by VA.

fields.name: L1HvdFG
fields.description: Level 1 Harvard cache Foreground fetch. Indicates the supported Level 1 cache foreground fetch operations, for a Harvard cache implementation. When supported, foreground fetch operations are blocking operations.

values.label: Not supported.
Supported Level 1 Harvard cache foreground fetch operations are: Fetch instruction cache range by VA. Fetch data cache range by VA.

name: ID_MMFR3_EL1
description: Provides information about the implemented memory model and memory management support in AArch32 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: Supersec
fields.description: Supersections. On a VMSA implementation, indicates whether Supersections are supported.

values.label: Supersections supported.
Supersections not supported.

fields.name: CMemSz
fields.description: Cached Memory Size. Indicates the physical memory size supported by the caches.

values.label: 4GB, corresponding to a 32-bit physical address range.
64GB, corresponding to a 36-bit physical address range.
1TB or more, corresponding to a 40-bit or larger physical address range.

fields.name: CohWalk
fields.description: Coherent Walk. Indicates whether Translation table updates require a clean to the Point of Unification.:

values.label: Updates to the translation tables require a clean to the Point of Unification to ensure visibility by subsequent translation table walks.
Updates to the translation tables do not require a clean to the Point of Unification to ensure visibility by subsequent translation table walks.

fields.name: PAN
fields.description: Privileged Access Never. Indicates support for the PAN bit in CPSR , SPSR , and DSPSR in AArch32 state.

values.label: PAN not supported.
PAN supported.
PAN supported and ATS1CPRP and ATS1CPWP instructions supported.

fields.name: MaintBcst
fields.description: Maintenance Broadcast. Indicates whether Cache, TLB, and branch predictor operations are broadcast.

values.label: Cache, TLB, and branch predictor operations only affect local structures.
Cache and branch predictor operations affect structures according to shareability and defined behavior of instructions. TLB operations only affect local structures.
Cache, TLB, and branch predictor operations affect structures according to shareability and defined behavior of instructions.

fields.name: BPMaint
fields.description: Branch Predictor Maintenance. Indicates the supported branch predictor maintenance operations in an implementation with hierarchical cache maintenance operations.

values.label: None supported.
Supported branch predictor maintenance operations are: Invalidate all branch predictors.
As for 0b0001 , and adds: Invalidate branch predictors by VA.

fields.name: CMaintSW
fields.description: Cache Maintenance by Set/Way. Indicates the supported cache maintenance operations by set/way, in an implementation with hierarchical caches.

values.label: None supported.
Supported hierarchical cache maintenance instructions by set/way are: Invalidate data cache by set/way. Clean data cache by set/way. Clean and invalidate data cache by set/way.

fields.name: CMaintVA
fields.description: Cache Maintenance by Virtual Address. Indicates the supported cache maintenance operations by VA, in an implementation with hierarchical caches.

values.label: None supported.

Supported hierarchical cache maintenance operations by VA are: Invalidate data cache by VA. Clean data cache by VA. Clean and invalidate data cache by VA. Invalidate instruction cache by VA. Invalidate all instruction cache entries.

name: ID_MMFR4_EL1
description: Provides information about the implemented memory model and memory management support in AArch32 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: EVT
fields.description: Enhanced Virtualization Traps. If EL2 is implemented, indicates support for the HCR2 .{TTLBIS, TOCU, TICAB, TID4} traps.

values.label: HCR2 .{TTLBIS, TOCU, TICAB, TID4} traps are not supported.
HCR2 .{TOCU, TICAB, TID4} traps are supported.
As 0b0001 , and HCR2 .TTLBIS trap is supported.

fields.name: CCIDX
fields.description: Support for use of the revised CCSIDR format and the presence of the CCSIDR2 is indicated.

values.label: 32-bit format implemented for all levels of the CCSIDR, and the CCSIDR2 register is not implemented.
64-bit format implemented for all levels of the CCSIDR, and the CCSIDR2 register is implemented.

fields.name: LSM
fields.description: Indicates support for LSMAOE and nTLSMD bits in HSCTLR and SCTLR .

values.label: LSMAOE and nTLSMD bits not supported.
LSMAOE and nTLSMD bits supported.

fields.name: HPDS
fields.description: Hierarchical permission disables bits in translation tables.

values.label: Disabling of hierarchical controls not supported.
Supports disabling of hierarchical controls using the TTBCR2 .HPD0, TTBCR2 .HPD1, and HTCR .HPD bits.
As for value 0b0001 , and adds possible hardware allocation of bits[62:59] of the Translation table descriptors from the final lookup level for IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED use.
Common not Private translations not supported.
Common not Private translations supported.

fields.name: XNX
fields.description: Support for execute-never control distinction by Exception level at stage 2.

values.label: Distinction between EL0 and EL1 execute-never control at stage 2 not supported.
Distinction between EL0 and EL1 execute-never control at stage 2 supported.

fields.name: AC2
fields.description: Indicates the extension of the ACTLR and HACTLR registers using ACTLR2 and HACTLR2 .

values.label: ACTLR2 and HACTLR2 are not implemented.
ACTLR2 and HACTLR2 are implemented.

fields.name: SpecSEI
fields.description: Describes whether the PE can generate SError exceptions from speculative reads of memory, including speculative instruction fetches.

values.label: The PE never generates an SError exception due to an External abort on a speculative read.
The PE might generate an SError exception due to an External abort on a speculative read.

name: ID_MMFR5_EL1
description: Provides information about the implemented memory model and memory management support in AArch32 state. For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers, see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: nTLBPA
fields.description: Indicates support for intermediate caching of translation table walks.

values.label: The intermediate caching of translation table walks might include non-coherent physical translation caches.
The intermediate caching of translation table walks does not include non-coherent physical translation caches.

fields.name: ETS
fields.description: Indicates support for Enhanced Translation Synchronization.

values.label: Enhanced Translation Synchronization is not supported.
Enhanced Translation Synchronization is not supported.
FEAT_ETS2 is implemented.
FEAT_ETS3 is implemented.

name: ID_PFR0_EL1
description: Gives top-level information about the instruction sets supported by the PE in AArch32 state. Must be interpreted with ID_PFR1_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers, see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

values.label: No RAS Extension.
Support for the Reliability, Availability, and Serviceability Extension is implemented. The ESB instruction and the Error synchronization event are supported.

As 0b0001 , and adds support for additional ERXMISC<m> System registers. Error records accessed through System registers conform to RAS System Architecture v1.1, which includes simplifications to ERR<n>STATUS and support for the optional RAS Timestamp Extension.
As 0b0010 , and requires that error records accessed through System registers conform to either RAS System Architecture v1.1 or RAS System Architecture v2.
AArch32 does not guarantee constant execution time of any instructions.
AArch32 provides the PSTATE.DIT mechanism to guarantee constant execution time of certain instructions.

fields.name: AMU
fields.description: Indicates support for Activity Monitors Extension.

values.label: Activity Monitors Extension is not implemented.
FEAT_AMUv1 is implemented.
FEAT_AMUv1p1 is implemented. As 0b0001 and adds support for virtualization of the activity monitor event counters.

fields.name: CSV2
fields.description: Speculative use of out of context branch targets.

values.label: The implementation does not disclose whether FEAT_CSV2 is implemented.
FEAT_CSV2 is implemented, but FEAT_CSV2_1p1 is not implemented.
FEAT_CSV2_1p1 is implemented.
Not implemented.
T32EE instruction set implemented.
Not implemented.
Jazelle extension implemented, without clearing of JOSCR .CV on exception entry.
Jazelle extension implemented, with clearing of JOSCR .CV on exception entry.
T32 instruction set not implemented.

T32 encodings before the introduction of Thumb-2 technology implemented: All instructions are 16-bit. A BL or BLX is a pair of 16-bit instructions. 32-bit instructions other than BL and BLX cannot be encoded.
T32 encodings after the introduction of Thumb-2 technology implemented, for all 16-bit and 32-bit T32 basic instructions.
A32 instruction set not implemented.
A32 instruction set implemented.

name: ID_PFR1_EL1
description: Gives information about the AArch32 programmers' model. Must be interpreted with ID_PFR0_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

values.label: GIC CPU interface system registers not implemented.
System register interface to versions 3.0 and 4.0 of the GIC CPU interface is supported.
System register interface to version 4.1 of the GIC CPU interface is supported.

fields.name: Virt_frac
fields.description: Virtualization fractional field. When the Virtualization field is 0b0000 , determines the support for Virtualization Extensions.

values.label: No Virtualization Extensions are implemented.

The following Virtualization Extensions are implemented: The SCR .SIF bit, if EL3 is implemented. The modifications to the SCR .AW and SCR .FW bits described in the Virtualization Extensions, if EL3 is implemented. The MSR (banked register) and MRS (banked register) instructions. The ERET instruction.

fields.name: Sec_frac
fields.description: Security fractional field. When the Security field is 0b0000 , determines the support for Security Extensions.

values.label: No Security Extensions are implemented.
The following Security Extensions are implemented: The VBAR register. The TTBCR .PD0 and TTBCR .PD1 bits.
As for 0b0001 , plus the ability to access Secure or Non-secure physical memory is supported.
Generic Timer is not implemented.
Generic Timer is implemented.
Generic Timer is implemented, and also includes support for CNTHCTL .EVNTIS and CNTKCTL .EVNTIS fields, and CNTPCTSS and CNTVCTSS counter views.
EL2, Hyp mode, and the HVC instruction not implemented.
EL2, Hyp mode, the HVC instruction, and all the features described by Virt_frac == 0b0001 implemented.
Not supported.
Support for two-stack programmers' model.
EL3, Monitor mode, and the SMC instruction not implemented.
EL3, Monitor mode, the SMC instruction, and all the features described by Sec_frac == 0b0001 implemented.
As for 0b0001 , and adds the ability to set the NSACR .RFR bit. Not permitted in Armv8 as the NSACR .RFR bit is RES0 .

fields.name: ProgMod
fields.description: Support for the standard programmers' model for Armv4 and later. Model must support User, FIQ, IRQ, Supervisor, Abort, Undefined, and System modes.

values.label: Not supported.
Supported.

name: ID_PFR2_EL1
description: Gives information about the AArch32 programmers' model. Must be interpreted with ID_PFR0_EL1 and ID_PFR1_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

values.label: If ID_PFR0_EL1 .RAS == 0b0001 , Support for the Reliability, Availability, and Serviceability Extension is implemented.

If ID_PFR0_EL1 .RAS == 0b0001 , as 0b0000 and adds support for additional ERXMISC<m> System registers. Error records accessed through System registers conform to RAS System Architecture v1.1, which includes simplifications to ERR<n>STATUS and support for the optional RAS Timestamp Extension.

fields.name: SSBS
fields.description: Speculative Store Bypassing controls in AArch64 state.

values.label: AArch32 provides no mechanism to control the use of Speculative Store Bypassing.
AArch32 provides the PSTATE.SSBS mechanism to mark regions that are Speculative Store Bypass Safe.

This PE does not disclose whether data loaded under speculation with a permission or domain fault can be used to form an address or generate condition codes or SVE predicate values to be used by other instructions in the speculative sequence.

Data loaded under speculation with a permission or domain fault cannot be used to form an address, generate condition codes, or generate SVE predicate values to be used by other instructions in the speculative sequence. The execution timing of any other instructions in the speculative sequence is not a function of the data loaded under speculation.

name: IFSR32_EL2
description: Allows access to the AArch32 IFSR register from AArch64 state only. Its value has no effect on execution in AArch64 state.

fields.name: FnV
fields.description: FAR not Valid, for a synchronous External abort other than a synchronous External abort on a translation table walk.

values.label: IFAR is valid.
IFAR is not valid, and holds an UNKNOWN value.

fields.name: ExT
fields.description: External abort type. This bit can be used to provide an IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED classification of External aborts. In an implementation that does not provide any classification of External aborts, this bit is RES0 . For aborts other than External aborts this bit always returns 0.

fields.name: FS
fields.description: Fault Status bits. Bits [10] and [3:0] are interpreted together.

values.label: PC alignment fault.
Debug exception.
Access flag fault, level 1.
Translation fault, level 1.
Access flag fault, level 2.
Translation fault, level 2.
Synchronous External abort, not on translation table walk.
Domain fault, level 1.
Domain fault, level 2.
Synchronous External abort, on translation table walk, level 1.
Permission fault, level 1.
Synchronous External abort, on translation table walk, level 2.
Permission fault, level 2.
TLB conflict abort.
IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED fault (Lockdown fault).
Synchronous parity or ECC error on memory access, not on translation table walk.
Synchronous parity or ECC error on translation table walk, level 1.
Synchronous parity or ECC error on translation table walk, level 2.

fields.name: LPAE
fields.description: On taking a Data Abort exception, this bit is set as follows:

values.label: Using the Short-descriptor translation table formats.
Using the Long-descriptor translation table formats.

fields.name: FS[3:0]
fields.description: This field is bits[3:0] of FS[4:0]. See FS[4] for the field description.

name: ISR_EL1
description: Shows the pending status of IRQ and FIQ interrupts and SError exceptions. When FEAT_NMI is implemented, also shows whether a pending IRQ or FIQ interrupt has Superpriority.

fields.name: IS
fields.description: IRQ with Superpriority pending bit. Indicates whether an IRQ interrupt with Superpriority is pending.

values.label: No pending IRQ with Superpriority.
An IRQ interrupt with Superpriority is pending.

fields.name: FS
fields.description: FIQ with Superpriority pending bit. Indicates whether an FIQ interrupt with Superpriority is pending.

values.label: No pending FIQ with Superpriority.
An FIQ interrupt with Superpriority is pending.

fields.name: A
fields.description: SError exception pending bit. Indicates whether an SError exception is pending.

values.label: No pending SError.
An SError exception is pending.

fields.name: I
fields.description: IRQ pending bit. Indicates whether an IRQ interrupt is pending.

values.label: No pending IRQ.
An IRQ interrupt is pending.

fields.name: F
fields.description: FIQ pending bit. Indicates whether an FIQ interrupt is pending.

values.label: No pending FIQ.
An FIQ interrupt is pending.

name: LORC_EL1
description: Enables and disables LORegions, and selects the current LORegion descriptor.

fields.name: DS
fields.description: Descriptor Select. Selects the current LORegion descriptor accessed by LORSA_EL1 , LOREA_EL1 , and LORN_EL1 . If this field points to an LORegion descriptor that is not supported by an implementation, then the registers LORN_EL1 , LOREA_EL1 , and LORSA_EL1 are RES0 .

fields.name: EN
fields.description: Enable. Indicates whether LORegions are enabled.

values.label: Disabled. Memory accesses do not match any LORegions.
Enabled. Memory accesses may match a LORegion.

name: LOREA_EL1
description: Holds the physical address of the end of the LORegion described in the current LORegion descriptor selected by LORC_EL1 .DS.

fields.name: EA[55:52]
fields.description: Extension to EA[47:16]. For more information, see EA[47:16].

fields.name: EA[51:48]
fields.description: Extension to EA[47:16]. For more information, see EA[47:16].

fields.name: EA[47:16]
fields.description: Bits [47:16] of the end physical address of an LORegion described in the current LORegion descriptor selected by LORC_EL1 .DS. Bits[15:0] of this address are 0xFFFF . For implementations with fewer than 48 bits, the upper bits of this field are RES0 . When FEAT_LPA is implemented and 52-bit addresses are in use, EA[51:48] form bits [51:48] of the end physical address of the LORegion. Otherwise, when 52-bit addresses are not in use, EA[51:48] is RES0 .

name: LORID_EL1
description: Indicates the number of LORegions and LORegion descriptors supported by the PE.

fields.name: RL
fields.description: Indicates support for matching Realm PA space addresses to LORegions.

values.label: LORegions do not match Realm PA space addresses.
LORegions can be configured to match Realm and Non-secure PA space addresses.

fields.name: LD
fields.description: Number of LORegion descriptors supported by the PE. This is an 8-bit binary number.

values.label: The number of LORegions descriptors supported.

fields.name: LR
fields.description: Number of LORegions supported by the PE. This is an 8-bit binary number. If LORID_EL1 indicates that no LORegions are implemented, then LoadLOAcquire and StoreLORelease will behave as LoadAcquire and StoreRelease.

values.label: The number of LORegions supported.

name: LORN_EL1
description: Holds the number of the LORegion described in the current LORegion descriptor selected by LORC_EL1 .DS.

fields.name: Num
fields.description: Number of the LORegion described in the current LORegion descriptor selected by LORC_EL1 .DS. The maximum number of LORegions supported by the PE is 256. If the maximum number is less than 256, then bits[8:N] are RES0 , where N is (Log 2 (Number of LORegions supported by the PE)). If this field points to a LORegion that is not supported by the PE, then the current LORegion descriptor does not match any LORegion.

name: LORSA_EL1
description: Indicates whether the current LORegion descriptor selected by LORC_EL1 .DS is enabled, and holds the physical address of the start of the LORegion.

fields.name: RL
fields.description: Configures the PA space that can match the LORegion.

values.label: Only Non-secure PA space addresses can match this region.
Non-secure and Realm PA space addresses can match this region.

fields.name: Valid
fields.description: Indicates whether the current LORegion descriptor is enabled.

values.label: LORegion descriptor is disabled.
LORegion descriptor is enabled.

name: MAIR2_EL1
description: Provides the memory attribute encodings corresponding to the possible AttrIndx values in a VMSAv8-64 or VMSAv9-128 translation table entry for stage 1 translations at EL1.

fields.name: Attr<n>
fields.description:
Memory Attribute encoding. When stage 1 Attributes Index Extension is enabled and AttrIndx[3] in a VMSAv8-64 or VMSAv9-128 translation table entry is 1, AttrIndx[2:0] gives the value of <n> in Attr<n>. When stage 1 Attributes Index Extension is enabled and AttrIndx[3] in a VMSAv8-64 or VMSAv9-128 translation table entry is 0, see MAIR_EL1.Attr Attr is encoded as follows: Attr Meaning 0b0000dd00 Device memory.
See encoding of 'dd' for the type of Device memory. 0b0000dd01 If FEAT_XS is implemented:
Device memory with the XS attribute set to 0.
See encoding of 'dd' for the type of Device memory.
Otherwise, UNPREDICTABLE . 0b0000dd1x UNPREDICTABLE . 0booooiiii where oooo != 0000
and iiii != 0000 Normal memory. See encoding of 'oooo' and 'iiii' for the
type of Normal memory. 0b01000000 If FEAT_XS is implemented:
Normal Inner Non-cacheable, Outer Non-cacheable memory
with the XS attribute set to 0.
Otherwise, UNPREDICTABLE . 0b10100000 If FEAT_XS is implemented:
Normal Inner Write-through Cacheable, Outer Write-through Cacheable,
Read-Allocate, No-Write Allocate, Non-transient memory
with the XS attribute set to 0.
Otherwise, UNPREDICTABLE . 0b11110000 If FEAT_MTE2 is implemented:
Tagged Normal Inner Write-Back, Outer Write-Back,
Read-Allocate, Write-Allocate Non-transient memory.
Otherwise, UNPREDICTABLE . 0bxxxx0000 where xxxx != 0000
and xxxx != 0100
and xxxx != 1010
and xxxx != 1111 UNPREDICTABLE . dd is encoded as follows: 'dd' Meaning 0b00 Device-nGnRnE memory. 0b01 Device-nGnRE memory. 0b10 Device-nGRE memory. 0b11 Device-GRE memory. oooo is encoded as follows: 'oooo' Meaning 0b0000 See encoding of Attr. 0b00RW where RW != 00 Normal memory, Outer Write-Through Transient. 0b0100 Normal memory, Outer Non-cacheable. 0b01RW where RW != 00 Normal memory, Outer Write-Back Transient. 0b10RW Normal memory, Outer Write-Through Non-transient. 0b11RW Normal memory, Outer Write-Back Non-transient. R encodes the Outer Read-Allocate policy and W encodes the Outer Write-Allocate policy. iiii is encoded as follows: 'iiii' Meaning 0b0000 See encoding of Attr. 0b00RW where RW != 00 Normal memory, Inner Write-Through Transient. 0b0100 Normal memory, Inner Non-cacheable. 0b01RW where RW != 00 Normal memory, Inner Write-Back Transient. 0b10RW Normal memory, Inner Write-Through Non-transient. 0b11RW Normal memory, Inner Write-Back Non-transient. R encodes the Inner Read-Allocate policy and W encodes the Inner Write-Allocate policy. In oooo and iiii , R and W are encoded as follows: 'R' or 'W' Meaning 0b0 No Allocate. 0b1 Allocate. When FEAT_XS is implemented, stage 1 Inner Write-Back Cacheable, Outer Write-Back Cacheable memory types have the XS attribute set to 0.
