name: MDCCINT_EL1
description: Enables interrupt requests to be signaled based on the DCC status flags.

fields.name: RX
fields.description: DCC interrupt request enable control for DTRRX. Enables a common COMMIRQ interrupt request to be signaled based on the DCC status flags.

values.label: No interrupt request generated by DTRRX.
Interrupt request will be generated on RXfull == 1.

fields.name: TX
fields.description: DCC interrupt request enable control for DTRTX. Enables a common COMMIRQ interrupt request to be signaled based on the DCC status flags.

values.label: No interrupt request generated by DTRTX.
Interrupt request will be generated on TXfull == 0.

name: MDCCSR_EL0
description: Read-only register containing control status flags for the DCC.

fields.name: RXfull
fields.description: DTRRX full. Read-only view of the equivalent bit in the EDSCR .

fields.name: TXfull
fields.description: DTRTX full. Read-only view of the equivalent bit in the EDSCR .

name: MDCR_EL2
description: Provides EL2 configuration options for self-hosted debug and the Performance Monitors Extension.

fields.name: EnSTEPOP
fields.description: If EL2 is not implemented or EL2 is disabled in the current Security state, then the Effective value of this field is 0b1 , other than for a direct read of the register.

values.label: Execution from MDSTEPOP_EL1 is disabled.
Execution from MDSTEPOP_EL1 is not disabled by this control.

fields.name: EBWE
fields.description: Extended Breakpoint and Watchpoint Enable. Enables use of additional breakpoints or watchpoints.

values.label: The Effective value of MDSCR_EL1 .EMBWE is 0. The Effective value of MDSELR_EL1 .BANK is zero at EL2.
The Effective values of MDSCR_EL1 .EMBWE and MDSELR_EL1 .BANK are not affected by this field.

fields.name: PMEE
fields.description: Performance Monitors Exception Enable. Controls the generation of the PMUIRQ signal and the PMU Profiling exception at EL0, EL1, and EL2.

values.label: The PMUIRQ signal is asserted on a PMU overflow, and the PMU Profiling exception is disabled.
The PMUIRQ signal and the PMU Profiling exception are both controlled by PMECR_EL1 .PMEE.
The PMUIRQ signal is deasserted, and the PMU Profiling exception is enabled.

fields.name: HPMFZS
fields.description: Hyp Performance Monitors Freeze-on-SPE event. Stop counters when PMBLIMITR_EL1 .{PMFZ,E} is {1,1} and profiling is stopped.

values.label: Do not freeze on a Statistical Profiling Buffer Management event.
Affected counters do not count following a Statistical Profiling Buffer Management event.

fields.name: PMSSE
fields.description: Performance Monitors Snapshot Enable. Controls the generation of Capture events.

values.label: Capture events are disabled.
Capture events are controlled by PMECR_EL1 .SSE.
Capture events are enabled and prohibited.
Capture events are enabled and allowed.

fields.name: HPMFZO
fields.description: Hyp Performance Monitors Freeze-on-overflow. Stop event counters on overflow.

values.label: Do not freeze on overflow.

Affected counters do not count when all of the following are true for any event counter PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 in the second range: PMOVSCLR_EL0 [m] is 1. Either FEAT_SEBEP is not implemented or PMEVTYPER<m>_EL0 .SYNC is 0.

fields.name: MTPME
fields.description: Multi-threaded PMU Enable. Enables use of the PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .MT bits.

values.label: FEAT_MTPMU is disabled. The Effective value of PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .MT is 0.
PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .MT bits not affected by this field.

fields.name: TDCC
fields.description: Trap DCC. Traps use of the Debug Comms Channel at EL1 and EL0 to EL2.

values.label: This control does not cause any register accesses to be trapped.

If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, accesses to the DCC registers at EL1 and EL0 generate a Trap exception to EL2, unless the access also generates a higher priority exception. Traps on the DCC data transfer registers are ignored when the PE is in Debug state.

fields.name: HLP
fields.description: Hypervisor Long Event Counter Enable. Determines which event counter bit generates an overflow recorded by PMOVSR [n].

values.label: Affected counters overflow on increment that causes unsigned overflow of PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 [31:0].
Affected counters overflow on increment that causes unsigned overflow of PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 [63:0].

fields.name: E2TB
fields.description: EL2 Trace Buffer. If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the trace buffer owning Security state, then this field controls the trace buffer owning translation regime. If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, then this field controls access to trace buffer System registers from EL1.

values.label: If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the trace buffer owning Security state, then the trace buffer owning Exception level is EL2. Otherwise, the trace buffer owning Exception level is EL1 and, if TraceBufferEnabled() == TRUE, tracing is prohibited at EL2. If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, then accesses to trace buffer System registers at EL1 are trapped to EL2, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.

Trace buffer owning Exception level is EL1. If TraceBufferEnabled() == TRUE, then tracing is prohibited at EL2. If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, then accesses to trace buffer System registers at EL1 are trapped to EL2, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.
Trace buffer owning Exception level is EL1. If TraceBufferEnabled() == TRUE, then tracing is prohibited at EL2. Accesses to trace buffer System registers at EL1 are not trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: HCCD
fields.description: Hypervisor Cycle Counter Disable. Prohibits PMCCNTR_EL0 from counting at EL2.

values.label: Cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is not affected by this mechanism.
Cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is prohibited at EL2.

fields.name: TTRF
fields.description: Traps use of the Trace Filter Control registers at EL1 to EL2, as follows: Access to TRFCR_EL1 is trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . Access to TRFCR is trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 .

values.label: Accesses to the specified registers at EL1 are not affected by this control.
Accesses to the specified registers at EL1 generate a trap exception to EL2 when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state.

fields.name: HPMD
fields.description: Guest Performance Monitors Disable. Controls PMU operation at EL2.

values.label: Counters are not affected by this mechanism.
Affected counters are prohibited from counting at EL2. If PMCR_EL0 .DP is 1, then PMCCNTR_EL0 is disabled at EL2. Otherwise, PMCCNTR_EL0 is not affected by this mechanism.

fields.name: HPMD
fields.description: Guest Performance Monitors Disable. Controls PMU operation at EL2 when ExternalSecureNoninvasiveDebugEnabled() is FALSE.

values.label: Counters are not affected by this mechanism.

If ExternalSecureNoninvasiveDebugEnabled() is FALSE, then all the following apply: Affected event counters are prohibited from counting at EL2. If PMCR_EL0 .DP is 1, then PMCCNTR_EL0 is disabled at EL2. Otherwise, PMCCNTR_EL0 is not affected by this mechanism.

fields.name: EnSPM
fields.description: Enable access to System PMU registers. When disabled, accesses to System PMU registers generate a trap to EL2.

values.label: Accesses of the specified System PMU registers at EL1 and EL0 are trapped to EL2, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
Accesses of the specified System PMU registers are not trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: TPMS
fields.description: Trap Performance Monitor Sampling. Enables a trap to EL2 on accesses of SPE registers.

values.label: Accesses of the specified SPE registers are not trapped by this mechanism.
Accesses of the specified SPE registers at EL1 are trapped to EL2, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.

fields.name: E2PB
fields.description: EL2 Profiling Buffer. If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Profiling Buffer owning Security state, then this field controls the Profiling Buffer owning translation regime. If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, then this field controls access to Profiling Buffer System registers from EL1.

values.label: If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Profiling Buffer owning Security state, then the Profiling Buffer owning Exception level is EL2. Otherwise, the Profiling Buffer owning Exception level is EL1 and, Profiling is prohibited at EL2. If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, then accesses to Profiling Buffer System registers at EL1 are trapped to EL2, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.

Profiling Buffer owning Exception level is EL1. Profiling is prohibited at EL2. If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, then accesses to Profiling Buffer System registers at EL1 are trapped to EL2, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.
Profiling Buffer owning Exception level is EL1. Profiling is prohibited at EL2. Accesses to Profiling Buffer System registers at EL1 are not trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: TDRA
fields.description: Trap Debug ROM Address register access. Traps System register accesses to the Debug ROM registers to EL2 when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state as follows: If EL1 is using AArch64, accesses to MDRAR_EL1 are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . If EL0 or EL1 is using AArch32 state, MRC or MCR accesses to the following registers are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x05 and MRRC or MCRR accesses are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x0C : DBGDRAR , DBGDSAR .

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

EL0 and EL1 System register accesses to the Debug ROM registers are trapped to EL2 when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state, unless it is trapped by the following: DBGDSCRext .UDCCdis. MDSCR_EL1 .TDCC.

fields.name: TDOSA
fields.description: Trap debug OS-related register access. Traps EL1 System register accesses to the powerdown debug registers to EL2, from both Execution states as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to the following registers are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : OSLAR_EL1 , OSLSR_EL1 , OSDLR_EL1 , and DBGPRCR_EL1 . Any IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED register with similar functionality that the implementation specifies as trapped by this bit. In AArch32 state, accesses to the following registers are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x05 : DBGOSLSR , DBGOSLAR , DBGOSDLR , and DBGPRCR . Any IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED register with similar functionality that the implementation specifies as trapped by this bit.

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
EL1 System register accesses to the powerdown debug registers are trapped to EL2 when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state.

fields.name: TDOSA
fields.description: Trap debug OS-related register access. Traps EL1 System register accesses to the powerdown debug registers to EL2, from both Execution states as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to the following registers are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : OSLAR_EL1 , OSLSR_EL1 , and DBGPRCR_EL1 . Any IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED register with similar functionality that the implementation specifies as trapped by this bit. In AArch32 state, accesses to the following registers are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x05 : DBGOSLSR , DBGOSLAR , and DBGPRCR . Any IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED register with similar functionality that the implementation specifies as trapped by this bit. It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether accesses to OSDLR_EL1 are trapped. It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether accesses to DBGOSDLR are trapped.

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
EL1 System register accesses to the powerdown debug registers are trapped to EL2 when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state.

fields.name: TDA
fields.description: Trap accesses of debug System registers. Enables a trap to EL2 on accesses of debug System registers.

values.label: Accesses of the specified debug System registers are not trapped by this mechanism.
Accesses of the specified debug System registers at EL1 and EL0 are trapped to EL2, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.

fields.name: TDE
fields.description: Trap Debug Exceptions. Controls routing of Debug exceptions, and defines the debug target Exception level, EL D .

values.label: The debug target Exception level is EL1.

If EL2 is enabled for the current Effective value of SCR_EL3 .NS, the debug target Exception level is EL2, otherwise the debug target Exception level is EL1. The MDCR_EL2.{TDRA, TDOSA, TDA} fields are treated as being 1 for all purposes other than returning the result of a direct read of the register.
Affected counters are disabled and do not count.
Affected counters are enabled by PMCNTENSET_EL0 .

fields.name: TPM
fields.description: Trap accesses of PMU registers. Enables a trap to EL2 on accesses of PMU registers.

values.label: Accesses of the specified PMU registers are not trapped by this mechanism.
Accesses of the specified PMU registers at EL1 and EL0 are trapped to EL2, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.

fields.name: TPMCR
fields.description: Trap PMCR_EL0 or PMCR accesses. Traps EL0 and EL1 accesses to EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state, as follows: In AArch64 state, accesses to PMCR_EL0 are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . In AArch32 state, accesses to PMCR are trapped to EL2, reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 .

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
EL0 and EL1 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL2 when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state, unless they are trapped by the following: PMUSERENR .EN. PMUSERENR_EL0 .EN.

fields.name: HPMN
fields.description: Defines the number of event counters PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 and, if FEAT_PMUv3_SS is implemented, event counter snapshot registers PMEVCNTSVR<n>_EL1 , that are accessible from EL1 and, if permitted, from EL0.

name: MDCR_EL3
description: Provides EL3 configuration options for self-hosted debug and the Performance Monitors Extension.

fields.name: EnPMS4
fields.description: Enable access to SPE registers. When disabled, accesses to SPE registers generate a trap to EL3.

values.label: Accesses of the specified SPE registers at EL2 and EL1 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
Accesses of the specified SPE registers are not trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: TRBEE
fields.description: Trace buffer management event Exception Enable.

values.label: Disabled. TRBE Profiling exceptions for all Exception levels are disabled. All of the following apply: No trace buffer management events are recorded in TRBSR_EL3 . TRBE Profiling exceptions are not generated. TRBSR_EL1 .IRQ drives the interrupt request signal TRBIRQ . Accesses to TRBSR_EL2 at EL2 are trapped to EL3. Accesses to TRBSR_EL1 at EL1 ignore the value of HCR_EL2 .NV1 and accesses to TRBSR_EL1 at EL2 ignore the value of HCR_EL2 .E2H.

Delegated. TRBE Profiling exceptions for EL3 are disabled, but might be enabled for EL2 or EL1 by TRFCR_EL2 .EE or TRFCR_EL1 .EE. All of the following apply: No trace buffer management events are recorded in TRBSR_EL3 . TRBSR_EL3 .IRQ is ignored and TRBE Profiling exceptions are not taken to EL3.

Enabled. TRBE Profiling exceptions for EL3 are enabled for trace buffer management events targeting EL3, as follows: Trace buffer management events due to a fault on a write to the trace buffer that would generate a Data Abort exception taken to EL3 if generated by a store instruction executed at the owning Exception level are recorded in TRBSR_EL3 . That is, any of the following faults: Granule Protection Check faults other than Granule Protection Faults (GPFs). If SCR_EL3 .GPF is 1, GPFs. If SCR_EL3 .EA is 1, External aborts. TRBE Profiling exceptions are generated and taken to EL3 when unmasked and TRBSR_EL3 .IRQ is 1.

Trap all. TRBE Profiling exceptions for EL3 are enabled for all trace buffer management events, as follows: All trace buffer management events are recorded in TRBSR_EL3 . TRBE Profiling exceptions are generated and taken to EL3 when unmasked and TRBSR_EL3 .IRQ is 1.

fields.name: PMSEE
fields.description: Profiling Buffer management event Exception Enable.

values.label: Disabled. SPE Profiling exceptions for all Exception levels are disabled. All of the following apply: No Profiling Buffer management events are recorded in PMBSR_EL3 . SPE Profiling exceptions are not generated. PMBSR_EL1 .S drives the interrupt request signal PMBIRQ . Accesses to PMBSR_EL2 at EL2 are trapped to EL3. Accesses to PMBSR_EL1 at EL1 ignore the value of HCR_EL2 .NV1 and accesses to PMBSR_EL1 at EL2 ignore the value of HCR_EL2 .E2H.

Delegated. SPE Profiling exceptions for EL3 are disabled, but might be enabled for EL2 or EL1 by PMSCR_EL2 .EE or PMSCR_EL1 .EE. All of the following apply: No Profiling Buffer management events are recorded in PMBSR_EL3 . PMBSR_EL3 .S is ignored and SPE Profiling exceptions are not taken to EL3.

Enabled. SPE Profiling exceptions for EL3 are enabled for Profiling Buffer management events targeting EL3, as follows: Profiling Buffer management events due to a fault on a write to the Profiling Buffer that would generate a Data Abort exception taken to EL3 if generated by a store instruction executed at the owning Exception level are recorded in PMBSR_EL3 . That is, any of the following faults: Granule Protection Check faults other than Granule Protection Faults (GPFs). If SCR_EL3 .GPF is 1, GPFs. If SCR_EL3 .EA is 1, External aborts. SPE Profiling exceptions are generated and taken to EL3 when unmasked and PMBSR_EL3 .S is 1.

Trap all. SPE Profiling exceptions for EL3 are enabled for all Profiling Buffer management events, as follows: All Profiling Buffer management events are recorded in PMBSR_EL3 . SPE Profiling exceptions are generated and taken to EL3 when unmasked and PMBSR_EL3 .S is 1.
Execution from MDSTEPOP_EL1 is disabled. EL2, EL1 and EL0 System register accesses to MDSTEPOP_EL1 are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.
Execution from MDSTEPOP_EL1 is not disabled by this control. System register accesses to MDSTEPOP_EL1 are not trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: ETBAD
fields.description: External Trace Buffer Access Disable. Controls access to the Trace Buffer registers from an external debugger.

values.label: Non-secure accesses from an external debugger to Trace Buffer registers are prohibited. If FEAT_RME is implemented, Secure and Realm accesses from an external debugger to Trace Buffer registers are prohibited and Root accesses to Trace Buffer registers are allowed. If FEAT_RME is not implemented, Secure accesses to Trace Buffer registers are allowed.
Secure and Non-secure accesses from an external debugger to Trace Buffer registers are prohibited. Root and Realm accesses to Trace Buffer registers are allowed.
Realm and Non-secure accesses from an external debugger to Trace Buffer registers are prohibited. Root and Secure accesses to Trace Buffer registers are allowed.
All accesses from an external debugger to Trace Buffer registers are allowed.

fields.name: EnITE
fields.description: Enable access to Instrumentation trace registers. When disabled, accesses to Instrumentation trace registers generate a trap to EL3.

values.label: Accesses of the specified Instrumentation trace registers at EL2 and EL1 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
Accesses of the specified Instrumentation trace registers are not trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: EPMSSAD
fields.description: External PMU Snapshot Access Disable. Controls access to the PMU Snapshot registers from an external debugger. External accesses of the following registers are affected by this control: PMCCNTSVR_EL1 , PMEVCNTSVR<n>_EL1 , and PMSSCR_EL1 . If FEAT_PMUv3_ICNTR is implemented, PMICNTSVR_EL1 .

values.label: Non-secure accesses from an external debugger to the affected PMU Snapshot registers are prohibited. If FEAT_RME is implemented, Secure and Realm accesses from an external debugger to the affected PMU Snapshot registers are prohibited. Other accesses from an external debugger to the specified registers are not affected by this control.

Secure and Non-secure accesses from an external debugger to the affected PMU Snapshot registers are prohibited. Other accesses from an external debugger to the specified registers are not affected by this control.

Realm and Non-secure accesses from an external debugger to the affected PMU Snapshot registers are prohibited. Other accesses from an external debugger to the specified registers are not affected by this control.
No accesses from an external debugger to the affected PMU Snapshot registers are prohibited by this control.

fields.name: EnPMSS
fields.description: Enable access to PMU Snapshot registers. When disabled, accesses to PMU Snapshot registers generate a trap to EL3.

values.label: Accesses of the specified PMU Snapshot registers at EL2 and EL1 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
Accesses of the specified PMU Snapshot registers are not trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: EBWE
fields.description: Extended Breakpoint and Watchpoint Enable. Enables use of additional breakpoints or watchpoints, and enables a trap to EL3 on accesses to debug registers.

values.label: The Effective values of MDSCR_EL1 .EMBWE and MDCR_EL2 .EBWE are 0. The Effective value of MDSELR_EL1 .BANK is zero at EL3. Accesses of MDSELR_EL1 at EL2 and EL1 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
The Effective values of MDSCR_EL1 .EMBWE, MDCR_EL2 .EBWE, and MDSELR_EL1 .BANK are not affected by this field. Accesses of MDSELR_EL1 are not trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: EnPMS3
fields.description: Enable access to SPE registers. When disabled, accesses to SPE registers generate a trap to EL3.

values.label: Accesses of the specified SPE registers at EL2 and EL1 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
Accesses of the specified SPE registers are not trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: PMEE
fields.description: Performance Monitors Exception Enable. Controls the generation of the PMUIRQ signal and the PMU Profiling exception at all Exception levels.

values.label: The PMUIRQ signal is asserted on a PMU overflow, and the PMU Profiling exception is disabled.
The PMUIRQ signal and the PMU Profiling exception are both controlled by MDCR_EL2 .PMEE.
The PMUIRQ signal is deasserted, and the PMU Profiling exception is enabled.

fields.name: EnTB2
fields.description: Enable access to Trace Buffer registers. When disabled, accesses to Trace Buffer registers generate a trap to EL3.

values.label: Accesses of the specified Trace Buffer registers at EL2 and EL1 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
Accesses of the specified Trace Buffer registers are not trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: E3BREC
fields.description: Branch Record Buffer EL3 Cold Reset Enable. With MDCR_EL3.E3BREW, controls branch recording at EL3.

values.label: When MDCR_EL3.E3BREW == 0: Branch recording at EL3 is disabled. When MDCR_EL3.E3BREW == 1: Branch recording at EL3 is enabled.
When MDCR_EL3.E3BREW == 0: Branch recording at EL3 is enabled. When MDCR_EL3.E3BREW == 1: Branch recording at EL3 is disabled.

fields.name: E3BREW
fields.description: Branch Record Buffer EL3 Warm Reset Enable. With MDCR_EL3.E3BREC, controls branch recording at EL3.

fields.name: EnPMSN
fields.description: Trap accesses to PMSNEVFR_EL1 . Controls access to Statistical Profiling PMSNEVFR_EL1 System register from EL2 and EL1.

values.label: Accesses to PMSNEVFR_EL1 at EL2 and EL1 generate a Trap exception to EL3.
Do not trap PMSNEVFR_EL1 to EL3.

fields.name: MPMX
fields.description: Monitor Performance Monitors Extended control. With MDCR_EL3.SPME, controls PMU operation at EL3.

values.label: Counters are not affected by this mechanism.
Affected counters are prohibited from counting at EL3. If PMCR_EL0 .DP is 1, PMCCNTR_EL0 is disabled at EL3. Otherwise, PMCCNTR_EL0 is not affected by this mechanism.

fields.name: MCCD
fields.description: Monitor Cycle Counter Disable. Prohibits the Cycle Counter, PMCCNTR_EL0 , from counting at EL3.

values.label: Cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is not affected by this mechanism.
Cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is prohibited at EL3.

fields.name: SBRBE
fields.description: Secure Branch Record Buffer Enable. Controls branch recording by the BRBE, and access to BRBE registers and instructions at EL2 and EL1.

values.label: Direct accesses to BRBE registers and instructions, except when in EL3, generate a Trap exception to EL3. EL0, EL1, and EL2 are prohibited regions.

Direct accesses to BRBE registers and instructions in Secure state, except when in EL3, generate a Trap exception to EL3. EL0, EL1, and EL2 in Secure state are prohibited regions. This control does not cause any direct accesses to BRBE registers when not in Secure state to be trapped, and does not cause any Exception levels when not in Secure state to be a prohibited region.
Direct accesses to BRBE registers and instructions, except when in EL3, generate a Trap exception to EL3. This control does not cause any Exception levels to be prohibited regions.
This control does not cause any direct accesses to BRBE registers or instruction to be trapped, and does not cause any Exception levels to be a prohibited region.

fields.name: PMSSE
fields.description: Performance Monitors Snapshot Enable. Controls the generation of Capture events.

values.label: Capture events are disabled.
Capture events are controlled by MDCR_EL2 .PMSSE.
Capture events are enabled and prohibited.
Capture events are enabled and allowed.

fields.name: MTPME
fields.description: Multi-threaded PMU Enable. Enables use of the PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .MT bits.

values.label: FEAT_MTPMU is disabled. The Effective value of PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .MT is 0.
PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .MT bits not affected by this field.

fields.name: TDCC
fields.description: Trap DCC. Traps use of the Debug Comms Channel at EL2, EL1, and EL0 to EL3.

values.label: This control does not cause any register accesses to be trapped.

Accesses to the DCC registers at EL2, EL1, and EL0 generate a Trap exception to EL3, unless the access also generates a higher priority exception. Traps on the DCC data transfer registers are ignored when the PE is in Debug state.

fields.name: NSTBE
fields.description: Non-secure Trace Buffer Extended. Together with MDCR_EL3.NSTB, controls the trace buffer owning Security state and accesses to trace buffer System registers from EL2 and EL1.

fields.name: NSTB
fields.description:
Non-secure Trace Buffer. Together with MDCR_EL3.NSTBE, controls the trace buffer owning Security state and accesses to trace buffer System registers from EL2 and EL1. NSTBE NSTB Meaning 0b0 0b00 Trace buffer owning Security state is Secure state.
When TraceBufferEnabled()==TRUE, tracing is prohibited in Realm and Non-secure states.
Accesses to trace buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1 are
trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.
When Secure state is not implemented, this encoding is reserved. 0b0 0b01 Trace buffer owning Security state is Secure state.
When TraceBufferEnabled()==TRUE, tracing is prohibited in Realm and Non-secure states.
Accesses to trace buffer System registers at Realm and Non-secure
EL2, and Realm and Non-secure EL1, are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher
priority exception.
When Secure state is not implemented, this encoding is reserved. 0b0 0b10 Trace buffer owning Security state is Non-secure state.
When TraceBufferEnabled()==TRUE, tracing is prohibited in Secure and Realm states.
Accesses to trace buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1 are
trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception. 0b0 0b11 Trace buffer owning Security state is Non-secure state.
When TraceBufferEnabled()==TRUE, tracing is prohibited in Secure and Realm states.
Accesses to trace buffer System registers at Secure and Realm EL2,
and Secure and Realm EL1, are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority
exception. 0b1 0b10 Trace buffer owning Security state is Realm state.
When TraceBufferEnabled()==TRUE, tracing is prohibited in Secure and Non-secure states.
Accesses to trace buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1 are
trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a highe priority exception. 0b1 0b11 Trace buffer owning Security state is Realm state.
When TraceBufferEnabled()==TRUE, tracing is prohibited in Secure and Non-secure states.
Accesses to trace buffer System registers at Secure and Non-secure
EL2, and Secure and Non-secure EL1, are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher
priority exception. All other combinations of MDCR_EL3.NSTBE and MDCR_EL3.NSTB are reserved.

fields.name: NSTB
fields.description: Non-secure Trace Buffer. Controls the trace buffer owning Security state and accesses to trace buffer System registers from EL2 and EL1.

values.label:
Trace buffer owning Security state is Secure state. When TraceBufferEnabled() ==TRUE, tracing is prohibited in Non-secure state.
Accesses to trace buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1  are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.

Trace buffer owning Security state is Secure state. When TraceBufferEnabled() ==TRUE, tracing is prohibited in Non-secure state.
Accesses to trace buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1 in Non-secure state are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.

Trace buffer owning Security state is Non-secure state. When TraceBufferEnabled() ==TRUE, tracing is prohibited in Secure state.
Accesses to trace buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1  are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.

Trace buffer owning Security state is Non-secure state. When TraceBufferEnabled() ==TRUE, tracing is prohibited in Secure state.
Accesses to trace buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1 in Secure state are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.

fields.name: SCCD
fields.description: Secure Cycle Counter Disable. Prohibits PMCCNTR_EL0 from counting in Secure state and EL3.

values.label: Cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is not affected by this mechanism.
Cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is prohibited in Secure state and EL3.

fields.name: ETAD
fields.description: External Trace Access Disable. Together with MDCR_EL3.ETADE, controls access to trace unit registers by an external debugger.

fields.name: ETAD
fields.description: External Trace Access Disable. Controls Non-secure access to trace unit registers by an external debugger.

values.label: No accesses from an external debugger to the trace unit registers are prohibited by this control.
Non-secure accesses from an external debugger to some trace unit registers are prohibited. See individual registers for the effect of this field.

fields.name: EPMAD
fields.description: External Performance Monitors Access Disable. Together with MDCR_EL3.EPMADE, controls access to Performance Monitor registers by an external debugger. External accesses of the following Performance Monitor registers are affected by this control: PMCCFILTR_EL0 , PMCCNTR_EL0 , PMCFGR , PMCNTENCLR_EL0 , PMCNTENSET_EL0 , PMCR_EL0 , PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 , PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 , PMINTENCLR_EL1 , PMINTENSET_EL1 , PMOVSCLR_EL0 , and PMOVSSET_EL0 . If PMEVFILT2R<n> is implemented, PMEVFILT2R<n> . If implemented, PMIIDR . If PMSWINC_EL0 is implemented in the external view, PMSWINC_EL0 . If FEAT_PMUv3p4 is implemented, PMMIR . If FEAT_PMUv3_EXT64 is implemented, PMCNTEN , PMINTEN , and PMOVS . If FEAT_PMUv3p9 is implemented, PMZR_EL0 . If FEAT_PMUv3_ICNTR is implemented, PMCGCR0 , PMICFILTR_EL0 , and PMICNTR_EL0 . If FEAT_PCSRv8p9 is implemented, PMPCSCTL .

fields.name: EPMAD
fields.description: External Performance Monitors Access Disable. Controls Non-secure access to Performance Monitor registers by an external debugger. External accesses of the following Performance Monitor registers are affected by this control: PMCCFILTR_EL0 , PMCCNTR_EL0 , PMCFGR , PMCNTENCLR_EL0 , PMCNTENSET_EL0 , PMCR_EL0 , PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 , PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 , PMINTENCLR_EL1 , PMINTENSET_EL1 , PMOVSCLR_EL0 , and PMOVSSET_EL0 . If PMEVFILT2R<n> is implemented, PMEVFILT2R<n> . If implemented, PMIIDR . If PMSWINC_EL0 is implemented in the external view, PMSWINC_EL0 . If FEAT_PMUv3p4 is implemented, PMMIR . If FEAT_PMUv3_EXT64 is implemented, PMCNTEN , PMINTEN , and PMOVS . If FEAT_PMUv3p9 is implemented, PMZR_EL0 . If FEAT_PMUv3_ICNTR is implemented, PMCGCR0 , PMICFILTR_EL0 , and PMICNTR_EL0 . If FEAT_PCSRv8p9 is implemented, PMPCSCTL .

values.label: No accesses from an external debugger to the Performance Monitor registers are prohibited by this control.
Non-secure accesses from an external debugger to the affected Performance Monitor registers are prohibited.

fields.name: EPMAD
fields.description: External Performance Monitors Access Disable. Controls access to Performance Monitor registers by an external debugger. External accesses of the following Performance Monitor registers are affected by this control: PMCCFILTR_EL0 , PMCCNTR_EL0 , PMCFGR , PMCNTENCLR_EL0 , PMCNTENSET_EL0 , PMCR_EL0 , PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 , PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 , PMINTENCLR_EL1 , PMINTENSET_EL1 , PMOVSCLR_EL0 , and PMOVSSET_EL0 . If PMEVFILT2R<n> is implemented, PMEVFILT2R<n> . If implemented, PMIIDR . If PMSWINC_EL0 is implemented in the external view, PMSWINC_EL0 . If FEAT_PMUv3p4 is implemented, PMMIR .

values.label: No accesses from an external debugger to the Performance Monitor registers are prohibited by this control.

If the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED authentication interface function ExternalSecureInvasiveDebugEnabled() returns FALSE, then accesses from an external debugger to the affected Performance Monitor registers are prohibited.

fields.name: EDAD
fields.description: External Debug Access Disable. Together with MDCR_EL3.EDADE, controls access to breakpoint registers, watchpoint registers, and OSLAR_EL1 by an external debugger. External accesses of the following debug registers are affected by this control: DBGBCR<n>_EL1 , DBGBVR<n>_EL1 , DBGWCR<n>_EL1 , DBGWVR<n>_EL1 , and OSLAR_EL1 .

fields.name: EDAD
fields.description: External Debug Access Disable. Controls Non-secure access to breakpoint registers, watchpoint registers, and OSLAR_EL1 by an external debugger. External accesses of the following debug registers are affected by this control: DBGBCR<n>_EL1 , DBGBVR<n>_EL1 , DBGWCR<n>_EL1 , DBGWVR<n>_EL1 , and OSLAR_EL1 .

values.label: No accesses from an external debugger to the debug registers are prohibited by this control.
Non-secure accesses from an external debugger to the affected debug registers are prohibited.

fields.name: EDAD
fields.description: External Debug Access Disable. Controls access to breakpoint registers, watchpoint registers, and OSLAR_EL1 by an external debugger. External accesses of the following debug registers are affected by this control: DBGBCR<n>_EL1 , DBGBVR<n>_EL1 , DBGWCR<n>_EL1 , DBGWVR<n>_EL1 , and OSLAR_EL1 .

values.label: No accesses from an external debugger to the debug registers are prohibited by this control.

If the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED authentication interface function ExternalSecureInvasiveDebugEnabled() returns FALSE, then accesses from an external debugger to the affected debug registers are prohibited.

fields.name: EDAD
fields.description: External Debug Access Disable. Controls access to breakpoint registers, watchpoint registers, and optionally OSLAR_EL1 by an external debugger. External accesses of the following debug registers are affected by this control: DBGBCR<n>_EL1 , DBGBVR<n>_EL1 , DBGWCR<n>_EL1 , and DBGWVR<n>_EL1 . It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether this control affects OSLAR_EL1 .

values.label: No accesses from an external debugger to the debug registers are prohibited by this control.

If the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED authentication interface function ExternalSecureInvasiveDebugEnabled() returns FALSE, then accesses from an external debugger to the affected debug registers are prohibited.

fields.name: TTRF
fields.description: Trap Trace Filter controls. Traps use of the Trace Filter control registers at EL2 and EL1 to EL3. The Trace Filter registers trapped by this control are: TRFCR_EL2 , TRFCR_EL12, TRFCR_EL1 , reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . HTRFCR and TRFCR , reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 .

values.label: Accesses to Trace Filter registers at EL2 and EL1 are not affected by this field.
Accesses to Trace Filter registers at EL2 and EL1 generate a Trap exception to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.

fields.name: STE
fields.description: Secure Trace enable. Enables tracing in Secure state.

values.label: Trace prohibited in Secure state unless overridden by the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED authentication interface.
Trace in Secure state is not affected by this field.

fields.name: SPME
fields.description: Secure Performance Monitors Enable. Controls PMU operation in Secure state and at EL3 when MDCR_EL3.MPMX is 0.

values.label: Affected counters are prohibited from counting in Secure state and at EL3. If PMCR_EL0 .DP is 1, PMCCNTR_EL0 is disabled in Secure state and at EL3. Otherwise, PMCCNTR_EL0 is not affected by this mechanism.
Counters are not affected by this mechanism.

fields.name: SPME
fields.description: Secure Performance Monitors Enable. Controls PMU operation in Secure state.

values.label: Event counting is prohibited in Secure state. If PMCR_EL0 .DP is 1, PMCCNTR_EL0 is disabled in Secure state. Otherwise, PMCCNTR_EL0 is not affected by this mechanism.
Event counting and PMCCNTR_EL0 are not affected by this mechanism.

fields.name: SPME
fields.description: Secure Performance Monitors Enable. Controls PMU operation in Secure state.

values.label: If ExternalSecureNoninvasiveDebugEnabled() is FALSE, then all the following apply: Event counting is prohibited in Secure state. If PMCR_EL0 .DP is 1, PMCCNTR_EL0 is disabled in Secure state. Otherwise, PMCCNTR_EL0 is not affected by this mechanism.
Event counting and PMCCNTR_EL0 are not affected by this mechanism.

fields.name: SDD
fields.description: AArch64 Secure Self-hosted invasive debug disable. Disables Software debug exceptions in Secure state, other than Breakpoint Instruction exceptions.

values.label: Debug exceptions in Secure state are not affected by this field.
Debug exceptions, other than Breakpoint Instruction exceptions, are disabled from all Exception levels in Secure state.

fields.name: SPD32
fields.description: AArch32 Secure self-hosted privileged debug. Enables or disables debug exceptions from Secure EL1 using AArch32, other than Breakpoint Instruction exceptions.

values.label: Legacy mode. Debug exceptions from Secure EL1 are enabled by the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED authentication interface.
Secure privileged debug disabled. Debug exceptions from Secure EL1 are disabled.
Secure privileged debug enabled. Debug exceptions from Secure EL1 are enabled.

fields.name: NSPB
fields.description:
Non-secure Profiling Buffer. Together with MDCR_EL3.NSPBE, controls the Profiling Buffer owning Security state and accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers from EL2 and EL1. NSPBE NSPB Meaning 0b0 0b00 Profiling Buffer owning Security state is Secure state.
Profiling is disabled in Realm and Non-secure states.
Accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1 are
trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.
When Secure state is not implemented, this encoding is reserved. 0b0 0b01 Profiling Buffer owning Security state is Secure state.
Profiling is disabled in Realm and Non-secure states.
Accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers at Realm and Non-secure
EL2, and Realm and Non-secure EL1, are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher
priority exception.
When Secure state is not implemented, this encoding is reserved. 0b0 0b10 Profiling Buffer owning Security state is Non-secure state.
Profiling is disabled in Secure and Realm states.
Accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1 are
trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception. 0b0 0b11 Profiling Buffer owning Security state is Non-secure state.
Profiling is disabled in Secure and Realm states.
Accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers at Secure and Realm EL2,
and Secure and Realm EL1, are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority
exception. 0b1 0b10 Profiling Buffer owning Security state is Realm state.
Profiling is disabled in Secure and Non-secure states.
Accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1 are
trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a highe priority exception. 0b1 0b11 Profiling Buffer owning Security state is Realm state.
Profiling is disabled in Secure and Non-secure states.
Accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers at Secure and Non-secure
EL2, and Secure and Non-secure EL1, are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher
priority exception. All other combinations of MDCR_EL3.NSPBE and MDCR_EL3.NSPB are reserved.

fields.name: NSPB
fields.description: Non-secure Profiling Buffer. Controls the Profiling Buffer owning Security state and accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers from EL2 and EL1.

values.label:
Profiling Buffer owning Security state is Secure state. Profiling is disabled in Non-secure state.
Accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1  are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.

Profiling Buffer owning Security state is Secure state. Profiling is disabled in Non-secure state.
Accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1 in Non-secure state are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.

Profiling Buffer owning Security state is Non-secure state. Profiling is disabled in Secure state.
Accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1  are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.

Profiling Buffer owning Security state is Non-secure state. Profiling is disabled in Secure state.
Accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers at EL2 and EL1 in Secure state are trapped to EL3, unless the access generates a higher priority exception.

fields.name: NSPBE
fields.description: Non-secure Profiling Buffer Extended. Together with MDCR_EL3.NSPB, controls the Profiling Buffer owning Security state and accesses to Statistical Profiling and Profiling Buffer System registers from EL2 and EL1.

fields.name: TDOSA
fields.description: Trap debug OS-related register access. Traps EL2 and EL1 System register accesses to the powerdown debug registers to EL3. Accesses to the registers are trapped as follows: Accesses from AArch64 state, OSLAR_EL1 , OSLSR_EL1 , OSDLR_EL1 , DBGPRCR_EL1 , and any IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED register with similar functionality that the implementation specifies as trapped by this field, are trapped to EL3 and reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . Accesses using MCR or MRC to DBGOSLAR , DBGOSLSR , DBGOSDLR , and DBGPRCR , are trapped to EL3 and reported using EC syndrome value 0x05 . Accesses to any IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED register with similar functionality that the implementation specifies as trapped by this field.

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
EL2 and EL1 System register accesses to the powerdown debug registers are trapped to EL3, unless it is trapped by HDCR .TDOSA or MDCR_EL2 .TDOSA.

fields.name: TDOSA
fields.description: Trap debug OS-related register access. Traps EL2 and EL1 System register accesses to the powerdown debug registers to EL3. The following registers are affected by this trap: AArch64: OSLAR_EL1 , OSLSR_EL1 , and DBGPRCR_EL1 . AArch32: DBGOSLAR , DBGOSLSR , and DBGPRCR . AArch64 and AArch32: Any IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED register with similar functionality that the implementation specifies as trapped by this field. It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether accesses to OSDLR_EL1 and DBGOSDLR are trapped.

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
EL2 and EL1 System register accesses to the powerdown debug registers are trapped to EL3, unless it is trapped by HDCR .TDOSA or MDCR_EL2 .TDOSA.

fields.name: TDA
fields.description: Trap accesses of debug System registers. Enables a trap to EL3 on accesses of debug System registers.

values.label: Accesses of the specified debug System registers are not trapped by this mechanism.
Accesses of the specified debug System registers at EL2, EL1, and EL0 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.

fields.name: EnPM2
fields.description: Enable access to PMU registers. When disabled, accesses to PMU registers generate a trap to EL3.

values.label: Accesses of the specified PMU registers at EL2, EL1, and EL0 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception. If FEAT_PMUv3_ICNTR is implemented, then: PMCNTENCLR_EL0 .F0, PMCNTENSET_EL0 .F0, PMOVSCLR_EL0 .F0, PMOVSSET_EL0 .F0, and PMZR_EL0 .F0 read-as-zero and ignore writes at EL2, EL1, and EL0. PMINTENCLR_EL1 .F0 and PMINTENSET_EL1 .F0 read-as-zero and ignore writes at EL2 and EL1.
Accesses of the specified PMU registers are not trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: TPM
fields.description: Trap accesses of PMU registers. Enables a trap to EL3 on accesses of PMU registers.

values.label: Accesses of the specified PMU registers are not trapped by this mechanism.
Accesses of the specified PMU registers at EL2, EL1, and EL0 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.

fields.name: EDADE
fields.description: External Debug Access Disable Extended. Together with MDCR_EL3.EDAD, controls access to breakpoint registers, watchpoint registers, and OSLAR_EL1 by an external debugger.

fields.name: ETADE
fields.description: External Trace Access Disable Extended. Together with MDCR_EL3.ETAD, controls access to trace unit registers by an external debugger.

fields.name: EPMADE
fields.description: External Performance Monitors Access Disable Extended. Together with MDCR_EL3.EPMAD, controls access to Performance Monitor registers by an external debugger.

fields.name: RLTE
fields.description: Realm Trace enable. Enables tracing in Realm state.

values.label: Trace prohibited in Realm state, unless overridden by the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED authentication interface.
Trace in Realm state is not affected by this field.

name: MDRAR_EL1
description: Defines the base physical address of a 4KB-aligned memory-mapped debug component, usually a ROM table that locates and describes the memory-mapped debug components in the system. Use of this register is deprecated.

fields.name: Valid
fields.description: This field indicates whether the ROM Table address is valid.

values.label: ROM Table address is not valid. Software must ignore ROMADDR.
ROM Table address is valid.

name: MDSCR_EL1
description: Main control register for the debug implementation.

fields.name: EnSTEPOP
fields.description: Software step control bit. Enable execution from MDSTEPOP_EL1 . Permitted values are:

values.label: Execution from MDSTEPOP_EL1 is disabled.
Execution from MDSTEPOP_EL1 is not disabled by this control.

fields.name: EHBWE
fields.description: Extended Halting Breakpoint and Watchpoint Enable. Used for save/restore of EDSCR2 .EHBWE.

fields.name: EnSPM
fields.description: Enable access to System PMU registers. When disabled, accesses to System PMU registers generate a trap to EL1.

values.label: Accesses of the specified System PMU registers at EL0 are trapped to EL1, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
Accesses of the specified System PMU registers are not trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: TTA
fields.description: Trap Trace Accesses. Used for save/restore of EDSCR2 .TTA.

fields.name: EMBWE
fields.description: Extended Monitor Breakpoint and Watchpoint Enable. Enables use of additional breakpoints or watchpoints.

values.label: Breakpoint and Watchpoint exceptions are disabled for each breakpoint <n> and watchpoint <n>, where n is greater than or equal to 16. The Effective value of MDSELR_EL1 .BANK is zero at EL1.
Breakpoint and Watchpoint exceptions are not affected by this mechanism. The Effective value of MDSELR_EL1 .BANK is not affected by this field.

fields.name: TFO
fields.description: Trace Filter override. Used for save/restore of EDSCR .TFO.

fields.name: RXfull
fields.description: Used for save/restore of EDSCR .RXfull. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 0, software must treat this bit as UNK/SBZP. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 1, this bit holds the value of EDSCR .RXfull. Reads and writes of this bit are indirect accesses to EDSCR .RXfull.

fields.name: TXfull
fields.description: Used for save/restore of EDSCR .TXfull. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 0, software must treat this bit as UNK/SBZP. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 1, this bit holds the value of EDSCR .TXfull. Reads and writes of this bit are indirect accesses to EDSCR .TXfull.

fields.name: RXO
fields.description: Used for save/restore of EDSCR .RXO. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 0, software must treat this bit as UNK/SBZP. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 1, this bit holds the value of EDSCR .RXO. Reads and writes of this bit are indirect accesses to EDSCR .RXO. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 1, if bits [27,6] of the value written to MDSCR_EL1 are {1,0}, that is, the RXO bit is 1 and the ERR bit is 0, the PE sets EDSCR .{RXO,ERR} to UNKNOWN values.

fields.name: TXU
fields.description: Used for save/restore of EDSCR .TXU. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 0, software must treat this bit as UNK/SBZP. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 1, this bit holds the value of EDSCR .TXU. Reads and writes of this bit are indirect accesses to EDSCR .TXU. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 1, if bits [26,6] of the value written to MDSCR_EL1 are {1,0}, that is, the TXU bit is 1 and the ERR bit is 0, the PE sets EDSCR .{TXU,ERR} to UNKNOWN values.

fields.name: INTdis
fields.description: Used for save/restore of EDSCR .INTdis. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 0, software must treat this bit as UNK/SBZP. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 1, this field holds the value of EDSCR .INTdis. Reads and writes of this field are indirect accesses to EDSCR .INTdis.

fields.name: TDA
fields.description: Used for save/restore of EDSCR .TDA. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 0, software must treat this bit as UNK/SBZP. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 1, this bit holds the value of EDSCR .TDA. Reads and writes of this bit are indirect accesses to EDSCR .TDA.

fields.name: SC2
fields.description: Used for save/restore of EDSCR .SC2. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 0, software must treat this bit as UNK/SBZP. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 1, this bit holds the value of EDSCR .SC2. Reads and writes of this bit are indirect accesses to EDSCR .SC2.

fields.name: MDE
fields.description: Monitor debug events. Enable Breakpoint, Watchpoint, and Vector Catch exceptions.

values.label: Breakpoint, Watchpoint, and Vector Catch exceptions disabled.
Breakpoint, Watchpoint, and Vector Catch exceptions enabled.

fields.name: HDE
fields.description: Used for save/restore of EDSCR .HDE. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 0, software must treat this bit as UNK/SBZP. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 1, this bit holds the value of EDSCR .HDE. Reads and writes of this bit are indirect accesses to EDSCR .HDE.

fields.name: KDE
fields.description: Local (kernel) debug enable. If EL D is using AArch64, enable debug exceptions within EL D . Permitted values are:

values.label: Debug exceptions, other than Breakpoint Instruction exceptions, disabled within EL D .
All debug exceptions enabled within EL D .

fields.name: TDCC
fields.description: Traps EL0 accesses to the Debug Communication Channel (DCC) registers to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, from both Execution states, as follows: In AArch64 state, MRS or MSR accesses to the following DCC registers are trapped, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : MDCCSR_EL0 . If not in Debug state, DBGDTR_EL0 , DBGDTRTX_EL0 , and DBGDTRRX_EL0 . In AArch32 state, MRC or MCR accesses to the following registers are trapped, reported using EC syndrome value 0x05 . DBGDSCRint , DBGDIDR , DBGDSAR , DBGDRAR . If not in Debug state, DBGDTRRXint , and DBGDTRTXint . In AArch32 state, LDC access to DBGDTRRXint and STC access to DBGDTRTXint are trapped, reported using EC syndrome value 0x06 . In AArch32 state, MRRC accesses to DBGDSAR and DBGDRAR are trapped, reported using EC syndrome value 0x0C .

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
EL0 using AArch64: EL0 accesses to the AArch64 DCC registers are trapped. EL0 using AArch32: EL0 accesses to the AArch32 DCC registers are trapped.

fields.name: ERR
fields.description: Used for save/restore of EDSCR .ERR. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 0, software must treat this bit as UNK/SBZP. When OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK == 1, this bit holds the value of EDSCR .ERR. Reads and writes of this bit are indirect accesses to EDSCR .ERR.

fields.name: SS
fields.description: Software step control bit. If EL D is using AArch64, enable Software step. Permitted values are:

values.label: Software step disabled
Software step enabled.

name: MDSELR_EL1
description: Selects the current breakpoints or watchpoints accessed by System register instructions.

values.label: Select 0 to 15.
Select 16 to 31.
Select 32 to 47.
Select 48 to 63.

name: MDSTEPOP_EL1
description: Used to execute instructions while Software Step is in active-not-pending state.

fields.name: OPCODE
fields.description: A64 instruction to be executed on the PE.

name: MECIDR_EL2
description: MEC identification register.

fields.name: MECIDWidthm1
fields.description: MECID width minus 1. The value of this field plus 1 is the MECID width in bits, that this PE supports.

name: MECID_A0_EL2
long_name: Alternate MECID for EL2 and EL2&0 translation regimes
description: Alternate MECID for EL2 and EL2&0 accesses translated by TTBR0_EL2 .

fields.name: MECID
fields.description: If MECIDWidth is less than 16 bits, bits[15:MECIDWidth] are RES0 . MECIDWidth is defined in MECIDR_EL2 .MECIDWidthm1.

name: MECID_A1_EL2
long_name: Alternate MECID for EL2&0 translation regimes.
description: Alternate MECID for EL2&0 accesses translated by TTBR1_EL2 .

fields.name: MECID
fields.description: If MECIDWidth is less than 16 bits, bits[15:MECIDWidth] are RES0 . MECIDWidth is defined in MECIDR_EL2 .MECIDWidthm1.

name: MECID_P0_EL2
long_name: Primary MECID for EL2 and EL2&0 translation regimes
description: Primary MECID for EL2 and EL2&0 accesses translated by TTBR0_EL2 .

fields.name: MECID
fields.description: If MECIDWidth is less than 16 bits, bits[15:MECIDWidth] are RES0 . MECIDWidth is defined in MECIDR_EL2 .MECIDWidthm1.

name: MECID_P1_EL2
long_name: Primary MECID for EL2&0 translation regimes
description: Primary MECID for EL2&0 accesses translated by TTBR1_EL2 .

fields.name: MECID
fields.description: If MECIDWidth is less than 16 bits, bits[15:MECIDWidth] are RES0 . MECIDWidth is defined in MECIDR_EL2 .MECIDWidthm1.

name: MECID_RL_A_EL3
long_name: Realm PA space Alternate MECID for EL3 stage 1 translation regime
description: Alternate MECID for EL3 accesses to the Realm physical address space, translated by TTBR0_EL3 .

fields.name: MECID
fields.description: If MECIDWidth is less than 16 bits, bits[15:MECIDWidth] are RES0 . MECIDWidth is defined in MECIDR_EL2 .MECIDWidthm1.

name: MFAR_EL3
description: Records the faulting physical address for a Granule Protection Check, synchronous External abort, or SError exception taken to EL3.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with MFAR_EL3.NSE, reports the physical address space of the access that triggered the exception. NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 When Secure state is implemented, Secure. Otherwise reserved. 0b0 0b1 Non-secure. 0b1 0b0 Root. 0b1 0b1 Realm.

fields.name: NSE
fields.description: Together with MFAR_EL3.NS, reports the physical address space of the access that triggered the exception. For a description of the values derived by evaluating NS and NSE together, see MFAR_EL3.NS.

fields.name: FPA[55:52]
fields.description: When FEAT_D128 is implemented, extension to MFAR_EL3.FPA[47:12].

fields.name: FPA[51:48]
fields.description: When FEAT_LPA is implemented, extension to MFAR_EL3.FPA[47:12].

fields.name: FPA
fields.description: Bits [47:12] of the Faulting Physical Address.

name: MIDR_EL1
description: Provides identification information for the PE, including an implementer code for the device and a device ID number.

fields.name: Implementer
fields.description: The Implementer code. This field must hold an implementer code that has been assigned by Arm.

values.label: Reserved for software use.
Arm Limited.
Broadcom Corporation.
Cavium Inc.
Digital Equipment Corporation.
Fujitsu Ltd.
Infineon Technologies AG.
Motorola or Freescale Semiconductor Inc.
NVIDIA Corporation.
Applied Micro Circuits Corporation.
Qualcomm Inc.
Marvell International Ltd.
Intel Corporation.
Ampere Computing.

fields.name: Variant
fields.description: Variant number. Typically, this field is used to distinguish between different product variants, or major revisions of a product.

values.label: Armv4.
Armv4T.
Armv5 (obsolete).
Armv5T.
Armv5TE.
Armv5TEJ.
Armv6.
Architectural features are individually identified in the ID_* registers.

fields.name: PartNum
fields.description: Primary Part Number for the device. On processors implemented by Arm, if the top four bits of the primary part number are 0x0 or 0x7 , the variant and architecture are encoded differently.

name: MLBI ALLE1
description: Invalidates all cached MPAM translations and MITT base addresses associated with the current Security state of EL2. The completion of a subsequent DSB instruction that applies to both loads and stores guarantees that the MLBI operation is complete. If SCR_EL3.NS is 0 and SCR_EL3.EEL2 is 0, no MLB entries are guaranteed to be invalidated.

name: MLBI VMALLE1
description: Invalidate cached MPAM translations and MITT base addresses associated with the current VMID and the current Security state of EL2. The completion of a subsequent DSB instruction that applies to both loads and stores guarantees that the MLBI operation is complete. If SCR_EL3.NS is 0 and SCR_EL3.EEL2 is 0, no MLB entries are guaranteed to be invalidated.

name: MLBI VPIDE1
long_name: MLB Invalidate by Virtual PARTID and VMID
description: Invalidate cached MPAM translations associated with the specified vPARTID, the current VMID, and the current Security state of EL2. The completion of a subsequent DSB instruction that applies to both loads and stores guarantees that the MLBI operation is complete. If SCR_EL3.NS is 0 and SCR_EL3.EEL2 is 0, no MLB entries are guaranteed to be invalidated.

fields.name: vPARTID
fields.description: The Virtual PARTID to be invalidated

name: MLBI VPMGE1
long_name: MLB Invalidate by Virtual PMG and VMID
description: Invalidate cached MPAM translations associated with the specified vPMG, the current VMID, and the current Security state of EL2. The completion of a subsequent DSB instruction that applies to both loads and stores guarantees that the MLBI operation is complete. If SCR_EL3.NS is 0 and SCR_EL3.EEL2 is 0, no MLB entries are guaranteed to be invalidated.

fields.name: vPMG
fields.description: The Virtual PMG to be invalidated

name: MPAMBW0_EL1
description: Enables software to configure a maximum fraction of memory bandwidth that the PE is permitted to use when executing at EL0 with its current PARTID.

fields.name: HW_SCALE_ENABLE
fields.description: Enables hardware bandwidth scaling of the MPAMBW0_EL1 .MAX value.

values.label: PE-side memory bandwidth control hardware scaling in EL0 is disabled.
PE-side memory bandwidth control hardware scaling in EL0 is enabled.

fields.name: ENABLED
fields.description: Enables the PE-side memory bandwidth control when in EL0.

values.label: The PE-side memory bandwidth control in EL0 is disabled.
The PE-side memory bandwidth control in EL0 is enabled.

fields.name: HARDLIM
fields.description: PE-side Maximum Bandwidth Limit Behavior Selection.

values.label: Soft limit: when MPAMBW0_EL1 .MAX bandwidth is exceeded, the PE is unregulated unless the downstream memory path is saturated. It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED how hardware determines when the downstream memory path is saturated.
Hard limit: when MPAMBW0_EL1 .MAX bandwidth is exceeded, the PE does not use any more bandwidth until the memory bandwidth for the PE falls below MPAMBW0_EL1 .MAX.

name: MPAMBWCAP_EL2
long_name: MPAM PE-side Maximum-bandwidth Limit Virtualization Register
description: Allows software executing at EL2 to provide MPAMBWCAP_EL2 .CAP as an upper bound to MPAMBW1_EL1 .MAX. If FEAT_SME is implemented, the upper bound also applies to MPAMBWSM_EL1 .MAX when executing at EL1: the maximum bandwidth allowed for the PE is MIN( MPAMBWSM_EL1 .MAX, MPAMBWCAP_EL2 .CAP). If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H,TGE} is not {1,1}: The upper bound also applies to MPAMBW0_EL1 .MAX: the maximum bandwidth allowed for the PE is MIN( MPAMBW0_EL1 .MAX, MPAMBWCAP_EL2 .CAP). If FEAT_SME is implemented, the upper bound also applies to MPAMBWSM_EL1 .MAX when executing at EL0: the maximum bandwidth allowed for the PE is MIN( MPAMBWSM_EL1 .MAX, MPAMBWCAP_EL2 .CAP). If MPAMBWCAP_EL2 .ENABLED is 1, a PARTID that has used more than min(CAP,MAX) is given no access to additional bandwidth.

fields.name: HW_SCALE_ENABLE
fields.description: Enables hardware bandwidth scaling of the MPAMBWCAP_EL2 .CAP value.

values.label: PE-side memory bandwidth control hardware scaling for EL2 capping is disabled.
PE-side memory bandwidth control hardware scaling for EL2 capping is enabled.

fields.name: ENABLED
fields.description: Enables the PE-side memory bandwidth control capping by EL2.

values.label: The PE-side memory bandwidth control capping by EL2 is disabled.
The PE-side memory bandwidth control capping by EL2 is enabled.

Hardware support for auto-scaling is implemented.

fields.name: MAX_LIM
fields.description: Indicates the implemented maximum-bandwidth limit partitioning behaviors.

values.label: Both soft limit and hard limit behaviors are implemented.
Soft limit behavior is implemented.
Hard limit behavior is implemented.
Reserved.

fields.name: BWA_WD
fields.description: Indicates the number of implemented bits in the bandwidth allocation fields MPAMBWn_ELx.MAX, MPAMBWSM_EL1 .MAX and MPAMBWCAP_EL2 .CAP.

values.label: Number of implemented bits in the bandwidth allocation fields.

Soft limit: when MPAMBWSM_EL1 .MAX bandwidth is exceeded, the PE is unregulated unless the downstream memory path is saturated. It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED how hardware determines when the downstream memory path is saturated.
Hard limit: when MPAMBWSM_EL1 .MAX bandwidth is exceeded, the PE does not use any more bandwidth until the memory bandwidth for the PE falls below MPAMBWSM_EL1 .MAX.

Support for PE-side bandwidth controls is implemented via MPAMBW registers.

fields.name: PMG_MAX
fields.description: Indicates the largest value of PMG supported by the implementation. The PMG field of every MPAMn_ELx register must implement at least enough bits to represent this value.

fields.name: HAS_INSTR_ALT_ID

Support for tagging instruction fetches with alternative PARTID and PMG values is implemented.

fields.name: HAS_ALT_ID

values.label: Support for alternative PARTID and PMG values is not implemented.

Support for MPAM virtualization is not implemented.
Support for MPAM virtualization is implemented.

fields.name: PARTID_MAX
fields.description: Indicates the largest value of PARTID supported by the implementation. The PARTID field of every MPAMn_ELx register must implement at least enough bits to represent this value.

name: MPAMSM_EL1
description: Holds information to generate MPAM labels for memory requests that are: Issued due to the execution of SME load and store instructions. Issued when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode due to the execution of SVE and SIMD&FP load and store instructions and SVE prefetch instructions. If an implementation uses a shared SMCU, then the MPAM labels in this register have precedence over the labels in MPAM0_EL1 , MPAM1_EL1 , MPAM2_EL2 , and MPAM3_EL3 . If an implementation includes an SMCU that is not shared with other PEs, then it is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether the MPAM labels in this register have precedence over the labels in MPAM0_EL1 , MPAM1_EL1 , MPAM2_EL2 , and MPAM3_EL3 . When FEAT_MPAMv1p0 or FEAT_MPAMv0p1 is implemented, the MPAM labels in this register are used if MPAM1_EL1 .MPAMEN is 1. When FEAT_MPAMv2 is implemented, the MPAM labels in this register are used if MPAMCTL_EL1 .MPAMEN is 1. For memory requests issued from EL0, the MPAM PARTID in this register is virtual and mapped into a physical PARTID when all of the following are true: EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}. The MPAM virtualization option is implemented and MPAMHCR_EL2 .EL0_VPMEN is 1. For memory requests issued from EL1, the MPAM PARTID in this register is virtual and mapped into a physical PARTID when all of the following are true: EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state. The MPAM virtualization option is implemented and MPAMHCR_EL2 .EL1_VPMEN is 1.

fields.name: PMG
fields.description: Performance monitoring group for requests issued due to the execution at any Exception level of SME load and store instructions and, when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode, SVE and SIMD&FP load and store instructions and SVE prefetch instructions.

fields.name: PARTID
fields.description: Partition ID for requests issued due to the execution at any Exception level of SME load and store instructions and, when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode, SVE and SIMD&FP load and store instructions and SVE prefetch instructions.

name: MPAMVIDCR_EL2
description: MPAMVIDCR_EL2 points to the MPAM Virtual Machine Structure in memory that maps virtual PARTID and PMG values to physical PARTID and PMG values. This register is intended for use by hypervisors configuring mappings between guest PARTIDs and PMGs and physical PARTIDs and PMGs.

fields.name: BADDR
fields.description: MPAM VMS base address, bits [55:12]. Bits [55:12] of the base address are the value of this field. Bits [11:0] of the base address are 0. Bits of this field that correspond to address bits beyond the implemented physical address size, as indicated by ID_AA64MMFR0_EL1 .PARange, are RES0 .

name: MPAMVIDSR_EL2
description: Enables hardware to report faults on accesses to the MVMS or an MITT.

fields.name: FSC
fields.description: Fault Status Code. Reports the fault type generated on an MVMS or MITT access.

values.label: The PE has not experienced any error on access to the MVMS or an MITT.
Granule Protection Check Fault on access to the MVMS or an MITT.
External Abort on access to the MVMS or an MITT.

fields.name: ELX
fields.description: EL that initiated the virtual ID lookup where the fault occurred.

values.label: EL0 initiated the virtual ID lookup where the fault occurred.
EL1 initiated the virtual ID lookup where the fault occurred.

fields.name: FADDR
fields.description: Bits [55:12] of the physical address of the MVMS or MITT entry accessed by the memory access that generated the fault. Bits [55:12] of the address are the value of this field. Bits [11:0] of the address are 0. Bits of this field that correspond to address bits beyond the implemented physical address size, as indicated by ID_AA64MMFR0_EL1 .PARange, are RES0 .

name: MPAMVIDSR_EL3
description: Enables hardware to report faults on accesses to the MVMS or an MITT.

fields.name: FSC
fields.description: Fault Status Code. Reports the fault type generated on an MVMS or MITT access.

values.label: The PE has not experienced any error on access to the MVMS or an MITT.
Granule Protection Check Fault on access to the MVMS or an MITT.
External Abort on access to the MVMS or an MITT.

fields.name: ELX
fields.description: EL that initiated the virtual ID lookup where the fault occurred.

values.label: EL0 initiated the virtual ID lookup where the fault occurred.
EL1 initiated the virtual ID lookup where the fault occurred.

fields.name: FADDR
fields.description: Bits [55:12] of the physical address of the MVMS or MITT entry accessed by the memory access that generated the fault. Bits [55:12] of the address are the value of this field. Bits [11:0] of the address are 0. Bits of this field that correspond to address bits beyond the implemented physical address size, as indicated by ID_AA64MMFR0_EL1 .PARange, are RES0 .

name: MPAMVPM0_EL2
description: MPAMVPM0_EL2 provides mappings from virtual PARTIDs 0 - 3 to physical PARTIDs. MPAMIDR_EL1 .VPMR_MAX field gives the index of the highest implemented MPAMVPM<n>_EL2 register. VPMR_MAX can be as large as 7 (8 registers) or 32 virtual PARTIDs. If MPAMIDR_EL1 .VPMR_MAX == 0, there is only a single MPAMVPM<n>_EL2 register, MPAMVPM0_EL2 . Virtual PARTID mapping is enabled by MPAMHCR_EL2 .EL1_VPMEN for the PARTID in MPAM1_EL1 and by MPAMHCR_EL2 .EL0_VPMEN for the PARTID in MPAM0_EL1 . A virtual-to-physical PARTID mapping entry, PhyPARTID<n>, is valid only when the MPAMVPMV_EL2 .VPM_V bit in bit position n is set to 1.

name: MPAMVPMV_EL2
long_name: MPAM Virtual Partition Mapping Valid Register
description: Valid bits for virtual PARTID mapping entries. Each bit m corresponds to virtual PARTID mapping entry m in the MPAMVPM<n>_EL2 registers where n = m >> 2.

fields.name: VPM_V<m>
fields.description: Contains valid bit for virtual PARTID mapping entry corresponding to virtual PARTID<m>.

name: MPIDR_EL1
description: In a multiprocessor system, provides an additional PE identification mechanism.

fields.name: Aff3
fields.description: Affinity level 3. See the description of Aff0 for more information. Aff3 is not supported in AArch32 state.

fields.name: U
fields.description: Indicates a Uniprocessor system, as distinct from PE 0 in a multiprocessor system.

values.label: Processor is part of a multiprocessor system.
Processor is part of a uniprocessor system.

fields.name: MT
fields.description: Indicates whether the lowest level of affinity consists of logical PEs that are implemented using an interdependent approach, such as multithreading. See the description of Aff0 for more information about affinity levels.

values.label: Performance of PEs with different affinity level 0 values, and the same values for affinity level 1 and higher, is largely independent.
Performance of PEs with different affinity level 0 values, and the same values for affinity level 1 and higher, is very interdependent.

fields.name: Aff2
fields.description: Affinity level 2. See the description of Aff0 for more information.

fields.name: Aff1
fields.description: Affinity level 1. See the description of Aff0 for more information.

fields.name: Aff0
fields.description: Affinity level 0. The value of the MPIDR .{Aff2, Aff1, Aff0} or MPIDR_EL1 .{Aff3, Aff2, Aff1, Aff0} set of fields of each PE must be unique within the system as a whole.

name: MVFR0_EL1
long_name: AArch32 Media and VFP Feature Register 0
description: Describes the features provided by the AArch32 Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation. Must be interpreted with MVFR1_EL1 and MVFR2_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: FPRound
fields.description: Floating-Point Rounding modes. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for rounding modes.

values.label: Not implemented, or only Round to Nearest mode supported, except that Round towards Zero mode is supported for VCVT instructions that always use that rounding mode regardless of the FPSCR setting.
All rounding modes supported.

fields.name: FPShVec
fields.description: Short Vectors. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for the use of short vectors.

values.label: Short vectors not supported.
Short vector operation supported.

fields.name: FPSqrt
fields.description: Square Root. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for the ARMv6 VFP square root operations.

values.label: Not supported in hardware.
Supported.

fields.name: FPDivide
fields.description: Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for VFP divide operations.

values.label: Not supported in hardware.
Supported.

fields.name: FPTrap
fields.description: Floating-Point Exception Trapping. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for exception trapping.

values.label: Not supported.
Supported.

fields.name: FPDP
fields.description: Floating-Point Double-Precision. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for double-precision operations.

values.label: Not supported in hardware.
Supported, VFPv2.
Supported, VFPv3, VFPv4, or Armv8. VFPv3 and Armv8 add an instruction to load a double-precision floating-point constant, and conversions between double-precision and fixed-point values.

fields.name: FPSP
fields.description: Floating-Point Single-Precision. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for single-precision operations.

values.label: Not supported in hardware.
Supported, VFPv2.
Supported, VFPv3 or VFPv4. VFPv3 adds an instruction to load a single-precision floating-point constant, and conversions between single-precision and fixed-point values.

fields.name: SIMDReg
fields.description: Advanced SIMD registers. Indicates whether the Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation provides support for the Advanced SIMD and floating-point register bank.

values.label: The implementation has no Advanced SIMD and floating-point support.
The implementation includes floating-point support with 16 x 64-bit registers.
The implementation includes Advanced SIMD and floating-point support with 32 x 64-bit registers.

name: MVFR1_EL1
long_name: AArch32 Media and VFP Feature Register 1
description: Describes the features provided by the AArch32 Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation. Must be interpreted with MVFR0_EL1 and MVFR2_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: SIMDFMAC
fields.description: Advanced SIMD Fused Multiply-Accumulate. Indicates whether the Advanced SIMD implementation provides fused multiply accumulate instructions.

values.label: Not implemented.
Implemented.

fields.name: FPHP
fields.description: Floating-Point Half-Precision. Indicates the level of half-precision floating-point support.

values.label: Not supported.
Floating-point half-precision conversion instructions are supported for conversion between single-precision and half-precision.
As for 0b0001 , and adds instructions for conversion between double-precision and half-precision.
As for 0b0010 , and adds support for half-precision floating-point arithmetic.

fields.name: SIMDHP
fields.description: Advanced SIMD Half-Precision. Indicates the level of half-precision floating-point support.

values.label: Not supported.
SIMD half-precision conversion instructions are supported for conversion between single-precision and half-precision.
As for 0b0001 , and adds support for half-precision floating-point arithmetic.

fields.name: SIMDSP
fields.description: Advanced SIMD Single-Precision. Indicates whether the Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation provides single-precision floating-point instructions.

values.label: Not implemented.
Implemented. This value is permitted only if the SIMDInt field is 0b0001 .

fields.name: SIMDInt
fields.description: Advanced SIMD Integer. Indicates whether the Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation provides integer instructions.

values.label: Not implemented.
Implemented.

fields.name: SIMDLS
fields.description: Advanced SIMD Load/Store. Indicates whether the Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation provides load/store instructions.

values.label: Not implemented.
Implemented.

fields.name: FPDNaN
fields.description: Default NaN mode. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support only for the Default NaN mode.

values.label: Not implemented, or hardware supports only the Default NaN mode.
Hardware supports propagation of NaN values.

fields.name: FPFtZ
fields.description: Flush to Zero mode. Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support only for the Flush-to-Zero mode of operation.

values.label: Not implemented, or hardware supports only the Flush-to-Zero mode of operation.
Hardware supports full denormalized number arithmetic.

name: MVFR2_EL1
long_name: AArch32 Media and VFP Feature Register 2
description: Describes the features provided by the AArch32 Advanced SIMD and floating-point implementation. Must be interpreted with MVFR0_EL1 and MVFR1_EL1 . For general information about the interpretation of the ID registers, see 'Principles of the ID scheme for fields in ID registers' .

fields.name: FPMisc
fields.description: Indicates whether the floating-point implementation provides support for miscellaneous VFP features.

values.label: Not implemented, or no support for miscellaneous features.
Support for Floating-point selection.
As 0b0001 , and Floating-point Conversion to Integer with Directed Rounding modes.
As 0b0010 , and Floating-point Round to Integer Floating-point.
As 0b0011 , and Floating-point MaxNum and MinNum.

fields.name: SIMDMisc
fields.description: Indicates whether the Advanced SIMD implementation provides support for miscellaneous Advanced SIMD features.

values.label: Not implemented, or no support for miscellaneous features.
Floating-point Conversion to Integer with Directed Rounding modes.
As 0b0001 , and Floating-point Round to Integer Floating-point.
As 0b0010 , and Floating-point MaxNum and MinNum.
