name: NZCV
description: Allows access to the condition flags.

fields.name: N
fields.description: Negative condition flag. Set to 1 if the result of the last flag-setting instruction was negative.

fields.name: Z
fields.description: Zero condition flag. Set to 1 if the result of the last flag-setting instruction was zero, and to 0 otherwise. A result of zero often indicates an equal result from a comparison.

fields.name: C
fields.description: Carry condition flag. Set to 1 if the last flag-setting instruction resulted in a carry condition, for example an unsigned overflow on an addition.

fields.name: V
fields.description: Overflow condition flag. Set to 1 if the last flag-setting instruction resulted in an overflow condition, for example a signed overflow on an addition.

name: OSDLR_EL1
description: Used to control the OS Double Lock.

values.label: OS Double Lock unlocked.
OS Double Lock locked, if DBGPRCR_EL1 .CORENPDRQ (Core no powerdown request) bit is set to 0 and the PE is in Non-debug state.

name: OSDTRRX_EL1
long_name: OS Lock Data Transfer Register, Receive
description: Used for save and restore of DBGDTRRX_EL0 . It is a component of the Debug Communications Channel.

fields.name: DTRRX
fields.description: Update DTRRX without side-effect. Writes to this register update the value in DTRRX and do not change RXfull. Reads of this register return the last value written to DTRRX and do not change RXfull. For the full behavior of the Debug Communications Channel, see 'The Debug Communication Channel and Instruction Transfer Register' .

name: OSDTRTX_EL1
long_name: OS Lock Data Transfer Register, Transmit
description: Used for save/restore of DBGDTRTX_EL0 . It is a component of the Debug Communications Channel.

fields.name: DTRTX
fields.description: Return DTRTX without side-effect. Reads of this register return the value in DTRTX and do not change TXfull. Writes of this register update the value in DTRTX and do not change TXfull. For the full behavior of the Debug Communications Channel, see 'The Debug Communication Channel and Instruction Transfer Register' .

name: OSECCR_EL1
long_name: OS Lock Exception Catch Control Register
description: Provides a mechanism for an operating system to access the contents of EDECCR that are otherwise invisible to software, so it can save/restore the contents of EDECCR over powerdown on behalf of the external debugger.

fields.name: EDECCR
fields.description: Used for save/restore to EDECCR over powerdown. Reads or writes to this field are indirect accesses to EDECCR .

name: OSLAR_EL1
description: Used to lock or unlock the OS Lock.

fields.name: OSLK
fields.description: On writes to OSLAR_EL1, bit[0] is copied to the OS Lock. Use OSLSR_EL1 .OSLK to check the current status of the lock.

name: OSLSR_EL1
description: Provides the status of the OS Lock.

fields.name: OSLM
fields.description: OS Lock model implemented. Identifies the form of OS save and restore mechanism implemented.

values.label: OS Lock not implemented.
OS Lock implemented.

fields.name: nTT
fields.description: Not 32-bit access. This bit is always RAZ. It indicates that a 32-bit access is needed to write the key to the OS Lock Access Register.

values.label: OS Lock unlocked.
OS Lock locked.

fields.name: OSLM[0]
fields.description: This field is bit[0] of OSLM[1:0]. See OSLM[1] for the field description.

name: PAN
description: Allows access to the Privileged Access Never bit.

values.label: Privileged reads and write are not disabled by this mechanism.
Disables privileged read and write accesses to addresses accessible at EL0 for an enabled stage 1 translation regime that defines the EL0 permissions.

name: PAR_EL1
description: Returns the output address (OA) from an Address translation instruction that executed successfully, or fault information if the instruction did not execute successfully.

fields.name: PA
fields.description: Output address. The output address (OA) corresponding to the supplied input address. This field returns address bits[55:12].

fields.name: D128
fields.description: Indicates if the PAR_EL1 uses the 128-bit format.

values.label: PAR_EL1 uses the 128-bit format. PAR_EL1[127:0] holds valid data.

fields.name: ATTR
fields.description:
Memory attributes for the returned output address. This field uses the same encoding as the Attr<n> fields in MAIR_EL1 , MAIR_EL2 , and MAIR_EL3 . If FEAT_MTE_PERM is implemented and the instruction performed a stage 2 translation, the following additional encoding is defined: ATTR Meaning 0b11100000 Tagged NoTagAccess Normal Inner Write-Back, Outer Write-Back,
Read-Allocate, Write-Allocate Non-transient memory. This encoding in MAIR_ELx is Reserved. The value returned in this field can be the resulting attribute that is actually implemented by the implementation, as determined by any permitted implementation choices and any applicable configuration bits, instead of the value that appears in the Translation table descriptor. The attributes presented are consistent with the stages of translation applied in the address translation instruction. If the instruction performed a stage 1 translation only, the attributes are from the stage 1 translation. If the instruction performed a stage 1 and stage 2 translation, the attributes are from the combined stage 1 and stage 2 translation.

fields.name: NSE
fields.description: Reports the NSE attribute for a translation table descriptor from the EL3 translation regime. For a description of the values derived by evaluating NS and NSE together, see PAR_EL1.NS. For a result from a Secure, Non-secure, or Realm translation regime, this bit is unknown.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Non-secure. The NS attribute for a translation table entry from a Secure translation regime, a Realm translation regime, and the EL3 translation regime.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Non-secure. The NS attribute for a translation table entry from a Secure translation regime.

fields.name: SH
fields.description: Shareability attribute, for the returned output address.

values.label: Non-shareable.
Outer Shareable.
Inner Shareable.

fields.name: F
fields.description: Indicates whether the instruction performed a successful address translation.

values.label: Address translation completed successfully.

name: PFAR_EL1
description: Records the faulting physical address for a synchronous External abort, or SError exception taken to EL1.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with PFAR_EL1.NSE, reports the physical address space of the access that triggered the exception. NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 When Secure state is implemented, Secure. Otherwise reserved. 0b0 0b1 Non-secure. 0b1 0b0 Reserved. 0b1 0b1 Realm.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Non-secure. Reports the physical address space of the access that triggered the exception.

values.label: Secure physical address space.
Non-secure physical address space.

fields.name: NSE
fields.description: Together with PFAR_EL1.NS, reports the physical address space of the access that triggered the exception. For a description of the values derived by evaluating NS and NSE together, see MFAR_EL3.NS.

fields.name: PA[55:52]
fields.description: When FEAT_D128 is implemented, extension to PFAR_EL1.PA[47:0].

fields.name: PA[51:48]
fields.description: When FEAT_LPA is implemented, extension to PFAR_EL1.PA[47:0].

fields.name: PA
fields.description: Physical Address. Bits [47:0] of the aborting physical address.

name: PFAR_EL2
description: Records the faulting physical address for a synchronous External abort, or SError exception taken to EL2.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with PFAR_EL2.NSE and PFAR_EL2.NSE2, reports the physical address space of the access that triggered the exception. NSE2 NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 0b0 When Secure state is implemented, Secure. Otherwise reserved. 0b0 0b0 0b1 Non-secure. 0b0 0b1 0b0 Reserved. 0b0 0b1 0b1 Realm. 0b1 0b0 0b0 System Agent. 0b1 0b0 0b1 NS Protected. 0b1 0b1 0b0 Reserved. 0b1 0b1 0b1 Reserved.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Together with PFAR_EL2.NSE, reports the physical address space of the access that triggered the exception. NSE NS Meaning 0b0 0b0 When Secure state is implemented, Secure. Otherwise reserved. 0b0 0b1 Non-secure. 0b1 0b0 Reserved. 0b1 0b1 Realm.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Non-secure. Reports the physical address space of the access that triggered the exception.

values.label: Secure physical address space.
Non-secure physical address space.

fields.name: NSE
fields.description: Together with PFAR_EL2.NS, reports the physical address space of the access that triggered the exception. For a description of the values derived by evaluating NS and NSE together, see MFAR_EL3.NS.

fields.name: NSE2
fields.description: Together with PFAR_EL2.NS and PFAR_EL2.NSE, reports the physical address space of the access that triggered the exception. For a description of the values derived by evaluating NS, NSE, and NSE2 together, see PFAR_EL2.NS.

fields.name: PA[55:52]
fields.description: When FEAT_D128 is implemented, extension to PFAR_EL2.PA[47:0].

fields.name: PA[51:48]
fields.description: When FEAT_LPA is implemented, extension to PFAR_EL2.PA[47:0].

fields.name: PA
fields.description: Physical Address. Bits [47:0] of the aborting physical address.

name: PM
description: Allows access to the Profiling exception Mask bit.

values.label: Does not cause the Profiling exception to be masked.
Causes the Profiling exception to be masked.

name: PMBIDR_EL1
description: Provides information to software as to whether the buffer can be programmed at the current Exception level.

fields.name: MaxBuffSize
fields.description: Maximum supported Profiling Buffer size. Reserved for software use.

fields.name: EA
fields.description: External Abort handling. Describes how the PE manages External aborts on writes made by the Statistical Profiling Unit to the Profiling Buffer.

values.label: Not described.
The PE ignores External aborts on writes made by the Statistical Profiling Unit.
An External abort on a write made by the Statistical Profiling Unit generates an asynchronous SError exception at the PE.

fields.name: AddrMode
fields.description: Address Modes. Describes the addressing modes available for the Profiling Buffer.

values.label: Only virtual address mode is supported.
Virtual and physical address modes are supported.
Reserved for software use under virtualization, to show that only physical address mode is supported.

fields.name: F
fields.description: Flag updates. Describes how address translations performed by the Statistical Profiling Unit manage the Access flag and dirty state.

values.label: Hardware management of the Access flag and dirty state for accesses made by the Statistical Profiling Unit is always disabled for all translation stages.

Hardware management of the Access flag and dirty state for accesses made by the Statistical Profiling Unit is controlled in the same way as explicit memory accesses in the Profiling Buffer owning translation regime.

fields.name: P
fields.description: Programming not allowed. When read at EL3, this field reads as zero. Otherwise, indicates that the Profiling Buffer owning Exception level is a higher Exception level or the Profiling Buffer owning Security state is not the current Security state.

values.label: Programming is allowed.
Programming not allowed.

fields.name: Align
fields.description: Defines the minimum alignment constraint for writes to PMBPTR_EL1 .

values.label: Byte.
Halfword.
Word.
Doubleword.
16 bytes.
32 bytes.
64 bytes.
128 bytes.
256 bytes.
512 bytes.
1KB.
2KB.

name: PMBLIMITR_EL1
description: Defines the upper limit for the profiling buffer, and enables the profiling buffer

fields.name: LIMIT
fields.description: Limit address. PMBLIMITR_EL1.LIMIT:Zeros(12) is the address of the first byte in memory after the last byte in the profiling buffer. If the smallest implemented translation granule is not 4KB, then bits[N-1:12] are RES0 , where N is the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED value, Log 2 (smallest implemented translation granule).

values.label: The Profiling Buffer pointers are virtual addresses.

The Profiling Buffer pointers are: Physical address in the owning security state if the owning translation regime has no stage 2 translation. Intermediate physical addresses in the owning security state if the owning translation regime has stage 2 translations.

fields.name: PMFZ
fields.description: Freeze PMU on SPE event. Stop PMU event counters when PMBSR_EL1 .S == 1.

values.label: Do not freeze PMU event counters on Statistical Profiling Buffer Management event.
Freeze PMU event counters on Statistical Profiling Buffer Management event.
Fill mode. Stop collection and raise maintenance interrupt on buffer fill.
Discard mode. All output is discarded.
All output is discarded.
Profiling buffer enabled.

name: PMBMAR_EL1
description: Controls Statistical Profiling Unit accesses to memory. If the Profiling Buffer pointers specify virtual addresses, the address properties are defined by the translation tables and this register is ignored.

fields.name: SH
fields.description: Profiling Buffer shareability domain. Defines the shareability domain for Normal memory used by the Profiling Buffer.

values.label: Non-shareable.
Outer Shareable.
Inner Shareable.

fields.name: Attr
fields.description:
Profiling Buffer memory type and attributes. Defines the memory type and, for Normal memory, the cacheability attributes, for memory addressed by the Profiling Buffer. The encoding of this field is the same as that of a MAIR_ELx .Attr<n> field, as follows: Attr Meaning 0b0000dd00 Device memory.
See encoding of 'dd' for the type of Device memory. 0b0000dd01 If FEAT_XS is implemented:
Device memory with the XS attribute set to 0.
See encoding of 'dd' for the type of Device memory.
Otherwise, UNPREDICTABLE . 0b0000dd1x UNPREDICTABLE . 0booooiiii where oooo != 0000
and iiii != 0000 Normal memory. See encoding of 'oooo' and 'iiii' for the
type of Normal memory. 0b01000000 If FEAT_XS is implemented:
Normal Inner Non-cacheable, Outer Non-cacheable memory
with the XS attribute set to 0.
Otherwise, UNPREDICTABLE . 0b10100000 If FEAT_XS is implemented:
Normal Inner Write-through Cacheable, Outer Write-through Cacheable,
Read-Allocate, No-Write Allocate, Non-transient memory
with the XS attribute set to 0.
Otherwise, UNPREDICTABLE . 0b11110000 If FEAT_MTE2 is implemented:
Tagged Normal Inner Write-Back, Outer Write-Back,
Read-Allocate, Write-Allocate Non-transient memory.
Otherwise, UNPREDICTABLE . 0bxxxx0000 where xxxx != 0000
and xxxx != 0100
and xxxx != 1010
and xxxx != 1111 UNPREDICTABLE . dd is encoded as follows: 'dd' Meaning 0b00 Device-nGnRnE memory. 0b01 Device-nGnRE memory. 0b10 Device-nGRE memory. 0b11 Device-GRE memory. oooo is encoded as follows: 'oooo' Meaning 0b0000 See encoding of Attr. 0b00RW where RW != 00 Normal memory, Outer Write-Through Transient. 0b0100 Normal memory, Outer Non-cacheable. 0b01RW where RW != 00 Normal memory, Outer Write-Back Transient. 0b10RW Normal memory, Outer Write-Through Non-transient. 0b11RW Normal memory, Outer Write-Back Non-transient. R encodes the Outer Read-Allocate policy and W encodes the Outer Write-Allocate policy. iiii is encoded as follows: 'iiii' Meaning 0b0000 See encoding of Attr. 0b00RW where RW != 00 Normal memory, Inner Write-Through Transient. 0b0100 Normal memory, Inner Non-cacheable. 0b01RW where RW != 00 Normal memory, Inner Write-Back Transient. 0b10RW Normal memory, Inner Write-Through Non-transient. 0b11RW Normal memory, Inner Write-Back Non-transient. R encodes the Inner Read-Allocate policy and W encodes the Inner Write-Allocate policy. In oooo and iiii , R and W are encoded as follows: 'R' or 'W' Meaning 0b0 No Allocate. 0b1 Allocate. When FEAT_XS is implemented, stage 1 Inner Write-Back Cacheable, Outer Write-Back Cacheable memory types have the XS attribute set to 0.

name: PMBPTR_EL1
description: Defines the current write pointer for the profiling buffer.

fields.name: PTR
fields.description: Current write address. Defines the virtual address of the next entry to be written to the buffer. If PMBIDR_EL1 .Align is not zero, then it is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether bits [M-1:0] are RES0 or read/write, where M is an integer between 1 and PMBIDR_EL1 .Align inclusive. The architecture places restrictions on the values software can write to the pointer when the SPU is not in Discard mode. For more information see 'Restrictions on the current write pointer' . On a management interrupt, PMBPTR_EL1 is frozen.

name: PMBSR_EL1
description: Provides syndrome information to software for a Profiling Buffer management event.

fields.name: MSS2
fields.description: Management event Specific Syndrome 2. Contains syndrome specific to the management event. The syndrome contents for each management event are described in the following sections.

fields.name: EC
fields.description: Event class. Top-level description of the cause of the Profiling Buffer management event.

values.label: Other Profiling Buffer management event. All Profiling Buffer management events other than those described by the other defined Event class codes.

Granule Protection Check fault on write to Profiling Buffer, other than Granule Protection Fault (GPF). That is, any of the following: Granule Protection Table (GPT) address size fault. GPT walk fault. External abort on GPT fetch. A GPF on translation table walk or update is reported as either a Stage 1 or Stage 2 Data Abort, as appropriate. Other GPFs are reported as a Stage 1 Data Abort.
Profiling Buffer management event for an IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED reason.
Stage 1 Data Abort on write to Profiling Buffer.
Stage 2 Data Abort on write to Profiling Buffer.

fields.name: DL
fields.description: Partial record lost. Following a buffer management event, indicates whether the last record written to the Profiling Buffer is complete. PMBSR_EL1.DL is also set to 1 when an asynchronous External abort is reported to the SPU.

values.label: PMBPTR_EL1 points to the first byte after the last complete record written to the Profiling Buffer by the SPU, and no asynchronous External abort has been reported to the SPU.

Part of a record was lost because of a Profiling Buffer management event, or an asynchronous External abort has been reported to the SPU. PMBPTR_EL1 might not be the address of the first byte after the last complete sample record written to the Profiling Buffer by the SPU.
An External abort has not been asserted.
An External abort has been asserted and detected by the Statistical Profiling Unit.

fields.name: S
fields.description: Service. Indicates that a Profiling Buffer management event has been recorded.

values.label: No Profiling Buffer management event for EL1 has been recorded.
A Profiling Buffer management event for EL1 has been recorded.
No collision events detected.
At least one collision event was recorded.

fields.description: Management Event Specific Syndrome. Contains syndrome specific to the Profiling Buffer management event. The syndrome contents for each Profiling Buffer management event are described in the following sections.

fields.description: Management Event Specific Syndrome. Contains syndrome specific to the Profiling Buffer management event. The syndrome contents for each Profiling Buffer management event are described in the following sections.

name: PMCCFILTR_EL0
long_name: Performance Monitors Cycle Count Filter Register
description: Determines the modes in which the Cycle Counter, PMCCNTR_EL0 , increments.

fields.name: VS
fields.description: SVE mode filtering. Controls counting cycles in Streaming and Non-streaming SVE modes.

values.label: This mechanism has no effect on the filtering of cycles.
The PE does not count cycles in Streaming SVE mode.
The PE does not count cycles in Non-streaming SVE mode.

fields.name: P
fields.description: EL1 filtering. Controls counting cycles in EL1.

values.label: This mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles.
The PE does not count cycles in EL1.

fields.name: U
fields.description: EL0 filtering. Controls counting cycles in EL0.

values.label: This mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles.
The PE does not count cycles in EL0.

fields.name: NSK
fields.description: Non-secure EL1 filtering. Controls counting cycles in Non-secure EL1. If PMCCFILTR_EL0.NSK is not equal to PMCCFILTR_EL0.P, then the PE does not count cycles in Non-secure EL1. Otherwise, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles in Non-secure EL1.

values.label: When PMCCFILTR_EL0.P == 0, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.P == 1, the PE does not count cycles in Non-secure EL1.
When PMCCFILTR_EL0.P == 0, the PE does not count cycles in Non-secure EL1. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.P == 1, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles.

fields.name: NSU
fields.description: Non-secure EL0 filtering. Controls counting cycles in Non-secure EL0. If PMCCFILTR_EL0.NSU is not equal to PMCCFILTR_EL0.U, then the PE does not count cycles in Non-secure EL0. Otherwise, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles in Non-secure EL0.

values.label: When PMCCFILTR_EL0.U == 0, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.U == 1, the PE does not count cycles in Non-secure EL0.
When PMCCFILTR_EL0.U == 0, the PE does not count cycles in Non-secure EL0. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.U == 1, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles.

fields.name: NSH
fields.description: EL2 filtering. Controls counting cycles in EL2.

values.label: The PE does not count cycles in EL2.
This mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles.

fields.name: M
fields.description: EL3 filtering. Controls counting cycles in  EL3. If PMCCFILTR_EL0.M is not equal to PMCCFILTR_EL0.P, then the PE does not count cycles in  EL3. Otherwise, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles in  EL3.

values.label: When PMCCFILTR_EL0.P == 0, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.P == 1, the PE does not count cycles in  EL3.
When PMCCFILTR_EL0.P == 0, the PE does not count cycles in  EL3. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.P == 1, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles.

fields.name: SH
fields.description: Secure EL2 filtering. Controls counting cycles in Secure EL2. If PMCCFILTR_EL0.SH is equal to PMCCFILTR_EL0.NSH, then the PE does not count cycles in Secure EL2. Otherwise, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles in Secure EL2.

values.label: When PMCCFILTR_EL0.NSH == 0, the PE does not count cycles in Secure EL2. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.NSH == 1, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles.
When PMCCFILTR_EL0.NSH == 0, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.NSH == 1, the PE does not count cycles in Secure EL2.

fields.name: RLK
fields.description: Realm EL1 filtering. Controls counting cycles in Realm EL1. If PMCCFILTR_EL0.RLK is not equal to PMCCFILTR_EL0.P, then the PE does not count cycles in Realm EL1. Otherwise, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles in Realm EL1.

values.label: When PMCCFILTR_EL0.P == 0, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.P == 1, the PE does not count cycles in Realm EL1.
When PMCCFILTR_EL0.P == 0, the PE does not count cycles in Realm EL1. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.P == 1, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles.

fields.name: RLU
fields.description: Realm EL0 filtering. Controls counting cycles in Realm EL0. If PMCCFILTR_EL0.RLU is not equal to PMCCFILTR_EL0.U, then the PE does not count cycles in Realm EL0. Otherwise, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles in Realm EL0.

values.label: When PMCCFILTR_EL0.U == 0, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.U == 1, the PE does not count cycles in Realm EL0.
When PMCCFILTR_EL0.U == 0, the PE does not count cycles in Realm EL0. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.U == 1, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles.

fields.name: RLH
fields.description: Realm EL2 filtering. Controls counting cycles in Realm EL2. If PMCCFILTR_EL0.RLH is equal to PMCCFILTR_EL0.NSH, then the PE does not count cycles in Realm EL2. Otherwise, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles in Realm EL2.

values.label: When PMCCFILTR_EL0.NSH == 0, the PE does not count cycles in Realm EL2. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.NSH == 1, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles.
When PMCCFILTR_EL0.NSH == 0, this mechanism has no effect on filtering of cycles. When PMCCFILTR_EL0.NSH == 1, the PE does not count cycles in Realm EL2.

name: PMCCNTR_EL0
description: Holds the value of the processor Cycle Counter, CCNT, that counts processor clock cycles. See 'Time as measured by the Performance Monitors cycle counter' for more information. PMCCFILTR_EL0 determines the modes and states in which the PMCCNTR_EL0 can increment.

fields.name: CCNT
fields.description: Cycle count. Depending on the values of PMCR_EL0 .{LC,D}, this field increments in one of the following ways: Every processor clock cycle. Every 64th processor clock cycle. Writing 1 to PMCR_EL0 .C sets this field to 0.

name: PMCCNTSVR_EL1
long_name: Performance Monitors Cycle Count Saved Value Register
description: Captures the PMU Cycle counter, PMCCNTR_EL0 .

fields.name: CCNT
fields.description: Sampled Cycle Count. The value of PMCCNTR_EL0 at the last successful Capture event.

name: PMCEID0_EL0
long_name: Performance Monitors Common Event Identification Register 0
description: Defines which Common architectural events and Common microarchitectural events are implemented, or counted, using PMU events in the ranges 0x0000 to 0x001F and 0x4000 to 0x401F . For more information about the Common events and the use of the PMCEID<n>_EL0 registers see 'The PMU event number space and common events' .

fields.name: IDhi<n>
fields.description: IDhi[n] corresponds to Common event ( 0x4000 + n). For each bit:

values.label: The Common event is not implemented, or not counted.
The Common event is implemented.

fields.name: ID<n>
fields.description: ID[n] corresponds to Common event n. For each bit:

values.label: The Common event is not implemented, or not counted.
The Common event is implemented.

name: PMCEID1_EL0
long_name: Performance Monitors Common Event Identification Register 1
description: Defines which Common architectural events and Common microarchitectural events are implemented, or counted, using PMU events in the ranges 0x0020 to 0x003F and 0x4020 to 0x403F . For more information about the Common events and the use of the PMCEID<n>_EL0 registers see 'The PMU event number space and common events' .

fields.name: IDhi<n>
fields.description: IDhi[n] corresponds to Common event ( 0x4020 + n). For each bit:

values.label: The Common event is not implemented, or not counted.
The Common event is implemented.

fields.name: ID<n>
fields.description: ID[n] corresponds to Common event ( 0x0020 + n). For each bit:

values.label: The Common event is not implemented, or not counted.
The Common event is implemented.

name: PMCNTENCLR_EL0
long_name: Performance Monitors Count Enable Clear Register
description: Allows software to disable the following counters: The cycle counter PMCCNTR_EL0 . The event counters PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 . When FEAT_PMUv3_ICNTR is implemented, the instruction counter PMICNTR_EL0 . Reading from this register shows which counters are enabled.

fields.name: F0
fields.description: PMICNTR_EL0 disable. On writes, allows software to disable PMICNTR_EL0 . On reads, returns the PMICNTR_EL0 enable status.

values.label: PMICNTR_EL0 disabled.
PMICNTR_EL0 enabled.

fields.name: C
fields.description: PMCCNTR_EL0 disable. On writes, allows software to disable PMCCNTR_EL0 . On reads, returns the PMCCNTR_EL0 enable status.

values.label: PMCCNTR_EL0 disabled.
PMCCNTR_EL0 enabled.

fields.name: P<m>
fields.description: PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 disable. On writes, allows software to disable PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 . On reads, returns the PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 enable status.

values.label: PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 disabled.
PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 enabled.

name: PMCNTENSET_EL0
long_name: Performance Monitors Count Enable Set Register
description: Allows software to enable the following counters: The cycle counter PMCCNTR_EL0 . The event counters PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 . When FEAT_PMUv3_ICNTR is implemented, the instruction counter PMICNTR_EL0 . Reading from this register shows which counters are enabled.

fields.name: F0
fields.description: PMICNTR_EL0 enable. On writes, allows software to enable PMICNTR_EL0 . On reads, returns the PMICNTR_EL0 enable status.

values.label: PMICNTR_EL0 disabled.
PMICNTR_EL0 enabled.

fields.name: C
fields.description: PMCCNTR_EL0 enable. On writes, allows software to enable PMCCNTR_EL0 . On reads, returns the PMCCNTR_EL0 enable status.

values.label: PMCCNTR_EL0 disabled.
PMCCNTR_EL0 enabled.

fields.name: P<m>
fields.description: PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 enable. On writes, allows software to enable PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 . On reads, returns the PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 enable status.

values.label: PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 disabled.
PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 enabled.

name: PMCR_EL0
description: Provides details of the Performance Monitors implementation, including the number of counters implemented, and configures and controls the counters.

fields.name: FZS
fields.description: Freeze-on-SPE event. Stop counters when PMBLIMITR_EL1 .{PMFZ,E} is {1,1} and profiling is stopped.

values.label: Do not freeze on a Statistical Profiling Buffer Management event.
Affected counters do not count following a Statistical Profiling Buffer Management event.

fields.name: IMP
fields.description: Implementer code. If this field is zero, then PMCR_EL0.IDCODE is RES0 and software must use MIDR_EL1 to identify the PE. Otherwise, this field and PMCR_EL0.IDCODE identify the PMU implementation to software. The implementer codes are allocated by Arm. A nonzero value has the same interpretation as MIDR_EL1 .Implementer.

fields.name: IDCODE
fields.description: Identification code. Arm deprecates use of this field. Each implementer must maintain a list of identification codes that are specific to the implementer. A specific implementation is identified by the combination of the implementer code and the identification code.

fields.name: N
fields.description: Indicates the number of event counters implemented. This value is in the range of 0b00000 - 0b11111 . If the value is 0b00000 , then only PMCCNTR_EL0 is implemented. If the value is 0b11111 , then PMCCNTR_EL0 and 31 event counters are implemented. When EL2 is implemented and enabled for the current Security state, reads of this field from EL1 and EL0 return the Effective value of MDCR_EL2 .HPMN.

fields.name: FZO
fields.description: Freeze-on-overflow. Stop event counters on overflow.

values.label: Do not freeze on overflow.

Affected counters do not count when any of the following applies: For any event counter PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 in the first range, PMOVSCLR_EL0 [m] is 1, and either FEAT_SEBEP is not implemented or PMEVTYPER<m>_EL0 .SYNC is 0. FEAT_PMUv3_ICNTR is implemented, PMOVSCLR_EL0 .F0 is 1, and either FEAT_SEBEP is not implemented or PMICFILTR_EL0 .SYNC is 0.

fields.name: LP
fields.description: Long event counter enable. Determines when unsigned overflow is recorded by PMOVSCLR_EL0 .P[n].

values.label: Event counter overflow on increment that causes unsigned overflow of PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 [31:0].
Event counter overflow on increment that causes unsigned overflow of PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 [63:0].

fields.name: LC
fields.description: Long cycle counter enable. Determines when unsigned overflow is recorded by PMOVSCLR_EL0 .C.

values.label: Cycle counter overflow on increment that causes unsigned overflow of PMCCNTR_EL0 [31:0].
Cycle counter overflow on increment that causes unsigned overflow of PMCCNTR_EL0 [63:0].

fields.name: DP
fields.description: Disable cycle counter when event counting is prohibited.

values.label: Cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is not affected by this mechanism.

Cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is disabled in prohibited regions and when event counting is frozen: If FEAT_PMUv3p1 is implemented, EL2 is implemented, and MDCR_EL2 .HPMD  is 1, then cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is disabled at EL2. If FEAT_SPE_DPFZS is implemented and event counting is frozen by PMCR_EL0.FZS, then cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is disabled. If FEAT_PMUv3p7 is implemented and event counting is frozen by PMCR_EL0.FZO, then cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is disabled. If FEAT_PMUv3p7 is implemented, EL3 is implemented, and MDCR_EL3 .MPMX is 1, then cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is disabled at EL3. If EL3 is implemented, MDCR_EL3 .SPME  is 0, and either FEAT_PMUv3p7 is not implemented or MDCR_EL3 .MPMX is 0, then cycle counting by PMCCNTR_EL0 is disabled at EL3 and in Secure state.

fields.name: X
fields.description: Enable export of events in an IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED PMU event export bus.

values.label: Do not export events.
Export events where not prohibited.
When enabled, PMCCNTR_EL0 counts every clock cycle.
When enabled, PMCCNTR_EL0 counts once every 64 clock cycles.

fields.name: C
fields.description: Cycle counter reset. The effects of writing to this field are:

values.label: No action.
Reset PMCCNTR_EL0 to zero.
No action.
Reset all affected event counters PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 to zero.
Affected counters are disabled and do not count.
Affected counters are enabled by PMCNTENSET_EL0 .

name: PMECR_EL1
description: Provides EL1 configuration options for the Performance Monitors.

fields.name: SSE
fields.description: Snapshot Enable. Controls the generation of Capture events.

values.label: Capture events are disabled.
Capture events are enabled and prohibited.
Capture events are enabled and allowed.

fields.name: KPME
fields.description: Local (Kernel) PMU Exception Enable. Enables PMU Profiling exceptions taken to the current Exception level.

values.label: PMU Profiling exceptions taken to the current Exception level are disabled.
PMU Profiling exceptions taken to the current Exception level are not affected by this field.

fields.name: PMEE
fields.description: Performance Monitors Exception Enable. Controls the generation of the PMUIRQ signal and the PMU Profiling exception at EL0 and EL1.

values.label: The PMUIRQ signal is asserted on a PMU overflow, and the PMU Profiling exception is disabled.
The PMUIRQ signal is deasserted, and the PMU Profiling exception is enabled.

name: PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0
description: Holds event counter n, which counts events, where n is 0 to 30.

fields.name: EVCNT
fields.description: Event counter n. Value of event counter n, where n is the number of this register and is a number from 0 to 30.

name: PMEVCNTSVR<n>_EL1
long_name: Performance Monitors Event Count Saved Value Registers
description: Captures the PMU Event counter <n>, PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 .

fields.name: EVCNT
fields.description: Sampled Event Count. The value of PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 at the last successful Capture event.

name: PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0
description: Configures event counter n, where n is 0 to 30.

fields.name: TC
fields.description: Threshold Control. Defines the threshold function. In the description of this field: V B [n] is the value the event specified by PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 would increment event counter n by on a processor cycle if the threshold function is disabled. For odd values of n, V[n-1] is the value that event counter n-1 increments by on the same processor cycle. V[n-1] is the result of applying the threshold and edge functions on event counter n-1. If event counter n-1 is disabled, then V[n-1] is zero. V[n-1] is not defined for even values of n. TH[n] is the value of PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.TH.

values.label: Not-equal. The counter increments by V B [n] on each processor cycle when V B [n] is not equal to TH[n].
Not-equal, count. The counter increments by 1 on each processor cycle when V B [n] is not equal to TH[n].
Equals. The counter increments by V B [n] on each processor cycle when V B [n] is equal to TH[n].
Equals, count. The counter increments by 1 on each processor cycle when V B [n] is equal to TH[n].
Greater-than-or-equal. The counter increments by V B [n] on each processor cycle when V B [n] is greater than or equal to TH[n].
Greater-than-or-equal, count. The counter increments by 1 on each processor cycle when V B [n] is greater than or equal to TH[n].
Less-than. The counter increments by V B [n] on each processor cycle when V B [n] is less than TH[n].
Less-than, count. The counter increments by 1 on each processor cycle when V B [n] is less than TH[n].

fields.name: TC
fields.description: Threshold Control. Defines the threshold function. In the description of this field: V B [n] is the value the event specified by PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 would increment event counter n by on a processor cycle if the threshold function is disabled. V[n-1] is the value that event counter n-1 increments by on the same processor cycle. V[n-1] is the result of applying the threshold and edge functions on event counter n-1. If event counter n-1 is disabled, then V[n-1] is zero. TH[n] is the value of PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.TH.

values.label: Not-equal. The counter increments by V[n-1] on each processor cycle when V B [n] is not equal to TH[n].
Equals. The counter increments by V[n-1] on each processor cycle when V B [n] is equal to TH[n].
Greater-than-or-equal. The counter increments by V[n-1] on each processor cycle when V B [n] is greater than or equal to TH[n].
Less-than. The counter increments by V[n-1] on each processor cycle when V B [n] is less than TH[n].

fields.name: TC
fields.description: Threshold Control. Defines the threshold function. In the description of this field: V B [n] is the value the event specified by PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 would increment event counter n by on a processor cycle if the threshold function is disabled. For odd values of n, V[n-1] is the value that event counter n-1 increments by on the same processor cycle. V[n-1] is the result of applying the threshold and edge functions on event counter n-1. If event counter n-1 is disabled, then V[n-1] is zero. V[n-1] is not defined for even values of n. TH[n] is the value of PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.TH.

values.label: Equal to not-equal. The counter increments on each processor cycle when V B [n] is not equal to TH[n] and V B [n] was equal to TH[n] on the previous processor cycle.

Equal to/from not-equal. The counter increments on each processor cycle when either: V B [n] is not equal to TH[n]  and V B [n] was equal to TH[n] on the previous processor cycle. V B [n] is equal to TH[n] and V B [n] was not equal to TH[n] on the previous processor cycle.
Not-equal to equal. The counter increments on each processor cycle when V B [n] is equal to TH[n] and V B [n] was not equal to TH[n] on the previous processor cycle.
Less-than to greater-than-or-equal. The counter increments on each processor cycle when V B [n] is greater than or equal to TH[n] and V B [n] was less than TH[n] on the previous processor cycle.

Less-than to/from greater-than-or-equal. The counter increments on each processor cycle when either: V B [n] is greater than or equal to TH[n] and V B [n] was less than TH[n] on the previous processor cycle. V B [n] is less than TH[n] and V B [n] was greater than or equal to TH[n] on the previous processor cycle.
Greater-than-or-equal to less-than. The counter increments on each processor cycle when V B [n] is less than TH[n] and V B [n] was greater than or equal to TH[n] on the previous processor cycle.

fields.name: TE
fields.description: Threshold Edge. Enables the edge condition. When PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.TE is 1, the event counter increments on cycles when the result of the threshold condition changes. See PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.TC for more information.

values.label: Threshold edge condition disabled.
Threshold edge condition enabled.

fields.name: SYNC

values.label: Asynchronous PMU Profiling exception is enabled.
Synchronous PMU Profiling exception is enabled.

fields.name: VS
fields.description: SVE mode filtering. Controls counting events in Streaming and Non-streaming SVE modes.

values.label: This mechanism has no effect on the filtering of events.
The PE does not count events in Streaming SVE mode.
The PE does not count events in Non-streaming SVE mode.

fields.name: TLC
fields.description: Threshold Linking Control. Extends PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.TC with additional controls for event linking. See PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.TC.

values.label: Threshold linking disabled.

Threshold linking enabled. If the threshold condition described by PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.TC is false, the counter increments by V[n-1]. Otherwise, the counter increments as described by PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.TC.
Threshold linking enabled. If the threshold condition described by PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.TC is true, the counter increments by V[n-1]. Otherwise, the counter does not increment.

fields.name: TH

fields.name: evtCount[15:10]
fields.description: Extension to evtCount[9:0]. For more information, see evtCount[9:0].

fields.name: evtCount[9:0]
fields.description: Event to count. The event number of the event that is counted by event counter PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 . The ranges of event numbers allocated to each type of event are shown in 'Allocation of the PMU event number space' . If FEAT_PMUv3p8 is implemented and PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.evtCount is programmed to an event that is reserved or not supported by the PE, no events are counted and the value returned by a direct or external read of the PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.evtCount field is the value written to the field. Arm recommends this behavior for all implementations of FEAT_PMUv3 . Otherwise, if PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.evtCount is programmed to an event that is reserved or not supported by the PE, the behavior depends on the value written: For the range 0x0000 to 0x003F , no events are counted and the value returned by a direct or external read of the PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.evtCount field is the value written to the field. If FEAT_PMUv3p1 is implemented, for the range 0x4000 to 0x403F , no events are counted and the value returned by a direct or external read of the PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.evtCount field is the value written to the field. For other values, it is UNPREDICTABLE what event, if any, is counted and the value returned by a direct or external read of the PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0.evtCount field is UNKNOWN . UNPREDICTABLE means the event must not expose privileged information.

fields.description: Unsigned overflow flag for PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 set. On writes, allows software to set the unsigned overflow flag for PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 to 1. On reads, returns the unsigned overflow flag for PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 overflow status.

values.label: PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 has not overflowed.
PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 has overflowed.

name: PMSCR_EL1
description: Provides EL1 controls for Statistical Profiling.

fields.name: EnVM
fields.description: Reserved for software use in nested virtualization. See also PMSCR_EL2 .EnVM.

fields.name: KE
fields.description: Kernel exception enable for SPE Profiling exceptions taken to EL1.

values.label: SPE Profiling exceptions taken to EL1 are always masked at EL1.
Enabled SPE Profiling exceptions taken to EL1 are masked at EL1 when PSTATE.PM is 1 and unmasked when PSTATE.PM is 0.

Disabled. SPE Profiling exceptions for EL1 are disabled. All of the following apply: Unless enabled by a higher Exception level, SPE Profiling exceptions are not generated. PMBSR_EL1 .S drives the interrupt request signal PMBIRQ . Accesses to PMBSR_EL1 at EL1 ignore the value of HCR_EL2 .NV1.

Reserved for software use in nested virtualization. Behaves as 0b00 for the purpose of controlling the SPE Profiling exception and interrupt request signal PMBIRQ , and as 0b11 for the purpose of accesses to PMBSR_EL1 .
Reserved for software use in nested virtualization. Behaves as 0b11 for the purposes of controlling the SPE Profiling exception and interrupt request signal PMBIRQ , and accesses to PMBSR_EL1 .

Enabled. SPE Profiling exceptions for EL1 are enabled, as follows: All Profiling Buffer management events are recorded in PMBSR_EL1 , unless they are configured to be recorded in PMBSR_EL3 by MDCR_EL3 .PMSEE or PMBSR_EL2 by PMSCR_EL2 .EE. SPE Profiling exceptions are generated and taken to EL1 when unmasked and PMBSR_EL1 .S is 1, unless the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, in which case the exception is taken to EL2. The interrupt request signal PMBIRQ is not asserted.

fields.name: PCT
fields.description: Physical Timestamp. If timestamp sampling is enabled and the Profiling Buffer owning Exception level is EL1, requests which timestamp counter value is collected. If FEAT_ECV is implemented, this is a two-bit field as shown. Otherwise, bit[7] is RES0 .

values.label: Virtual timestamp. The collected timestamp is the physical counter minus the value of CNTVOFF_EL2 .
Physical timestamp. The collected timestamp is the physical counter.

Guest physical timestamp. The collected timestamp is the physical counter minus a physical offset. If any of the following are true, the physical offset is zero, otherwise the physical offset is the value of CNTPOFF_EL2 : SCR_EL3 .ECVEn == 0. CNTHCTL_EL2 .ECV == 0. FEAT_ECV_POFF is not implemented.

fields.name: PCT
fields.description: Physical Timestamp. Reserved. This field reads as 0b01 and ignores writes. Software should treat this field as UNK/SBZP.

fields.name: TS
fields.description: Timestamp sample enable. Enables recording of a Timestamp packet when the owning Exception level is EL1.

values.label: Timestamp packet recording disabled.
Timestamp packet recording enabled.
Physical addresses are not collected.
Physical addresses are collected.

fields.name: CX
fields.description: CONTEXTIDR_EL1 sample enable. Enables recording of a Context packet containing the value of CONTEXTIDR_EL1 .

values.label: CONTEXTIDR_EL1 recording disabled.
CONTEXTIDR_EL1 recording enabled.
Sampling disabled at EL1.
Sampling enabled at EL1.

fields.name: E0SPE
fields.description: EL0 Statistical Profiling Enable. Controls sampling at EL0 when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .TGE is 0 or if EL2 is disabled or not implemented.

values.label: Sampling disabled at EL0.
Sampling enabled at EL0.

name: PMSCR_EL2
description: Provides EL2 controls for Statistical Profiling.

fields.name: EnVM
fields.description: Enable use of physical address Profiling Buffer pointers.

values.label: Use of physical address Profiling Buffer pointers is disabled. The PE behaves as if PMBLIMITR_EL1 .nVM is 0.
Use of physical address Profiling Buffer pointers is permitted.

fields.name: KE
fields.description: Kernel exception enable for SPE Profiling exceptions taken to EL2.

values.label: SPE Profiling exceptions taken to EL2 are always masked at EL2.
Enabled SPE Profiling exceptions taken to EL2 are masked at EL2 when PSTATE.PM is 1 and unmasked when PSTATE.PM is 0.

Disabled. SPE Profiling exceptions for EL2 and EL1 are disabled. All of the following apply: No Profiling Buffer management events are recorded in PMBSR_EL2 . Unless enabled by a higher Exception level, SPE Profiling exceptions are not generated. PMBSR_EL1 .S drives the interrupt request signal PMBIRQ . Accesses to PMBSR_EL1 at EL1 ignore the value of HCR_EL2 .NV1 and accesses to PMBSR_EL1 at EL2 ignore the value of HCR_EL2 .E2H.

Delegated. SPE Profiling exceptions for EL2 are disabled, but might be enabled for  EL1 by PMSCR_EL1 .EE. All of the following apply: No Profiling Buffer management events are recorded in PMBSR_EL2 . PMBSR_EL2 .S is ignored and SPE Profiling exceptions are not taken to EL2, other than for the case when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1.

Enabled. SPE Profiling exceptions for EL2 are enabled for Profiling Buffer management events targeting EL2, as follows: Profiling Buffer management events due to a fault on a write to the Profiling Buffer that would generate a Data Abort exception taken to EL2 if generated by a store instruction executed at the owning Exception level are recorded in PMBSR_EL2 , unless they are configured to be recorded in PMBSR_EL3 by MDCR_EL3 .PMSEE.
    If the Profiling Buffer owning Exception level is EL2, then this means any fault on a write to the Profiling Buffer.
    If the Profiling Buffer owning Exception level is EL1, then this means any of the following faults on a write to the Profiling Buffer: Stage 2 faults. If HCR_EL2 .TEA is 1, synchronous External aborts. If HCR_EL2 .GPF is 1, Granule Protection Faults (GPFs). Profiling Buffer management events due to Granule Protection Check faults other than GPFs on a write to the Profiling Buffer are recorded in PMBSR_EL2 , unless they are configured to be recorded in PMBSR_EL3 by MDCR_EL3 .PMSEE. SPE Profiling exceptions are generated and taken to EL2 when unmasked and PMBSR_EL2 .S is 1.

Trap all. SPE Profiling exceptions for EL2 are enabled for all Profiling Buffer management events, as follows: All Profiling Buffer management events are recorded in PMBSR_EL2 , unless they are configured to be recorded in PMBSR_EL3 by MDCR_EL3 .PMSEE. SPE Profiling exceptions are generated and taken to EL2 when unmasked and PMBSR_EL2 .S is 1.

fields.name: PCT
fields.description: Physical Timestamp. If timestamp sampling is enabled, determines which counter is collected. The behavior depends on the Profiling Buffer owning Exception level. If FEAT_ECV is implemented, this is a two-bit field as shown. Otherwise, bit[7] is RES0 .

values.label: Virtual timestamp. The collected timestamp is the physical counter minus a virtual offset. If the Profiling Buffer owning Exception level is EL2 and any of the following are true, then the virtual offset is zero: The sampled operation executed at EL2 and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is 1. The sampled operation executed at EL0 and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}. Otherwise, the virtual offset is the value of CNTVOFF_EL2 .
If the Profiling Buffer owning Exception level is EL1, then the timestamp value is selected by PMSCR_EL1 .PCT. Otherwise, physical timestamp. The collected timestamp is the physical counter.

If the Profiling Buffer owning Exception level is EL1 and PMSCR_EL1 .PCT is 0b00 , then guest virtual timestamp. The collected timestamp is the physical counter minus the value of CNTVOFF_EL2 . Otherwise, guest physical timestamp. The collected timestamp is the physical counter minus a physical offset. If any of the following are true, then the physical offset is zero, otherwise the physical offset is the value of CNTPOFF_EL2 : SCR_EL3 .ECVEn is 0. CNTHCTL_EL2 .ECV is 0. FEAT_ECV_POFF is not implemented.

fields.name: TS
fields.description: Timestamp sample enable. Enables recording of a Timestamp packet when the owning Exception level is EL2.

values.label: Timestamp packet recording disabled.
Timestamp packet recording enabled.
Physical addresses are not collected.
Physical addresses are collected.

fields.name: CX
fields.description: CONTEXTIDR_EL2 sample enable. Enables recording of a Context packet containing the value of CONTEXTIDR_EL2 .

values.label: CONTEXTIDR_EL2 recording disabled.
CONTEXTIDR_EL2 recording enabled.
Sampling disabled at EL2.
Sampling enabled at EL2.
Sampling disabled at EL0.
Sampling enabled at EL0.

name: PMSDSFR_EL1
description: Controls sample filtering by Data Source.

fields.name: S<m>

values.label: If PMSFCR_EL1 .FDS is 1, do not record load operations that have bits [5:0] of the Data Source packet set to <m>.
Load operations with Data Source <m> are unaffected by PMSFCR_EL1 .FDS.

name: PMSELR_EL0
long_name: Performance Monitors Event Counter Selection Register
description: Selects the current event counter PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 or the cycle counter PMCCNTR_EL0 . Used in conjunction with PMXEVTYPER_EL0 to determine the event that increments a selected counter, and the modes and states in which the selected counter increments. Used in conjunction with PMXEVCNTR_EL0 to determine the value of a selected counter.

fields.name: SEL
fields.description: Event counter select. Selects the counter accessed by subsequent accesses to PMXEVTYPER_EL0 and PMXEVCNTR_EL0 .

values.label: Select event counter PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 , where n is the value of this field: MRS and MSR of PMXEVTYPER_EL0 access PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 . MRS and MSR of PMXEVCNTR_EL0 access PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 .
Select the cycle counter, PMCCNTR_EL0 : MRS and MSR of PMXEVTYPER_EL0 access PMCCFILTR_EL0 . MRS and MSR of PMXEVCNTR_EL0 are CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE . For more information, see PMXEVCNTR_EL0 .

name: PMSFCR_EL1
description:
Controls sample filtering. The filter is the logical AND of the filters controlled by 
FDS, 
FnE, 
FL, FT, and FE bits. For example, if PMSFCR_EL1.FE is 1 and PMSFCR_EL1.FT is 1, then only samples including the selected types and the selected events will be recorded.

values.label: Data Source filtering disabled.
Data Source filtering enabled. Samples of load instructions reporting a Data Source not selected by PMSDSFR_EL1 will not be recorded.
Inverted event filtering disabled.
Inverted event filtering enabled. Samples including the events selected by PMSNEVFR_EL1 will not be recorded.
Latency filtering disabled.
Latency filtering enabled. Samples with a total latency less than PMSLATFR_EL1.MINLAT will not be recorded.

fields.name: FT
fields.description: Filter by type. The filter is controlled by the TYPE and, if FEAT_SPE_EFT is implemented, TYPEm fields.

values.label: Type filtering disabled.
Type filtering enabled. Samples not one of the selected types will not be recorded.
Event filtering disabled.
Event filtering enabled. Samples not including the events selected by PMSEVFR_EL1 will not be recorded.

name: PMSICR_EL1
description: Software must write zero to PMSICR_EL1 before enabling sample profiling for a sampling session. Software must then treat PMSICR_EL1 as an opaque, 64-bit, read/write register used for context switches only.

fields.name: ECOUNT
fields.description: Secondary sample interval counter. Provides the secondary counter used after the primary counter reaches zero. While the secondary counter is nonzero and profiling is enabled, the secondary counter decrements by 1 for each member of the sample population. The primary counter also continues to decrement since it is also nonzero. When the secondary counter reaches zero, a member of the sampling population is selected for sampling.

fields.name: COUNT
fields.description: Primary sample interval counter. Provides the primary counter used for sampling. When the PE moves from a state or Exception level where profiling is disabled to a state or Exception level where profiling is enabled, if the value of this register is zero, then the primary counter is loaded from PMSIRR_EL1 . While the primary counter is nonzero and sampling is enabled, the primary counter decrements by 1 for each member of the sample population. The sample interval counter counts either a number of operations or a number of instructions, depending on the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED value of PMSIDR_EL1 .ArchInst. When the counter reaches zero, the behavior depends on the value of PMSIRR_EL1 .RND and whether FEAT_SPE_ERnd is implemented: If PMSIRR_EL1 .RND is 1 and FEAT_SPE_ERnd is implemented, then the secondary counter is set to a random or pseudorandom value in the range 0x00 to 0xFF . Otherwise, a member of the sampling population is selected for sampling. The primary counter is loaded from PMSIRR_EL1 . The primary counter is loaded from PMSIRR_EL1 means: PMSICR_EL1.COUNT[31:8] is set to the value of PMSIRR_EL1 .INTERVAL. If PMSIRR_EL1 .RND is 1 and FEAT_SPE_ERnd is not implemented, then PMSICR_EL1.COUNT[7:0] is set to a random or pseudorandom value in the range 0x00 to 0xFF . Otherwise, PMSICR_EL1.COUNT[7:0] is set to 0x00 . For more information, see Initializing the sample interval counters and Behavior of the sample interval counter while profiling is enabled .

name: PMSIDR_EL1
description: Describes the Statistical Profiling implementation to software.

fields.name: SME
fields.description: SPE for SME. Describes support for the Scalable Matrix Extensions to Statistical Profiling.

values.label: The SME extensions to the Statistical Profiling Extension are not implemented.
Adds support for Statistical Profiling of the Scalable Matrix Extensions.
Alternate clock domain not implemented, or CPU clock domain.
The external Streaming Mode Compute Unit provides the alternate clock domain.
IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED clock domain.

fields.name: FPF
fields.description: Floating-point flag. Describes whether Operation Type packets for sampled scalar and SIMD operations contain floating-point and Advanced SIMD indications.

values.label: Operation Type packets for scalar and Advanced SIMD operations do not contain floating-point or Advanced SIMD information.
Operation Type packets for scalar and Advanced SIMD operations contain floating-point or Advanced SIMD information.

fields.name: EFT
fields.description: Extended filtering by type. Describes whether Extended filtering by operation type is supported.

values.label: PMSFCR_EL1 .{SIMDm, FPm, STm, Bm, LDm, SIMD, FP} are RES0 .
PMSFCR_EL1 .{SIMDm, FPm, STm, Bm, LDm, SIMD, FP} are implemented.

fields.name: CRR

values.label: Operation Type packets for branch operations do not contain Call Return information.
Operation Type packets for branch operations contain Call Return information.

fields.name: PBT

values.label: Previous branch target Address packet not implemented.

fields.name: Format
fields.description: Defines the format of the sample records.

values.label: Format 0.

fields.name: CountSize
fields.description: Defines the size of the counters.

values.label: 12-bit saturating counters.
16-bit saturating counters.

fields.name: MaxSize
fields.description: Defines the largest size for a single record, rounded up to a power-of-two. If this is the same as the minimum alignment ( PMBIDR_EL1 .Align), then each record is exactly this size.

values.label: 16 bytes.
32 bytes.
64 bytes.
128 bytes.
256 bytes.
512 bytes.
1KB.
2KB.

fields.name: Interval
fields.description: Minimum sampling interval. This provides guidance from the implementer to the smallest sampling interval. The interval is defined as a number of either operations or instructions, depending on the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED value of PMSIDR_EL1.ArchInst.

values.label: 256 operations or instructions, or no minimum specified.
512 operations or instructions.
768 operations or instructions.
1,024 operations or instructions.
1,536 operations or instructions.
2,048 operations or instructions.
3,072 operations or instructions.
4,096 operations or instructions.
PMSDSFR_EL1 is not implemented and PMSFCR_EL1 .FDS is RES0 .
PMSDSFR_EL1 and PMSFCR_EL1 .FDS are implemented.
PMSNEVFR_EL1 is not implemented and PMSFCR_EL1 .FnE is RES0 .
PMSNEVFR_EL1 and PMSFCR_EL1 .FnE are implemented.

fields.name: ERnd
fields.description: Defines how the random number generator is used in determining the interval between samples, when enabled by PMSIRR_EL1 .RND.

values.label: The random number is added at the start of the interval, and the sample is taken and a new interval started when the combined interval expires.
The random number is added and the new interval started after the interval programmed in PMSIRR_EL1 .INTERVAL expires, and the sample is taken when the random interval expires.

fields.name: LDS
fields.description: Data source indicator for sampled load instructions.

values.label: Loaded data source not implemented.
Loaded data source implemented.

fields.name: ArchInst
fields.description: Architectural instruction profiling. Defines the subject of the profiling population.

values.label: Micro-op sampling implemented.
Architecture instruction sampling implemented.

name: PMSIRR_EL1
description: Defines the interval between samples.

fields.name: INTERVAL
fields.description: Bits [31:8] of the PMSICR_EL1 interval counter reload value. Software must set this to a nonzero value. If this field is zero, then an UNKNOWN sampling interval is used. If PMSIDR_EL1 .Interval is nonzero, then it provides guidance from the implementer to the smallest sampling interval that should be used. Setting this field to a nonzero value smaller than the indicated value is likely to cause a statistically significant number of sample collisions. See Sample collisions . Setting the sample interval to a value greater than or equal to the indicated value does not guarantee a statistically insignificant number of sample collisions, as this is typically dependent on the program being profiled.

fields.name: RND
fields.description: Controls randomization of the sampling interval.

values.label: Disable randomization of sampling interval.
Add (pseudo-)random jitter to sampling interval.

name: PMSSCR_EL1
long_name: Performance Monitors Snapshot Status and Capture Register
description: Holds status information about the captured counters and provides a mechanism for software to initiate a sample.

fields.name: NC
fields.description: No Capture. Indicates whether the PMU counters have been captured.

values.label: PMU counters captured.
PMU counters not captured.
On a read, the Capture event has completed.
On a read, the Capture event has not completed. On a write, request a Capture event.

name: PMSWINC_EL0
description: Increments a counter that is configured to count the Software increment event, event 0x00 . For more information, see 'SW_INCR' .

values.label: Write is ignored.
Increment PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 , if PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 is configured to count software increment events.

name: PMUACR_EL1
long_name: Performance Monitors User Access Control Register
description: Enables or disables EL0 access to specfic Performance Monitors.

fields.name: F0
fields.description: EL0 accesses to PMICNTR_EL0 enable. With PMUSERENR_EL0 .UEN and PMUSERENR_EL0 .IR, controls EL0 accesses to PMICNTR_EL0 .

values.label: If the Effective value of PMUSERENR_EL0 .UEN is 1, then EL0 accesses to PMICNTR_EL0 and associated controls are RAZ/WI, and permitted EL0 writes to PMZR_EL0 .F0 are ignored.
If the Effective value of PMUSERENR_EL0 .UEN is 1, then EL0 accesses to PMICNTR_EL0 and associated controls are either read-only or read/write, depending on the value of PMUSERENR_EL0 .IR.

fields.name: C
fields.description: EL0 accesses to PMCCNTR_EL0 enable. With PMUSERENR_EL0 .EN, PMUSERENR_EL0 .UEN, and PMUSERENR_EL0 .CR, controls EL0 accesses to PMCCNTR_EL0 .

values.label: If the Effective value of PMUSERENR_EL0 .UEN is 1, then EL0 accesses to PMCCNTR_EL0 and associated controls are RAZ/WI, and permitted EL0 writes to PMZR_EL0 .C are ignored.
If the Effective value of PMUSERENR_EL0 .UEN is 1, then EL0 accesses to PMCCNTR_EL0 and associated controls are either read-only or read/write, depending on the value of PMUSERENR_EL0 .CR.

fields.name: P<m>
fields.description: EL0 accesses to PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 enable. With PMUSERENR_EL0 .EN, PMUSERENR_EL0 .UEN, and PMUSERENR_EL0 .ER, controls EL0 accesses to PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 .

values.label: If the Effective value of PMUSERENR_EL0 .UEN is 1, then EL0 accesses to PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 and associated controls are RAZ/WI, and permitted EL0 writes to PMZR_EL0 .P<m> are ignored.
If the Effective value of PMUSERENR_EL0 .UEN is 1, then EL0 accesses to PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 and associated controls are either read-only or read/write, depending on the value of PMUSERENR_EL0 .ER.

name: PMUSERENR_EL0
description: Enables or disables EL0 access to the Performance Monitors.

fields.name: TID
fields.description: Trap ID registers. Traps EL0 read access to common event identification registers.

values.label: Accesses to PMCEID<n>_EL0 and PMCEID<n> are not trapped by this mechanism.
EL0 read accesses to PMCEID<n>_EL0 and PMCEID<n> are trapped.

fields.name: IR
fields.description: Instruction counter Read-only. When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN is 1, controls whether EL0 writes to instruction counter are ignored.

values.label: EL0 accesses are not affected by this mechanism.

If the Effective value of PMUSERENR_EL0 .UEN is 1, then all of the following apply at EL0: Writes to PMICNTR_EL0 are ignored. The controls associated with instruction counter are RAZ/WI. Writes to PMZR_EL0 .F0 are ignored.

fields.name: UEN
fields.description: User Enable, with access controlled by PMUACR_EL1 . Enables EL0 read/write access to PMU registers, other than PMCR_EL0 .

values.label: If FEAT_PMUv3_ICNTR is implemented, then EL0 accesses to PMICFILTR_EL0 and PMICNTR_EL0 are trapped. EL0 accesses to the other specified PMU registers, PMCR_EL0 , and PMCR are trapped, unless enabled by PMUSERENR_EL0.{ER,CR,SW,EN}.

fields.name: ER
fields.description: Event counters Read enable or Read-only. When PMUSERENR_EL0.{UEN,EN} is {0,0}, PMUSERENR_EL0.ER enables EL0 reads of the event counters and EL0 reads and writes of the select register. When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN is 1, EL0 reads of the event counters and EL0 writes to PMZR_EL0 are enabled by PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN, unless trapped by another control, and PMUSERENR_EL0.ER controls the behavior of EL0 writes to the event counters and PMZR_EL0 .

values.label: When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN == 0, EL0 reads of the event counters and EL0 reads and writes of the select register are disabled, unless enabled by PMUSERENR_EL0.EN. When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN == 1, permitted EL0 writes are not affected by this mechanism.

When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN == 0, EL0 reads of the event counters and EL0 reads and writes of the select register are enabled, unless trapped by another control. When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN == 1, permitted EL0 writes to the event counters and PMZR_EL0 .P[30:0] are ignored.

fields.name: ER
fields.description: Event counters Read enable. When PMUSERENR_EL0.EN is 0, PMUSERENR_EL0.ER enables EL0 reads of the event counters and EL0 reads and writes of the select register.

values.label: EL0 reads of the event counters and EL0 reads and writes of the select register are disabled, unless enabled by PMUSERENR_EL0.EN.
EL0 reads of the event counters and EL0 reads and writes of the select register are enabled, unless trapped by another control.

fields.name: CR
fields.description: Cycle counter Read enable or Read-only. When PMUSERENR_EL0.{UEN,EN} is {0,0}, PMUSERENR_EL0.CR enables EL0 reads of the cycle counter. When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN is 1, EL0 reads of the cycle counter and EL0 writes to PMZR_EL0 are enabled by PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN, unless trapped by another control, and PMUSERENR_EL0.CR controls the behavior of EL0 writes to the cycle counter and PMZR_EL0 .

values.label: When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN == 0, EL0 reads of the cycle counter are disabled, unless enabled by PMUSERENR_EL0.EN. When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN == 1, permitted EL0 writes are not affected by this mechanism.

When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN == 0, EL0 reads of the cycle counter are enabled, unless trapped by another control. When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN == 1, permitted EL0 writes to the cycle counter and PMZR_EL0 .C are ignored.

fields.name: CR
fields.description: Cycle counter Read enable. When PMUSERENR_EL0.EN is 0, PMUSERENR_EL0.CR enables EL0 reads of the cycle counter.

values.label: EL0 reads of the cycle counter are disabled, unless enabled by PMUSERENR_EL0.EN.
EL0 reads of the cycle counter are enabled, unless trapped by another control.

fields.name: SW
fields.description: Software increment register Write enable. When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN is 0, PMUSERENR_EL0.SW enables EL0 writes to the Software increment register. When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN is 1, EL0 writes to the Software increment register are enabled by PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN, unless trapped by another control, and PMUSERENR_EL0.SW controls the behavior of EL0 writes to the Software increment register.

values.label: When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN == 0, EL0 writes to the Software increment register are disabled, unless enabled by PMUSERENR_EL0.EN. When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN == 1, permitted EL0 writes are not affected by this mechanism.

When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN == 0, EL0 writes to the Software increment register are enabled, unless trapped by another control. When PMUSERENR_EL0.UEN == 1, permitted EL0 writes to the Software increment register ignore the value of PMUACR_EL1 .

fields.name: SW
fields.description: Software increment register Write enable. When PMUSERENR_EL0.EN is 0, PMUSERENR_EL0.SW enables EL0 writes to the Software increment register.

values.label: EL0 writes to the Software increment register are disabled, unless enabled by PMUSERENR_EL0.EN.
EL0 writes to the Software increment register are enabled, unless trapped by another control.

fields.name: EN
fields.description: Enable. Enables EL0 read/write access to PMU registers, other than the instruction counter.

values.label: EL0 accesses to the specified PMU System registers are trapped, unless enabled by PMUSERENR_EL0.{UEN,ER,CR,SW}.
EL0 accesses to the specified PMU System registers are enabled, unless trapped by another control.

name: PMXEVCNTR_EL0
long_name: Performance Monitors Selected Event Count Register
description: Reads or writes the value of the selected event counter, PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 . PMSELR_EL0 .SEL determines which event counter is selected.

fields.name: PMEVCNTR<n>
fields.description: Value of the selected event counter, PMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 , where n is the value stored in PMSELR_EL0 .SEL.

name: PMXEVTYPER_EL0
long_name: Performance Monitors Selected Event Type Register
description: When PMSELR_EL0 .SEL selects an event counter, this accesses a PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 register. When PMSELR_EL0 .SEL selects the cycle counter, this accesses PMCCFILTR_EL0 .

fields.name: EVTYPERn
fields.description: When PMSELR_EL0 .SEL == 31, this register accesses PMCCFILTR_EL0 . Otherwise, this register accesses PMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 where n is the value in PMSELR_EL0 .SEL.

name: PMZR_EL0
description: Zero the set of counters specified by the mask written to PMZR_EL0.

values.label: Write is ignored.
Set PMICNTR_EL0 to zero.
Write is ignored.
Set PMCCNTR_EL0 to zero.
Write is ignored.
Set PMEVCNTR<m>_EL0 to zero.

name: RCWMASK_EL1
description: Contains the mask used by RCW instructions.

fields.name: RCWMASK
fields.description: Mask used to decide which bit-fields are writable to the 128-bit Descriptor by RCW or RCWS instructions. If RCWMASK_EL1 is indirectly read by 128-bit variants of RCW or RCWS instructions: The Effective value of RCWMASK[n] is the same as RCWMASK_EL1[n], except as follows: If n >= 17, and n <= 55, the Effective value of RCWMASK[n] is the same as RCWMASK_EL1[16]. If n is in {126:125, 120:119, 114, 107:101, 90:56, 1:0}, the Effective value of RCWMASK[n] is 0. If n >= 121, n <= 124, and FEAT_S1POE is not implemented, the Effective value of RCWMASK[n] is 0. If FEAT_MEC is not implemented, the Effective value of RCWMASK[108] is 0. If RCWMASK_EL1 is indirectly read by 64-bit variants of RCW or RCWS instructions: The Effective value of RCWMASK[n] is the same as RCWMASK_EL1[n], except as follows: If n >= 18, and n <= 49, the Effective value of RCWMASK[n] is the same as RCWMASK_EL1[17]. RCWMASK_EL1 register bits {126:125, 120:119, 114, 107:101, 90:64, 52, 49:18, 0} are RES0 . If FEAT_S1POE is not implemented, RCWMASK_EL1 register bits {124:121} are RES0 . If FEAT_MEC is not implemented, RCWMASK_EL1[108] is RES0 .

name: RCWSMASK_EL1
long_name: Software Read Check Write Instruction Mask (EL1)
description: Contains the software mask used by RCWS instructions.

fields.name: RCWSMASK
fields.description: Software Mask used to decide which bit-fields are writable to the 128-bit Descriptor by RCWS instructions. If RCWSMASK_EL1 is indirectly read by 128-bit variants of RCWS instructions: The Effective value of RCWSMASK[n] is the same as RCWSMASK_EL1[n], except as follows: If n >= 17, and n <= 55, the Effective value of RCWSMASK[n] is the same as RCWSMASK_EL1[16]. If n is in {126:125, 120:119, 114, 107:101, 90:56, 1:0}, the Effective value of RCWSMASK[n] is 0. If n >= 121, n <= 124, and FEAT_S1POE is not implemented, the Effective value of RCWSMASK[n] is 0. If FEAT_MEC is not implemented, the Effective value of RCWSMASK bit RCWSMASK_EL1[108] is 0. If RCWSMASK_EL1 is indirectly read by 64-bit variants of RCWS instructions: The Effective value of RCWSMASK[n] is the same as RCWSMASK_EL1[n], except as follows: If n >= 18, and n <= 49, the Effective value of RCWSMASK[n] is the same as RCWSMASK_EL1[17]. If n == 52 and Protection is enabled, the Effective value of RCWSMASK[52] is 0. RCWSMASK_EL1 register bits {126:125, 120:119, 114, 107:101, 90:64, 49:18, 0} are RES0 . If FEAT_S1POE is not implemented, RCWSMASK_EL1 register bits {124:121} are RES0 . If FEAT_MEC is not implemented, RCWSMASK_EL1[108] is RES0 .

name: REVIDR_EL1
description: Provides implementation-specific minor revision information.

name: RGSR_EL1
description: Random Allocation Tag Seed Register.

fields.name: SEED
fields.description: Seed register used for generating values returned by RandomTag() or, if FEAT_MTE_EIRG is implemented, ChooseTag()

fields.name: TAG
fields.description: Tag generated by the most recent IRG instruction or, if FEAT_MTE_EIRG is implemented, EIRG state

name: RMR_EL1
description: When this register is implemented: A write to the register at EL1 can request a Warm reset. If EL1 can use all Execution states, this register specifies the Execution state that the PE boots into on a Warm reset.

fields.name: RR
fields.description: Reset Request. Setting this bit to 1 requests a Warm reset.

fields.name: AA64
fields.description: When EL1 can use AArch32, determines which Execution state the PE boots into after a Warm reset:

values.label: AArch32.
AArch64.

name: RMR_EL2
description: When this register is implemented: A write to the register at EL2 can request a Warm reset. If EL2 can use all Execution states, this register specifies the Execution state that the PE boots into on a Warm reset.

fields.name: RR
fields.description: Reset Request. Setting this bit to 1 requests a Warm reset.

fields.name: AA64
fields.description: When EL2 can use AArch32, determines which Execution state the PE boots into after a Warm reset:

values.label: AArch32.
AArch64.

name: RMR_EL3
description: If EL3 is implemented and this register is implemented: A write to the register at EL3 can request a Warm reset. If EL3 can use all Execution states, this register specifies the Execution state that the PE boots into on a Warm reset.

fields.name: RR
fields.description: Reset Request. Setting this bit to 1 requests a Warm reset.

fields.name: AA64
fields.description: When EL3 can use AArch32, determines which Execution state the PE boots into after a Warm reset:

values.label: AArch32.
AArch64.

name: RNDR
description: Random Number. Returns a 64-bit random number from an approved Random Bit Generator, where the Deterministic Random Bit Generator within the Random Bit Generator is reseeded from an approved entropy source at an IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED rate. See 'Properties of the generated random number' . If the hardware returns a genuine random number, PSTATE.NZCV is set to 0b0000 . If the instruction cannot return a genuine random number in a reasonable period of time, PSTATE.NZCV is set to 0b0100 and the data value returned is 0.

fields.name: RNDR
fields.description: Random Number. Returns a 64-bit Random Number from an approved Random Bit Generator, where the Deterministic Random Bit Generator within the Random Bit Generator is reseeded from an approved entropy source at an IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED rate. See 'Properties of the generated random number' .

name: RNDRRS
description: Random Number with fresh full entropy. Returns a 64-bit random number from an approved Random Bit Generator, using either a Non-deterministic Random Bit Generator or one where the Deterministic Random Bit Generator is reseeded, where possible, from an approved entropy source before the return of the random number. See 'Properties of the generated random number' . If the hardware returns a genuine random number, PSTATE.NZCV is set to 0b0000 . If the instruction cannot return a genuine random number in a reasonable period of time, PSTATE.NZCV is set to 0b0100 and the data value returned is 0. When FEAT_RNG_TRAP is implemented and SCR_EL3 .TRNDR is 1, reads of this register are trapped to EL3.

fields.name: RNDRRS
fields.description: Random Number with fresh full entropy. Returns a 64-bit random number from an approved Random Bit Generator, using either a Non-deterministic Random Bit Generator or one where the Deterministic Random Bit Generator is reseeded, where possible, from an approved entropy source before the return of the random number. See 'Properties of the generated random number' .

name: RVBAR_EL1
long_name: Reset Vector Base Address Register (if EL2 and EL3 not implemented)
description: If EL1 is the highest Exception level implemented, contains the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED address that execution starts from after reset when executing in AArch64 state.

fields.name: ResetAddress
fields.description: The IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED address that execution starts from after reset when executing in 64-bit state. Bits[1:0] of this register are 00, as this address must be aligned, and the address must be within the physical address size supported by the PE.

name: RVBAR_EL2
long_name: Reset Vector Base Address Register (if EL3 not implemented)
description: If EL2 is the highest Exception level implemented, contains the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED address that execution starts from after reset when executing in AArch64 state.

fields.name: ResetAddress
fields.description: The IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED address that execution starts from after reset when executing in 64-bit state. Bits[1:0] of this register are 00, as this address must be aligned, and the address must be within the physical address size supported by the PE.

name: RVBAR_EL3
long_name: Reset Vector Base Address Register (if EL3 implemented)
description: If EL3 is the highest Exception level implemented, contains the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED address that execution starts from after reset when executing in AArch64 state.

fields.name: ResetAddress
fields.description: The IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED address that execution starts from after reset when executing in 64-bit state. Bits[1:0] of this register are 00, as this address must be aligned, and the address must be within the physical address size supported by the PE.
