name: S3_<op1>_<Cn>_<Cm>_<op2>
description: This area of the instruction set space is reserved for IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED registers.

name: SCR2_EL3
description: Defines the configuration of the current Security state.

fields.name: SRMASK2En
fields.description: Enables access to the following registers: HCRMASK_EL2 . HCRXMASK_EL2 . If FEAT_NV3 is implemented, NVHCRX_EL2 , NVHCRMASK_EL2 , and NVHCRXMASK_EL2 .

values.label: EL2 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL3.
No accesses are trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: NV3En
fields.description: Enables access to the NVHCR_EL2 register at EL2.

values.label: EL2 accesses to the specified register is trapped to EL3. The Effective value of HCRX_EL2 .NVTGE is 0.
No accesses are trapped by this mechanism.

fields.name: FDIT
fields.description: Enforce data-independent timing for execution at EL2, EL1 and EL0.

values.label: This control does not affect data-independent timing of execution.
Data-independent timing of execution is enforced.

name: SCR_EL3
description: Defines the configuration of the current Security state. It specifies: The Security state of EL0, EL1, and EL2. The Security state is Secure, Non-secure, or Realm. The Execution state at lower Exception levels. Whether IRQ, FIQ, SError exceptions, and External abort exceptions are taken to EL3. Whether various operations are trapped to EL3.

fields.name: NSE
fields.description: This field, evaluated with SCR_EL3.NS, selects the Security state of EL2 and lower Exception levels.

fields.name: NSE
fields.description: The Effective value of this bit is 0b0 .

fields.name: HACDBSEn
fields.description: Enables access to the HACDBSBR_EL2 and HACDBSCONS_EL2 registers at EL2.

values.label: EL2 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL3.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: HDBSSEn
fields.description: Enables access to HDBSSBR_EL2 and HDBSSPROD_EL2 registers at EL2.

values.label: EL2 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL3.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: FGTEn2
fields.description: Fine-Grained Traps Enable 2. When EL2 is implemented, enables the traps to EL2 controlled by HDFGRTR2_EL2 , HDFGWTR2_EL2 , HFGITR2_EL2 , HFGRTR2_EL2 , and HFGWTR2_EL2 , and controls access to those registers.

values.label: EL2 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL3. The values in these registers are treated as 0.
EL2 accesses to the specified registers are not trapped to EL3 by this mechanism.

fields.name: EnDSE
fields.description: Enable for delegated SError exceptions pended by SCR_EL3.DSE.

values.label: Delegated SError exceptions pended by SCR_EL3.DSE are disabled.
Delegated SError exceptions pended by SCR_EL3.DSE are enabled.

fields.name: DSE
fields.description: Delegated SError exception for EL2, EL1, and EL0.

values.label: This mechanism is not making a delegated SError exception pending.
A delegated SError exception for EL2, EL1, and EL0 is pending because of this mechanism.

fields.name: VTLBIDEn
fields.description: Enable EL2 access to VTLBID<n>_EL2 and VTLBIDOS<n>_EL2 registers.

values.label: EL2 accesses to VTLBID<n>_EL2 and VTLBIDOS<n>_EL2 are trapped to EL3 using EC syndrome value 0x18 . The Effective values of VTLBID<n>_EL2 and VTLBIDOS<n>_EL2 are zero. EL1 and EL2 accesses to TLBIDIDR_EL1 are trapped to EL3 using EC syndrome value 0x18 .
EL2 accesses to VTLBID<n>_EL2 and VTLBIDOS<n>_EL2 are not prevented by this control.

fields.name: EnIDCP128
fields.description: Enables access to IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED 128-bit System registers.

values.label: Accesses at EL2, EL1, EL0 to IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED 128-bit System registers are trapped to EL3 using EC syndrome value 0x14 , unless the access generates a higher priority exception. Disables the functionality of the 128-bit IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED System registers that are accessible at EL3.
No accesses are trapped by this control.

fields.name: SRMASKEn
fields.description: Enables access to the following MASK registers: CPACRMASK_EL1 , and CPACRMASK_EL12. SCTLRMASK_EL1 , and SCTLRMASK_EL12. SCTLR2MASK_EL1 , and SCTLR2MASK_EL12. TCRMASK_EL1 , and TCRMASK_EL12. TCR2MASK_EL1 , and TCR2MASK_EL12. ACTLRMASK_EL1 and ACTLRMASK_EL12, if they are implemented. CPTRMASK_EL2 . SCTLRMASK_EL2 . SCTLR2MASK_EL2 . TCRMASK_EL2 . TCR2MASK_EL2 . ACTLRMASK_EL2 , if it is implemented.

values.label: EL2 accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL3. EL1 accesses to the specified EL1 registers are trapped to EL3. The values in the registers are treated as 0.
No accesses are trapped by this control.

fields.name: PFAREn
fields.description: Enable access to Physical Fault Address Registers. When disabled, accesses to Physical Fault Address Registers generate a trap to EL3.

values.label: Accesses of the specified Physical Fault Address Registers at EL2 and EL1 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: TWERR
fields.description: Trap writes of Error Record registers. Enables a trap to EL3 on writes of Error Record registers.

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
Writes of the specified Error Record registers at EL2 and EL1 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.

fields.name: TMEA
fields.description: Trap Masked External Aborts. Controls whether a masked error exception at a lower Exception level is taken to EL3.

values.label: Synchronous External abort exceptions and SError exceptions at EL2, EL1, and EL0 are unaffected by this mechanism. That is, these exceptions are not taken to EL3 unless routed to EL3 by another control.

When executing at Exception levels below EL3, all of the following apply: When PSTATE.A is 1, synchronous External abort exceptions are taken to EL3, unless they are taken from EL1 or EL0 and routed to EL2 by another control. Masked physical SError exceptions are taken to EL3, unless they are taken from EL1 or EL0 and routed to EL2 by another control.

fields.name: EnFPM
fields.description: Enables direct and indirect accesses to FPMR from EL2, EL1, and EL0. When accesses to FPMR are disabled by this control: Direct accesses to FPMR from EL2, EL1, and EL0 are trapped to EL3 and reported with EC syndrome value 0x18 . Execution of FP8 data-processing instructions that indirectly access FPMR is UNDEFINED at EL2, EL1 and EL0.

values.label: Direct and indirect accesses to FPMR are disabled at EL2, EL1 and EL0.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: MECEn
fields.description: Enables access to the following EL2 MECID registers, from EL2: MECID_P0_EL2 . MECID_A0_EL2 MECID_P1_EL2 MECID_A1_EL2 VMECID_P_EL2 VMECID_A_EL2 Accesses to these registers are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: EL2 accesses to any of the specified registers are trapped to EL3. The values of the specified registers are treated as 0 for all purposes other than direct reads or writes to the register from EL3.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: GPF
fields.description: Controls the reporting of Granule protection faults at EL0, EL1 and EL2.

values.label: This control does not cause exceptions to be routed from EL0, EL1 or EL2 to EL3.
GPFs at EL0, EL1 and EL2 are routed to EL3 and reported as Granule Protection Check exceptions.

fields.name: D128En
fields.description: 128-bit System Register trap control. Enables access to 128-bit System Registers via MRRS , MSRR instructions. MRRS and MSRR accesses from EL1 and EL2 using AArch64 to the following registers are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x14 : TTBR0_EL1 . TTBR1_EL1 . RCWMASK_EL1 , RCWSMASK_EL1 . PAR_EL1 . MRRS and MSRR accesses from EL2 using AArch64 to the following registers are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x14 : TTBR1_EL2 and accesses using the register name TTBR1_EL12. TTBR0_EL2 and accesses using the register name TTBR0_EL12. VTTBR_EL2 .

values.label: EL1 and EL2 accesses to the specified registers are disabled, and trapped to EL3.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: AIEn
fields.description: MAIR2_ELx, AMAIR2_ELx Register access trap control. Accesses from EL1 and EL2 using AArch64 to the following registers are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : AMAIR2_EL1 . MAIR2_EL1 . Accesses from EL2 using AArch64 to the following registers are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : AMAIR2_EL2 and accesses using the register name AMAIR2_EL12. MAIR2_EL2 and accesses using the register name MAIR2_EL12.

values.label: EL1 and EL2 accesses to the specified registers are disabled, and trapped to EL3. The values in these registers are treated as 0.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: PIEn
fields.description: Permission Indirection, Overlay Register access trap control. Enables access to Permission Indirection and Overlay registers. Accesses from EL0, EL1 and EL2 using AArch64 to the following registers are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : POR_EL0 . Accesses from EL1 and EL2 using AArch64 to the following registers are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : PIRE0_EL1 . PIR_EL1 . POR_EL1 . S2POR_EL1 . Accesses from EL2 using AArch64 to the following registers are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : PIRE0_EL2 and accesses using the register name PIRE0_EL12. PIR_EL2 and accesses using the register name PIR_EL12. POR_EL2 and accesses using the register name POR_EL12. S2PIR_EL2 .

values.label: EL0, EL1 and EL2 accesses to the specified registers are disabled, and trapped to EL3.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: SCTLR2En
fields.description: SCTLR2_ELx register trap control. Enables access to SCTLR2_EL1 and SCTLR2_EL2 registers.

values.label: EL1 and EL2 accesses to SCTLR2_EL1 and SCTLR2_EL2 registers are disabled, and trapped to EL3. The values in these registers are treated as 0.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: TCR2En
fields.description: TCR2_ELx register trap control. Enables access to TCR2_EL1 and TCR2_EL2 registers.

values.label: EL1 and EL2 accesses to TCR2_EL1 and TCR2_EL2 registers are disabled, and trapped to EL3. The values in these registers are treated as 0.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: RCWMASKEn
fields.description: RCW and RCWS Mask register trap control. Enables access to RCWMASK_EL1 , RCWSMASK_EL1 .

values.label: EL1 and EL2 accesses to RCWMASK_EL1 and RCWSMASK_EL1 registers are disabled, and trapped to EL3.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: EnTP2
fields.description: Traps instructions executed at EL2, EL1, and EL0 that access TPIDR2_EL0 to EL3. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at EL2, EL1, and EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: TRNDR
fields.description: Controls trapping of reads of RNDR and RNDRRS . The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: This control does not cause RNDR and RNDRRS to be trapped. When FEAT_RNG is implemented: ID_AA64ISAR0_EL1 .RNDR returns the value 0b0001 . When FEAT_RNG is not implemented: ID_AA64ISAR0_EL1 .RNDR returns the value 0b0000 . MRS reads of RNDR and RNDRRS are UNDEFINED .
ID_AA64ISAR0_EL1 .RNDR returns the value 0b0001 . Any attempt to read RNDR or RNDRRS is trapped to EL3.

fields.name: GCSEn
fields.description: Guarded Control Stack enable. Controls access to the Guarded Control Stack registers from EL2, EL1, and EL0, and controls whether the Guarded Control Stack is enabled. The Guarded Control Stack registers trapped by this mechanism are: GCSCRE0_EL1 . GCSCR_EL1 . GCSCR_EL2 . GCSCR_EL12. GCSPR_EL0 . GCSPR_EL1 . GCSPR_EL2 . GCSPR_EL12.

values.label: Trap read and write accesses to all Guarded Control Stack registers to EL3. All Guarded Control Stack behavior is disabled at EL2, EL1, and EL0.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped, and does not disable Guarded Control Stack behavior at EL2, EL1, or EL0.

fields.name: HXEn
fields.description: Enables access to the HCRX_EL2 register at EL2 from EL3.

values.label: Accesses at EL2 to HCRX_EL2 are trapped to EL3.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: ADEn
fields.description: Enables access to the ACCDATA_EL1 register at EL1 and EL2.

values.label: Accesses to ACCDATA_EL1 at EL1 and EL2 are trapped to EL3, unless the accesses are trapped to EL2 by the EL2 fine-grained trap.
This control does not cause accesses to ACCDATA_EL1 to be trapped.

fields.name: EnAS0
fields.description: Traps execution of an ST64BV0 instruction at EL0, EL1, or EL2 to EL3.

values.label: EL0 execution of an ST64BV0 instruction is trapped to EL3, unless it is trapped to EL1 by SCTLR_EL1 .EnAS0, or to EL2 by either HCRX_EL2 .EnAS0 or SCTLR_EL2 .EnAS0. EL1 execution of an ST64BV0 instruction is trapped to EL3, unless it is trapped to EL2 by HCRX_EL2 .EnAS0. EL2 execution of an ST64BV0 instruction is trapped to EL3.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
Accesses to AMEVCNTVOFF0<n>_EL2 and AMEVCNTVOFF1<n>_EL2 at EL2 are trapped to EL3. Indirect reads of the virtual offset registers are zero.
Accesses to AMEVCNTVOFF0<n>_EL2 and AMEVCNTVOFF1<n>_EL2 are not affected by this field.

fields.name: TWEDEL
fields.description: TWE Delay. A 4-bit unsigned number that, when SCR_EL3.TWEDEn is 1, encodes the minimum delay in taking a trap of WFE* caused by SCR_EL3.TWE as 2 (TWEDEL + 8) cycles.

fields.name: TWEDEn
fields.description: TWE Delay Enable. Enables a configurable delayed trap of the WFE* instruction caused by SCR_EL3.TWE. Traps are reported using EC syndrome value 0x01 .

values.label: The delay for taking the trap is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .
The delay for taking the trap is at least the number of cycles defined in SCR_EL3.TWEDEL.

fields.name: ECVEn
fields.description: ECV Enable. Enables access to the CNTPOFF_EL2 register.

values.label: EL2 accesses to CNTPOFF_EL2 are trapped to EL3, and the value of CNTPOFF_EL2 is treated as 0 for all purposes other than direct reads or writes to the register from EL3.
EL2 accesses to CNTPOFF_EL2 are not trapped to EL3 by this mechanism.

fields.name: FGTEn
fields.description: Fine-Grained Traps Enable. When EL2 is implemented, enables the traps to EL2 controlled by HAFGRTR_EL2 , HDFGRTR_EL2 , HDFGWTR_EL2 , HFGRTR_EL2 , HFGITR_EL2 , and HFGWTR_EL2 , and controls access to those registers. If EL2 is not implemented but EL3 is implemented, FEAT_FGT implements the MDCR_EL3 .TDCC traps.

values.label: EL2 accesses to HAFGRTR_EL2 , HDFGRTR_EL2 , HDFGWTR_EL2 , HFGRTR_EL2 , HFGITR_EL2 and HFGWTR_EL2 registers are trapped to EL3, and the traps to EL2 controlled by those registers are disabled.
EL2 accesses to HAFGRTR_EL2 , HDFGRTR_EL2 , HDFGWTR_EL2 , HFGRTR_EL2 , HFGITR_EL2 and HFGWTR_EL2 registers are not trapped to EL3 by this mechanism.

fields.name: ATA
fields.description: Memory tagging enable override: Overrides enabling of Memory tagging at EL2, EL1 and EL0. If not trapped to EL2, accesses to the following registers at EL1 and EL2 are trapped to EL3 and reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : GCR_EL1 . RGSR_EL1 . TFSRE0_EL1 . TFSR_EL1 . TFSR_EL2 . Accesses with the register name TFSR_EL12 that are not UNDEFINED .

values.label: Disables the use of Memory tagging at EL2, EL1 and EL0. The specified registers are trapped to EL3 unless trapped to a lower Exception level.
This field does not disable the use of Memory tagging at EL2, EL1 and EL0. The field does not trap the specified registers to EL3.

fields.name: EnSCXT
fields.description: Enables access to the SCXTNUM_EL2 , SCXTNUM_EL1 , and SCXTNUM_EL0 registers.

values.label: Accesses at EL0, EL1 and EL2 to SCXTNUM_EL0 , SCXTNUM_EL1 , or SCXTNUM_EL2 registers are trapped to EL3 if they are not trapped by a higher priority exception, and the values of these registers are treated as 0.
This control does not cause any accesses to be trapped, or register values to be treated as 0.

fields.name: TID5
fields.description: Trap ID group 5. EL2 and EL1 reads of the group 5 ID register GMID_EL1 are trapped to EL3, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 , unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

The specified EL2 accesses to ID group 5 registers are trapped to EL3. When EL2 is not enabled in the current Security state, or when HCR_EL2 .TID5 is 0, EL1 read accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL3.

fields.name: TID3
fields.description: Trap ID group 3. EL2 and EL1 reads of the following group 3 registers are trapped to EL3, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 , unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception: ID_PFR0_EL1 , ID_PFR1_EL1 , ID_DFR0_EL1 , ID_AFR0_EL1 , ID_MMFR0_EL1 , ID_MMFR1_EL1 , ID_MMFR2_EL1 , ID_MMFR3_EL1 , ID_ISAR0_EL1 , ID_ISAR1_EL1 , ID_ISAR2_EL1 , ID_ISAR3_EL1 , ID_ISAR4_EL1 , ID_ISAR5_EL1 , MVFR0_EL1 , MVFR1_EL1 , and MVFR2_EL1 . ID_AA64PFR0_EL1 , ID_AA64PFR1_EL1 , ID_AA64DFR0_EL1 , ID_AA64DFR1_EL1 , ID_AA64ISAR0_EL1 , ID_AA64ISAR1_EL1 , ID_AA64MMFR0_EL1 , ID_AA64MMFR1_EL1 , ID_AA64AFR0_EL1 , and ID_AA64AFR1_EL1 . ID_PFR2_EL1 , and ID_MMFR4_EL1 . ID_MMFR5_EL1 . ID_AA64MMFR3_EL1 . ID_AA64MMFR4_EL1 . ID_AA64PFR2_EL1 . ID_AA64MMFR2_EL1 and ID_ISAR6_EL1 . ID_DFR1_EL1 . ID_AA64DFR2_EL1 . ID_AA64ZFR0_EL1 . ID_AA64SMFR0_EL1 . ID_AA64FPFR0_EL1 . ID_AA64ISAR2_EL1 . ID_AA64ISAR3_EL1 . This field traps all MRS accesses to registers in the following range that are not already mentioned in this field description: op0 == 3, op1 == 0, CRn == 0, CRm == {2-7}, op2 == {0-7}.

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

The specified EL2 read accesses to ID group 3 registers are trapped to EL3. When EL2 is not enabled in the current Security state, or when HCR_EL2 .TID3 is 0, EL1 read accesses to the specified registers are trapped to EL3.

fields.name: FIEN
fields.description: Fault Injection enable. Trap accesses to the registers ERXPFGCDN_EL1 , ERXPFGCTL_EL1 , and ERXPFGF_EL1 from EL1 and EL2 to EL3, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: Accesses to the specified registers from EL1 and EL2 generate a Trap exception to EL3.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: NMEA
fields.description: Non-maskable External Aborts. Controls whether PSTATE.A masks SError exceptions at EL3.

values.label: SError exceptions are not taken at EL3 if PSTATE.A == 1.
SError exceptions are taken at EL3 regardless of the value of PSTATE.A.

fields.name: EASE
fields.description: External aborts to SError exception vector.

values.label: Synchronous External abort exceptions taken to EL3 are taken to the appropriate synchronous exception vector offset from VBAR_EL3 .
Synchronous External abort exceptions taken to EL3 are taken to the appropriate SError exception vector offset from VBAR_EL3 .
All behaviors associated with Secure EL2 are disabled. All registers, including timer registers, defined by FEAT_SEL2 are UNDEFINED , and those timers are disabled.
All behaviors associated with Secure EL2 are enabled.

fields.name: API
fields.description: Controls the use of the following instructions related to Pointer Authentication. PACGA. AUTDA, AUTDB, AUTDZA, AUTDZB, AUTIA, AUTIA1716, AUTIASP, AUTIAZ, AUTIB, AUTIB1716, AUTIBSP, AUTIBZ, AUTIZA, AUTIZB, PACDA, PACDB, PACDZA, PACDZB, PACIA, PACIA1716, PACIASP, PACIAZ, PACIB, PACIB1716, PACIBSP, PACIBZ, PACIZA, PACIZB, RETAA, RETAB, BRAA, BRAB, BLRAA, BLRAB, BRAAZ, BRABZ, BLRAAZ, BLRABZ, ERETAA, ERETAB, LDRAA and LDRAB, when any of the following are true: In EL0, when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, and the associated SCTLR_EL1 .En<N><M> == 1. In EL0, when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, and the associated SCTLR_EL2 .En<N><M> == 1. In EL1, when the associated SCTLR_EL1 .En<N><M> == 1. In EL2, when the associated SCTLR_EL2 .En<N><M> == 1. When FEAT_PAuth_LR is implemented, AUTIASPPC, AUTIASPPCR, AUTIA171615, AUTIBSPPC, AUTIBSPPCR, AUTIB171615, PACIASPPC, PACNBIASPPC, PACIA171615, PACIBSPPC, PACNBIBSPPC, PACIB171615, RETAASPPC, RETAASPPCR, RETABSPPC, RETABSPPCR, when any of the following are true: In EL0, when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, and the associated SCTLR_EL1 .En<N><M> == 1. In EL0, when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, and the associated SCTLR_EL2 .En<N><M> == 1. In EL1, when the associated SCTLR_EL1 .En<N><M> == 1. In EL2, when the associated SCTLR_EL2 .En<N><M> == 1.

values.label: The specified instructions are trapped to EL3, when the instructions are enabled, unless they are trapped to EL2 as a result of the higher priority HCR_EL2 .API trap.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: APK
fields.description: Trap registers holding "key" values for Pointer Authentication. Traps accesses to the following registers, using EC syndrome value 0x18 , from EL1 or EL2 to EL3 unless they are trapped to EL2 as a result of the HCR_EL2.APK bit or other traps: APIAKeyLo_EL1 , APIAKeyHi_EL1 , APIBKeyLo_EL1 , APIBKeyHi_EL1 . APDAKeyLo_EL1 , APDAKeyHi_EL1 , APDBKeyLo_EL1 , APDBKeyHi_EL1 . APGAKeyLo_EL1 , and APGAKeyHi_EL1 .

values.label: Access to the registers holding "key" values for pointer authentication from EL1 or EL2 are trapped to EL3 unless they are trapped to EL2 as a result of the HCR_EL2 .APK bit or other traps.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: TERR
fields.description: Trap accesses of Error Record registers. Enables a trap to EL3 on accesses of Error Record registers.

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
Accesses of the specified Error Record registers at EL2 and EL1 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.

fields.name: TLOR
fields.description: Trap LOR registers. Traps Non-secure and Realm accesses to the LORSA_EL1 , LOREA_EL1 , LORN_EL1 , LORC_EL1 , and LORID_EL1 registers from EL1 and EL2 to EL3, unless the access has been trapped to EL2.

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
Non-secure and Realm EL1 and EL2 accesses to the LOR registers that are not UNDEFINED are trapped to EL3, unless it is trapped by HCR_EL2 .TLOR.

fields.name: TWE
fields.description: Traps EL2, EL1, and EL0 execution of WFE instructions to EL3, from any Security state and both Execution states, reported using EC syndrome value 0x01 . When FEAT_WFxT is implemented, this trap also applies to the WFET instruction.

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

Any attempt to execute a WFE instruction at any Exception level lower than EL3 is trapped to EL3, if the instruction would otherwise have caused the PE to enter a low-power state and it is not trapped by SCTLR .nTWE, HCR .TWE, SCTLR_EL1 .nTWE, SCTLR_EL2 .nTWE, or HCR_EL2 .TWE.

fields.name: TWI
fields.description: Traps EL2, EL1, and EL0 execution of WFI instructions to EL3, from any Security state and both Execution states, reported using EC syndrome value 0x01 . When FEAT_WFxT is implemented, this trap also applies to the WFIT instruction.

values.label: This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

Any attempt to execute a WFI instruction at any Exception level lower than EL3 is trapped to EL3, if the instruction would otherwise have caused the PE to enter a low-power state and it is not trapped by SCTLR .nTWI, HCR .TWI, SCTLR_EL1 .nTWI, SCTLR_EL2 .nTWI, or HCR_EL2 .TWI.

fields.name: ST
fields.description: Traps Secure EL1 accesses to the Counter-timer Physical Secure timer registers to EL3, from AArch64 state only, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: Secure EL1 using AArch64 accesses to the CNTPS_TVAL_EL1 , CNTPS_CTL_EL1 , and CNTPS_CVAL_EL1 are trapped to EL3 when Secure EL2 is disabled. If Secure EL2 is enabled, the behavior is as if the value of this field was 0b1 .
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: RW
fields.description: Execution state control for lower Exception levels.

values.label: All lower Exception levels are using AArch32.

The next lower Exception level is using AArch64: If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the Security state determined by SCR_EL3.{NSE,NS}, then EL2 is using AArch64 and EL2 controls the Execution state for EL1. If EL2 is not implemented or EL2 is disabled in the Security state determined by SCR_EL3.{NSE,NS}, then EL1 is using AArch64. When executing at EL0, if the next higher Exception level is using AArch64, then the Execution state is determined by PSTATE.nRW. Otherwise, EL0 uses AArch32.

fields.name: SIF
fields.description: Secure instruction fetch. When the PE is in Secure state, this bit disables instruction execution from memory marked in the first stage of translation as being Non-secure.

values.label: Secure state instruction execution from memory marked in the first stage of translation as being Non-secure is permitted.
Secure state instruction execution from memory marked in the first stage of translation as being Non-secure is not permitted.

fields.name: HCE
fields.description: Hypervisor Call instruction enable. Enables HVC instructions at EL3 and, if EL2 is enabled in the current Security state, at EL2 and EL1, in both Execution states, reported using EC syndrome value 0x00 .

values.label: HVC instructions are UNDEFINED .
HVC instructions are enabled at EL3, EL2, and EL1.

fields.name: SMD
fields.description: Secure Monitor Call disable. Disables SMC instructions at EL1 and above, from any Security state and both Execution states, reported using EC syndrome value 0x00 .

values.label: SMC instructions are enabled at EL3, EL2 and EL1.
SMC instructions are UNDEFINED .

fields.name: EA
fields.description: External Abort and SError exception routing.

values.label: When executing at Exception levels below EL3, External aborts and SError exceptions are not taken to EL3. In addition, when executing at EL3: SError exceptions are not taken. External aborts are taken to EL3.
When executing at any Exception level, External aborts and SError exceptions are taken to EL3.
When executing at Exception levels below EL3, physical FIQ interrupts are not taken to EL3. When executing at EL3, physical FIQ interrupts are not taken.
When executing at any Exception level, physical FIQ interrupts are taken to EL3.
When executing at Exception levels below EL3, physical IRQ interrupts are not taken to EL3. When executing at EL3, physical IRQ interrupts are not taken.
When executing at any Exception level, physical IRQ interrupts are taken to EL3.

fields.name: NS
fields.description: Non-secure bit. This field is used in combination with SCR_EL3.NSE to select the Security state of EL2 and lower Exception levels.

values.label: Indicates that EL0 and EL1 are in Secure state. When FEAT_SEL2 is implemented and SCR_EL3.EEL2 == 1, then EL2 is using AArch64 and in Secure state.
Indicates that Exception levels lower than EL3 are in Non-secure state, so memory accesses from those Exception levels cannot access Secure memory.

name: SCTLR2MASK_EL1
description: Mask register to prevent updates of fields in SCTLR2_EL1 on writes to SCTLR2_EL1 or SCTLR2ALIAS_EL1.

values.label: SCTLR2_EL1 .TLBOSNIS is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .TLBOSNIS is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .FDIT is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .FDIT is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .BTD is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .BTD is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .BTD0 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .BTD0 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnIA2 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnIA2 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnIB2 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnIB2 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnDA2 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnDA2 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnDB2 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnDB2 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .CPTM0 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .CPTM0 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .CPTM is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .CPTM is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .CPTA0 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .CPTA0 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .CPTA is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .CPTA is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnPACM0 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnPACM0 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnPACM is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnPACM is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnIDCP128 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnIDCP128 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EASE is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EASE is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnANERR is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnANERR is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnADERR is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .EnADERR is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .NMEA is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL1 .NMEA is not writeable.

name: SCTLR2MASK_EL2
description: Mask register to prevent updates of fields in SCTLR2_EL2 on writes.

values.label: SCTLR2_EL2 .TLBOSNIS is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .TLBOSNIS is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .FDIT is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .FDIT is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .BTD is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .BTD is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .BTD0 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .BTD0 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnIA2 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnIA2 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnIB2 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnIB2 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnDA2 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnDA2 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnDB2 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnDB2 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .CPTM0 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .CPTM0 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .CPTM is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .CPTM is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .CPTA0 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .CPTA0 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .CPTA is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .CPTA is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnPACM0 is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnPACM0 is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnPACM is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnPACM is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnIDCP128 is writeable.

SCTLR2_EL2 .EnANERR is not writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnADERR is writeable.
SCTLR2_EL2 .EnADERR is not writeable.

name: SCTLR2_EL1
description: Provides top-level control of the system, including its memory system, at EL1 and EL0.

fields.name: TLBOSNIS
fields.description: TLBI for Outer shareable excludes Inner shareable. This control applies to TLBI OS instructions executed at EL1.

values.label: TLBI OS instructions affect TLBs in the Inner and Outer shareability domains.
TLBI OS instructions affect TLBs in the Outer shareability domain, but not in the Inner shareability domain.

fields.name: FDIT
fields.description: Enforce data-independent timing for execution at EL0.

values.label: This control does not affect data-independent timing of execution.
Data-independent timing of execution is enforced.

fields.name: BTD
fields.description: Controls the implicit BTI behavior of the following instructions at EL1: PACIASP and PACIBSP

values.label: The implicit BTI behavior of the instructions is unaffected.
When the instructions are executed, they have no implicit BTI behavior.

fields.name: BTD0
fields.description: Controls the implicit BTI behavior of the following instructions at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime: PACIASP and PACIBSP

values.label: The implicit BTI behavior of the instructions is unaffected.
When the instructions are executed, they have no implicit BTI behavior.

fields.name: EnIA2
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIAKey_EL1 key, at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime. This field is used in conjunction with SCTLR_EL1 .EnIA as follows: EnIA2 EnIA Behavior at EL0 0b0 0b0 Disabled 0b0 0b1 Enabled 0b1 0b0 Enabled 0b1 0b1 Disabled

fields.name: EnIB2
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIBKey_EL1 key, at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime. This field is used in conjunction with SCTLR_EL1 .EnIB as follows: EnIB2 EnIB Behavior at EL0 0b0 0b0 Disabled 0b0 0b1 Enabled 0b1 0b0 Enabled 0b1 0b1 Disabled

fields.name: EnDA2
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDAKey_EL1 key, at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime. This field is used in conjunction with SCTLR_EL1 .EnDA as follows: EnDA2 EnDA Behavior at EL0 0b0 0b0 Disabled 0b0 0b1 Enabled 0b1 0b0 Enabled 0b1 0b1 Disabled

fields.name: EnDB2
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDBKey_EL1 key, at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime. This field is used in conjunction with SCTLR_EL1 .EnDB as follows: EnDB2 EnDB Behavior at EL0 0b0 0b0 Disabled 0b0 0b1 Enabled 0b1 0b0 Enabled 0b1 0b1 Disabled

fields.name: CPTM0
fields.description: This field controls unprivileged Checked Pointer Arithmetic for Multiplication.

values.label: Pointer Arithmetic for Multiplication is not checked.
Pointer Arithmetic for Multiplication is checked.

fields.name: CPTM
fields.description: This field controls Checked Pointer Arithmetic for Multiplication at EL1.

values.label: Pointer Arithmetic for Multiplication is not checked.
Pointer Arithmetic for Multiplication is checked.

fields.name: CPTA0
fields.description: This field controls unprivileged Checked Pointer Arithmetic for Addition.

values.label: Pointer Arithmetic for Addition is not checked.
Pointer Arithmetic for Addition is checked.

fields.name: CPTA
fields.description: This field controls Checked Pointer Arithmetic for Addition at EL1.

values.label: Pointer Arithmetic for Addition is not checked.
Pointer Arithmetic for Addition is checked.

fields.name: EnPACM0
fields.description: PACM Enable at EL0. Controls the effect of a PACM instruction at EL0.

values.label: The effects of PACM are disabled at EL0.
A PACM instruction at EL0 causes PSTATE.PACM to be set to 0b1 .

fields.name: EnPACM
fields.description: PACM Enable at EL1. Controls the effect of a PACM instruction at EL1.

values.label: The effects of PACM are disabled at EL1.
A PACM instruction at EL1 causes PSTATE.PACM to be set to 0b1 .

fields.name: EnIDCP128
fields.description: Enables access to IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED 128-bit System registers.

values.label: Accesses at EL0 to IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED 128-bit System registers are trapped to EL1 using an ESR_EL1.EC value of 0x14 , unless the access generates a higher priority exception. Disables the functionality of the 128-bit IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED System registers that are accessible at EL1.
No accesses are trapped by this control.

fields.name: EASE
fields.description: External Aborts to SError exception vector.

values.label: Synchronous External abort exceptions taken to EL1 are taken to the appropriate synchronous exception vector offset from VBAR_EL1 .
Synchronous External abort exceptions taken to EL1 are taken to the appropriate SError exception vector offset from VBAR_EL1 .
External aborts on Normal memory reads generate synchronous Data Abort exceptions in the EL1&0 translation regime.
External aborts on Normal memory reads generate synchronous Data Abort or asynchronous SError exceptions in the EL1&0 translation regime.
External aborts on Device memory reads generate synchronous Data Abort exceptions in the EL1&0 translation regime.
External aborts on Device memory reads generate synchronous Data Abort or asynchronous SError exceptions in the EL1&0 translation regime.

fields.name: NMEA
fields.description: Non-maskable External Aborts. Controls whether PSTATE.A masks physical SError exceptions at EL1.

values.label: Physical SError exceptions are not taken at EL1 if PSTATE.A == 1, unless routed to a higher Exception level.
Physical SError exceptions are taken at EL1 regardless of the value of PSTATE.A, unless routed to a higher Exception level.

name: SCTLR2_EL2
description: Provides top-level control of the system, including its memory system, at EL2. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, these controls also apply to execution at EL0.

fields.name: TLBOSNIS
fields.description: TLBI for Outer shareable excludes Inner shareable. This control applies to TLBI OS instructions executed at EL2.

values.label: TLBI OS instructions affect TLBs in the Inner and Outer shareability domains.
TLBI OS instructions affect TLBs in the Outer shareability domain, but not in the Inner shareability domain.

fields.name: FDIT
fields.description: Enforce data-independent timing for execution at EL0.

values.label: This control does not affect data-independent timing of execution.
Data-independent timing of execution is enforced.

fields.name: BTD
fields.description: Controls the implicit BTI behavior of the following instructions at EL2: PACIASP and PACIBSP

values.label: The implicit BTI behavior of the instructions is unaffected.
When the instructions are executed, they have no implicit BTI behavior.

fields.name: BTD0
fields.description: Controls the implicit BTI behavior of the following instructions at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime: PACIASP and PACIBSP

values.label: The implicit BTI behavior of the instructions is unaffected.
When the instructions are executed, they have no implicit BTI behavior.

fields.name: EnIA2
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIAKey_EL1 key, at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime. This field is used in conjunction with SCTLR_EL2 .EnIA as follows: EnIA2 EnIA Behavior at EL0 0b0 0b0 Disabled 0b0 0b1 Enabled 0b1 0b0 Enabled 0b1 0b1 Disabled

fields.name: EnIB2
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIBKey_EL1 key, at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime. This field is used in conjunction with SCTLR_EL2 .EnIB as follows: EnIB2 EnIB Behavior at EL0 0b0 0b0 Disabled 0b0 0b1 Enabled 0b1 0b0 Enabled 0b1 0b1 Disabled

fields.name: EnDA2
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDAKey_EL1 key, at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime. This field is used in conjunction with SCTLR_EL2 .EnDA as follows: EnDA2 EnDA Behavior at EL0 0b0 0b0 Disabled 0b0 0b1 Enabled 0b1 0b0 Enabled 0b1 0b1 Disabled

fields.name: EnDB2
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDBKey_EL1 key, at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime. This field is used in conjunction with SCTLR_EL2 .EnDB as follows: EnDB2 EnDB Behavior at EL0 0b0 0b0 Disabled 0b0 0b1 Enabled 0b1 0b0 Enabled 0b1 0b1 Disabled

fields.name: EnPACM

values.label: The effects of PACM are disabled at EL2.

fields.name: EnIDCP128

values.label: Accesses at EL0 to IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED 128-bit System registers are trapped to EL2 using an ESR_EL2.EC value of 0x14 , unless the access generates a higher priority exception. Disables the functionality of the 128-bit IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED System registers that are accessible at EL2.

fields.name: EASE
fields.description: External Aborts to SError exception vector.

values.label: Synchronous External abort exceptions taken to EL2 are taken to the appropriate synchronous exception vector offset from VBAR_EL2 .
Synchronous External abort exceptions taken to EL2 are taken to the appropriate SError exception vector offset from VBAR_EL2 .
External aborts on Normal memory reads generate synchronous Data Abort exceptions in the EL2 and EL2&0 translation regimes.
External aborts on Normal memory reads generate synchronous Data Abort or asynchronous SError exceptions in the EL2 and EL2&0 translation regimes.
External aborts on Device memory reads generate synchronous Data Abort exceptions in the EL2 and EL2&0 translation regimes.
External aborts on Device memory reads generate synchronous Data Abort or asynchronous SError exceptions in the EL2 and EL2&0 translation regimes.

fields.name: NMEA
fields.description: Non-maskable External Aborts. Controls whether physical SError exceptions are masked at EL2 when PSTATE.A is 1 or HCR_EL2.{AMO, TGE} is {0, 0}.

values.label: The masking of physical SError exceptions at EL2 is unaffected by this mechanism.
Physical SError exceptions are taken at EL2 regardless of whether they are masked by any mechanism, unless they are routed to EL3.

fields.name: EMEC
fields.description: Enables MEC. When enabled, memory accesses to the Realm physical address space are associated with a MECID.

values.label: MEC is not enabled for the Realm physical address space.

name: SCTLR_EL1
description: Provides top-level control of the system, including its memory system, at EL1 and EL0.

fields.name: TIDCP
fields.description: Trap IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED functionality. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, traps EL0 accesses to the encodings reserved for IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED functionality to EL1.

values.label: No instructions accessing the System register or System instruction spaces are trapped by this mechanism.

Instructions accessing the following System register or System instruction spaces are trapped to EL1 by this mechanism: In AArch64 state, EL0 access to the encodings in the following reserved encoding spaces are trapped: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED System instructions, which are accessed using SYS and SYSL, with CRn == {11, 15}, and are reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED System instructions, which are accessed using SYSP, with CRn == {11, 15}, and are reported using EC syndrome value 0x14 . IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED System registers, which are accessed using MRS and MSR with the S3_<op1>_<Cn>_<Cm>_<op2> register name, and are reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED System registers, which are accessed using MRRS and MSRR with the S3_<op1>_<Cn>_<Cm>_<op2> register name, and are reported using EC syndrome value 0x14 . In AArch32 state, EL0 MCR and MRC access to the following encodings are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 : All coproc==p15, CRn==c9, opc1 == {0-7}, CRm == {c0-c2, c5-c8}, opc2 == {0-7}. All coproc==p15, CRn==c10, opc1 =={0-7}, CRm == {c0, c1, c4, c8}, opc2 == {0-7}. All coproc==p15, CRn==c11, opc1=={0-7}, CRm == {c0-c8, c15}, opc2 == {0-7}.

fields.name: SPINTMASK
fields.description: SP Interrupt Mask enable. When SCTLR_EL1.NMI is 1, controls whether PSTATE.SP acts as an interrupt mask, and controls the value of PSTATE.ALLINT on taking an exception to EL1.

values.label: Does not cause PSTATE.SP to mask interrupts. PSTATE.ALLINT is set to 1 on taking an exception to EL1.

When PSTATE.SP is 1 and execution is at EL1, an IRQ or FIQ interrupt that is targeted to EL1 is masked regardless of any indication of Superpriority. PSTATE.ALLINT is set to 0 on taking an exception to EL1.
This control does not affect interrupt masking behavior.

This control enables all of the following: The use of the PSTATE.ALLINT interrupt mask. IRQ and FIQ interrupts to have Superpriority as an additional attribute. PSTATE.SP to be used as an interrupt mask.

fields.name: EnTP2
fields.description: Traps instructions executed at EL0 that access TPIDR2_EL0 to EL1, or to EL2 when EL2 is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.
This field has no effect on Tag checking.
Explicit Memory Read Effects generated by instructions executed in EL1 are Tag Unchecked.

fields.name: TCSO0
fields.description: When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, Tag Checking Store Only in EL0.

values.label: This field has no effect on Tag checking.
Explicit Memory Read Effects generated by instructions executed in EL0 are Tag Unchecked.

fields.name: EPAN
fields.description: Enhanced Privileged Access Never. When PSTATE.PAN is 1, determines whether an EL1 data access to a page with stage 1 EL0 instruction access permission generates a Permission fault as a result of the Privileged Access Never mechanism.

values.label: No additional Permission faults are generated by this mechanism.

An EL1 data access to a page with stage 1 EL0 data access permission or stage 1 EL0 instruction access permission generates a Permission fault. Any speculative data accesses that would generate a Permission fault as a result of PSTATE.PAN = 1 if the accesses were not speculative, will not cause an allocation into a cache. When executing at EL1, this does not prevent unprivileged speculative accesses generated from the EL0 hardware-defined context from causing allocation into a cache.

fields.name: EnALS
fields.description: When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, traps execution of an LD64B or ST64B instruction at EL0 to EL1.

values.label: Execution of an LD64B or ST64B instruction at EL0 is trapped to EL1.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: EnAS0
fields.description: When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, traps execution of an ST64BV0 instruction at EL0 to EL1.

values.label: Execution of an ST64BV0 instruction at EL0 is trapped to EL1.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: EnASR
fields.description: When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, traps execution of an ST64BV instruction at EL0 to EL1.

values.label: Execution of an ST64BV instruction at EL0 is trapped to EL1.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: TWEDEL
fields.description: TWE Delay. A 4-bit unsigned number that, when SCTLR_EL1.TWEDEn is 1, encodes the minimum delay in taking a trap of WFE* caused by SCTLR_EL1.nTWE as 2 (TWEDEL + 8) cycles.

fields.name: TWEDEn
fields.description: TWE Delay Enable. Enables a configurable delayed trap of the WFE* instruction caused by SCTLR_EL1.nTWE.

values.label: The delay for taking the trap is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .
The delay for taking the trap is at least the number of cycles defined in SCTLR_EL1.TWEDEL.

fields.name: DSSBS
fields.description: Default PSTATE.SSBS value on Exception Entry.

values.label: PSTATE.SSBS is set to 0 on an exception to EL1.
PSTATE.SSBS is set to 1 on an exception to EL1.

fields.name: ATA
fields.description: Allocation Tag Access in EL1. Controls use of Memory tagging in EL1.

values.label: Use of Memory tagging is disabled at EL1.
Use of Memory tagging is enabled at EL1.

fields.name: ATA0
fields.description: Allocation Tag Access in EL0. Controls access to Allocation Tags and Tag Check operations in EL0 when HCR_EL2 ,{E2H,TGE} is not {1,1}.

values.label: Use of Memory tagging is disabled at EL0.
Use of Memory tagging is enabled at EL0.

fields.name: TCF
fields.description: Tag Check Fault in EL1. Controls the effect of Tag Check Faults due to Loads and Stores in EL1.

values.label: Tag Check Faults have no effect on the PE.
Tag Check Faults cause a synchronous exception.
Tag Check Faults are asynchronously accumulated.
Tag Check Faults cause a synchronous exception on reads, and are asynchronously accumulated on writes.

fields.name: TCF0
fields.description: Tag Check Fault in EL0. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, controls the effect of Tag Check Faults due to Loads and Stores in EL0.

values.label: Tag Check Faults have no effect on the PE.
Tag Check Faults cause a synchronous exception.
Tag Check Faults are asynchronously accumulated.
Tag Check Faults cause a synchronous exception on reads, and are asynchronously accumulated on writes.

fields.name: ITFSB
fields.description: When synchronous exceptions are not being generated by Tag Check Faults, this field controls whether on exception entry into EL1, all Tag Check Faults due to instructions executed before exception entry, that are reported asynchronously, are synchronized into TFSRE0_EL1 and TFSR_EL1 registers.

values.label: Tag Check Faults are not synchronized on entry to EL1.
Tag Check Faults are synchronized on entry to EL1.

fields.name: BT1
fields.description: Configures the Branch Type compatibility of the implicit BTI behavior for the following instructions at EL1: PACIASP . PACIBSP . If FEAT_PAuth_LR is implemented, PACIASPPC . If FEAT_PAuth_LR is implemented, PACIBSPPC .

values.label: When the PE is executing at EL1, when the specified instructions have an implicit BTI behavior, they are compatible with the same BTYPE values as BTI.jc.
When the PE is executing at EL1, when the specified instructions have an implicit BTI behavior, they are compatible with the same BTYPE values as BTI.c.

fields.name: BT0
fields.description: Configures the Branch Type compatibility of the implicit BTI behavior for the following instructions at EL0: PACIASP . PACIBSP . If FEAT_PAuth_LR is implemented, PACIASPPC . If FEAT_PAuth_LR is implemented, PACIBSPPC .

values.label: When the PE is executing at EL0, when the specified instructions have an implicit BTI behavior, they are compatible with the same BTYPE values as BTI.jc.
When the PE is executing at EL0, when the specified instructions have an implicit BTI behavior, they are compatible with the same BTYPE values as BTI.c.

fields.name: EnFPM
fields.description: Enables direct and indirect accesses to FPMR from EL0. When accesses to FPMR are disabled by this control: Direct accesses to FPMR from EL0 are trapped to EL1, or to EL2 when EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1. These exceptions are reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . Execution of FP8 data-processing instructions that indirectly access FPMR is UNDEFINED at EL0.

values.label: Direct and indirect accesses to FPMR are disabled at EL0.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: MSCEn
fields.description: Memory Copy and Memory Set instructions Enable. Enables execution of the Memory Copy and Memory Set instructions at EL0.

values.label: Execution of the Memory Copy and Memory Set instructions is UNDEFINED at EL0.
This control does not cause any instructions to be UNDEFINED .

fields.name: CMOW
fields.description: Controls the required permissions for the following cache maintenance instructions executed at EL0: Any DC instruction that operates by VA and performs a clean and invalidate operation. Any IC instruction that operates by VA.

values.label: This control does not cause any instruction to generate a stage 1 Permission fault.
For these instructions, when executed at EL0, the absence of stage 1 unprivileged write permission generates a stage 1 permission fault.

fields.name: EnIA
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIAKey_EL1 key, at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime.

values.label: For execution at EL1, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is not enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL1 .EnIA2.

For execution at EL1, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL1 .EnIA2.

fields.name: EnIA
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIAKey_EL1 key, at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime.

values.label: Pointer authentication of instruction addresses at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is not enabled.
Pointer authentication of instruction addresses at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is enabled.

fields.name: EnIB
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIBKey_EL1 key, at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime.

values.label: For execution at EL1, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is not enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL1 .EnIB2.

For execution at EL1, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL1 .EnIB2.

fields.name: EnIB
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIBKey_EL1 key, at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime.

values.label: Pointer authentication of instruction addresses at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is not enabled.
Pointer authentication of instruction addresses at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is enabled.

fields.name: LSMAOE
fields.description: Load Multiple and Store Multiple Atomicity and Ordering Enable.

values.label: For all memory accesses at EL0, T32 and A32 Load Multiple and Store Multiple can have an interrupt taken during the sequence memory accesses, and the memory accesses are not required to be ordered.
The ordering and interrupt behavior of T32 and A32 Load Multiple and Store Multiple at EL0 is as defined for Armv8.0.

fields.name: nTLSMD
fields.description: No Trap Load Multiple and Store Multiple to Device-nGRE/Device-nGnRE/Device-nGnRnE memory.

values.label: All memory accesses by T32 and A32 Load Multiple and Store Multiple at EL0 that are marked at stage 1 as Device-nGRE/Device-nGnRE/Device-nGnRnE memory are trapped and generate a stage 1 Alignment fault.
All memory accesses by T32 and A32 Load Multiple and Store Multiple at EL0 that are marked at stage 1 as Device-nGRE/Device-nGnRE/Device-nGnRnE memory are not trapped.

fields.name: EnDA
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of data addresses, using the APDAKey_EL1 key, at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime.

values.label: For execution at EL1, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is not enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL1 .EnDA2.

For execution at EL1, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL1 .EnDA2.

fields.name: EnDA
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of data addresses, using the APDAKey_EL1 key, at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime.

values.label: Pointer authentication of data addresses at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is not enabled.
Pointer authentication of data addresses at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is enabled.

fields.name: UCI
fields.description: Traps EL0 execution of cache maintenance instructions, to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled in the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, from AArch64 state only, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 , as follows: DC CVAU , DC CIVAC , DC CVAC , and IC IVAU . If FEAT_MTE is implemented, DC CIGVAC , DC CIGDVAC , DC CGVAC , and DC CGDVAC . If FEAT_DPB is implemented, DC CVAP . If FEAT_DPB and FEAT_MTE are implemented, DC CGVAP and DC CGDVAP . If FEAT_DPB2 is implemented, DC CVADP . If FEAT_DPB2 and FEAT_MTE are implemented, DC CGVADP and DC CGDVADP . If FEAT_OCCMO is implemented, DC CIVAOC , DC CIGDVAOC , DC CVAOC and DC CGDVAOC .

values.label: For each of the specified instructions, if the execution of the instruction can be trapped, access at EL0 using AArch64 is trapped.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: EE
fields.description: Endianness of data accesses at EL1, and stage 1 translation table walks in the EL1&0 translation regime.

values.label: Explicit data accesses at EL1, and stage 1 translation table walks in the EL1&0 translation regime are little-endian.
Explicit data accesses at EL1, and stage 1 translation table walks in the EL1&0 translation regime are big-endian.

fields.name: RES1
fields.description: Explicit data accesses at EL1, and stage 1 translation table walks in the EL1&0 translation regime are big-endian.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Explicit data accesses at EL1, and stage 1 translation table walks in the EL1&0 translation regime are little-endian.

fields.name: E0E
fields.description: Endianness of data accesses at EL0.

values.label: Explicit data accesses at EL0 are little-endian.
Explicit data accesses at EL0 are big-endian.

fields.name: RES1
fields.description: Explicit data accesses at EL0 are big-endian.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Explicit data accesses at EL0 are little-endian.

fields.name: SPAN
fields.description: Set Privileged Access Never, on taking an exception to EL1.

values.label: PSTATE.PAN is set to 1 on taking an exception to EL1.
The value of PSTATE.PAN is left unchanged on taking an exception to EL1.

fields.name: EIS
fields.description: Exception Entry is a context synchronization event.

values.label: The taking of an exception to EL1 is not a context synchronization event.
The taking of an exception to EL1 is a context synchronization event.

fields.name: IESB
fields.description: Implicit Error Synchronization event enable. Possible values are:

values.label: Disabled.
An implicit error synchronization event is added: At each exception taken to EL1. Before the operational pseudocode of each Exception Return instruction executed at EL1.

fields.name: TSCXT
fields.description: Trap EL0 Access to the SCXTNUM_EL0 register, when EL0 is using AArch64.

values.label: EL0 access to SCXTNUM_EL0 is not disabled by this mechanism.

EL0 access to SCXTNUM_EL0 is disabled, causing an exception to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1. The value of SCXTNUM_EL0 is treated as 0.

fields.name: WXN
fields.description: Write permission implies XN (Execute-never). For the EL1&0 translation regime, this bit can restrict execute permissions on writeable pages.

values.label: This control has no effect on memory access permissions.
In the EL1&0 translation regime, any region of memory that is writeable at EL1 is XN at EL1, and any region of memory that is writeable at EL0 is XN at EL0.

fields.name: nTWE
fields.description: Traps EL0 execution of WFE instructions to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, from both Execution states, reported using EC syndrome value 0x01 . When FEAT_WFxT is implemented, this trap also applies to the WFET instruction.

values.label: Any attempt to execute a WFE instruction at EL0 is trapped, if the instruction would otherwise have caused the PE to enter a low-power state.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: nTWI
fields.description: Traps EL0 execution of WFI instructions to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, from both Execution states, reported using EC syndrome value 0x01 . When FEAT_WFxT is implemented, this trap also applies to the WFIT instruction.

values.label: Any attempt to execute a WFI instruction at EL0 is trapped, if the instruction would otherwise have caused the PE to enter a low-power state.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: UCT
fields.description: Traps EL0 accesses to the CTR_EL0 to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, from AArch64 state only, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: Accesses to the CTR_EL0 from EL0 using AArch64 are trapped.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: DZE
fields.description: Traps EL0 execution of the following instructions to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, from AArch64 state only, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 : DC ZVA . If FEAT_MTE is implemented, DC GVA and DC GZVA . If FEAT_MTETC is implemented, DC GBVA and DC ZGBVA . Traps EL0 execution of DC ZVA instructions to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, from AArch64 state only, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: Any attempt to execute an instruction that this trap applies to at EL0 using AArch64 is trapped. Reading DCZID_EL0 .DZP from EL0 returns 1, indicating that the instructions this trap applies to are not supported.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: EnDB
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of data addresses, using the APDBKey_EL1 key, at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime.

values.label: For execution at EL1, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is not enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL1 .EnDB2.

For execution at EL1, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL1 .EnDB2.

fields.name: EnDB
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of data addresses, using the APDBKey_EL1 key, at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime.

values.label: Pointer authentication of data addresses at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is not enabled.
Pointer authentication of data addresses at EL1 and at EL0 in the EL1&0 translation regime, using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is enabled.

fields.name: I
fields.description: Stage 1 instruction access Cacheability control, for accesses at EL0 and EL1:

values.label: All instruction access to Stage 1 Normal memory from EL0 and EL1 are Stage 1 Non-cacheable. If the value of SCTLR_EL1.M is 0, instruction accesses from stage 1 of the EL1&0 translation regime are to Normal, Outer Shareable, Inner Non-cacheable, Outer Non-cacheable memory.

This control has no effect on the Stage 1 Cacheability of instruction access to Stage 1 Normal memory from EL0 and EL1. If the value of SCTLR_EL1.M is 0, instruction accesses from stage 1 of the EL1&0 translation regime are to Normal, Outer Shareable, Inner Write-Through, Outer Write-Through memory.

fields.name: EOS
fields.description: Exception Exit is a context synchronization event.

values.label: An exception return from EL1 is not a context synchronization event
An exception return from EL1 is a context synchronization event

fields.name: EnRCTX
fields.description: Enable EL0 access to the following System instructions: CFPRCTX , DVPRCTX and CPPRCTX instructions. If FEAT_SPECRES2 is implemented, COSPRCTX . CFP RCTX , DVP RCTX and CPP RCTX instructions. If FEAT_SPECRES2 is implemented, COSP RCTX .

values.label: EL0 access to these instructions is disabled, and these instructions are trapped to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1.
EL0 access to these instructions is enabled.

fields.name: UMA
fields.description: User Mask Access. Traps EL0 execution of MSR and MRS instructions that access the PSTATE.{D, A, I, F} masks to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, from AArch64 state only, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: Any attempt at EL0 using AArch64 to execute an MRS , MSR(register) , or MSR(immediate) instruction that accesses the DAIF is trapped.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: SED
fields.description: SETEND instruction disable. Disables SETEND instructions at EL0 using AArch32.

values.label: SETEND instruction execution is enabled at EL0 using AArch32.

SETEND instructions are UNDEFINED at EL0 using AArch32 and any attempt at EL0 to access a SETEND instruction generates an exception to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, reported using EC syndrome value 0x00 .

fields.name: ITD
fields.description: IT Disable. Disables some uses of IT instructions at EL0 using AArch32.

values.label: All IT instruction functionality is enabled at EL0 using AArch32.

Any attempt at EL0 using AArch32 to execute any of the following is UNDEFINED and generates an exception, reported using EC syndrome value 0x00 , to EL1 or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1: All encodings of the IT instruction with hw1[3:0]!=1000. All encodings of the subsequent instruction with the following values for hw1: 0b11xxxxxxxxxxxxxx : All 32-bit instructions, and the 16-bit instructions B, UDF, SVC, LDM, and STM. 0b1011xxxxxxxxxxxx : All instructions in 'Miscellaneous 16-bit instructions' . 0b10100xxxxxxxxxxx : ADD Rd, PC, #imm 0b01001xxxxxxxxxxx : LDR Rd, [PC, #imm] 0b0100x1xxx1111xxx : ADD Rdn, PC; CMP Rn, PC; MOV Rd, PC; BX PC; BLX PC. 0b010001xx1xxxx111 : ADD PC, Rm; CMP PC, Rm; MOV PC, Rm. This pattern also covers unpredictable cases with BLX Rn. These instructions are always UNDEFINED , regardless of whether they would pass or fail the condition code check that applies to them as a result of being in an IT block. It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether the IT instruction is treated as: A 16-bit instruction, that can only be followed by another 16-bit instruction. The first half of a 32-bit instruction. This means that, for the situations that are UNDEFINED , either the second 16-bit instruction or the 32-bit instruction is UNDEFINED . An implementation might vary dynamically as to whether IT is treated as a 16-bit instruction or the first half of a 32-bit instruction.

fields.name: nAA
fields.description:
Non-aligned access. This bit controls generation of Alignment faults at EL1 and EL0 under certain conditions. The following instructions generate an Alignment fault if all bytes being accessed are not within a single naturally aligned 16-byte quantity, for access: LDAPR, LDAPRH, LDAPUR, LDAPURH, LDAPURSH, LDAPURSW, LDAR, LDARH, LDLAR, LDLARH. STLLR, STLLRH, STLR, STLRH, STLUR, and STLURH. If FEAT_LRCPC3 is implemented, the post index versions of LDAPR and the pre index versions of STLR. If FEAT_LRCPC3 and Advanced SIMD and floating-point instructions are implemented, LDAPUR (SIMD&FP), LDAP1 (SIMD&FP), STLUR (SIMD&FP), and STL1 (SIMD&FP).
If FEAT_LRCPC3 is implemented, the following instructions generate an Alignment fault if all bytes being accessed for a single register are not within a single naturally aligned 16-byte quantity, for access: LDIAPP, STILP.

values.label: Unaligned accesses by the specified instructions generate an Alignment fault.
This control does not generate Alignment faults.

fields.name: CP15BEN
fields.description: System instruction memory barrier enable. Enables accesses to the DMB, DSB, and ISB System instructions in the (coproc== 0b1111 ) encoding space from EL0:

values.label: EL0 using AArch32: EL0 execution of the CP15DMB , CP15DSB , and CP15ISB instructions is UNDEFINED and generates an exception to EL1, or to EL2 when it is implemented and enabled for the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x00 .
EL0 using AArch32: EL0 execution of the CP15DMB , CP15DSB , and CP15ISB instructions is enabled.

fields.name: SA0
fields.description: SP Alignment check enable for EL0. When set to 1, if a load or store instruction executed at EL0 uses the SP as the base address and the SP is not aligned to a 16-byte boundary, then an SP alignment fault exception is generated. For more information, see 'SP alignment checking' . When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, this bit has no effect on execution at EL0.

fields.name: SA
fields.description: SP Alignment check enable. When set to 1, if a load or store instruction executed at EL1 uses the SP as the base address and the SP is not aligned to a 16-byte boundary, then an SP alignment fault exception is generated. For more information, see 'SP alignment checking' . When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, this bit has no effect on the PE.

fields.name: C
fields.description: Stage 1 Cacheability control, for data accesses.

values.label: All data access to Stage 1 Normal memory from EL0 and EL1, and all Normal memory accesses from unified cache to the EL1&0 Stage 1 translation tables, are treated as Stage 1 Non-cacheable.
This control has no effect on the Stage 1 Cacheability of: Data access to Normal memory from EL0 and EL1. Normal memory accesses to the EL1&0 Stage 1 translation tables.

fields.name: A
fields.description: Alignment check enable. This is the enable bit for Alignment fault checking at EL1 and EL0.

values.label: Alignment fault checking is disabled when executing at EL1 or EL0. Alignment checks on some instructions are not disabled by this control. For more information, see 'Alignment of data accesses' .

Alignment fault checking is enabled when executing at EL1 or EL0. All instructions that load or store one or more registers have an alignment check that the address being accessed is aligned to the size of the data element(s) being accessed. If this check fails it causes an Alignment fault, which is taken as a Data Abort exception.

fields.name: M
fields.description: MMU enable for EL1&0 stage 1 address translation.

values.label: EL1&0 stage 1 address translation disabled. See the SCTLR_EL1.I field for the behavior of instruction accesses to Normal memory.
EL1&0 stage 1 address translation enabled.

name: SCTLR_EL2
description: Provides top-level control of the system, including its memory system, at EL2. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, these controls apply also to execution at EL0.

fields.name: TIDCP
fields.description: Trap IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED functionality. Traps EL0 accesses to the encodings reserved for IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED functionality to EL2.

values.label: No instructions accessing the System register or System instruction spaces are trapped by this mechanism.

If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .TGE is 0, no instructions accessing the System register or System instruction spaces are trapped by this mechanism. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, instructions accessing the following System register or System instruction spaces are trapped to EL2 by this mechanism: In AArch64 state, EL0 access to the encodings in the following reserved encoding spaces are trapped: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED System instructions, which are accessed using SYS and SYSL, with CRn == {11, 15}, and are reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED System instructions, which are accessed using SYSP, with CRn == {11, 15}, and are reported using EC syndrome value 0x14 . IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED System registers, which are accessed using MRS and MSR with the S3_<op1>_<Cn>_<Cm>_<op2> register name, and are reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED System registers, which are accessed using MRRS and MSRR with the S3_<op1>_<Cn>_<Cm>_<op2> register name, and are reported using EC syndrome value 0x14 . In AArch32 state, EL0 MCR and MRC accesses to the following encodings are trapped and reported using EC syndrome value 0x03 : All coproc==p15, CRn==c9, opc1 == {0-7}, CRm == {c0-c2, c5-c8}, opc2 == {0-7}. All coproc==p15, CRn==c10, opc1 =={0-7}, CRm == {c0, c1, c4, c8}, opc2 == {0-7}. All coproc==p15, CRn==c11, opc1=={0-7}, CRm == {c0-c8, c15}, opc2 == {0-7}.

fields.name: SPINTMASK
fields.description: SP Interrupt Mask enable. When SCTLR_EL2.NMI is 1, controls whether PSTATE.SP acts as an interrupt mask, and controls the value of PSTATE.ALLINT on taking an exception to EL2.

values.label: Does not cause PSTATE.SP to mask interrupts. PSTATE.ALLINT is set to 1 on taking an exception to EL2.

When PSTATE.SP is 1 and execution is at EL2, an IRQ or FIQ interrupt that is targeted to EL2 is masked regardless of any indication of Superpriority. PSTATE.ALLINT is set to 0 on taking an exception to EL2.
This control does not affect interrupt masking behavior.

This control enables all of the following: The use of the PSTATE.ALLINT interrupt mask. IRQ and FIQ interrupts to have Superpriority as an additional attribute. PSTATE.SP to be used as an interrupt mask.

fields.name: EnTP2
fields.description: Traps instructions executed at EL0 that access TPIDR2_EL0 to EL2 when EL2 is implemented and enabled for the current Security state. The exception is reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 .

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.
This field has no effect on Tag checking.
Explicit Memory Read Effects generated by instructions executed in EL2 are Tag Unchecked.

fields.name: TCSO0
fields.description: Tag Checking Store Only in EL0.

values.label: This field has no effect on Tag checking.
Explicit Memory Read Effects generated by instructions executed in EL0 are Tag Unchecked.

fields.name: EPAN
fields.description: Enhanced Privileged Access Never. When PSTATE.PAN is 1, determines whether an EL2 data access to a page with EL0 instruction access permission generates a Permission fault as a result of the Privileged Access Never mechanism.

values.label: No additional Permission faults are generated by this mechanism.

An EL2 data access to a page with stage 1 EL0 data access permission or stage 1 EL0 instruction access permission generates a Permission fault. Any speculative data accesses that would generate a Permission fault as a result of PSTATE.PAN = 1 if the accesses were not speculative, will not cause an allocation into a cache. When executing at EL2, and the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, this does not prevent unprivileged speculative accesses generated from the EL0 hardware-defined context from causing allocation into a cache.

fields.name: EnALS
fields.description: Traps execution of an LD64B or ST64B instruction at EL0 to EL2.

values.label: Execution of an LD64B or ST64B instruction at EL0 is trapped to EL2.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: EnAS0
fields.description: Traps execution of an ST64BV0 instruction at EL0 to EL2.

values.label: Execution of an ST64BV0 instruction at EL0 is trapped to EL2.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: EnASR
fields.description: Traps execution of an ST64BV instruction at EL0 to EL2.

values.label: Execution of an ST64BV instruction at EL0 is trapped to EL2.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: TWEDEL
fields.description: TWE Delay. A 4-bit unsigned number that, when SCTLR_EL2.TWEDEn is 1, encodes the minimum delay in taking a trap of WFE* caused by SCTLR_EL2.nTWE as 2 (TWEDEL + 8) cycles.

fields.name: TWEDEn
fields.description: TWE Delay Enable. Enables a configurable delayed trap of the WFE* instruction caused by SCTLR_EL2.nTWE.

values.label: The delay for taking a WFE* trap is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .
The delay for taking a WFE* trap is at least the number of cycles defined in SCTLR_EL2.TWEDEL.

fields.name: DSSBS
fields.description: Default PSTATE.SSBS value on exception entry.

values.label: PSTATE.SSBS is set to 0 on an exception to EL2.
PSTATE.SSBS is set to 1 on an exception to EL2.

fields.name: ATA
fields.description: Allocation Tag Access in EL2. Controls use of Memory tagging in EL2.

values.label: Use of Memory tagging is disabled at EL2.
Use of Memory tagging is enabled at EL2.

fields.name: ATA0
fields.description: Allocation Tag Access in EL0. Controls use of Memory Tagging in EL0.

values.label: Use of Memory tagging is disabled at EL0.
Use of Memory tagging is enabled at EL2.

fields.name: TCF
fields.description: Tag Check Fault in EL2. Controls the effect of Tag Check Faults due to Loads and Stores in EL2.

values.label: Tag Check Faults have no effect on the PE.
Tag Check Faults cause a synchronous exception.
Tag Check Faults are asynchronously accumulated.
Tag Check Faults cause a synchronous exception on reads, and are asynchronously accumulated on writes.

fields.name: TCF0
fields.description: Tag Check Fault in EL0. Controls the effect of Tag Check Faults due to Loads and Stores in EL0.

values.label: Tag Check Faults have no effect on the PE.
Tag Check Faults cause a synchronous exception.
Tag Check Faults are asynchronously accumulated.
Tag Check Faults cause a synchronous exception on reads, and are asynchronously accumulated on writes.

fields.name: ITFSB
fields.description: When synchronous exceptions are not being generated by Tag Check Faults, this field controls whether on exception entry into EL2, all Tag Check Faults due to instructions executed before exception entry, that are reported asynchronously, are synchronized into TFSRE0_EL1 , TFSR_EL1 , and TFSR_EL2 registers.

values.label: Tag Check Faults are not synchronized on entry to EL2.
Tag Check Faults are synchronized on entry to EL2.

fields.name: BT
fields.description: Indicates the Branch Type compatibility of the implicit BTI behavior for the following instructions at EL2: PACIASP . PACIBSP . If FEAT_PAuth_LR is implemented, PACIASPPC . If FEAT_PAuth_LR is implemented, PACIBSPPC . When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, this bit is named BT1.

values.label: When the PE is executing at EL2, when the specified instructions have an implicit BTI behavior, they are compatible with the same BTYPE values as BTI.jc.
When the PE is executing at EL2, when the specified instructions have an implicit BTI behavior, they are compatible with the same BTYPE values as BTI.c.

fields.name: BT0
fields.description: Indicates the Branch Type compatibility of the implicit BTI behavior for the following instructions at EL0: PACIASP . PACIBSP . If FEAT_PAuth_LR is implemented, PACIASPPC . If FEAT_PAuth_LR is implemented, PACIBSPPC .

values.label: When the PE is executing at EL0, when the specified instructions have an implicit BTI behavior, they are compatible with the same BTYPE values as BTI.jc.
When the PE is executing at EL0, when the specified instructions have an implicit BTI behavior, they are compatible with the same BTYPE values as BTI.c.

fields.name: EnFPM
fields.description: Enables direct and indirect accesses to FPMR from EL0. When accesses to FPMR are disabled by this control: Direct accesses to FPMR from EL0 are trapped to EL2 and reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 . Execution of FP8 data-processing instructions that indirectly access FPMR is UNDEFINED at EL0.

values.label: Direct and indirect accesses to FPMR are disabled at EL0.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.
