fields.name: MSCEn
fields.description: Memory Copy and Memory Set instructions Enable. Enables execution of the Memory Copy and Memory Set instructions at EL0.

values.label: Execution of the Memory Copy and Memory Set instructions is UNDEFINED at EL0.
This control does not cause any instructions to be UNDEFINED .

fields.name: CMOW
fields.description: Controls the required permissions for the following cache maintenance instructions executed at EL0: Any DC instruction that operates by VA and performs a clean and invalidate operation. Any IC instruction that operates by VA.

values.label: This control does not cause any instruction to generate a stage 1 Permission fault.
For these instructions, when executed at EL0, the absence of stage 1 unprivileged write permission generates a stage 1 permission fault.

fields.name: EnIA
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIAKey_EL1 key, at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime.

values.label: For execution at EL2, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is not enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL2 .EnIA2.

For execution at EL2, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL2 .EnIA2.

fields.name: EnIA
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIAKey_EL1 key, at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime.

values.label: Pointer authentication of instruction addresses at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is not enabled.
Pointer authentication of instruction addresses at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is enabled.

fields.name: EnIB
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIBKey_EL1 key, at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime.

values.label: For execution at EL2, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is not enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL2 .EnIB2.

For execution at EL2, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of instruction addresses using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL2 .EnIB2.

fields.name: EnIB
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIBKey_EL1 key, at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime.

values.label: Pointer authentication of instruction addresses at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is not enabled.
Pointer authentication of instruction addresses at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is enabled.

fields.name: LSMAOE
fields.description: Load Multiple and Store Multiple Atomicity and Ordering Enable.

values.label: For all memory accesses at EL0, T32 and A32 Load Multiple and Store Multiple can have an interrupt taken during the sequence memory accesses, and the memory accesses are not required to be ordered.
The ordering and interrupt behavior of T32 and A32 Load Multiple and Store Multiple at EL0 is as defined for Armv8.0.

fields.name: nTLSMD
fields.description: No Trap Load Multiple and Store Multiple to Device-nGRE/Device-nGnRE/Device-nGnRnE memory.

values.label: All memory accesses by T32 and A32 Load Multiple and Store Multiple at EL0 that are marked at stage 1 as Device-nGRE/Device-nGnRE/Device-nGnRnE memory are trapped and generate a stage 1 Alignment fault.
All memory accesses by T32 and A32 Load Multiple and Store Multiple at EL0 that are marked at stage 1 as Device-nGRE/Device-nGnRE/Device-nGnRnE memory are not trapped.

fields.name: EnDA
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of data addresses, using the APDAKey_EL1 key, at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime.

values.label: For execution at EL2, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is not enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL2 .EnDA2.

For execution at EL2, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL2 .EnDA2.

fields.name: EnDA
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of data addresses, using the APDAKey_EL1 key, at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime.

values.label: Pointer authentication of data addresses at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is not enabled.
Pointer authentication of data addresses at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is enabled.

fields.name: UCI
fields.description: Traps execution of cache maintenance instructions at EL0 to EL2, from AArch64 state only, reported using EC syndrome value 0x18 , as follows: DC CVAU , DC CIVAC , DC CVAC , and IC IVAU . If FEAT_MTE is implemented, DC CIGVAC , DC CIGDVAC , DC CGVAC , and DC CGDVAC . If FEAT_DPB is implemented, DC CVAP . If FEAT_DPB and FEAT_MTE are implemented, DC CGVAP and DC CGDVAP . If FEAT_DPB2 is implemented, DC CVADP . If FEAT_DPB2 and FEAT_MTE are implemented, DC CGVADP and DC CGDVADP . If FEAT_OCCMO is implemented, DC CIVAOC , DC CIGDVAOC , DC CVAOC and DC CGDVAOC .

values.label: For each of the specified instructions, if the execution of the instruction can be trapped, access at EL0 using AArch64 is trapped to EL2.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: EE
fields.description: Endianness of data accesses at EL2, stage 1 translation table walks in the EL2 or EL2&0 translation regime, and stage 2 translation table walks in the EL1&0 translation regime.

values.label: Explicit data accesses at EL2, stage 1 translation table walks in the EL2 or EL2&0 translation regime, and stage 2 translation table walks in the EL1&0 translation regime are little-endian.
Explicit data accesses at EL2, stage 1 translation table walks in the EL2 or EL2&0 translation regime, and stage 2 translation table walks in the EL1&0 translation regime are big-endian.

fields.name: RES1
fields.description: Explicit data accesses at EL1, and stage 1 translation table walks in the EL1&0 translation regime are big-endian.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Explicit data accesses at EL1, and stage 1 translation table walks in the EL1&0 translation regime are little-endian.

fields.name: E0E
fields.description: Endianness of data accesses at EL0.

values.label: Explicit data accesses at EL0 are little-endian.
Explicit data accesses at EL0 are big-endian.

fields.name: RES1
fields.description: Explicit data accesses at EL0 are big-endian.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Explicit data accesses at EL0 are little-endian.

fields.name: SPAN
fields.description: Set Privileged Access Never, on taking an exception to EL2.

values.label: PSTATE.PAN is set to 1 on taking an exception to EL2.
The value of PSTATE.PAN is left unchanged on taking an exception to EL2.

fields.name: EIS
fields.description: Exception entry is a context synchronization event.

values.label: The taking of an exception to EL2 is not a context synchronization event.
The taking of an exception to EL2 is a context synchronization event.
Disabled.
An implicit error synchronization event is added: At each exception taken to EL2. Before the operational pseudocode of each Exception Return instruction executed at EL2.

fields.name: TSCXT
fields.description: Trap EL0 Access to the SCXTNUM_EL0 register, when EL0 is using AArch64.

values.label: EL0 access to SCXTNUM_EL0 is not disabled by this mechanism.
EL0 access to SCXTNUM_EL0 is disabled, causing an exception to EL2, and the SCXTNUM_EL0 value is treated as 0.

fields.name: WXN
fields.description: Write permission implies XN (Execute-never). For the EL2 or EL2&0 translation regime, this bit can restrict execute permissions on writeable pages.

values.label: This control has no effect on memory access permissions.
In the EL2 or EL2&0 translation regime, any region of memory that is writeable at EL2 is XN at EL2. In the EL2&0 translation regime, any region of memory that is writeable at EL0 is XN at EL0.

fields.name: nTWE
fields.description: Traps execution of WFE instructions at EL0 to EL2, from both Execution states. When FEAT_WFxT is implemented, this trap also applies to the WFET instruction.

values.label: Any attempt to execute a WFE instruction at EL0 is trapped to EL2, if the instruction would otherwise have caused the PE to enter a low-power state.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: nTWI
fields.description: Traps execution of WFI instructions at EL0 to EL2, from both Execution states. When FEAT_WFxT is implemented, this trap also applies to the WFIT instruction.

values.label: Any attempt to execute a WFI instruction at EL0 is trapped EL2, if the instruction would otherwise have caused the PE to enter a low-power state.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: UCT
fields.description: Traps EL0 accesses to the CTR_EL0 to EL2, from AArch64 state only.

values.label: Accesses to the CTR_EL0 from EL0 using AArch64 are trapped to EL2.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: DZE
fields.description: Traps execution of the following instructions at EL0 to EL2, from AArch64 state only: DC ZVA . If FEAT_MTE is implemented, DC GVA and DC GZVA . If FEAT_MTETC is implemented, DC GBVA and DC ZGBVA .

values.label: Any attempt to execute an instruction that this trap applies to at EL0 using AArch64 is trapped to EL2. Reading DCZID_EL0 .DZP from EL0 returns 1, indicating that the instructions that this trap applies to are not supported.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: EnDB
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of data addresses, using the APDBKey_EL1 key, at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime.

values.label: For execution at EL2, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is not enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL2 .EnDB2.

For execution at EL2, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is enabled. For execution at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, pointer authentication of data addresses using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is controlled by a combination of this field and SCTLR2_EL2 .EnDB2.

fields.name: EnDB
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of data addresses, using the APDBKey_EL1 key, at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime.

values.label: Pointer authentication of data addresses at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is not enabled.
Pointer authentication of data addresses at EL2 and at EL0 in the EL2&0 translation regime, using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is enabled.

fields.name: I
fields.description: Instruction access Cacheability control, for accesses at EL2 and, when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, EL0.

values.label: All instruction accesses to Normal memory from EL2 are Non-cacheable for all levels of instruction and unified cache. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, all instruction accesses to Normal memory from EL0 are Non-cacheable for all levels of instruction and unified cache. If SCTLR_EL2.M is 0, instruction accesses from stage 1 of the EL2 or EL2&0 translation regime are to Normal, Outer Shareable, Inner Non-cacheable, Outer Non-cacheable memory.

This control has no effect on the Cacheability of instruction access to Normal memory from EL2 and, when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, instruction access to Normal memory from EL0. If the value of SCTLR_EL2.M is 0, instruction accesses from stage 1 of the EL2 or EL2&0 translation regime are to Normal, Outer Shareable, Inner Write-Through, Outer Write-Through memory.

fields.name: EOS
fields.description: Exception exit is a context synchronization event.

values.label: An exception return from EL2 is not a context synchronization event.
An exception return from EL2 is a context synchronization event.

fields.name: EnRCTX
fields.description: Enable EL0 access to the following System instructions: CFPRCTX , DVPRCTX and CPPRCTX instructions. If FEAT_SPECRES2 is implemented, COSPRCTX . CFP RCTX , DVP RCTX and CPP RCTX instructions. If FEAT_SPECRES2 is implemented, COSP RCTX .

values.label: EL0 access to these instructions is disabled, and these instructions are trapped to EL2.
EL0 access to these instructions is enabled.

fields.name: SED
fields.description: SETEND instruction disable. Disables SETEND instructions at EL0 using AArch32.

values.label: SETEND instruction execution is enabled at EL0 using AArch32.
SETEND instructions are UNDEFINED at EL0 using AArch32.

fields.name: SED
fields.description: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .TGE is 0, the field is IGNORED for all purposes other than direct reads and writes of the register.

fields.name: ITD
fields.description: IT Disable. Disables some uses of IT instructions at EL0 using AArch32.

values.label: All IT instruction functionality is enabled at EL0 using AArch32.

Any attempt at EL0 using AArch32 to execute any of the following is UNDEFINED : All encodings of the IT instruction with hw1[3:0]!=1000. All encodings of the subsequent instruction with the following values for hw1: 0b11xxxxxxxxxxxxxx : All 32-bit instructions, and the 16-bit instructions B, UDF, SVC, LDM, and STM. 0b1011xxxxxxxxxxxx : All instructions in 'Miscellaneous 16-bit instructions' . 0b10100xxxxxxxxxxx : ADD Rd, PC, #imm 0b01001xxxxxxxxxxx : LDR Rd, [PC, #imm] 0b0100x1xxx1111xxx : ADD Rdn, PC; CMP Rn, PC; MOV Rd, PC; BX PC; BLX PC. 0b010001xx1xxxx111 : ADD PC, Rm; CMP PC, Rm; MOV PC, Rm. This pattern also covers UNPREDICTABLE cases with BLX Rn. These instructions are always UNDEFINED , regardless of whether they would pass or fail the condition code check that applies to them as a result of being in an IT block. It is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether the IT instruction is treated as: A 16-bit instruction, that can only be followed by another 16-bit instruction. The first half of a 32-bit instruction. This means that, for the situations that are UNDEFINED , either the second 16-bit instruction or the 32-bit instruction is UNDEFINED . An implementation might vary dynamically whether IT is treated as a 16-bit instruction or the first half of a 32-bit instruction.

fields.name: nAA
fields.description: Non-aligned access. This bit controls generation of Alignment faults under certain conditions at EL2, and, when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, EL0. The following instructions generate an Alignment fault if all bytes being accessed are not within a single naturally aligned 16-byte quantity, for access: LDAPR, LDAPRH, LDAPUR, LDAPURH, LDAPURSH, LDAPURSW, LDAR, LDARH, LDLAR, LDLARH. STLLR, STLLRH, STLR, STLRH, STLUR, and STLURH. If FEAT_LRCPC3 is implemented, the post index versions of LDAPR and the pre index versions of STLR. If FEAT_LRCPC3 and Advanced SIMD and floating-point instructions are implemented, LDAPUR (SIMD&FP), LDAP1 (SIMD&FP), STLUR (SIMD&FP), and STL1 (SIMD&FP). If FEAT_LRCPC3 is implemented, the following instructions generate an Alignment fault if all bytes being accessed for a single register are not within a single naturally aligned 16-byte quantity, for access: LDIAPP, STILP.

values.label: Unaligned accesses by the specified instructions generate an Alignment fault.
Unaligned accesses by the specified instructions do not generate an Alignment fault.

fields.name: CP15BEN
fields.description: System instruction memory barrier enable. Enables accesses to the DMB, DSB, and ISB System instructions in the (coproc== 0b1111 ) encoding space from EL0:

values.label: EL0 using AArch32: EL0 execution of the CP15DMB , CP15DSB , and CP15ISB instructions is UNDEFINED .
EL0 using AArch32: EL0 execution of the CP15DMB , CP15DSB , and CP15ISB instructions is enabled.

fields.name: SA0
fields.description: SP Alignment check enable for EL0. When set to 1, if a load or store instruction executed at EL0 uses the SP as the base address and the SP is not aligned to a 16-byte boundary, then an SP alignment fault exception is generated. For more information, see 'SP alignment checking' .

fields.name: SA
fields.description: SP Alignment check enable. When set to 1, if a load or store instruction executed at EL2 uses the SP as the base address and the SP is not aligned to a 16-byte boundary, then an SP alignment fault exception is generated. For more information, see 'SP alignment checking' .

fields.name: C
fields.description: Data access Cacheability control, for accesses at EL2 and, when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, EL0

values.label: The following are Non-cacheable for all levels of data and unified cache: Data accesses to Normal memory from EL2. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, Normal memory accesses to the EL2 translation tables. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}: Data accesses to Normal memory from EL0. Normal memory accesses to the EL2&0 translation tables.

This control has no effect on the Cacheability of: Data access to Normal memory from EL2. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, Normal memory accesses to the EL2 translation tables. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}: Data accesses to Normal memory from EL0. Normal memory accesses to the EL2&0 translation tables.

fields.name: A
fields.description: Alignment check enable. This is the enable bit for Alignment fault checking at EL2 and, when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, EL0.

values.label: Alignment fault checking is disabled when executing at EL2. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, alignment fault checking disabled when executing at EL0. Alignment checks on some instructions are not disabled by this control. For more information, see 'Alignment of data accesses' .

Alignment fault checking is enabled when executing at EL2. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, alignment fault checking enabled when executing at EL0. All instructions that load or store one or more registers have an alignment check that the address being accessed is aligned to the size of the data element(s) being accessed. If this check fails it causes an Alignment fault, which is taken as a Data Abort exception.

fields.name: M
fields.description: MMU enable for EL2 or EL2&0 stage 1 address translation.

values.label: When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, EL2 stage 1 address translation disabled. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, EL2&0 stage 1 address translation disabled. See the SCTLR_EL2.I field for the behavior of instruction accesses to Normal memory.

When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, EL2 stage 1 address translation enabled. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}, EL2&0 stage 1 address translation enabled.

name: SCTLR_EL3
description: Provides top-level control of the system, including its memory system, at EL3.

fields.name: SPINTMASK
fields.description: SP Interrupt Mask enable. When SCTLR_EL3.NMI is 1, controls whether PSTATE.SP acts as an interrupt mask, and controls the value of PSTATE.ALLINT on taking an exception to EL3.

values.label: Does not cause PSTATE.SP to mask interrupts. PSTATE.ALLINT is set to 1 on taking an exception to EL3.

When PSTATE.SP is 1 and execution is at EL3, an IRQ or FIQ interrupt that is targeted to EL3 is masked regardless of any indication of Superpriority. PSTATE.ALLINT is set to 0 on taking an exception to EL3.
This control does not affect interrupt masking behavior.

This control enables all of the following: The use of the PSTATE.ALLINT interrupt mask. IRQ and FIQ interrupts to have Superpriority as an additional attribute. PSTATE.SP to be used as an interrupt mask.
This field has no effect on Tag checking.
Explicit Memory Read Effects generated by instructions executed in EL3 are Tag Unchecked.

fields.name: DSSBS
fields.description: Default PSTATE.SSBS value on Exception Entry.

values.label: PSTATE.SSBS is set to 0 on an exception to EL3.
PSTATE.SSBS is set to 1 on an exception to EL3.

fields.name: ATA
fields.description: Allocation Tag Access in EL3. Controls use of Memory tagging in EL3.

values.label: Use of Memory tagging is disabled at EL3.
Use of Memory tagging is enabled at EL3.

fields.name: TCF
fields.description: Tag Check Fault in EL3. Controls the effect of Tag Check Faults due to Loads and Stores in EL3.

values.label: Tag Check Faults have no effect on the PE.
Tag Check Faults cause a synchronous exception.
Tag Check Faults are asynchronously accumulated.
Tag Check Faults cause a synchronous exception on reads, and are asynchronously accumulated on writes.

fields.name: ITFSB
fields.description: When synchronous exceptions are not being generated by Tag Check Faults, this field controls whether on exception entry into EL3, all Tag Check Faults due to instructions executed before exception entry, that are reported asynchronously, are synchronized into TFSRE0_EL1 and TFSR_ELx registers.

values.label: Tag Check Faults are not synchronized on entry to EL3.
Tag Check Faults are synchronized on entry to EL3.

fields.name: BT
fields.description: Indicates the Branch Type compatibility of the implicit BTI behavior for the following instructions at EL3: PACIASP . PACIBSP . If FEAT_PAuth_LR is implemented, PACIASPPC . If FEAT_PAuth_LR is implemented, PACIBSPPC .

values.label: When the PE is executing at EL3, when the specified instructions have an implicit BTI behavior, they are compatible with the same BTYPE values as BTI.jc.
When the PE is executing at EL3, when the specified instructions have an implicit BTI behavior, they are compatible with the same BTYPE values as BTI.c.

fields.name: EnIA
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIAKey_EL1 key, in the EL3 translation regime. Possible values of this bit are:

values.label: Pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is not enabled.
Pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIAKey_EL1 key, is enabled.

fields.name: EnIB
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIBKey_EL1 key, in the EL3 translation regime. Possible values of this bit are:

values.label: Pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is not enabled.
Pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APIBKey_EL1 key, is enabled.

fields.name: EnDA
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APDAKey_EL1 key, in the EL3 translation regime.

values.label: Pointer authentication of data addresses, using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is not enabled.
Pointer authentication of data addresses, using the APDAKey_EL1 key, is enabled.

fields.name: EE
fields.description: Endianness of data accesses at EL3, and stage 1 translation table walks in the EL3 translation regime.

values.label: Explicit data accesses at EL3, and stage 1 translation table walks in the EL3 translation regime are little-endian.
Explicit data accesses at EL3, and stage 1 translation table walks in the EL3 translation regime are big-endian.

fields.name: RES1
fields.description: Explicit data accesses at EL1, and stage 1 translation table walks in the EL1&0 translation regime are big-endian.

fields.name: RES0
fields.description: Explicit data accesses at EL1, and stage 1 translation table walks in the EL1&0 translation regime are little-endian.

fields.name: EIS
fields.description: Exception Entry is a context synchronization event.

values.label: The taking of an exception to EL3 is not a context synchronization event.
The taking of an exception to EL3 is a context synchronization event.
Disabled.
An implicit error synchronization event is added: At each exception taken to EL3. Before the operational pseudocode of each Exception Return instruction executed at EL3.

fields.name: WXN
fields.description: Write permission implies XN (Execute-never). For the EL3 translation regime, this bit can force all memory regions that are writable to be treated as XN.

values.label: This control has no effect on memory access permissions.
Any region that is writable in the EL3 translation regime is forced to XN for accesses from software executing at EL3.

fields.name: EnDB
fields.description: Controls enabling of pointer authentication of instruction addresses, using the APDBKey_EL1 key, in the EL3 translation regime.

values.label: Pointer authentication of data addresses, using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is not enabled.
Pointer authentication of data addresses, using the APDBKey_EL1 key, is enabled.

fields.name: I
fields.description: Instruction access Cacheability control, for accesses at EL3:

values.label: All instruction access to Normal memory from EL3 are Non-cacheable for all levels of instruction and unified cache. If the value of SCTLR_EL3.M is 0, instruction accesses from stage 1 of the EL3 translation regime are to Normal, Outer Shareable, Inner Non-cacheable, Outer Non-cacheable memory.

This control has no effect on the Cacheability of instruction access to Normal memory from EL3. If the value of SCTLR_EL3.M is 0, instruction accesses from stage 1 of the EL3 translation regime are to Normal, Outer Shareable, Inner Write-Through, Outer Write-Through memory.

fields.name: EOS
fields.description: Exception Exit is a context synchronization event.

values.label: An exception return from EL3 is not a context synchronization event
An exception return from EL3 is a context synchronization event

fields.name: nAA
fields.description: Non-aligned access. This bit controls generation of Alignment faults at EL3 under certain conditions. The following instructions generate an Alignment fault if all bytes being accessed are not within a single naturally aligned 16-byte quantity, for access: LDAPR, LDAPRH, LDAPUR, LDAPURH, LDAPURSH, LDAPURSW, LDAR, LDARH, LDLAR, LDLARH. STLLR, STLLRH, STLR, STLRH, STLUR, and STLURH. If FEAT_LRCPC3 is implemented, the post index versions of LDAPR and the pre index versions of STLR. If FEAT_LRCPC3 and Advanced SIMD and floating-point instructions are implemented, LDAPUR (SIMD&FP), LDAP1 (SIMD&FP), STLUR (SIMD&FP), and STL1 (SIMD&FP). If FEAT_LRCPC3 is implemented, the following instructions generate an Alignment fault if all bytes being accessed for a single register are not within a single naturally aligned 16-byte quantity, for access: LDIAPP, STILP.

values.label: Unaligned accesses by the specified instructions generate an Alignment fault.
Unaligned accesses by the specified instructions do not generate an Alignment fault.

fields.name: SA
fields.description: SP Alignment check enable. When set to 1, if a load or store instruction executed at EL3 uses the SP as the base address and the SP is not aligned to a 16-byte boundary, then a SP alignment fault exception is generated. For more information, see 'SP alignment checking' .

values.label: All data access to Normal memory from EL3, and all Normal memory accesses to the EL3 translation tables, are Non-cacheable for all levels of data and unified cache.
This control has no effect on the Cacheability of: Data access to Normal memory from EL3. Normal memory accesses to the EL3 translation tables.

fields.name: A
fields.description: Alignment check enable. This is the enable bit for Alignment fault checking at EL3.

values.label: Alignment fault checking is disabled when executing at EL3. Alignment checks on some instructions are not disabled by this control. For more information, see 'Alignment of data accesses' .

Alignment fault checking is enabled when executing at EL3. All instructions that load or store one or more registers have an alignment check that the address being accessed is aligned to the size of the data element(s) being accessed. If this check fails it causes an Alignment fault, which is taken as a Data Abort exception.

fields.name: M
fields.description: MMU enable for EL3 stage 1 address translation. Possible values of this bit are:

values.label: EL3 stage 1 address translation disabled. See the SCTLR_EL3.I field for the behavior of instruction accesses to Normal memory.
EL3 stage 1 address translation enabled.

name: SCXTNUM_EL0
description: Provides a number that can be used to separate out different context numbers with the EL0 exception level, for the purpose of protecting against side-channels using branch prediction and similar resources.

fields.name: SCXTNUM
fields.description: Software Context Number. A number to identify the context within the EL0 exception level.

name: SCXTNUM_EL1
description: Provides a number that can be used to separate out different context numbers with the EL1 exception level, for the purpose of protecting against side-channels using branch prediction and similar resources.

fields.name: SCXTNUM
fields.description: Software Context Number. A number to identify the context within the EL1 exception level.

name: SCXTNUM_EL2
description: Provides a number that can be used to separate out different context numbers with the EL2 exception level, for the purpose of protecting against side-channels using branch prediction and similar resources.

fields.name: SCXTNUM
fields.description: Software Context Number. A number to identify the context within the EL2 exception level.

name: SCXTNUM_EL3
description: Provides a number that can be used to separate out different context numbers with the EL3 exception level, for the purpose of protecting against side-channels using branch prediction and similar resources.

fields.name: SCXTNUM
fields.description: Software Context Number. A number to identify the context within the EL3 exception level.

name: SDER32_EL2
description: Allows access to the AArch32 register SDER from Secure EL2 and EL3 only.

values.label: This bit has no effect on non-invasive debug.
Non-invasive debug is allowed in Secure EL0 using AArch32.
This bit does not affect the generation of debug exceptions at Secure EL0.
If EL1 is using AArch32, debug exceptions from Secure EL0 are enabled.

name: SDER32_EL3
description: Allows access to the AArch32 register SDER from AArch64 state only. Its value has no effect on execution in AArch64 state.

values.label: This bit has no effect on non-invasive debug.
Non-invasive debug is allowed in Secure EL0 using AArch32.
This bit does not affect the generation of debug exceptions at Secure EL0.
If EL1 is using AArch32, debug exceptions from Secure EL0 are enabled.

name: SMCR_EL1
description: This register controls aspects of Streaming SVE that are visible at Exception levels EL1 and EL0.

fields.name: FA64
fields.description:
Controls whether execution of an A64 instruction
 at EL1
 is considered legal when executed in Streaming SVE mode. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2.{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}, controls whether execution of an A64 instruction at EL0 is considered legal when executed in Streaming SVE mode.

values.label: This control does not cause any instruction to be treated as legal when executed in Streaming SVE mode.

This control causes all implemented A64 instructions to be treated as legal when executed in Streaming SVE mode 
at EL1 and EL0, if they are treated as legal at more privileged Exception levels in the current Security state.

fields.name: EZT0
fields.description:
Traps execution at EL1 and EL0 of the LDR, LUTI2, LUTI4, MOVT, STR, and ZERO instructions that access the ZT0 register to 
EL1, or to EL2 when EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state and HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1. The exception is reported using ESR_EL1 .EC or ESR_EL2 .EC value 0x1D , with an ISS code of 0x0000004 , at a lower priority than a trap due to PSTATE.SM or PSTATE.ZA.

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at EL1 and EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instruction to be trapped.

fields.name: LEN
fields.description:
Requests an Effective Streaming SVE vector length (SVL) at EL1 of (LEN+1)*128 bits.
This field also defines the Effective Streaming SVE vector length at EL0 when EL2 is not implemented, or EL2 is not enabled in the current Security state, or the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is not {1, 1}. The Streaming SVE vector length can be any power of two from 128 bits to 2048 bits inclusive. An implementation can support any subset of the architecturally permitted lengths. When the PE is in Streaming SVE mode, the Effective SVE vector length (VL) is equal to SVL. When FEAT_SVE is implemented, and the PE is not in Streaming SVE mode, VL is equal to the Effective Non-streaming SVE vector length. See ZCR_EL1 . For all purposes other than returning the result of a direct read of SMCR_EL1, the PE selects the Effective Streaming SVE vector length by performing checks in the following order: If the requested length is less than the minimum implemented Streaming SVE vector length, then the Effective length is the minimum implemented Streaming SVE vector length. If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, and the requested length is greater than the Effective length at EL2, then the Effective length at EL2 is used. If EL3 is implemented and the requested length is greater than the Effective length at EL3, then the Effective length at EL3 is used. Otherwise, the Effective length is the highest supported Streaming SVE vector length that is less than or equal to the requested length. An indirect read of SMCR_EL1.LEN appears to occur in program order relative to a direct write of the same register, without the need for explicit synchronization.

name: SMCR_EL2
description: This register controls aspects of Streaming SVE that are visible at Exception levels EL2, EL1, and EL0.

fields.name: FA64
fields.description:
Controls whether execution of an A64 instruction
 at EL2, EL1, and EL0 when EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state
 is considered legal when executed in Streaming SVE mode.

values.label: This control does not cause any instruction to be treated as legal when executed in Streaming SVE mode.

This control causes all implemented A64 instructions to be treated as legal when executed in Streaming SVE mode 
at EL2, EL1, and EL0, if they are treated as legal at EL3.

fields.name: EZT0
fields.description:
Traps execution at EL2, EL1, and EL0 of the LDR, LUTI2, LUTI4, MOVT, STR, and ZERO instructions that access the ZT0 register to 
EL2, when EL2 is enabled in the current Security state. The exception is reported using ESR_EL2 .EC value 0x1D , with an ISS code of 0x0000004 , at a lower priority than a trap due to PSTATE.SM or PSTATE.ZA.

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at EL2, EL1, and EL0 to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instruction to be trapped.

fields.name: LEN
fields.description:
Requests an Effective Streaming SVE vector length (SVL) at EL2 of (LEN+1)*128 bits.
This field also defines the Effective Streaming SVE vector length at EL0 when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .{E2H, TGE} is {1, 1}. The Streaming SVE vector length can be any power of two from 128 bits to 2048 bits inclusive. An implementation can support any subset of the architecturally permitted lengths. When the PE is in Streaming SVE mode, the Effective SVE vector length (VL) is equal to SVL. When FEAT_SVE is implemented, and the PE is not in Streaming SVE mode, VL is equal to the Effective Non-streaming SVE vector length. See ZCR_EL2 . For all purposes other than returning the result of a direct read of SMCR_EL2, the PE selects the Effective Streaming SVE vector length by performing checks in the following order: If the requested length is less than the minimum implemented Streaming SVE vector length, then the Effective length is the minimum implemented Streaming SVE vector length. If EL3 is implemented and the requested length is greater than the Effective length at EL3, then the Effective length at EL3 is used. Otherwise, the Effective length is the highest supported Streaming SVE vector length that is less than or equal to the requested length. An indirect read of SMCR_EL2.LEN appears to occur in program order relative to a direct write of the same register, without the need for explicit synchronization.

name: SMCR_EL3
description: This register controls aspects of Streaming SVE that are visible at all Exception levels.

fields.name: FA64
fields.description:
Controls whether execution of an A64 instruction
 is considered legal when executed in Streaming SVE mode.

values.label: This control does not cause any instruction to be treated as legal when executed in Streaming SVE mode.

This control causes all implemented A64 instructions to be treated as legal when executed in Streaming SVE mode 
.

fields.name: EZT0
fields.description:
Traps execution at all Exception levels of the LDR, LUTI2, LUTI4, MOVT, STR, and ZERO instructions that access the ZT0 register to 
EL3. The exception is reported using ESR_EL3 .EC value 0x1D , with an ISS code of 0x0000004 , at a lower priority than a trap due to PSTATE.SM or PSTATE.ZA.

values.label: This control causes execution of these instructions at all Exception levels to be trapped.
This control does not cause execution of any instruction to be trapped.

fields.name: LEN
fields.description: Requests an Effective Streaming SVE vector length (SVL) at EL3 of (LEN+1)*128 bits. The Streaming SVE vector length can be any power of two from 128 bits to 2048 bits inclusive. An implementation can support any subset of the architecturally permitted lengths. When the PE is in Streaming SVE mode, the Effective SVE vector length (VL) is equal to SVL. When FEAT_SVE is implemented, and the PE is not in Streaming SVE mode, VL is equal to the Effective Non-streaming SVE vector length. See ZCR_EL3 . For all purposes other than returning the result of a direct read of SMCR_EL3, the PE selects the Effective Streaming SVE vector length by performing checks in the following order: If the requested length is less than the minimum implemented Streaming SVE vector length, then the Effective length is the minimum implemented Streaming SVE vector length. Otherwise, the Effective length is the highest supported Streaming SVE vector length that is less than or equal to the requested length. An indirect read of SMCR_EL3.LEN appears to occur in program order relative to a direct write of the same register, without the need for explicit synchronization.

name: SMIDR_EL1
description: Provides additional identification mechanisms for scheduling purposes, for a PE that supports Streaming SVE mode.

fields.name: NSMC
fields.description: If SMIDR_EL1.{SH,Affinity} indicates that the implementation of Streaming SVE mode is shared, then this field identifies the number of SMCUs, minus 1, associated with the concatenated SMIDR_EL1.{Affinity2,Affinity} 32-bit value. If SMIDR_EL1.{SH,Affinity} indicates that the implementation of Streaming SVE mode is not shared, then this field is zero and should be ignored by software.

values.label: The implementation of Streaming SVE mode associated with this PE is not shared or is a single SMCU.
The number of SMCUs in the group of SMCUs providing the implementation of Streaming SVE mode for this PE, minus 1.
Reserved.

fields.name: HIP
fields.description: Highest Implemented Priority. If Streaming SVE mode execution priority is supported, this field indicates the range of priority levels implemented by the PE, and the Highest Implemented Priority value.

values.label: All streaming execution priorities from 0 to 15 are implemented. The Highest Implemented Priority value is 15.
All streaming execution priorities less than or equal to this value are implemented. The Highest Implemented Priority value is the value of this field.

fields.name: Affinity2
fields.description: The most significant 20 bits of the SMCU affinity for this PE, to be used in conjunction with SMIDR_EL1.Affinity.

fields.name: Implementer
fields.description: The Implementer code. This field must hold an implementer code that has been assigned by Arm.

values.label: Reserved for software use.

fields.name: Revision
fields.description: Revision number for the Streaming Mode Compute Unit (SMCU).

fields.name: SMPS
fields.description: Indicates support for Streaming SVE mode execution priority.

values.label: Priority control not supported.
Priority control supported.

fields.name: SH
fields.description: Indicates whether the implementation of Streaming SVE mode in this PE is shared with other PEs.

values.label: Refer to SMIDR_EL1.Affinity.
Reserved.
The implementation of Streaming SVE mode is not shared with other PEs.
The implementation of Streaming SVE mode is shared with other PEs.

fields.name: Affinity
fields.description: The least significant 12 bits of the SMCU affinity for this PE.

name: SMPRIMAP_EL2
description: Maps the value in SMPRI_EL1 to a streaming execution priority value for instructions executed at EL1 and EL0 in the same Security states as EL2.

fields.name: P15
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 15. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '15'. This value is the highest streaming execution priority.

fields.name: P14
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 14. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '14'.

fields.name: P13
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 13. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '13'.

fields.name: P12
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 12. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '12'.

fields.name: P11
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 11. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '11'.

fields.name: P10
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 10. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '10'.

fields.name: P9
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 9. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '9'.

fields.name: P8
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 8. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '8'.

fields.name: P7
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 7. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '7'.

fields.name: P6
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 6. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '6'.

fields.name: P5
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 5. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '5'.

fields.name: P4
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 4. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '4'.

fields.name: P3
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 3. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '3'.

fields.name: P2
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 2. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '2'.

fields.name: P1
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 1. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '1'.

fields.name: P0
fields.description: Priority Mapping Entry 0. This entry is used when priority mapping is supported and enabled, and the SMPRI_EL1 .Priority value is '0'. This value is the lowest streaming execution priority.

name: SMPRI_EL1
description: Configures the streaming execution priority for instructions executed on a shared Streaming Mode Compute Unit (SMCU) when the PE is in Streaming SVE mode at any Exception level.

fields.name: Priority
fields.description: Streaming execution priority value. Either this value is used directly, or it is mapped into an effective priority value using SMPRIMAP_EL2 . This value is used directly when any of the following are true: The current Exception level is EL3 or EL2. The current Exception level is EL1 or EL0, if EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state and HCRX_EL2 .SMPME is '0'. The current Exception level is EL1 or EL0, if EL2 is either not implemented or not enabled in the current Security state.

name: SPMACCESSR_EL1
description: Controls access to System PMUs from EL0.

fields.name: P<m>
fields.description: System PMU <m> access. Controls access to System PMU <m>.

values.label: MRS read and MSR write System register accesses to System PMU <m> at EL0 are trapped to EL1, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
MSR write System register accesses to System PMU <m> at EL0 are trapped to EL1, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

name: SPMACCESSR_EL2
description: Controls access to System PMUs from EL1 and EL0.

fields.name: P<m>

values.label: MRS read and MSR write System register accesses to System PMU <m> at EL1 and EL0 are trapped to EL2, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
MSR write System register accesses to System PMU <m> at EL1 and EL0 are trapped to EL2, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

name: SPMACCESSR_EL3
description: Controls access to System PMUs from EL2, EL1 and EL0.

fields.name: P<m>
fields.description: System PMU <m> access. Controls access to System PMU <m>.

values.label: MRS read and MSR write System register accesses to System PMU <m> at EL2, EL1, and EL0 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
MSR write System register accesses to System PMU <m> at EL2, EL1, and EL0 are trapped to EL3, unless the instruction generates a higher priority exception.
This control does not cause any instructions to be trapped.

name: SPMCFGR_EL1
description: Describes the capabilities of System PMU <s>.

fields.name: NCG
fields.description: Counter Groups. Defines the number of counter groups implemented by System PMU <s>, minus one. If this field is zero, then one counter group is implemented and SPMCGCR<n>_EL1 read-as-zero. Otherwise, for each counter group <m>, SPMCGCR<m DIV 8>_EL1.N<m MOD 8> defines the number of counters in the group. Locating the first counter in each group depends on the number of implemented groups. Each counter group starts with counter: SPMEVTYPER<m×32>_EL0, meaning there are at most 32 counters per group, if there are 2 counter groups. SPMEVTYPER<m×16>_EL0, meaning there are at most 16 counters per group, if there are 3 or 4 counter groups. SPMEVTYPER<m×8>_EL0, meaning there are at most 8 counters per group, if there are between 5 and 8 counter groups. SPMEVTYPER<m×4>_EL0, meaning there are at most 4 counters per group, if there are more than 8 counter groups.

description: Disable event counters in System PMU <s>.

fields.name: TRO

fields.name: HDBG

name: SPMDEVAFF_EL1
long_name: System Performance Monitors Device Affinity Register
description: For additional information, see the CoreSight Architecture Specification. For a System PMU that has affinity with a single PE or a group of PEs, SPMDEVAFF_EL1 is a copy of MPIDR_EL1 or part of MPIDR_EL1 : If the System PMU has affinity with a single PE, then the affinity level is 0 and  SPMDEVAFF_EL1 reads the same value as MPIDR_EL1 , and SPMDEVAFF_EL1.F0V reads-as-one to indicate affinity level 0. If the System PMU has affinity with a group of PEs, then the affinity level is 1, 2, or 3, parts of SPMDEVAFF_EL1 reads the same value as parts of MPIDR_EL1 , and the rest of SPMDEVAFF_EL1 indicates the level. For example, if the group of PEs is a subset of the PEs at affinity level 1, then all of the following are true: All the PEs in the group have the same values in MPIDR_EL1 .{Aff3,Aff2}, and these values are equal to SPMDEVAFF_EL1.{Aff3,Aff2}. SPMDEVAFF_EL1.Aff1 is nonzero and not 0x80 , and SPMDEVAFF_EL1.{Aff0,F0V} read-as-zero, to indicate at least affinity level 1. The subset of PEs at level 1 that the System PMU has affinity with is indicated by the least-significant set bit in SPMDEVAFF_EL1.Aff1. In this example, if SPMDEVAFF_EL1.Aff1[2:0] is 0b100 , then the System PMU has affinity with the up-to 8 PEs that have MPIDR_EL1 .Aff1[7:3] == SPMDEVAFF_EL1.Aff1[7:3]. Depending on the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED nature of the system, it might be possible that SPMDEVAFF_EL1 is read before system firmware has configured the System PMU and/or the PE or group of PEs that the System PMU has affinity with. When this is the case, SPMDEVAFF_EL1 reads-as-zero.

fields.name: Aff3
fields.description: PE affinity level 3. The MPIDR_EL1 .Aff3 field, viewed from the highest Exception level of the associated PE or PEs.

fields.name: F0V
fields.description: Indicates that the SPMDEVAFF_EL1.Aff0 field is valid.

values.label: SPMDEVAFF_EL1.Aff0 is not valid, and the PE affinity is above level 0 or a subset of level 0.
SPMDEVAFF_EL1.Aff0 is valid, and the PE affinity is at level 0.

name: SPMDEVARCH_EL1
long_name: System Performance Monitors Device Architecture Register
description: Provides discovery information for System PMU <s>.

fields.name: ARCHITECT
fields.description: Architect. Defines the architect of the component. Bits [31:28] are the JEP106 continuation code (JEP106 bank ID, minus 1) and bits [27:21] are the JEP106 ID code.

fields.name: PRESENT
fields.description: DEVARCH present. Defines that SPMDEVARCH_EL1 register is present.

values.label: Device Architecture information not present.
Device Architecture information present.

fields.name: REVISION
fields.description: Revision. Defines the architecture revision of the component.

fields.name: ARCHVER
fields.description: Architecture Version. Defines the architecture version of the component.

fields.name: ARCHPART
fields.description: Architecture Part. Defines the architecture of the component.

name: SPMEVCNTR<n>_EL0
long_name: System Performance Monitors Event Count Register
description: Event counter <n> in System PMU <s>, where n is 0 to 63.

name: SPMEVFILT2R<n>_EL0
long_name: System Performance Monitors Event Filter Control Register 2
description: With SPMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 and SPMEVFILTR<n>_EL0 , configures when event counter SPMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 in System PMU <s> increments. The contents of this register are IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED . For more information, see SPMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .

name: SPMEVFILTR<n>_EL0
long_name: System Performance Monitors Event Filter Control Register
description: With SPMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 and SPMEVFILT2R<n>_EL0 , configures when event counter SPMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 in System PMU <s> increments. The contents of this register are IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED . For more information, see SPMEVTYPER<n>_EL0 .

name: SPMEVTYPER<n>_EL0
long_name: System Performance Monitors Event Type Register
description: With SPMEVFILTR<n>_EL0 and SPMEVFILT2R<n>_EL0 , configures when event counter SPMEVCNTR<n>_EL0 in System PMU <s> increments. The contents of this register are IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED . An Event Type Select Register typically contains: A field defining the event that the counter is responsive to, in the least-significant bits. Controls for per-counter filtering, such as by mode or state. Additional controls, such as for a per-counter state machine.

name: SPMIIDR_EL1
description: Provides discovery information for System PMU <s>.

fields.name: ProductID
fields.description: Part number, bits [11:0]. The part number is selected by the designer of the component.

fields.name: Variant
fields.description: Component major revision. Defines either a variant of the component defined by SPMIIDR_EL1.ProductID, or the major revision of the component. When defining a major revision, SPMIIDR_EL1.Variant and SPMIIDR_EL1.Revision together form the revision number of the component, with SPMIIDR_EL1.Variant being the most significant part and SPMIIDR_EL1.Revision the least significant part. When a component is changed, SPMIIDR_EL1.Variant or SPMIIDR_EL1.Revision is increased to ensure that software can differentiate the different revisions of the component. If SPMIIDR_EL1.Variant is increased then SPMIIDR_EL1.Revision should be set to 0b0000 .

fields.name: Revision
fields.description: Component minor revision. When a component is changed: If SPMIIDR_EL1.Variant and SPMIIDR_EL1.Revision together form the revision number of the component then: SPMIIDR_EL1.Variant or SPMIIDR_EL1.Revision is increased to ensure that software can differentiate the different revisions of the component. If Variant is increased then Revision should be set to 0b0000 . Otherwise, SPMIIDR_EL1.Revision is increased to ensure that software can differentiate the different revisions of the component.

fields.name: Implementer
fields.description: Contains the JEP106 manufacturer's identification code of the designer of the System PMU. The code identifies the designer of the component, which might not be the same as the implementer of the device containing the component. For an implementation designed by Arm, this field reads as 0x43B .

name: SPMINTENCLR_EL1
long_name: System Performance Monitors Interrupt Enable Clear Register
description: Disables the generation of interrupt requests on overflows from event counters in System PMU <s>.

fields.name: P<m>
fields.description: Event counter <m> overflow interrupt request disable.

values.label: Event counter <m> in System PMU <s> interrupt request is disabled.
Event counter <m> in System PMU <s> interrupt request is enabled.

name: SPMINTENSET_EL1
long_name: System Performance Monitors Interrupt Enable Set Register
description: Enables the generation of interrupt requests on overflows from event counters in System PMU <s>.

fields.name: P<m>
fields.description: Event counter <m> overflow interrupt request enable.

values.label: Event counter <m> in System PMU <s> interrupt request is disabled.
Event counter <m> in System PMU <s> interrupt request is enabled.

name: SPMOVSCLR_EL0
long_name: System Performance Monitors Overflow Flag Status Clear Register
description: Clears the state of overflow bits for event counters in System PMU <s>.

fields.name: P<m>
fields.description: Event counter <m> unsigned overflow bit clear.

values.label: Event counter <m> in System PMU <s> has not overflowed.
Event counter <m> in System PMU <s> has overflowed.

name: SPMOVSSET_EL0
long_name: System Performance Monitors Overflow Flag Status Set Register
description: Sets the state of overflow bits for event counters in System PMU <s>.

fields.name: P<m>
fields.description: Event counter <m> unsigned overflow bit set.

values.label: Event counter <m> in System PMU <s> has not overflowed.
Event counter <m> in System PMU <s> has overflowed.

name: SPMROOTCR_EL3
long_name: System Performance Monitors Root and Realm Control Register
description: Controls observability of Root and Realm events by System PMU <s>.

fields.name: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED
fields.description: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED observation controls. Additional IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED bits to control certain types of filter or events by System PMU <s>.

fields.name: NAO
fields.description: Non-attributable Observation. Controls whether events or monitorable characteristics not attributable with any source can be monitored by System PMU <s>.

values.label: Events not attributable with any event source are not counted by System PMU <s>.
Counting non-attributable events by System PMU <s> is not prevented by this mechanism.

fields.name: RLO
fields.description: Realm Observation. Controls whether events or monitorable characteristics attributable to a Realm event source can be monitored by System PMU <s>.

values.label: Events attributable to a Realm event source are not counted by System PMU <s>.
Counting events by System PMU <s> that are attributable to a Realm event source is not prevented by this mechanism.

fields.name: RTO
fields.description: Root Observation. Controls whether events or monitorable characteristics attributable to a Root event source can be monitored by System PMU <s>.

values.label: Events attributable to a Root event source are not counted by System PMU <s>.
Counting events by System PMU <s> that are attributable to a Root event source is not prevented by this mechanism.

name: SPMSCR_EL1
long_name: System Performance Monitors Secure Control Register
description: Controls observability of Secure events by System PMU <s>, and optionally controls Secure attributes for message signaled interrupts and Non-secure access to the performance monitor registers.

fields.name: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED
fields.description: IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED observation controls. Additional IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED bits to control certain types of filter or events by System PMU <s>.

fields.name: NAO
fields.description: Non-attributable Observation. Controls whether events or monitorable characteristics not attributable with any source can be monitored by System PMU <s>.

values.label: Events not attributable with any event source are not counted by System PMU <s>, unless overridden by SPMSCR_EL1.SO.
Counting non-attributable events by System PMU <s> is not prevented by this mechanism.

fields.name: SO
fields.description: Secure Observation. Controls whether events or monitorable characteristics attributable to a Secure event source can be monitored by System PMU <s>.

values.label: Events attributable to a Secure event source are not counted by System PMU <s>.
Counting events by System PMU <s> that are attributable to a Secure event source is not prevented by this mechanism.

name: SPMSELR_EL0
description: Selects the System PMU and event counter registers to access.

fields.name: SYSPMUSEL
fields.description: System PMU Select. Selects a System PMU <s> to access.

fields.name: BANK
fields.description: System PMU bank access control. Selects a bank of 16 System PMU event counters and related controls to access.

values.label: Select event counters 0 to 15.
Select event counters 16 to 31.
Select event counters 32 to 47.
Select event counters 48 to 63.

name: SPMZR_EL0
long_name: System Performance Monitors Zero with Mask
description: Zero the set of System PMU event counters specified by the mask written to SPMZR_EL0.

values.label: Write is ignored.
Set event counter<m> in System PMU <s> to zero.

name: SPSel
description: Allows the Stack Pointer to be selected between SP_EL0 and SP_ELx.

fields.name: SP
fields.description: Stack pointer to use. Possible values of this bit are:

values.label: Use SP_EL0 at all Exception levels.

Use SP_ELx for Exception level ELx. When FEAT_NMI is implemented and SCTLR_ELx .SPINTMASK is 1, if execution is at ELx, an IRQ or FIQ interrupt that is targeted to ELx is masked regardless of any indication of Superpriority.

name: SPSR_abt
description: Holds the saved process state when an exception is taken to Abort mode.

name: SPSR_EL1
description: Holds the saved process state when an exception is taken to EL1.

fields.name: UINJ
fields.description: Inject Undefined Instruction exception. Set to 0 on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.UINJ on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: PPEND
fields.description: PMU Profiling exception pending bit. Set to the value of PSTATE.PPEND on taking an exception to EL1, and conditionally  copied to PSTATE.PPEND on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: N
fields.description: Negative Condition flag. Set to the value of PSTATE.N on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.N on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: Z
fields.description: Zero Condition flag. Set to the value of PSTATE.Z on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.Z on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: C
fields.description: Carry Condition flag. Set to the value of PSTATE.C on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.C on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: V
fields.description: Overflow Condition flag. Set to the value of PSTATE.V on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.V on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: Q
fields.description: Overflow or saturation flag. Set to the value of PSTATE.Q on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.Q on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: IT
fields.description: If-Then. Set to the value of PSTATE.IT on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.IT on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: DIT
fields.description: Data Independent Timing. Set to the value of PSTATE.DIT on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.DIT on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: SSBS
fields.description: Speculative Store Bypass. Set to the value of PSTATE.SSBS on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.SSBS on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: PAN
fields.description: Privileged Access Never. Set to the value of PSTATE.PAN on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.PAN on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: SS
fields.description: Software Step. Set to the value of PSTATE.SS on taking an exception to EL1, and conditionally  copied to PSTATE.SS on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: IL
fields.description: Illegal Execution state. Set to the value of PSTATE.IL on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.IL on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: GE
fields.description: Greater than or Equal flags. Set to the value of PSTATE.GE on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.GE on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: IT[7:2]
fields.description: This field is bits[7:2] of IT[7:0]. See IT[1:0] for the field description.

fields.name: E
fields.description: Endianness. Set to the value of PSTATE.E on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.E on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: A
fields.description: SError exception mask. Set to the value of PSTATE.A on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.A on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: I
fields.description: IRQ interrupt mask. Set to the value of PSTATE.I on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.I on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: F
fields.description: FIQ interrupt mask. Set to the value of PSTATE.F on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.F on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: T
fields.description: T32 Instruction set state. Set to the value of PSTATE.T on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.T on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

fields.name: M[4]
fields.description: Execution state. Set to 0b1 , the value of PSTATE.nRW, on taking an exception to EL1 from AArch32 state, and copied to PSTATE.nRW on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

values.label: AArch32 execution state.

fields.name: M[3:0]
fields.description: AArch32 Mode. Set to the value of PSTATE.M[3:0] on taking an exception to EL1, and  copied to PSTATE.M[3:0] on executing an exception return operation in EL1.

values.label: User.
FIQ.
IRQ.
Supervisor.
Abort.
Undefined.
System.

fields.name: SS
fields.description: Software Step. Set to the value of PSTATE.SS on taking an exception to EL3, and conditionally  copied to PSTATE.SS on executing an exception return operation in EL3.

fields.name: IL
fields.description: Illegal Execution state. Set to the value of PSTATE.IL on taking an exception to EL3, and  copied to PSTATE.IL on executing an exception return operation in EL3.

fields.name: GE
fields.description: Greater than or Equal flags. Set to the value of PSTATE.GE on taking an exception to EL3, and  copied to PSTATE.GE on executing an exception return operation in EL3.

fields.name: IT[7:2]
fields.description: This field is bits[7:2] of IT[7:0]. See IT[1:0] for the field description.

fields.name: E
fields.description: Endianness. Set to the value of PSTATE.E on taking an exception to EL3, and  copied to PSTATE.E on executing an exception return operation in EL3.

fields.name: A
fields.description: SError exception mask. Set to the value of PSTATE.A on taking an exception to EL3, and  copied to PSTATE.A on executing an exception return operation in EL3.

fields.name: I
fields.description: IRQ interrupt mask. Set to the value of PSTATE.I on taking an exception to EL3, and  copied to PSTATE.I on executing an exception return operation in EL3.

fields.name: F
fields.description: FIQ interrupt mask. Set to the value of PSTATE.F on taking an exception to EL3, and  copied to PSTATE.F on executing an exception return operation in EL3.

fields.name: T
fields.description: T32 Instruction set state. Set to the value of PSTATE.T on taking an exception to EL3, and  copied to PSTATE.T on executing an exception return operation in EL3.

fields.name: M[4]
fields.description: Execution state. Set to 0b1 , the value of PSTATE.nRW, on taking an exception to EL3 from AArch32 state, and copied to PSTATE.nRW on executing an exception return operation in EL3.

values.label: AArch32 execution state.

fields.name: M[3:0]
fields.description: AArch32 Mode. Set to the value of PSTATE.M[3:0] on taking an exception to EL3, and  copied to PSTATE.M[3:0] on executing an exception return operation in EL3.

values.label: User.
FIQ.
IRQ.
Supervisor.

Abort.
Hyp.
Undefined.
System.

name: SPSR_fiq
description: Holds the saved process state when an exception is taken to FIQ mode.

name: SPSR_irq
description: Holds the saved process state when an exception is taken to IRQ mode.

name: SPSR_und
description: Holds the saved process state when an exception is taken to Undefined mode.

name: SP_EL0
description: Holds the stack pointer associated with EL0. At higher Exception levels, this is used as the current stack pointer when the value of SPSel .SP is 0.

name: SP_EL1
description: Holds the stack pointer associated with EL1. The value of SPSel .SP determines the current stack pointer.

name: SP_EL2
description: Holds the stack pointer associated with EL2. The value of SPSel .SP determines the current stack pointer.

name: SP_EL3
description: Holds the stack pointer associated with EL3. The value of SPSel .SP determines the current stack pointer.

name: SSBS
description: Allows access to the Speculative Store Bypass Safe bit.

fields.name: SSBS
fields.description: Speculative Store Bypass Safe. Prohibits speculative loads or stores which might practically allow a cache timing side channel. A speculative value in a register is used in a potentially speculatively exploitable manner if it is used to form an address, generate condition codes, or generate SVE predicate values to be used by other instructions in the speculative sequence or if the execution timing of any other instructions in the speculative sequence is a function of the data loaded under speculation.

values.label: Hardware is not permitted to use speculative register values in a potentially speculatively exploitable manner if the speculative read that loads the register is from earlier in the coherence order than the entry generated by the latest store to that location using the same virtual address as the load instruction.

When the value of PSTATE.SSBS is 1, hardware is permitted to use speculative register values in a potentially speculatively exploitable manner if the speculative read that loads the register is from earlier in the coherence order than the entry generated by the latest store to that location using the same virtual address as the load instruction.

name: SVCR
description: Allows access to the Streaming SVE mode in PSTATE.SM, and the SME storage enable in PSTATE.ZA.

fields.name: ZA
fields.description: Access to PSTATE.ZA, SME storage enable. When this storage is disabled, execution of an instruction which can access it is trapped. The exception is reported using an ESR_ELx .{EC, SMTC} value of { 0x1D , 0x3 }. The possible values of this bit are:

values.label:
SME ZA storage and, if implemented, ZT0 storage are
invalid and not accessible. This control causes execution at any Exception level of instructions that can access this storage to be trapped.

SME ZA storage and, if implemented, ZT0 storage are
valid and accessible. This control does not cause execution of any instructions to be trapped.

fields.name: SM
fields.description: Access to PSTATE.SM, Streaming SVE mode. When the PE is in Streaming SVE mode, the Streaming SVE vector length (SVL) applies to SVE instructions, and execution at any Exception level of an instruction which is illegal in that mode is trapped. The exception is reported using an ESR_ELx .{EC, SMTC} value of { 0x1D , 0x1 }. When the PE is not in Streaming SVE mode, the SVE vector length (VL) applies to SVE instructions, and execution at any Exception level of an instruction which is only legal in that mode is trapped. The exception is reported using an ESR_ELx .{EC, SMTC} value of { 0x1D , 0x2 }. The possible values of this bit are:

values.label: The PE is not in Streaming SVE mode.
The PE is in Streaming SVE mode.

name: SYS S1_<op1>_<Cn>_<Cm>_<op2>, SYSL S1_<op1>_<Cn>_<Cm>_<op2>, SYSP S1_<op1>_<Cn>_<Cm>_<op2>
description: This area of the System instruction encoding space is reserved for IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED System instructions.

name: TCO
description: When FEAT_MTE is implemented, this register allows tag checks to be disabled globally. When FEAT_MTE2 is not implemented, it is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED if accesses to this register access PSTATE.TCO or are RAZ/WI.

fields.name: TCO
fields.description: Allows memory tag checks to be globally disabled.

values.label: Loads and Stores are not affected by this control.
Loads and Stores are unchecked.

name: TCR2_EL1
description: The control register for stage 1 of the EL1&0 translation regime.

fields.name: FNGNA1
fields.description: Force non-global for unassured translations using TTBR1_EL1 .

values.label: This bit has no effect on the interpretation of the nG bit.

Translations using TTBR1_EL1 are treated as non-global regardless of the value of the nG bit if all of the following is true: The translation is for the EL1&0 translation regime. Stage 1 and stage 2 translation are enabled. Protection is enabled. The final stage 1 translation using the descriptor does not have the Assured Translation property.

fields.name: FNGNA0
fields.description: Force non-global for unassured translations using TTBR0_EL1 .

values.label: This bit has no effect on the interpretation of the nG bit.

Translations using TTBR0_EL1 are treated as non-global regardless of the value of the nG bit if all of the following is true: The translation is for the EL1&0 translation regime. Stage 1 and stage 2 translation are enabled. Protection is enabled. The final stage 1 translation using the descriptor does not have the Assured Translation property.
This bit has no effect on the interpretation of the nG bit.
Translations using TTBR1_EL1 are treated as non-global regardless of the value of the nG bit.
This bit has no effect on the interpretation of the nG bit.
Translations using TTBR0_EL1 are treated as non-global regardless of the value of the nG bit.
Use of two ASIDs is disabled.
Use of two ASIDs is enabled.

fields.name: DisCH1
fields.description: Disable the Contiguous bit for the Start Table for TTBR1_EL1 .

values.label: The Contiguous bit of Block or Page descriptors of the Start Table for TTBR1_EL1 is not affected by this field.
The Contiguous bit of Block or Page descriptors of the Start Table for TTBR1_EL1 is treated as 0.

fields.name: DisCH0
fields.description: Disable the Contiguous bit for the Start Table for TTBR0_EL1 .

values.label: The Contiguous bit of Block or Page descriptors of the Start Table for TTBR0_EL1 is not affected by this field.
The Contiguous bit of Block or Page descriptors of the Start Table for TTBR0_EL1 is treated as 0.

fields.name: HAFT
fields.description: Hardware managed Access Flag for Table descriptors. Enables the Hardware managed Access Flag for Table descriptors.

values.label: Hardware managed Access Flag for Table descriptors is disabled.
Hardware managed Access Flag for Table descriptors is enabled.

fields.name: PTTWI
fields.description: Permit Translation table walk Incoherence. Permits RCWS instructions to generate writes that have the Reduced Coherence property.

values.label: Write accesses generated by RCWS at EL1&0 do not have the Reduced Coherence property.
Write accesses generated by RCWS at EL1&0 have the Reduced Coherence property if HCRX_EL2 .PTTWI is 1.
Translation system follows VMSAv8-64 translation process.
Translation system follows VMSAv9-128 translation process.
Attribute Indexing Extension Disabled.
Attribute Indexing Extension Enabled.

fields.name: POE
fields.description: Enables Permission Overlays for privileged accesses from EL1&0 translation regime.

values.label: Permission overlay disabled for EL1 access in stage 1 of EL1&0 translation regime.
Permission overlay enabled for EL1 access in stage 1 of EL1&0 translation regime.

fields.name: E0POE
fields.description: Enables Permission Overlays for unprivileged accesses from EL1&0 translation regime.

values.label: Permission overlay disabled for EL0 access in stage 1 of EL1&0 translation regime.
Permission overlay enabled for EL0 access in stage 1 of EL1&0 translation regime.

fields.name: PIE
fields.description: Enables usage of Indirect Permission Scheme.

values.label: Direct permission model.
Indirect permission model.

fields.name: PnCH
fields.description: Protected attribute enable. Enables use of bit[52] of the stage 1 translation table entries as the Protected bit, for translations using TTBRn_EL1.

values.label: For translations using TTBRn_EL1, bit[52] of each stage 1 translation table entry is not the Protected bit.
For translations using TTBRn_EL1, bit[52] of each stage 1 translation table entry is the Protected bit.

name: TCR2_EL2
description: The control register for stage 1 of the EL2&0 translation regime.

fields.name: AMEC0
fields.description: This field controls the enabling of the Alternate MECID translations for the EL2 translation regime. TCR2_EL2.AMEC0 is provided to enable the safe update of MECID_A0_EL2 , by disabling access and speculation to AMEC==1 Block or Page descriptors during the update.

values.label: Use of a Block or Page descriptor containing AMEC == 1 generates a Translation fault.
Accesses translated by a Block or Page descriptor containing AMEC == 1 are associated with the MECID configured in MECID_A0_EL2 .

fields.name: HAFT
fields.description: Hardware managed Access Flag for Table descriptors. Enables the Hardware managed Access Flag for Table descriptors.

values.label: Hardware managed Access Flag for Table descriptors is disabled.
Hardware managed Access Flag for Table descriptors is enabled.

fields.name: PTTWI
fields.description: Permit Translation table walk Incoherence. Permits RCWS instructions to generate writes that have the Reduced Coherence property.

values.label: Write accesses generated by RCWS at EL2 or EL2&0 do not have the Reduced Coherence property.
Write accesses generated by RCWS at EL2 or EL2&0 have the Reduced Coherence property.
Attribute Indexing Extension Disabled.
Attribute Indexing Extension Enabled.

values.label: Permission overlay disabled for EL2 access in stage 1 of EL2 translation regime.
Permission overlay enabled for EL2 access in stage 1 of EL2 translation regime.

fields.name: PIE
fields.description: Enables usage of Indirect Permission Scheme.

values.label: Direct permission model.
Indirect permission model.

fields.name: PnCH
fields.description: Protected attribute enable. Enables use of bit[52] of stage 1 translation table entries as the Protected bit, for translations using TTBR0_EL2 when HCR_EL2.E2H is 0.

values.label: For translations using TTBRn_EL2, bit[52] of each stage 1 translation table entry is not the Protected bit.
For translations using TTBR0_EL2, bit[52] of each stage 1 translation table entry is the Protected bit.
