name: TPIDR2_EL0
long_name: EL0 Read/Write Software Thread ID Register 2
description: Provides a location where SME-aware software executing at EL0 can store thread identifying information, for context management purposes. The PE makes no use of this register.

fields.name: ThreadID
fields.description: Thread identifying information stored by software running at this Exception level.

name: TPIDRRO_EL0
long_name: EL0 Read-Only Software Thread ID Register
description: Provides a location where software executing at EL1 or higher can store thread identifying information that is visible to software executing at EL0, for OS management purposes. The PE makes no use of this register.

fields.name: ThreadID
fields.description: Thread ID. Thread identifying information stored by software running at this Exception level.

name: TPIDR_EL0
long_name: EL0 Read/Write Software Thread ID Register
description: Provides a location where software executing at EL0 can store thread identifying information, for OS management purposes. The PE makes no use of this register.

fields.name: ThreadID
fields.description: Thread ID. Thread identifying information stored by software running at this Exception level.

name: TPIDR_EL1
description: Provides a location where software executing at EL1 can store thread identifying information, for OS management purposes. The PE makes no use of this register.

fields.name: ThreadID
fields.description: Thread ID. Thread identifying information stored by software running at this Exception level.

name: TPIDR_EL2
description: Provides a location where software executing at EL2 can store thread identifying information, for OS management purposes. The PE makes no use of this register.

fields.name: ThreadID
fields.description: Thread ID. Thread identifying information stored by software running at this Exception level.

name: TPIDR_EL3
description: Provides a location where software executing at EL3 can store thread identifying information, for OS management purposes. The PE makes no use of this register.

fields.name: ThreadID
fields.description: Thread ID. Thread identifying information stored by software running at this Exception level.

name: TRBBASER_EL1
description: Defines the base address for the trace buffer.

fields.name: BASE
fields.description: Trace Buffer Base pointer address. (TRBBASER_EL1.BASE << 12) is the address of the first byte in the trace buffer. Bits [11:0] of the Base pointer address are always zero. If the smallest implemented translation granule is not 4KB, then TRBBASER_EL1[N-1:12] are RES0 , where N is the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED value Log 2 (smallest implemented translation granule).

name: TRBIDR_EL1
description: Describes constraints on using the Trace Buffer Unit to software, including whether the Trace Buffer Unit can be programmed at the current Exception level.

fields.name: MaxBuffSize
fields.description: Maximum supported trace buffer size. Reserved for software use.

fields.name: MPAM2
fields.description: FEAT_MPAMv2 extensions. Indicates Memory Partitioning and Monitoring (MPAM) support using FEAT_MPAMv2 in the Trace Buffer Unit when using External mode.

values.label: Trace Buffer External Mode is not implemented or the version of MPAM supported by this field is not implemented by the PE.
FEAT_MPAMv2 is implemented by the Trace Buffer Unit, using default PARTID and PMG values in External mode.
Trace Buffer MPAM extensions implemented, using FEAT_MPAMv2 .

fields.name: MPAM
fields.description: FEAT_MPAMv0p1 or FEAT_MPAMv1p0 extensions. Indicates Memory Partitioning and Monitoring (MPAM) support using FEAT_MPAMv0p1 or FEAT_MPAMv1p0 in the Trace Buffer Unit when using External mode.

values.label: Trace Buffer External Mode is not implemented or the version of MPAM supported by this field is not implemented by the PE.
FEAT_MPAMv0p1 or FEAT_MPAMv1p0 is implemented by the Trace Buffer Unit, using default PARTID and PMG values in External mode.
Trace Buffer MPAM extensions implemented, using FEAT_MPAMv0p1 or FEAT_MPAMv1p0 .

fields.name: EA
fields.description: External Abort handling. Describes how the PE manages External aborts on writes made by the Trace Buffer Unit to the trace buffer.

values.label: Not described.
The PE ignores External aborts on writes made by the Trace Buffer Unit.
An External abort on a write made by the Trace Buffer Unit generates an asynchronous SError exception at the PE.

fields.name: AddrMode
fields.description: Address Modes. Describes the addressing modes available for the trace buffer.

values.label: Virtual and physical address modes are supported.
Only virtual address mode is supported.
Reserved for software use under virtualization, to show that only physical address mode is supported.

fields.name: F
fields.description: Flag updates. Describes how address translations performed by the Trace Buffer Unit manage the Access flag and dirty state.

values.label: Hardware management of the Access flag and dirty state for accesses made by the Trace Buffer Unit is always disabled for all translation stages.

Hardware management of the Access flag and dirty state for accesses made by the Trace Buffer Unit is controlled in the same way as explicit memory accesses in the trace buffer owning translation regime.

fields.name: P
fields.description: Programming not allowed. When read at EL3, this field reads as zero. Otherwise, indicates that the trace buffer owning Exception level is a higher Exception level or the trace buffer owning Security state is not the current Security state.

values.label: Programming is allowed.
Programming not allowed.

fields.name: Align
fields.description: Defines the minimum alignment constraint for writes to TRBPTR_EL1 and TRBTRG_EL1 .

values.label: Byte.
Halfword.
Word.
Doubleword.
16 bytes.
32 bytes.
64 bytes.
128 bytes.
256 bytes.
512 bytes.
1KB.
2KB.

name: TRBLIMITR_EL1
description: Defines the top address for the trace buffer, and controls the trace buffer modes and enable.

fields.name: LIMIT
fields.description: Trace Buffer Limit pointer address. (TRBLIMITR_EL1.LIMIT << 12) is the address of the last byte in the trace buffer plus one. Bits [11:0] of the Limit pointer address are always zero. If the smallest implemented translation granule is not 4KB, then TRBLIMITR_EL1[N-1:12] are RES0 , where N is the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED value Log 2 (smallest implemented translation granule).

fields.name: XE
fields.description: Trace Buffer Unit External mode enable. Used for save/restore of TRBLIMITR_EL1 .XE.

values.label: Trace Buffer Unit is not enabled by this control.
If SelfHostedTraceEnabled() is FALSE, the Trace Buffer Unit is enabled.
The trace buffer pointers are virtual addresses.

The trace buffer pointers are: Physical address in the owning security state if the owning translation regime has no stage 2 translation. Intermediate physical addresses in the owning security state if the owning translation regime has stage 2 translations.
Stop on trigger. Flush then stop collection and raise maintenance interrupt on Trigger Event.
IRQ on trigger. Continue collection and raise maintenance interrupt on Trigger Event.
Ignore trigger. Continue collection and do not raise maintenance interrupt on Trigger Event.
Fill mode. Stop collection and raise maintenance interrupt on current write pointer wrap.
Wrap mode. Continue collection and raise maintenance interrupt on current write pointer wrap.
Circular Buffer mode. Continue collection and do not raise maintenance interrupt on current write pointer wrap.

fields.name: E
fields.description: Trace Buffer Unit enable. Controls whether the Trace Buffer Unit is enabled when SelfHostedTraceEnabled() == TRUE.

values.label: Trace Buffer Unit is not enabled by this control.
If SelfHostedTraceEnabled() is TRUE, the Trace Buffer Unit is enabled.

name: TRBMAR_EL1
description: Controls Trace Buffer Unit accesses to memory. If the trace buffer pointers specify virtual addresses, the address properties are defined by the translation tables and this register is ignored.

fields.name: PAS
fields.description: Physical address specifier. Defines the PAS attribute for memory addressed by the buffer in External mode.

values.label: Secure.
Non-secure.
Root.
Realm.

fields.name: SH
fields.description: Trace buffer shareability domain. Defines the shareability domain for Normal memory used by the trace buffer.

values.label: Non-shareable.
Outer Shareable.
Inner Shareable.

fields.name: Attr
fields.description:
Trace buffer memory type and attributes. Defines the memory type and, for Normal memory, the cacheability attributes, for memory addressed by the trace buffer. The encoding of this field is the same as that of a MAIR_ELx .Attr<n> field, as follows: Attr Meaning 0b0000dd00 Device memory.
See encoding of 'dd' for the type of Device memory. 0b0000dd01 If FEAT_XS is implemented:
Device memory with the XS attribute set to 0.
See encoding of 'dd' for the type of Device memory.
Otherwise, UNPREDICTABLE . 0b0000dd1x UNPREDICTABLE . 0booooiiii where oooo != 0000
and iiii != 0000 Normal memory. See encoding of 'oooo' and 'iiii' for the
type of Normal memory. 0b01000000 If FEAT_XS is implemented:
Normal Inner Non-cacheable, Outer Non-cacheable memory
with the XS attribute set to 0.
Otherwise, UNPREDICTABLE . 0b10100000 If FEAT_XS is implemented:
Normal Inner Write-through Cacheable, Outer Write-through Cacheable,
Read-Allocate, No-Write Allocate, Non-transient memory
with the XS attribute set to 0.
Otherwise, UNPREDICTABLE . 0b11110000 If FEAT_MTE2 is implemented:
Tagged Normal Inner Write-Back, Outer Write-Back,
Read-Allocate, Write-Allocate Non-transient memory.
Otherwise, UNPREDICTABLE . 0bxxxx0000 where xxxx != 0000
and xxxx != 0100
and xxxx != 1010
and xxxx != 1111 UNPREDICTABLE . dd is encoded as follows: 'dd' Meaning 0b00 Device-nGnRnE memory. 0b01 Device-nGnRE memory. 0b10 Device-nGRE memory. 0b11 Device-GRE memory. oooo is encoded as follows: 'oooo' Meaning 0b0000 See encoding of Attr. 0b00RW where RW != 00 Normal memory, Outer Write-Through Transient. 0b0100 Normal memory, Outer Non-cacheable. 0b01RW where RW != 00 Normal memory, Outer Write-Back Transient. 0b10RW Normal memory, Outer Write-Through Non-transient. 0b11RW Normal memory, Outer Write-Back Non-transient. R encodes the Outer Read-Allocate policy and W encodes the Outer Write-Allocate policy. iiii is encoded as follows: 'iiii' Meaning 0b0000 See encoding of Attr. 0b00RW where RW != 00 Normal memory, Inner Write-Through Transient. 0b0100 Normal memory, Inner Non-cacheable. 0b01RW where RW != 00 Normal memory, Inner Write-Back Transient. 0b10RW Normal memory, Inner Write-Through Non-transient. 0b11RW Normal memory, Inner Write-Back Non-transient. R encodes the Inner Read-Allocate policy and W encodes the Inner Write-Allocate policy. In oooo and iiii , R and W are encoded as follows: 'R' or 'W' Meaning 0b0 No Allocate. 0b1 Allocate. When FEAT_XS is implemented, stage 1 Inner Write-Back Cacheable, Outer Write-Back Cacheable memory types have the XS attribute set to 0.

name: TRBMPAM_EL1
description: Defines the PARTID, PMG, and MPAM_SP values used by the trace buffer unit in external mode.

fields.name: PMG
fields.description: Performance Monitoring Group. Selects the PMG.

fields.name: EN
fields.description: Enable. Enables use of non-default MPAM values.

values.label: Use default MPAM values.
Use TRBMPAM_EL1.{PARTID, PMG}.

name: TRBPTR_EL1
description: Defines the current write pointer for the trace buffer.

fields.name: PTR
fields.description: Trace Buffer current write pointer address. Defines the virtual address of the next entry to be written to the trace buffer. If TRBIDR_EL1 .Align is not zero, then it is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED whether bits [M-1:0] are RES0 or read/write, where M is an integer between 1 and TRBIDR_EL1 .Align inclusive. The architecture places restrictions on the values that software can write to the pointer. For more information, see 'Restrictions on Programming the Trace Buffer Unit' .

fields.name: EC
fields.description: Event class. Top-level description of the cause of the trace buffer management event.

values.label: Other trace buffer management event. All trace buffer management events other than those described by the other defined Event class codes.

Granule Protection Check fault on write to trace buffer, other than Granule Protection Fault (GPF). That is, any of the following: Granule Protection Table (GPT) address size fault. GPT walk fault. External abort on GPT fetch. A GPF on translation table walk or update is reported as either a Stage 1 or Stage 2 Data Abort, as appropriate. Other GPFs are reported as a Stage 1 Data Abort.
Trace buffer management event for an IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED reason.
Stage 1 Data Abort on write to trace buffer.
Stage 2 Data Abort on write to trace buffer.

name: TRBTRG_EL1
description: Specifies the number of bytes of trace to capture following a Detected Trigger before a Trigger Event.

fields.name: TRG
fields.description: Trigger count. Specifies the number of bytes of trace to capture following a Detected Trigger before a Trigger Event. TRBTRG_EL1 decrements by 1 for every byte of trace written to the trace buffer when all of the following are true: TRBTRG_EL1 is nonzero. TRBSR_EL1 .TRG is 1. The architecture places restrictions on the values that software can write to the counter.

name: TRCACATR<n>
long_name: Trace Address Comparator Access Type Register <n>
description: Defines the type of access for the corresponding TRCACVR<n> Register. This register configures the context type, Exception levels, alignment, masking that is applied by the Address Comparator, and how the Address Comparator behaves when it is one half of an Address Range Comparator.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_RL_EL2
fields.description: Realm EL2 address comparison control. Controls whether a comparison can occur at EL2 in Realm state.

values.label: When TRCACATR<n> .EXLEVEL_NS_EL2 is 0 the Address Comparator performs comparisons in Realm EL2. When TRCACATR<n> .EXLEVEL_NS_EL2 is 1 the Address Comparator does not perform comparisons in Realm EL2.
When TRCACATR<n> .EXLEVEL_NS_EL2 is 0 the Address Comparator does not perform comparisons in Realm EL2. When TRCACATR<n> .EXLEVEL_NS_EL2 is 1 the Address Comparator performs comparisons in Realm EL2.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_RL_EL1
fields.description: Realm EL1 address comparison control. Controls whether a comparison can occur at EL1 in Realm state.

values.label: When TRCACATR<n> .EXLEVEL_NS_EL1 is 0 the Address Comparator performs comparisons in Realm EL1. When TRCACATR<n> .EXLEVEL_NS_EL1 is 1 the Address Comparator does not perform comparisons in Realm EL1.
When TRCACATR<n> .EXLEVEL_NS_EL1 is 0 the Address Comparator does not perform comparisons in Realm EL1. When TRCACATR<n> .EXLEVEL_NS_EL1 is 1 the Address Comparator performs comparisons in Realm EL1.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_RL_EL0
fields.description: Realm EL0 address comparison control. Controls whether a comparison can occur at EL0 in Realm state.

values.label: When TRCACATR<n> .EXLEVEL_NS_EL0 is 0 the Address Comparator performs comparisons in Realm EL0. When TRCACATR<n> .EXLEVEL_NS_EL0 is 1 the Address Comparator does not perform comparisons in Realm EL0.
When TRCACATR<n> .EXLEVEL_NS_EL0 is 0 the Address Comparator does not perform comparisons in Realm EL0. When TRCACATR<n> .EXLEVEL_NS_EL0 is 1 the Address Comparator performs comparisons in Realm EL0.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_NS_EL2
fields.description: Non-secure EL2 address comparison control. Controls whether a comparison can occur at EL2 in Non-secure state.

values.label: The Address Comparator performs comparisons in Non-secure EL2.
The Address Comparator does not perform comparisons in Non-secure EL2.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_NS_EL1
fields.description: Non-secure EL1 address comparison control. Controls whether a comparison can occur at EL1 in Non-secure state.

values.label: The Address Comparator performs comparisons in Non-secure EL1.
The Address Comparator does not perform comparisons in Non-secure EL1.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_NS_EL0
fields.description: Non-secure EL0 address comparison control. Controls whether a comparison can occur at EL0 in Non-secure state.

values.label: The Address Comparator performs comparisons in Non-secure EL0.
The Address Comparator does not perform comparisons in Non-secure EL0.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_S_EL3
fields.description: EL3 address comparison control. Controls whether a comparison can occur at EL3.

values.label: The Address Comparator performs comparisons at EL3.
The Address Comparator does not perform comparisons at EL3.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_S_EL2
fields.description: Secure EL2 address comparison control. Controls whether a comparison can occur at EL2 in Secure state.

values.label: The Address Comparator performs comparisons in Secure EL2.
The Address Comparator does not perform comparisons in Secure EL2.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_S_EL1
fields.description: Secure EL1 address comparison control. Controls whether a comparison can occur at EL1 in Secure state.

values.label: The Address Comparator performs comparisons in Secure EL1.
The Address Comparator does not perform comparisons in Secure EL1.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_S_EL0
fields.description: Secure EL0 address comparison control. Controls whether a comparison can occur at EL0 in Secure state.

values.label: The Address Comparator performs comparisons in Secure EL0.
The Address Comparator does not perform comparisons in Secure EL0.

fields.name: CONTEXT
fields.description: Selects a Context Identifier Comparator or Virtual Context Identifier Comparator:

values.label: Comparator 0.
Comparator 1.
Comparator 2.
Comparator 3.
Comparator 4.
Comparator 5.
Comparator 6.
Comparator 7.

fields.name: CONTEXTTYPE
fields.description: Controls whether the Address Comparator is dependent on a Context Identifier Comparator, a Virtual Context Identifier Comparator, or both comparisons.

values.label: The Address Comparator is not dependent on the Context Identifier Comparators or Virtual Context Identifier Comparators.

The Address Comparator is dependent on the Context Identifier Comparator that TRCACATR<n> .CONTEXT specifies. The Address Comparator signals a match only if both the Context Identifier Comparator and the address comparison match.

The Address Comparator is dependent on the Virtual Context Identifier Comparator that TRCACATR<n> .CONTEXT specifies. The Address Comparator signals a match only if both the Virtual Context Identifier Comparator and the address comparison match.

The Address Comparator is dependent on the Context Identifier Comparator and Virtual Context Identifier Comparator that TRCACATR<n> .CONTEXT specifies. The Address Comparator signals a match only if the Context Identifier Comparator, the Virtual Context Identifier Comparator, and address comparison all match.

name: TRCACVR<n>
description: Contains the address value.

fields.name: ADDRESS
fields.description: Address Value. The Address Comparators can support implementations that use multiple address widths. When the trace unit compares the ADDRESS field with an address that has a width less than this field, then the address must be zero-extended to the ADDRESS field width. The trace unit then compares all implemented bits. For example, in a system that supports both 32-bit and 64-bit addresses, when the PE is in AArch32 state the comparator must zero-extend the 32-bit address and compare against the full 64 bits that are stored in TRCACVR<n>.ADDRESS. This requires that the trace analyzer always programs all implemented bits of TRCACVR<n>.ADDRESS. The result of writing a value other than all zeros or all ones to ADDRESS at bits[63:P] is an UNKNOWN value, where P is defined as: 56, when FEAT_LVA3 is implemented. 52, when FEAT_LVA is implemented. 48, otherwise. The result of writing a value of all zeros or all ones to ADDRESS at bits[63:P] is the written value, and a read of the register returns the written value.

name: TRCAUTHSTATUS
description: Provides information about the state of the IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED authentication interface for debug. For additional information, see the CoreSight Architecture Specification.

fields.name: RTNID
fields.description: Root non-invasive debug. This field has the same value as DBGAUTHSTATUS_EL1 .RTNID.

values.label: Not implemented.

fields.name: RLNID
fields.description: Realm non-invasive debug. This field has the same value as DBGAUTHSTATUS_EL1 .RLNID.

values.label: Not implemented.

fields.name: HNID
fields.description: Hyp Non-invasive Debug. Indicates whether a separate enable control for EL2 non-invasive debug features is implemented and enabled.

values.label: Separate Hyp non-invasive debug enable not implemented, or EL2 non-invasive debug features not implemented.
Implemented and disabled.
Implemented and enabled.

fields.name: HID
fields.description: Hyp Invasive Debug. Indicates whether a separate enable control for EL2 invasive debug features is implemented and enabled.

values.label: Separate Hyp invasive debug enable not implemented, or EL2 invasive debug features not implemented.
Implemented and disabled.
Implemented and enabled.

fields.name: SNID
fields.description: Secure Non-invasive Debug. Indicates whether Secure non-invasive debug features are implemented and enabled.

values.label: Secure non-invasive debug features not implemented.
Implemented and disabled.
Implemented and enabled.

fields.name: SID
fields.description: Secure Invasive Debug. Indicates whether Secure invasive debug features are implemented and enabled.

values.label: Secure invasive debug features not implemented.
Implemented and disabled.
Implemented and enabled.

fields.name: NSNID
fields.description: Non-secure Non-invasive Debug. Indicates whether Non-secure non-invasive debug features are implemented and enabled.

values.label: Non-secure non-invasive debug features not implemented.
Implemented and disabled.
Implemented and enabled.

fields.name: NSID
fields.description: Non-secure Invasive Debug. Indicates whether Non-secure invasive debug features are implemented and enabled.

values.label: Non-secure invasive debug features not implemented.
Implemented and disabled.
Implemented and enabled.

name: TRCAUXCTLR
description: The function of this register is IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED .

name: TRCBBCTLR
description: Controls the regions in the memory map where branch broadcasting is active.

values.label: Exclude Mode. Branch broadcasting is not active for instructions in the address ranges defined by TRCBBCTLR.RANGE. If TRCBBCTLR.RANGE == 0x00 then branch broadcasting is active for all instructions.

Include Mode. Branch broadcasting is active for instructions in the address ranges defined by TRCBBCTLR.RANGE. If TRCBBCTLR.RANGE == 0x00 then the behavior of the trace unit is CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE . That is, the trace unit might or might not consider any instructions to be in a branch broadcasting region.

fields.name: RANGE[<m>]
fields.description: Selects whether Address Range Comparator <m> is used with branch broadcasting.

values.label: The address range that Address Range Comparator <m> defines, is not selected.
The address range that Address Range Comparator <m> defines, is selected.

name: TRCCCCTLR
description: Set the threshold value for cycle counting.

fields.name: THRESHOLD
fields.description: Sets the threshold value for instruction trace cycle counting. The minimum threshold value that can be programmed into THRESHOLD is given in TRCIDR3 .CCITMIN. If the THRESHOLD value is smaller than the value in TRCIDR3 .CCITMIN then the behavior is CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE . That is, cycle counts might or might not be included in the trace and the cycle count threshold is not known. Writing a value of zero when TRCCONFIGR .CCI enables instruction trace cycle counting results in CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE behavior. That is, cycle counts might or might not be included in the trace and the cycle count threshold is not known.

name: TRCCIDCCTLR0
long_name: Trace Context Identifier Comparator Control Register 0
description: Contains Context identifier mask values for the TRCCIDCVR<n> registers, for n = 0 to 3.

fields.name: COMP3[<m>]
fields.description: TRCCIDCVR3 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCCIDCVR3. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCCIDCVR3.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCCIDCVR3[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCCIDCVR3[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.

fields.name: COMP2[<m>]
fields.description: TRCCIDCVR2 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCCIDCVR2. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCCIDCVR2.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCCIDCVR2[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCCIDCVR2[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.

fields.name: COMP1[<m>]
fields.description: TRCCIDCVR1 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCCIDCVR1. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCCIDCVR1.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCCIDCVR1[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCCIDCVR1[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.

fields.name: COMP0[<m>]
fields.description: TRCCIDCVR0 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCCIDCVR0. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCCIDCVR0.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCCIDCVR0[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCCIDCVR0[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.

name: TRCCIDCCTLR1
long_name: Trace Context Identifier Comparator Control Register 1
description: Contains Context identifier mask values for the TRCCIDCVR<n> registers, for n = 4 to 7.

fields.name: COMP7[<m>]
fields.description: TRCCIDCVR7 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCCIDCVR7. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCCIDCVR7.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCCIDCVR7[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCCIDCVR7[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.

fields.name: COMP6[<m>]
fields.description: TRCCIDCVR6 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCCIDCVR6. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCCIDCVR6.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCCIDCVR6[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCCIDCVR6[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.

fields.name: COMP5[<m>]
fields.description: TRCCIDCVR5 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCCIDCVR5. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCCIDCVR5.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCCIDCVR5[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCCIDCVR5[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.

fields.name: COMP4[<m>]
fields.description: TRCCIDCVR4 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCCIDCVR4. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCCIDCVR4.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCCIDCVR4[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCCIDCVR4[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Context identifier comparison.

name: TRCCIDCVR<n>
long_name: Trace Context Identifier Comparator Value Registers <n>
description: Contains a Context identifier value.

fields.name: VALUE
fields.description: Context identifier value. The width of this field is indicated by TRCIDR2 .CIDSIZE. Unimplemented bits are RES0 . After a PE Reset, the trace unit assumes that the Context identifier is zero until the PE updates the Context identifier.

name: TRCCLAIMCLR
description: In conjunction with TRCCLAIMSET , provides Claim Tag bits that can be separately set and cleared to indicate whether functionality is in use by a debug agent. For additional information, see the CoreSight Architecture Specification.

fields.name: CLR[<m>]
fields.description: Claim Tag Clear. Indicates the current status of Claim Tag bit <m>, and is used to clear Claim Tag bit <m> to 0.

values.label: On a read: Claim Tag bit <m> is not set. On a write: Ignored.
On a read: Claim Tag bit <m> is set. On a write: Clear Claim tag bit <m> to 0.

name: TRCCLAIMSET
description: In conjunction with TRCCLAIMCLR , provides Claim Tag bits that can be separately set and cleared to indicate whether functionality is in use by a debug agent. For additional information, see the CoreSight Architecture Specification.

fields.name: SET[<m>]
fields.description: Claim Tag Set. Indicates whether Claim Tag bit <m> is implemented, and is used to set Claim Tag bit <m> to 1.

values.label: On a read: Claim Tag bit <m> is not implemented. On a write: Ignored.
On a read: Claim Tag bit <m> is implemented. On a write: Set Claim Tag bit <m> to 1.

name: TRCCNTCTLR<n>
description: Controls the operation of Counter <n>.

fields.name: CNTCHAIN
fields.description: For TRCCNTCTLR3 and TRCCNTCTLR1, this field controls whether the Counter decrements when a reload event occurs for Counter <n-1>.

values.label: The Counter does not decrement when a reload event for Counter <n-1> occurs.
Counter <n> decrements when a reload event for Counter <n-1> occurs. This concatenates Counter <n> and Counter <n-1>, to provide a larger count value.

fields.name: RLDSELF
fields.description: Controls whether a reload event occurs for the Counter, when the Counter reaches zero.

values.label: Normal mode. The Counter is in Normal mode.
Self-reload mode. The Counter is in Self-reload mode.

fields.name: RLDEVENT_TYPE
fields.description: Selects an event, that when it occurs causes a reload event for Counter <n> Chooses the type of Resource Selector.

values.label: A single Resource Selector. TRCCNTCTLR<n>.RLDEVENT.SEL[4:0] selects the single Resource Selector, from 0-31, used to activate the resource event.

A Boolean-combined pair of Resource Selectors. TRCCNTCTLR<n>.RLDEVENT.SEL[3:0] selects the Resource Selector pair, from 0-15, that has a Boolean function that is applied to it whose output is used to activate the resource event. TRCCNTCTLR<n>.RLDEVENT.SEL[4] is RES0 .

fields.name: RLDEVENT_SEL
fields.description: Selects an event, that when it occurs causes a reload event for Counter <n> Defines the selected Resource Selector or pair of Resource Selectors. TRCCNTCTLR<n>.RLDEVENT.TYPE controls whether TRCCNTCTLR<n>.RLDEVENT.SEL is the index of a single Resource Selector, or the index of a pair of Resource Selectors.

fields.name: CNTEVENT_TYPE
fields.description: Selects an event, that when it occurs causes Counter <n> to decrement. Chooses the type of Resource Selector.

values.label: A single Resource Selector. TRCCNTCTLR<n>.CNTEVENT.SEL[4:0] selects the single Resource Selector, from 0-31, used to activate the resource event.

A Boolean-combined pair of Resource Selectors. TRCCNTCTLR<n>.CNTEVENT.SEL[3:0] selects the Resource Selector pair, from 0-15, that has a Boolean function that is applied to it whose output is used to activate the resource event. TRCCNTCTLR<n>.CNTEVENT.SEL[4] is RES0 .

fields.name: CNTEVENT_SEL
fields.description: Selects an event, that when it occurs causes Counter <n> to decrement. Defines the selected Resource Selector or pair of Resource Selectors. values.label: Trace output to any IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED trace output interface is disabled.

fields.name: ATB
fields.description: AMBA Trace Bus (ATB) trigger enable. If a CoreSight ATB interface is implemented then when ETEEvent 0 occurs the trace unit sets: ATID == 0x7D . ATDATA to the value of TRCTRACEIDR . If the width of ATDATA is greater than the width of TRCTRACEIDR .TRACEID then the trace unit zeros the upper ATDATA bits. If ETEEvent 0 is programmed to occur based on program execution, such as an Address Comparator, the ATB trigger might not be inserted into the ATB stream at the same time as any trace generated by that program execution is output by the trace unit. Typically, the generated trace might be buffered in a trace unit which means that the ATB trigger would be output before the associated trace is output. If ETEEvent 0 is asserted multiple times in close succession, the trace unit is required to generate an ATB trigger for the first assertion, but might ignore one or more of the subsequent assertions. Arm recommends that the window in which ETEEvent 0 is ignored is limited only by the time taken to output an ATB trigger.

values.label: ATB trigger is disabled.
ATB trigger is enabled.
The trace unit does not generate an Event element <m>.
The trace unit generates an Event element <m> when ETEEvent <m> occurs.

name: TRCEXTINSELR<n>
description: Use this to set, or read, which External Inputs are resources to the trace unit. The name TRCEXTINSELR is an alias of TRCEXTINSELR0.

fields.name: evtCount
fields.description: PMU event to select. The event number as defined by the Arm ARM. Software must program this field with a PMU event that is supported by the PE being programmed. There are three ranges of PMU event numbers: PMU event numbers in the range 0x0000 to 0x003F are common architectural and microarchitectural events. PMU event numbers in the range 0x0040 to 0x00BF are Arm recommended common architectural and microarchitectural PMU events. PMU event numbers in the range 0x00C0 to 0x03FF are IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED PMU events. If evtCount is programmed to a PMU event that is reserved or not supported by the PE, the behavior depends on the PMU event type: For the range 0x0000 to 0x003F , then the PMU event is not active, and the value returned by a direct or external read of the evtCount field is the value written to the field. For IMPLEMENTATION DEFINED PMU events, it is UNPREDICTABLE what PMU event, if any, is counted, and the value returned by a direct or external read of the evtCount field is UNKNOWN .

fields.description: Selects whether the Address Range Comparators selected by TRCQCTLR.RANGE indicate address ranges where the trace unit is permitted to generate Q elements or address ranges where the trace unit is not permitted to generate Q elements:

Include Mode. The Address Range Comparators selected by TRCQCTLR.RANGE indicate address ranges where the trace unit can generate Q elements. If all the implemented bits in RANGE are set to 0 then Q elements are disabled.

fields.name: RANGE[<m>]
fields.description: Specifies whether Address Range Comparator <m> controls Q elements.

values.label: The address range that Address Range Comparator <m> defines is not selected.
The address range that Address Range Comparator <m> defines is selected.

name: TRCRSCTLR<n>
description: Controls the selection of the resources in the trace unit.

fields.name: PAIRINV
fields.description: Controls whether the combined result from a resource selector pair is inverted.

values.label: Do not invert the combined output of the 2 resource selectors.
Invert the combined output of the 2 resource selectors.

fields.name: INV
fields.description: Controls whether the resource, that TRCRSCTLR<n>.GROUP and TRCRSCTLR<n>.SELECT selects, is inverted.

values.label: Do not invert the output of this selector.
Invert the output of this selector.
External Input Selectors.
PE Comparator Inputs.
Counters and Sequencer.

Single Address Comparators.
Address Range Comparators.
Context Identifier Comparators.

fields.name: SELECT
fields.description: Resource Specific Controls. Contains the controls specific to the resource group selected by GROUP, described in the following sections.

name: TRCRSR
description: Use this to set, or read, the status of the resources.

values.label: Tracing is not active.
Tracing is active.
An ETEEvent <m> has not occurred.
An ETEEvent <m> has occurred while the resources were in the Paused state.

fields.name: EXTIN[<m>]
fields.description: The sticky status of the External Input Selectors.

values.label: An event selected by External Input Selector <m> has not occurred.
At least one event selected by External Input Selector <m> has occurred while the resources were in the Paused state.

name: TRCSEQEVR<n>
long_name: Trace Sequencer State Transition Control Register <n>
description: Moves the Sequencer state: Backwards, from state n+1 to state n when a programmed resource event occurs. Forwards, from state n to state n+1 when a programmed resource event occurs.

fields.name: B_TYPE
fields.description: Backward field. Selects an event that causes the Sequencer to move from state n+1 to state n. For example,  if TRCSEQEVR2.B.SEL == 0x14 , then when event 0x14 occurs, the Sequencer moves from state 3 to state 2. Chooses the type of Resource Selector.

values.label: A single Resource Selector. TRCSEQEVR<n>.B.SEL[4:0] selects the single Resource Selector, from 0-31, used to activate the resource event.

A Boolean-combined pair of Resource Selectors. TRCSEQEVR<n>.B.SEL[3:0] selects the Resource Selector pair, from 0-15, that has a Boolean function that is applied to it whose output is used to activate the resource event. TRCSEQEVR<n>.B.SEL[4] is RES0 .

fields.name: B_SEL
fields.description: Backward field. Selects an event that causes the Sequencer to move from state n+1 to state n. For example,  if TRCSEQEVR2.B.SEL == 0x14 , then when event 0x14 occurs, the Sequencer moves from state 3 to state 2. Defines the selected Resource Selector or pair of Resource Selectors. TRCSEQEVR<n>.B.TYPE controls whether TRCSEQEVR<n>.B.SEL is the index of a single Resource Selector, or the index of a pair of Resource Selectors.

fields.name: F_TYPE
fields.description: Forward field. Selects an event that causes the Sequencer to move from state n to state n+1. For example,  if TRCSEQEVR1.F.SEL == 0x12 , then when event 0x12 occurs, the Sequencer moves from state 1 to state 2. Chooses the type of Resource Selector.

values.label: A single Resource Selector. TRCSEQEVR<n>.F.SEL[4:0] selects the single Resource Selector, from 0-31, used to activate the resource event.

A Boolean-combined pair of Resource Selectors. TRCSEQEVR<n>.F.SEL[3:0] selects the Resource Selector pair, from 0-15, that has a Boolean function that is applied to it whose output is used to activate the resource event. TRCSEQEVR<n>.F.SEL[4] is RES0 .

fields.name: F_SEL
fields.description: Forward field. Selects an event that causes the Sequencer to move from state n to state n+1. For example,  if TRCSEQEVR1.F.SEL == 0x12 , then when event 0x12 occurs, the Sequencer moves from state 1 to state 2. Defines the selected Resource Selector or pair of Resource Selectors. TRCSEQEVR<n>.F.TYPE controls whether TRCSEQEVR<n>.F.SEL is the index of a single Resource Selector, or the index of a pair of Resource Selectors.

name: TRCSEQRSTEVR
description: Moves the Sequencer to state 0 when a programmed resource event occurs.

fields.name: RST_TYPE
fields.description: Reset field. Selects an event that causes the Sequencer to move to state 0. Chooses the type of Resource Selector.

values.label: A single Resource Selector. TRCSEQRSTEVR.RST.SEL[4:0] selects the single Resource Selector, from 0-31, used to activate the resource event.

A Boolean-combined pair of Resource Selectors. TRCSEQRSTEVR.RST.SEL[3:0] selects the Resource Selector pair, from 0-15, that has a Boolean function that is applied to it whose output is used to activate the resource event. TRCSEQRSTEVR.RST.SEL[4] is RES0 .

fields.name: RST_SEL
fields.description: Reset field. Selects an event that causes the Sequencer to move to state 0. Defines the selected Resource Selector or pair of Resource Selectors. TRCSEQRSTEVR.RST.TYPE controls whether TRCSEQRSTEVR.RST.SEL is the index of a single Resource Selector, or the index of a pair of Resource Selectors.

name: TRCSEQSTR
description: Use this to set, or read, the Sequencer state.

fields.name: STATE
fields.description: Set or returns the state of the Sequencer.

values.label: State 0.
State 1.
State 2.
State 3.

name: TRCSSCCR<n>
description: Controls the corresponding Single-shot Comparator Control resource.

fields.name: RST
fields.description: Selects the Single-shot Comparator Control mode.

values.label: The Single-shot Comparator Control is in single-shot mode.
The Single-shot Comparator Control is in multi-shot mode.

fields.name: ARC[<m>]
fields.description: Selects one or more Address Range Comparators for Single-shot control.

values.label: The Address Range Comparator <m>, is not selected for Single-shot control.
The Address Range Comparator <m>, is selected for Single-shot control.

fields.name: SAC[<m>]
fields.description: Selects one or more Single Address Comparators for Single-shot control.

values.label: The Single Address Comparator <m>, is not selected for Single-shot control.
The Single Address Comparator <m>, is selected for Single-shot control.

name: TRCSSCSR<n>
long_name: Trace Single-shot Comparator Control Status Register <n>
description: Returns the status of the corresponding Single-shot Comparator Control.

fields.name: STATUS
fields.description: Single-shot Comparator Control status. Indicates if any of the comparators selected by this Single-shot Comparator control have matched. The selected comparators are defined by TRCSSCCR<n> .ARC, TRCSSCCR<n> .SAC, and TRCSSPCICR<n> .PC.

values.label: No match has occurred. When the first match occurs, this field takes a value of 1. It remains at 1 until explicitly modified by a write to this register.

One or more matches has occurred. If TRCSSCCR<n> .RST == 0 then: There is only one match and no more matches are possible. Software must reset this field to 0 to re-enable the Single-shot Comparator Control.

fields.name: PENDING
fields.description: Single-shot pending status. The Single-shot Comparator Control fired while the resources were in the Paused state.

values.label: No match has occurred.
One or more matches has occurred.

fields.name: PC
fields.description: PE Comparator Input support. Indicates if the Single-shot Comparator Control supports PE Comparator Inputs.

values.label: This Single-shot Comparator Control does not support PE Comparator Inputs. Selecting any PE Comparator Inputs using the associated TRCSSPCICR<n> results in CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE behavior of the Single-shot Comparator Control resource. The Single-shot Comparator Control might match unexpectedly or might not match.
This Single-shot Comparator Control supports PE Comparator Inputs.

fields.name: DV
fields.description: Data value comparator support. Data value comparisons are not implemented in ETE and are reserved for other trace architectures. Allocated in other trace architectures.

values.label: This Single-shot Comparator Control does not support data value comparisons.
This Single-shot Comparator Control supports data value comparisons.

fields.name: DA
fields.description: Data Address Comparator support. Data address comparisons are not implemented in ETE and are reserved for other trace architectures. Allocated in other trace architectures.

values.label: This Single-shot Comparator Control does not support data address comparisons.
This Single-shot Comparator Control supports data address comparisons.

fields.name: INST
fields.description: Instruction Address Comparator support. Indicates if the Single-shot Comparator Control supports instruction address comparisons.

values.label: This Single-shot Comparator Control does not support instruction address comparisons.
This Single-shot Comparator Control supports instruction address comparisons.

name: TRCSSPCICR<n>
long_name: Trace Single-shot Processing Element Comparator Input Control Register <n>
description: Returns the status of the corresponding Single-shot Comparator Control.

fields.name: PC[<m>]
fields.description: Selects one or more PE Comparator Inputs for Single-shot control.

values.label: The single PE Comparator Input <m>, is not selected as for Single-shot control.
The single PE Comparator Input <m>, is selected as for Single-shot control.

name: TRCSTALLCTLR
description: Enables trace unit functionality that prevents trace unit buffer overflows.

values.label: Trace unit buffer overflow prevention is disabled.
Trace unit buffer overflow prevention is enabled.

fields.name: ISTALL
fields.description: Instruction stall control. Controls if a trace unit can stall the PE when the trace buffer space is less than LEVEL.

values.label: The trace unit must not stall the PE.
The trace unit can stall the PE.

fields.name: LEVEL
fields.description: Threshold level field. The field can support 16 monotonic levels from 0b0000 to 0b1111 . The value 0b0000 defines the Minimal invasion level. This setting has a greater risk of a trace unit buffer overflow. The value 0b1111 defines the Maximum invasion level. This setting has a reduced risk of a trace unit buffer overflow.

name: TRCSTATR
description: Returns the trace unit status.

values.label: The programmers' model is not stable.
The programmers' model is stable.
The trace unit is not idle.
The trace unit is idle.

name: TRCSYNCPR
description: Controls how often trace protocol synchronization requests occur.

fields.name: PERIOD
fields.description: Defines the number of bytes of trace between each periodic trace protocol synchronization request.

values.label: Trace protocol synchronization is disabled.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 8 bytes of trace.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 9 bytes of trace.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 10 bytes of trace.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 11 bytes of trace.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 12 bytes of trace.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 13 bytes of trace.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 14 bytes of trace.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 15 bytes of trace.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 16 bytes of trace.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 17 bytes of trace.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 18 bytes of trace.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 19 bytes of trace.
Trace protocol synchronization request occurs after 2 20 bytes of trace.

name: TRCTRACEIDR
description: Sets the trace ID for instruction trace.

fields.name: TRACEID
fields.description: Trace ID field. Sets the trace ID value for instruction trace. The width of the field is indicated by the value of TRCIDR5 .TRACEIDSIZE. Unimplemented bits are RES0 .

name: TRCTSCTLR
description: Controls the insertion of global timestamps in the trace stream.

fields.name: EVENT_TYPE
fields.description: Chooses the type of Resource Selector.

values.label: A single Resource Selector. TRCTSCTLR.EVENT.SEL[4:0] selects the single Resource Selector, from 0-31, used to activate the resource event.

A Boolean-combined pair of Resource Selectors. TRCTSCTLR.EVENT.SEL[3:0] selects the Resource Selector pair, from 0-15, that has a Boolean function that is applied to it whose output is used to activate the resource event. TRCTSCTLR.EVENT.SEL[4] is RES0 .

fields.name: EVENT_SEL
fields.description: Defines the selected Resource Selector or pair of Resource Selectors. TRCTSCTLR.EVENT.TYPE controls whether TRCTSCTLR.EVENT.SEL is the index of a single Resource Selector, or the index of a pair of Resource Selectors.

name: TRCVICTLR
description: Controls instruction trace filtering.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_RL_EL2
fields.description: Filter instruction trace for EL2 in Realm state.

values.label: When TRCVICTLR.EXLEVEL_NS_EL2 is 0 the trace unit generates instruction trace for EL2 in Realm state. When TRCVICTLR.EXLEVEL_NS_EL2 is 1 the trace unit does not generate instruction trace for EL2 in Realm state.

When TRCVICTLR.EXLEVEL_NS_EL2 is 0 the trace unit does not generate instruction trace for EL2 in Realm state. When TRCVICTLR.EXLEVEL_NS_EL2 is 1 the trace unit generates instruction trace for EL2 in Realm state.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_RL_EL1
fields.description: Filter instruction trace for EL1 in Realm state.

values.label: When TRCVICTLR.EXLEVEL_NS_EL1 is 0 the trace unit generates instruction trace for EL1 in Realm state. When TRCVICTLR.EXLEVEL_NS_EL1 is 1 the trace unit does not generate instruction trace for EL1 in Realm state.

When TRCVICTLR.EXLEVEL_NS_EL1 is 0 the trace unit does not generate instruction trace for EL1 in Realm state. When TRCVICTLR.EXLEVEL_NS_EL1 is 1 the trace unit generates instruction trace for EL1 in Realm state.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_RL_EL0
fields.description: Filter instruction trace for EL0 in Realm state.

values.label: When TRCVICTLR.EXLEVEL_NS_EL0 is 0 the trace unit generates instruction trace for EL0 in Realm state. When TRCVICTLR.EXLEVEL_NS_EL0 is 1 the trace unit does not generate instruction trace for EL0 in Realm state.

When TRCVICTLR.EXLEVEL_NS_EL0 is 0 the trace unit does not generate instruction trace for EL0 in Realm state. When TRCVICTLR.EXLEVEL_NS_EL0 is 1 the trace unit generates instruction trace for EL0 in Realm state.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_NS_EL2
fields.description: Filter instruction trace for EL2 in Non-secure state.

values.label: The trace unit generates instruction trace for EL2 in Non-secure state.
The trace unit does not generate instruction trace for EL2 in Non-secure state.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_NS_EL1
fields.description: Filter instruction trace for EL1 in Non-secure state.

values.label: The trace unit generates instruction trace for EL1 in Non-secure state.
The trace unit does not generate instruction trace for EL1 in Non-secure state.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_NS_EL0
fields.description: Filter instruction trace for EL0 in Non-secure state.

values.label: The trace unit generates instruction trace for EL0 in Non-secure state.
The trace unit does not generate instruction trace for EL0 in Non-secure state.
The trace unit generates instruction trace for EL3.
The trace unit does not generate instruction trace for EL3.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_S_EL2
fields.description: Filter instruction trace for EL2 in Secure state.

values.label: The trace unit generates instruction trace for EL2 in Secure state.
The trace unit does not generate instruction trace for EL2 in Secure state.

fields.name: EXLEVEL_S_EL1
fields.description: Filter instruction trace for EL1 in Secure state.

values.label: The trace unit generates instruction trace for EL1 in Secure state.
The trace unit does not generate instruction trace for EL1 in Secure state.

values.label: The trace unit generates instruction trace for EL0 in Secure state.

Forced tracing of PE Resets is enabled.
Stopped State. The ViewInst start/stop function is in the stopped state.
Started State. The ViewInst start/stop function is in the started state.

fields.name: EVENT_TYPE
fields.description: Chooses the type of Resource Selector.

values.label: A single Resource Selector. TRCVICTLR.EVENT.SEL[4:0] selects the single Resource Selector, from 0-31, used to activate the resource event.

A Boolean-combined pair of Resource Selectors. TRCVICTLR.EVENT.SEL[3:0] selects the Resource Selector pair, from 0-15, that has a Boolean function that is applied to it whose output is used to activate the resource event. TRCVICTLR.EVENT.SEL[4] is RES0 .

fields.name: EVENT_SEL
fields.description: Defines the selected Resource Selector or pair of Resource Selectors. TRCVICTLR.EVENT.TYPE controls whether TRCVICTLR.EVENT.SEL is the index of a single Resource Selector, or the index of a pair of Resource Selectors.

fields.name: Reserved
fields.description: This field is reserved: Bits [4:1] are RES0 . Bit [0] is RES1 .

name: TRCVIIECTLR
description: Use this to select, or read, the Address Range Comparators for the ViewInst include/exclude function.

fields.name: EXCLUDE[<m>]
fields.description: Exclude Address Range Comparator <m>. Selects whether Address Range Comparator <m> is in use with the ViewInst exclude function.

values.label: The address range that Address Range Comparator <m> defines, is not selected for the ViewInst exclude function.
The address range that Address Range Comparator <m> defines, is selected for the ViewInst exclude function.

fields.name: INCLUDE[<m>]
fields.description: Include Address Range Comparator <m>. Selects whether Address Range Comparator <m> is in use with the ViewInst include function. Selecting no comparators for the ViewInst include function indicates that all instructions are included by default. The ViewInst exclude function then indicates which ranges are excluded.

values.label: The address range that Address Range Comparator <m> defines, is not selected for the ViewInst include function.
The address range that Address Range Comparator <m> defines, is selected for the ViewInst include function.

name: TRCVIPCSSCTLR
long_name: Trace ViewInst Start/Stop PE Comparator Control Register
description: Use this to select, or read, which PE Comparator Inputs can control the ViewInst start/stop function.

fields.name: STOP[<m>]
fields.description: Selects whether PE Comparator Input <m> is in use with the ViewInst start/stop function for the purpose of stopping trace.

values.label: The PE Comparator Input <m> is not selected as a stop resource.
The PE Comparator Input <m> is selected as a stop resource.

fields.name: START[<m>]
fields.description: Selects whether PE Comparator Input <m> is in use with the ViewInst start/stop function for the purpose of starting trace.

values.label: The PE Comparator Input <m> is not selected as a start resource.
The PE Comparator Input <m> is selected as a start resource.

name: TRCVISSCTLR
description: Use this to select, or read, the Single Address Comparators for the ViewInst start/stop function.

fields.name: STOP[<m>]
fields.description: Selects whether Single Address Comparator <m> is used with the ViewInst start/stop function for the purpose of stopping trace.

values.label: The Single Address Comparator <m> is not selected as a stop resource.
The Single Address Comparator <m> is selected as a stop resource.

fields.name: START[<m>]
fields.description: Selects whether Single Address Comparator <m> is used with the ViewInst start/stop function for the purpose of starting trace.

values.label: The Single Address Comparator <m> is not selected as a start resource.
The Single Address Comparator <m> is selected as a start resource.

name: TRCVMIDCCTLR0
long_name: Trace Virtual Context Identifier Comparator Control Register 0
description: Virtual Context Identifier Comparator mask values for the TRCVMIDCVR<n> registers, where n=0-3.

fields.name: COMP3[<m>]
fields.description: TRCVMIDCVR3 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCVMIDCVR3. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCVMIDCVR3.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCVMIDCVR3[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCVMIDCVR3[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.

fields.name: COMP2[<m>]
fields.description: TRCVMIDCVR2 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCVMIDCVR2. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCVMIDCVR2.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCVMIDCVR2[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCVMIDCVR2[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.

fields.name: COMP1[<m>]
fields.description: TRCVMIDCVR1 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCVMIDCVR1. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCVMIDCVR1.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCVMIDCVR1[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCVMIDCVR1[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.

fields.name: COMP0[<m>]
fields.description: TRCVMIDCVR0 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCVMIDCVR0. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCVMIDCVR0.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCVMIDCVR0[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCVMIDCVR0[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.

name: TRCVMIDCCTLR1
long_name: Trace Virtual Context Identifier Comparator Control Register 1
description: Virtual Context Identifier Comparator mask values for the TRCVMIDCVR<n> registers, where n=4-7.

fields.name: COMP7[<m>]
fields.description: TRCVMIDCVR7 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCVMIDCVR7. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCVMIDCVR7.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCVMIDCVR7[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCVMIDCVR7[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.

fields.name: COMP6[<m>]
fields.description: TRCVMIDCVR6 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCVMIDCVR6. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCVMIDCVR6.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCVMIDCVR6[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCVMIDCVR6[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.

fields.name: COMP5[<m>]
fields.description: TRCVMIDCVR5 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCVMIDCVR5. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCVMIDCVR5.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCVMIDCVR5[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCVMIDCVR5[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.

fields.name: COMP4[<m>]
fields.description: TRCVMIDCVR4 mask control. Specifies the mask value that the trace unit applies to TRCVMIDCVR4. Each bit in this field corresponds to a byte in TRCVMIDCVR4.

values.label: The trace unit includes TRCVMIDCVR4[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.
The trace unit ignores TRCVMIDCVR4[(m×8+7):(m×8)] when it performs the Virtual context identifier comparison.

name: TRCVMIDCVR<n>
long_name: Trace Virtual Context Identifier Comparator Value Register <n>
description: Contains the Virtual Context Identifier Comparator value.

fields.name: VALUE
fields.description: Virtual context identifier value. The width of this field is indicated by TRCIDR2 .VMIDSIZE. Unimplemented bits are RES0 . After a PE Reset, the trace unit assumes that the Virtual context identifier is zero until the PE updates the Virtual context identifier .

name: TRFCR_EL1
description: Provides EL1 controls for Trace.

fields.name: DnVM
fields.description: Reserved for software use in nested virtualization. See also TRFCR_EL2 .DnVM.

fields.name: KE
fields.description: Kernel exception enable for TRBE Profiling exceptions taken to EL1.

values.label: TRBE Profiling exceptions taken to EL1 are always masked at EL1.
Enabled TRBE Profiling exceptions taken to EL1 are masked at EL1 when PSTATE.PM is 1 and unmasked when PSTATE.PM is 0.

Disabled. TRBE Profiling exceptions for EL1 are disabled. All of the following apply: Unless enabled by a higher Exception level, TRBE Profiling exceptions are not generated. TRBSR_EL1 .IRQ drives the interrupt request signal TRBIRQ . Accesses to TRBSR_EL1 at EL1 ignore the value of HCR_EL2 .NV1.

Reserved for software use in nested virtualization. Behaves as 0b00 for the purpose of controlling the TRBE Profiling exception and interrupt request signal TRBIRQ , and as 0b11 for the purpose of accesses to TRBSR_EL1 .
Reserved for software use in nested virtualization. Behaves as 0b11 for the purposes of controlling the TRBE Profiling exception and interrupt request signal TRBIRQ , and accesses to TRBSR_EL1 .

Enabled. TRBE Profiling exceptions for EL1 are enabled, as follows: All trace buffer management events are recorded in TRBSR_EL1 , unless they are configured to be recorded in TRBSR_EL3 by MDCR_EL3 .TRBEE or TRBSR_EL2 by TRFCR_EL2 .EE. TRBE Profiling exceptions are generated and taken to EL1 when unmasked and TRBSR_EL1 .IRQ is 1, unless the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1, in which case the exception is taken to EL2. The interrupt request signal TRBIRQ is not asserted.

fields.name: TS
fields.description: Timestamp Control. Controls which timebase is used for trace timestamps.

values.label: Reserved for software use in nested virtualization. Behaves as 0b01 . See also TRFCR_EL2 .TS,
Virtual timestamp. The traced timestamp is the physical counter value minus the value of CNTVOFF_EL2 .

Guest physical timestamp. The traced timestamp is the physical counter value minus a physical offset. If any of the following are true, then the physical offset is zero, otherwise the physical offset is the value of CNTPOFF_EL2 : SCR_EL3 .ECVEn == 0. CNTHCTL_EL2 .ECV == 0. FEAT_ECV_POFF is not implemented.
Physical timestamp. The traced timestamp is the physical counter value.

fields.name: CX
fields.description: Reserved for software use in nested virtualization. See also TRFCR_EL2 .CX.

values.label: Trace is prohibited at EL1.
Trace is allowed at EL1.
Trace is prohibited at EL0.
Trace is allowed at EL0.

name: TRFCR_EL2
description: Provides EL2 controls for Trace.

fields.name: DnVM
fields.description: Disable use of physical address trace buffer pointers.

values.label: Use of physical address trace buffer pointers is permitted.
Use of physical address trace buffer pointers is disabled. The PE behaves as if TRBLIMITR_EL1 .nVM is 0.

fields.name: KE
fields.description: Kernel exception enable for TRBE Profiling exceptions taken to EL2.

values.label: TRBE Profiling exceptions taken to EL2 are always masked at EL2.
Enabled TRBE Profiling exceptions taken to EL2 are masked at EL2 when PSTATE.PM is 1 and unmasked when PSTATE.PM is 0.

Disabled. TRBE Profiling exceptions for EL2 and EL1 are disabled. All of the following apply: No trace buffer management events are recorded in TRBSR_EL2 . Unless enabled by a higher Exception level, TRBE Profiling exceptions are not generated. TRBSR_EL1 .IRQ drives the interrupt request signal TRBIRQ . Accesses to TRBSR_EL1 at EL1 ignore the value of HCR_EL2 .NV1 and accesses to TRBSR_EL1 at EL2 ignore the value of HCR_EL2 .E2H.

Delegated. TRBE Profiling exceptions for EL2 are disabled, but might be enabled for  EL1 by TRFCR_EL1 .EE. All of the following apply: No trace buffer management events are recorded in TRBSR_EL2 . TRBSR_EL2 .IRQ is ignored and TRBE Profiling exceptions are not taken to EL2, other than for the case when the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .TGE is 1.

Enabled. TRBE Profiling exceptions for EL2 are enabled for trace buffer management events targeting EL2, as follows: Trace buffer management events due to a fault on a write to the trace buffer that would generate a Data Abort exception taken to EL2 if generated by a store instruction executed at the owning Exception level are recorded in TRBSR_EL2 , unless they are configured to be recorded in TRBSR_EL3 by MDCR_EL3 .TRBEE.
    If the trace buffer owning Exception level is EL2, then this means any fault on a write to the trace buffer.
    If the trace buffer owning Exception level is EL1, then this means any of the following faults on a write to the trace buffer: Stage 2 faults. If HCR_EL2 .TEA is 1, synchronous External aborts. If HCR_EL2 .GPF is 1, Granule Protection Faults (GPFs). Trace buffer management events due to Granule Protection Check faults other than GPFs on a write to the trace buffer are recorded in TRBSR_EL2 , unless they are configured to be recorded in TRBSR_EL3 by MDCR_EL3 .TRBEE. TRBE Profiling exceptions are generated and taken to EL2 when unmasked and TRBSR_EL2 .IRQ is 1.

Trap all. TRBE Profiling exceptions for EL2 are enabled for all trace buffer management events, as follows: All trace buffer management events are recorded in TRBSR_EL2 , unless they are configured to be recorded in TRBSR_EL3 by MDCR_EL3 .TRBEE. TRBE Profiling exceptions are generated and taken to EL2 when unmasked and TRBSR_EL2 .IRQ is 1.

fields.name: TS
fields.description: Timestamp Control. Controls which timebase is used for trace timestamps.

values.label: Timestamp controlled by TRFCR_EL1 .TS or TRFCR .TS.
Virtual timestamp. The traced timestamp is the physical counter value minus the value of CNTVOFF_EL2 .

Guest physical timestamp. The traced timestamp is the physical counter value minus a physical offset. If any of the following are true, the physical offset is zero, otherwise the physical offset is the value of CNTPOFF_EL2 : SCR_EL3 .ECVEn == 0. CNTHCTL_EL2 .ECV == 0. FEAT_ECV_POFF is not implemented.
Physical timestamp. The traced timestamp is the physical counter value.
CONTEXTIDR_EL2 and VMID trace prohibited.
CONTEXTIDR_EL2 and VMID trace allowed.
Trace is prohibited at EL2.
Trace is allowed at EL2.
Trace is prohibited at EL0.
Trace is allowed at EL0.

name: TTBR0_EL1
description: Holds the base address of the translation table for the initial lookup for stage 1 of the translation of an address from the lower VA range in the EL1&0 translation regime, and other information for this translation regime.

fields.name: BADDR
fields.description: Translation table base address: Bits A[55:x] of the stage 1 translation table base address bits are in register bits[87:80, 47:x]. Bits A[(x-1):0] of the stage 1 translation table base address are zero. Address bit x is the minimum address bit required to align the translation table to the size of the table. x is calculated based on LOG2(StartTableSize), as described in VMSAv9-128. The smallest permitted value of x is 5.

fields.name: ASID
fields.description: An ASID for the translation table base address. The TCR_EL1 .A1 field selects either TTBR0_EL1.ASID or TTBR1_EL1 .ASID. If the implementation has only 8 bits of ASID, then the upper 8 bits of this field are RES0 .

fields.name: BADDR[42:0]
fields.description: This field is bits[42:0] of BADDR[50:0]. See BADDR[50:43] for the field description.

fields.name: SKL
fields.description: Skip Level associated with translation table walks using TTBR0_EL1. This determines the number of levels to be skipped from the regular start level of the stage 1 EL1&0 translation table walks using TTBR0_EL1 .

values.label: Skip 0 level from the regular start level.
Skip 1 level from the regular start level.
Skip 2 levels from the regular start level.
Skip 3 levels from the regular start level.

fields.name: CnP
fields.description: Common not Private. This bit indicates whether each entry that is pointed to by TTBR0_EL1 is a member of a common set that can be used by every PE in the Inner Shareable domain for which the value of TTBR0_EL1.CnP is 1.

values.label: The translation table entries pointed to by TTBR0_EL1, for the current translation regime and ASID, are permitted to differ from corresponding entries for TTBR0_EL1 for other PEs in the Inner Shareable domain. This is not affected by: The value of TTBR0_EL1.CnP on those other PEs. The value of the current ASID. If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, the value of the current VMID.

The translation table entries pointed to by TTBR0_EL1 are the same as the translation table entries for every other PE in the Inner Shareable domain for which the value of TTBR0_EL1.CnP is 1 and all of the following apply: The translation table entries are pointed to by TTBR0_EL1. The translation tables relate to the same translation regime. The ASID is the same as the current ASID. If EL2 is implemented and enabled in the current Security state, the value of the current VMID.

name: TTBR0_EL2
description: When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is not 1, holds the base address of the translation table for the initial lookup for stage 1 of an address translation in the EL2 translation regime, and other information for this translation regime. When the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is 1, holds the base address of the translation table for the initial lookup for stage 1 of the translation of an address from the lower VA range in the EL2&0 translation regime, and other information for this translation regime.

fields.name: BADDR[55:5]

Bits A[(x-1):0] of the stage 1 translation table base address are zero. Address bit x is the minimum address bit required to align the translation table to the size of the table. x is calculated based on LOG2(StartTableSize), as described in VMSAv9-128. The smallest permitted value of x is 5.

fields.name: ASID

fields.name: BADDR[55:5][42:0]
fields.description: This field is bits[42:0] of BADDR[55:5][50:0]. See BADDR[55:5][50:43] for the field description.

fields.name: SKL
fields.description: Skip Level associated with translation table walks using TTBR0_EL2. This determines the number of levels to be skipped from the regular start level of the stage 1 EL2&0 translation table walks using TTBR0_EL2 .

values.label: Skip 0 level from the regular start level.

values.label: The translation table entries pointed to by TTBR0_EL2 for the current translation regime, and ASID if applicable, are permitted to differ from corresponding entries for TTBR0_EL2 for other PEs in the Inner Shareable domain. The translation table entries pointed to by TTBR0_EL2 are the same as the translation table entries for every other PE in the Inner Shareable domain for which the value of TTBR0_EL2.CnP is 1 and all of the following apply: The translation table entries are pointed to by TTBR0_EL2. The translation tables relate to the same translation regime. If that translation regime is the EL2&0 regime, the ASID is the same as the current ASID.

name: UAO
description: Allows access to the User Access Override bit.

values.label: Explicit Memory Effects generated by unprivileged memory access instructions and described as unprivileged are unprivileged.
Explicit Memory Effects generated by unprivileged memory access instructions and described as unprivileged are privileged.

name: VBAR_EL1
description: Holds the vector base address for any exception that is taken to EL1.

fields.name: VBA
fields.description: Vector Base Address. Base address of the exception vectors for exceptions taken to EL1. If the implementation supports FEAT_LVA3 , then: If tagged addresses are not being used, bits [63:56] of VBAR_EL1 must be the same or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. Otherwise: If the implementation supports FEAT_LVA , then: If tagged addresses are being used, bits [55:52] of VBAR_EL1 must be the same or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If tagged addresses are not being used, bits [63:52] of VBAR_EL1 must be the same or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If the implementation does not support FEAT_LVA , then: If tagged addresses are being used, bits [55:48] of VBAR_EL1 must be the same or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If tagged addresses are not being used, bits [63:48] of VBAR_EL1 must be the same or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception.

name: VBAR_EL2
description: Holds the vector base address for any exception that is taken to EL2.

fields.name: VBA
fields.description: Vector Base Address. Base address of the exception vectors for exceptions taken to EL2. If FEAT_LVA3 is implemented: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is 1: If tagged addresses are not being used, bits [63:56] of VBAR_EL2 must be the same or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is not 1: If tagged addresses are not being used, bits [63:56] of VBAR_EL2 must be 0 or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. Otherwise : If FEAT_LVA is implemented: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is 1: If tagged addresses are being used, bits [55:52] of VBAR_EL2 must be the same or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If tagged addresses are not being used, bits [63:52] of VBAR_EL2 must be the same or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is not 1: If tagged addresses are being used, bits [55:52] of VBAR_EL2 must be 0 or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If tagged addresses are not being used, bits [63:52] of VBAR_EL2 must be 0 or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If FEAT_LVA is not implemented: If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is 1: If tagged addresses are being used, bits [55:48] of VBAR_EL2 must be the same or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If tagged addresses are not being used, bits [63:48] of VBAR_EL2 must be the same or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If the Effective value of HCR_EL2 .E2H is not 1: If tagged addresses are being used, bits [55:48] of VBAR_EL2 must be 0 or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If tagged addresses are not being used, bits [63:48] of VBAR_EL2 must be 0 or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception.

name: VBAR_EL3
description: Holds the vector base address for any exception that is taken to EL3.

fields.name: VBA
fields.description: Vector Base Address. Base address of the exception vectors for exceptions taken to EL3. If the implementation supports FEAT_LVA3 , then: If tagged addresses are not being used, bits [63:56] of VBAR_EL3 must be 0 or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. Otherwise: If the implementation supports FEAT_LVA , then: If tagged addresses are being used, bits [55:52] of VBAR_EL3 must be 0 or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If tagged addresses are not being used, bits [63:52] of VBAR_EL3 must be 0 or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If the implementation does not support FEAT_LVA , then: If tagged addresses are being used, bits [55:48] of VBAR_EL3 must be 0 or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception. If tagged addresses are not being used, bits [63:48] of VBAR_EL3 must be 0 or else the use of the vector address will result in a recursive exception.

name: VDISR_EL2
description: Records that a virtual SError exception has been consumed by an ESB instruction executed at EL1 or EL0. An indirect write to VDISR_EL2 made by an ESB instruction does not require an explicit synchronization operation for the value written to be observed by a direct read of one of the following registers occurring in program order after the ESB instruction: DISR_EL1 . DISR .

fields.name: A
fields.description: Set to 1 when an ESB instruction defers a virtual SError exception.

name: VDISR_EL3
description: Records that a delegated SError exception has been consumed by an ESB instruction executed at EL2, EL1, or EL0 when the Effective value of SCR_EL3 .DSE is 1. An indirect write to VDISR_EL3 made by an ESB instruction does not require an explicit synchronization operation for the value written to be observed by a direct read of DISR_EL1 occurring in program order after the ESB instruction.

fields.name: A
fields.description: Set to 1 when an ESB instruction defers a delegated SError exception.

name: VMECID_A_EL2
long_name: Alternate MECID for EL1&0 stage 2 translation regime
description: Alternate MECID for EL1&0 stage 2 translation regime.

fields.name: MECID
fields.description: If MECIDWidth is less than 16 bits, bits[15:MECIDWidth] are RES0 . MECIDWidth is defined in MECIDR_EL2 .MECIDWidthm1.

name: VMECID_P_EL2
long_name: Primary MECID for EL1&0 stage 2 translation regime
description: Primary MECID for EL1&0 stage 2 translation regime.

fields.name: MECID
fields.description: If MECIDWidth is less than 16 bits, bits[15:MECIDWidth] are RES0 . MECIDWidth is defined in MECIDR_EL2 .MECIDWidthm1.

name: VMPIDR_EL2
description: Holds the value of the Virtualization Multiprocessor ID. This is the value returned by EL1 reads of MPIDR_EL1, which in a multiprocessor system, provides an additional PE identification system.

fields.name: Aff3
fields.description: Affinity level 3. See the description of VMPIDR_EL2.Aff0 for more information. Aff3 is not supported in AArch32 state.

fields.name: U
fields.description: Indicates a Uniprocessor system, as distinct from PE 0 in a multiprocessor system.

values.label: Processor is part of a multiprocessor system.
Processor is part of a uniprocessor system.

fields.name: MT
fields.description: Indicates whether the lowest level of affinity consists of logical PEs that are implemented using a multithreading type approach. See the description of VMPIDR_EL2.Aff0 for more information about affinity levels.

values.label: Performance of PEs at the lowest affinity level is largely independent.
Performance of PEs at the lowest affinity level is very interdependent.

fields.name: Aff2
fields.description: Affinity level 2. See the description of VMPIDR_EL2.Aff0 for more information.

fields.name: Aff1
fields.description: Affinity level 1. See the description of VMPIDR_EL2.Aff0 for more information.

fields.name: Aff0
fields.description: Affinity level 0. The value of the MPIDR .{Aff2, Aff1, Aff0} or MPIDR_EL1 .{Aff3, Aff2, Aff1, Aff0} set of fields of each PE must be unique within the system as a whole.

name: VNCR_EL2
description: When FEAT_NV2 is implemented, holds the base address that is used to define the memory location that is accessed by transformed reads and writes of System registers.

fields.name: BADDR
fields.description: Base Address. When a register read/write is transformed to be a Load or Store, the address of the load/store is to VNCR_EL2.BADDR:Offset<11:0>. Bits[63:n] are RESS where n is one of the following: If FEAT_LVA3 is implemented, n is 57. Otherwise, if FEAT_LVA is implemented, n is 53. If FEAT_LVA is not implemented, n is 49. If the bits marked as RESS do not all have the same value as bit[n-1], then there is a CONSTRAINED UNPREDICTABLE choice between: Generating a Translation fault when translating the transformed register read/write. The bits behave as the same value as bit[n-1] for all purposes other than reading back the register. The bits are treated as the same value as bit[n-1] for all purposes.

name: VPIDR_EL2
description: Holds the value of the Virtualization Processor ID. This is the value returned by EL1 reads of MIDR_EL1 .

fields.name: Implementer
fields.description: The Implementer code. This field must hold an implementer code that has been assigned by Arm.

values.label: Reserved for software use.
